### Pass 148 - Local native-proof preflight (2026-05-06)

The "do not wait three minutes when the game is not running" pass. The live
native-proof runner is the highest-value release-blocker workflow, but it still
waited the full poll timeout when pointed at a local sidecar with no local
Palworld process. That was correct but noisy for automation and first-time
release checks.

- **Local Palworld process preflight.** `scripts/run-native-proof.ps1` now checks
  for `Palworld-Win64-Shipping` / `Palworld` before polling when the sidecar URL
  is local (`localhost`, `127.0.0.1`, or `::1`). If no process is found and the
  current proof is not already `delivery_proven`, it writes a blocked
  `latest-native-proof.json` artifact immediately and exits with an actionable
  message.
- **Remote proof runs stay supported.** New `-SkipPalworldProcessCheck` bypasses
  the local-process check for remote sidecars, unusual launchers, or support
  sessions where the watcher is not running on the same machine as Palworld.
- **Proof artifacts preserve the blocker.** The native-proof artifact writer now
  accepts `AdditionalBlockers[]`, so preflight failures are visible through the
  same release-evidence path used by `/api/release/readiness`.
- **Source guard tightened.** The existing release/publication meta-test now pins
  the process-check opt-out, local-URL gating, process detector, and persisted
  blocker path without increasing the executable test count.

Verification: PowerShell parser check for `scripts/run-native-proof.ps1`;
focused `MetaTests` `15 / 15`; `dotnet test` `645 / 645`; focused
publication audit PASS; full audit `15/15` PASS at
`artifacts/full-audit/20260506-081257/RESULTS.md`. No new test case, route,
MCP tool, feature, or drift-gated count changed.

### Pass 147 - Ollama serving-profile proof lanes (2026-05-06)

The "make the easiest local GGUF path less mysterious" pass. The previous
serving-profile passes made vLLM, SGLang, and llama.cpp lanes explicit, but
Ollama was still mostly represented as an OpenAI-compatible endpoint plus the
existing native warmup path. This pass adds Ollama-specific low-latency,
residency, memory, concurrency, and publication-proof guidance to the same
machine-readable `Capability.ServingProfile` surface.

- **Ollama startup and residency hints.** GGUF lanes now surface
  `OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH`, `OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE`, native `/api/chat` preload /
  `keep_alive`, `ollama ps` context and GPU-residency checks, and a reminder
  that ordinary companion turns should not inherit giant proof-lane contexts.
- **Ollama KV-cache and Flash Attention proof.** Serving profiles now name
  `OLLAMA_FLASH_ATTENTION=1` before testing `OLLAMA_KV_CACHE_TYPE=q8_0`, keep
  `q4_0` KV cache proof-only for strict JSON/tool-call PalLLM routes, and treat
  Ollama KV-cache dtype as a global server policy rather than a per-pack or
  per-request PalLLM feature.
- **Ollama concurrency, queue, and security controls.** Default one-player
  companion lanes now advise `OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL=1`, with
  `OLLAMA_MAX_LOADED_MODELS` and `OLLAMA_MAX_QUEUE` sized to real player QPS
  only after p95 latency, queue / 503 behavior, and GPU residency are proven.
  Non-loopback Ollama requires an authenticated local reverse proxy,
  deliberate `OLLAMA_ORIGINS`, and `OLLAMA_NO_CLOUD=1` for air-gapped release
  evidence.
- **Promotion receipts.** Ollama lanes now require `ollama ps`
  PROCESSOR/CONTEXT receipts, cold-vs-warm `load_duration`, native usage
  timing fields (`total_duration`, `prompt_eval_duration`, `eval_duration`,
  token counts), queue / 503 behavior, exact JSON/tool-call parse success, and
  deterministic fallback behavior before changing context, keep-alive,
  concurrency, or KV-cache defaults.
- **Docs and catalog synced.** `MODEL_COLLABORATION`, `API`, `ARCHITECTURE`,
  and the feature catalog now describe Ollama as a first-class measured serving
  lane alongside llama.cpp, vLLM, and SGLang. No route, schema, MCP, feature
  count, fallback, or test-count changes.

Research basis: current official Ollama context-length docs, FAQ, API usage
metrics, and environment configuration. The active `D:\Coding` sibling scan
reinforced only the proof-before-promote posture for keep-alive and KV-cache
settings; no sibling code, identity, or non-PalLLM product scope was copied.

Verification: focused `ModelTierTests` `19 / 19`; `dotnet test`
`645 / 645`; full audit `15/15` PASS at
`artifacts/full-audit/20260506-071104/RESULTS.md`.

### Pass 146 - GGUF serving-profile proof lanes (2026-05-06)

The "make the portable local lane first-class" pass. Recent model-serving
passes made vLLM and SGLang policy explicit, but pure GGUF / llama.cpp lanes
were still inheriting vLLM-shaped speculation hints. That was confusing for
the default local-first Windows operator path.

- **llama.cpp / GGUF serving hints in `Capability.ServingProfile`.** GGUF
  lanes now surface native startup guidance for loopback binding, measured
  context and slot counts, batch and ubatch sizing, automatic flash attention
  / GPU-layer posture, prompt cache sizing, and proof-gated KV-cache
  compression with `--cache-type-k` / `--cache-type-v`.
- **Native speculation instead of vLLM JSON.** Text-only GGUF lanes now emit
  llama.cpp `--spec-type ngram-simple` / `ngram-mod` proof-lane guidance
  instead of vLLM `--speculative-config` snippets. Strict JSON, tool-call,
  judge, and save-replay lanes still stay no-spec until repeated-run proof
  exists.
- **Cache, admission, and security receipts.** Serving profiles now require
  measuring second-turn latency, prompt-cache reuse, slot eviction, cache RAM
  pressure, active KV memory, accepted/generated token statistics, exact parse
  success, and fallback behavior before GGUF cache/speculation settings are
  promoted. Non-loopback llama.cpp endpoints must use API-key protection and
  keep MCP proxy/tools/props/slots surfaces admin-only.
- **Docs and catalog synced.** `MODEL_COLLABORATION`, `API`, `ARCHITECTURE`,
  and the feature catalog now describe GGUF proof lanes as measured serving
  policy, not PalLLM chat-path dependencies. `HANDOFF.md` also had a stale
  "docs plus manifests" pseudo-path corrected to "docs and manifests" so the path
  reference audit stays clean.

Research basis: current official llama.cpp server and speculative-decoding
docs, plus current vLLM/SGLang context from the previous serving passes.
Active `D:\Coding` sibling scan contributed the idea of a native GGUF fallback
checklist only; no sibling code, identity, or non-PalLLM product scope was
copied.

Verification: focused `ModelTierTests` `19 / 19`; `dotnet test`
`645 / 645`; full audit `15/15` PASS at
`artifacts/full-audit/20260506-061607/RESULTS.md`.

### Pass 145 - Honor PalSavedRoot/RuntimeFolderName in pal scripts (2026-05-06)

The "stop ignoring the operator's runtime-root override" correctness pass.
`Get-PalLlmRuntimeRoot` in `scripts/PalLLM.Tooling.ps1` was hardcoded to
return `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Pal\Saved\PalLLM` regardless of the operator's
`PalLLM:PalSavedRoot` and `PalLLM:RuntimeFolderName` settings. Any operator
who redirected the runtime tree to a fast SSD or a network share saw
multiple downstream scripts read from the wrong place.

- **`Get-PalLlmRuntimeRoot` now honors config.** Checks the same candidate
  appsettings.json paths `connect-ollama` and `pal-config-*` already
  consult (LOCALAPPDATA-published config, sidecar publish dir, source-tree
  config), parses the two knobs, and resolves the runtime root the same
  way the runtime itself does:
  - `PalSavedRoot` set + `RuntimeFolderName` -> `<PalSavedRoot>/<RuntimeFolderName>`
  - `PalSavedRoot` unset + `RuntimeFolderName` non-default -> `%LOCALAPPDATA%/Pal/Saved/<RuntimeFolderName>`
  - Neither set or no readable config -> `%LOCALAPPDATA%/Pal/Saved/PalLLM` (historical default)
  Pure read; never writes. Falls back to the default on malformed JSON
  rather than throwing.
- **`pal pack copy` now uses the helper.** Previously hardcoded the
  default LOCALAPPDATA path, so an operator with a custom PalSavedRoot
  copied the sample pack to the wrong tree (where the sidecar would
  never find it).
- **`pal next`'s filesystem pack probe now uses the helper.**
  Previously scanned the default LOCALAPPDATA pack dir, so the
  NO PERSONALITY suggestion fired even on a custom-rooted deployment
  that had packs installed.
- **Five existing helper consumers get the fix transparently.**
  `compute-release-checksums.ps1`, `install-mod.ps1`, `pal-health.ps1`,
  `pal-proof.ps1`, `PalLLM.InstallManifest.ps1`, and `play-palllm.ps1`
  all already called `Get-PalLlmRuntimeRoot`; they now read the
  configured root automatically with zero edits.

No new code paths, no contract changes. Pure correctness fix that
multiplies across every script in the PalLlm.Tooling.ps1 consumer set.

Smoke verified:
- `. scripts/PalLLM.Tooling.ps1; Get-PalLlmRuntimeRoot` returns the
  default `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Pal\Saved\PalLLM` on a config-less machine.
- `pal pack copy -RuntimeRoot <tmp>` still honors the explicit override
  (precedence: param > config > default).

Verification: `dotnet test` `645 / 645`; full audit `15/15` PASS at
`artifacts/full-audit/20260506-051746/RESULTS.md`.

### Pass 144 - SGLang serving-profile proof lanes + MCP count resync (2026-05-06)

The "make the non-vLLM serving path first-class" pass. Recent local-model
passes covered LoRA, MTP, and KV-cache policy; this pass fills the remaining
SGLang gap in the same machine-readable `Capability.ServingProfile` surface.

- **SGLang serving hints in the model-collaboration contract.** vLLM remains
  fully covered, but SGLang lanes now get explicit advisory policy too:
  keep radix cache enabled for normal PalLLM turns, tune
  `--mem-fraction-static`, `--max-running-requests`, and
  `--chunked-prefill-size` against p95 player latency, launch
  qualification runs with `--enable-metrics`, and reserve
  `--enable-deterministic-inference` for reproducible proof / replay / judge
  lanes with a supported attention backend.
- **Structured-output and request-shape guardrails.** SGLang structured
  output lanes now surface `--grammar-backend xgrammar` guidance plus
  `json_schema` and `structural_tag` qualification checks. Ordinary companion
  traffic stays on the OpenAI-compatible chat API; EBNF, structural tags, and
  native sampling params are proof/tool-lane concerns until parser tests pin
  them.
- **Cache, metrics, admission, and security proof.** Serving profiles now
  require recording SGLang `cache_hit_rate`, token usage, TTFT, ITL,
  running/queued request counts, and PalLLM fallback behavior before changing
  live policy. Non-loopback SGLang endpoints must be API-key protected, keep
  `/metrics` private, and sit behind body/rate limits.
- **Doc/code count resync.** Several current docs and manifests still said
  "36 MCP tools" after Pass 141 moved the live surface to 38. Resynced
  `.cursorrules`, `agents.json`, README, MCP docs, readiness docs,
  `SelfDescriptionBuilder`, and the feature-catalog MCP entry so `/api/describe`
  and agent-facing docs agree with the live runtime.

Research basis: current official SGLang server-argument, prefix-cache,
structured-output, deterministic-inference, and production-metrics docs.
Active `D:\Coding` sibling scan contributed only transferable operator-proof
patterns (runtime qualification, external-validation packets, support evidence);
no sibling code, identity, or non-PalLLM product scope was copied.

Verification: focused `ModelTierTests` `19 / 19`; `dotnet test`
`645 / 645`; full audit `15/15` PASS at
`artifacts/full-audit/20260506-051738/RESULTS.md`.

### Pass 143 - SUGGESTIONS.md architecture doc (2026-05-06)

The "lift the cumulative architecture out of CHANGELOG entries and into a
discoverable doc" pass. The runtime-suggestion surface accumulated across
11 passes (132-142). A new contributor or coding agent reading the
codebase had to assemble the design from scattered changelog bullets.

- **New doc: `docs/SUGGESTIONS.md`.** Single page consolidating the
  whole surface:
  - **Why this surface exists** -- the problem it solves (scattered
    "is anything broken?" signals before Pass 132).
  - **The data model** -- `HealthSuggestion` record fields with their
    contracts, `HealthSuggestionInputs` value record listing the 20
    runtime fields the builder reads, `HealthSuggestionBuilder.Severity`
    constants with their assignment rule (urgent for chat-breaking
    failures, warn for dark lanes that don't break chat, info for
    common operator states).
  - **Ordering contract** -- urgent > warn > info, stable secondary so
    dashboards don't flap between polls.
  - **Live hint codes** -- a 12-row table mapping code -> severity ->
    trigger condition. Maintained alongside any future builder edit.
  - **Consumer surfaces** -- a 9-row table mapping audience (API /
    operator-CLI / operator-UI / agent-MCP) to entry point and how it
    consumes the surface. Documents the invariant that any new hint
    reaches all 9 surfaces with no consumer-side edit.
  - **How to add a new hint code** -- a five-step recipe (builder edit
    + inputs extension + tests + this doc + audit), with the explicit
    list of files NOT to touch (the leverage proposition).
  - **Pass history** -- newest-first list of every pass that built up
    the surface, with one-line summaries; the operator can read this
    backwards to recover the design timeline.
  - **Related** -- cross-links to INVARIANTS, COOKBOOK, API, and
    MCP_QUICKSTART so the doc isn't an island.
- **`COOKBOOK.md` Index row added** pointing at
  `SUGGESTIONS.md#how-to-add-a-new-hint-code`. The recipe shows up
  alongside the existing nine "how do I add X?" recipes (HTTP endpoint,
  fallback strategy, MCP tool, etc.) without duplicating prose -- the
  cookbook just links into SUGGESTIONS.md for the actual steps.
- **`INDEX.md` cross-link** under the agent-doc group so an agent
  loading the doc map sees the new file without scrolling.
- **`docs/PROJECT_NUMBERS.json` docsCount** rolls `60 -> 61`.

No code, route, MCP tool, feature catalog, fallback strategy, or test
changes. Pure documentation pass that turns 11 passes of accumulated
architecture into one referencable doc. Future contributors who land
on the codebase no longer need to read CHANGELOG entries 132-142 to
understand the runtime-suggestion design -- one doc explains it.

Verification: Drift_Doc_freshness PASS (the new doc's 2026-05-06
stamp is well within the 45-day cap); Drift_Dangling_markdown_links
PASS (238 link targets checked, all resolve); `dotnet test`
`645 / 645`; full audit `15/15` PASS at
`artifacts/full-audit/20260506-051306/RESULTS.md`.

### Pass 142 - One-click Copy on Suggestion command cards (2026-05-06)

The "lower the friction on the most common operator action" pass. Every
Suggestion card the dashboard renders carries a copy-paste Command from
the builder (`pal pack copy companion-warrior`, `pal doctor`, etc.).
Operators were previously triple-clicking the monospace block + Ctrl-C
to copy. This pass adds a one-click Copy button next to every command.

- **Per-command Copy button.** Each card with a non-null `Command`
  field now renders a flex row: the existing monospace `<pre>` block on
  the left, a `<button>Copy</button>` on the right. Same height as the
  command block.
- **Modern + fallback clipboard paths.** First tries
  `navigator.clipboard.writeText(command)` on secure contexts; falls
  back to a hidden-textarea + `document.execCommand("copy")` for older
  browsers and non-HTTPS local pages (the dashboard runs on
  `http://localhost:5088` by default so this path matters).
- **Transient visual feedback.** On click the button text swaps to
  "Copied!" with a green background, or "Copy failed" with red, then
  resets after 1.5 seconds. The class toggle (`is-copied` /
  `is-copy-failed`) drives the colour swap; the JS click handler does
  the timed reset.
- **Event delegation on the list.** The handler is wired once at module
  load (`wireSuggestionsCopyHandler()`); `renderSuggestions()` rewriting
  the list doesn't need to re-bind. Same pattern the rest of the
  dashboard uses for dynamically-rendered cards.
- **Mobile-friendly.** The `.suggestions-command-row` flex container
  collapses to column at viewport &lt;640px so the button sits below
  rather than being squeezed off-screen.
- **No backend / contract changes.** Pure HTML / CSS / JS consumption
  of the existing Pass-132 Suggestions[] surface. Test count, route
  count, MCP tool count, and OpenAPI snapshot all unchanged.

The dashboard Suggestions surface, end of Pass 142, is now in a
tight production shape:
- Auto-hides when healthy (Pass 134).
- Severity-aware colour cards (Pass 135 + 137).
- Severity-first ordering so Suggestions[0] is always the most
  pressing (Pass 139).
- Topbar count badge visible from any scroll position (Pass 141).
- One-click Copy on every actionable command (Pass 142).

Verification: `dotnet test` `645 / 645`; full audit `15/15` PASS at
`artifacts/full-audit/20260506-050736/RESULTS.md`.

### Pass 141 - pal_status MCP tool + dashboard topbar badge (2026-05-06)

The "make state visible from any scroll position" pass. Pass 140 finished the
CLI / MCP coverage; this pass closes the two remaining visibility gaps: an
agent-facing one-shot summary tool that pairs with the `pal status` CLI verb,
and a topbar count badge so dashboard operators see urgent state without
scrolling.

- **New MCP tool: `pal_status`.** The structured agent-facing equivalent
  of `pal status`. Single tool call returns:
  - `Score` (0-100) + `Grade` (Excellent / Good / Degraded / Critical) +
    `Summary` from `OperatorHealthScorer`.
  - `SuggestionsTotal`, `SuggestionsUrgent`, `SuggestionsWarn`,
    `SuggestionsInfo` -- per-severity counts with the invariant
    `urgent + warn + info == total`.
  - `TopSuggestionCode` -- the most pressing entry's `Code` (always
    present in the response; null when the runtime is healthy so
    consumers don't need a missing-key check).
  - Headline configuration flags: `InferenceConfigured`,
    `InferenceCircuitState`, `VisionEnabled`, `TtsEnabled`,
    `BridgeEnabled`, `LoadedPackCount`, `OutboxPendingCount`,
    `InboxPendingCount`.
  Replaces the previous three-call dance (`pal_health_score` +
  `pal_health_suggestions` + `pal_describe`) with one call when an agent
  just needs "is everything OK?". MCP tool count rolls `37 -> 38`.
- **Topbar suggestions badge in the Field Console.** A colour-coded
  count chip in the status strip that appears whenever live
  Suggestions[] are firing, hidden via `.suggestions-badge.is-empty`
  when the runtime is healthy. Visible from any scroll position on
  any panel -- an operator deep in the chat panel still sees the
  urgent signal without scrolling back to the Suggestions card.
  Clicking jumps to the full Suggestions surface (`href="#suggestions"`).
  Colour matches the highest severity bucket present
  (red urgent / amber warn / blue info). Text breaks down totals:
  `"3 signals (1 urgent, 2 warn, 0 info)"`. Driven by the same
  `refreshSuggestions` poll the Suggestions panel already runs, so no
  extra HTTP traffic.
- **One new test** (`McpEndpoint_ToolsCall_Status_ReturnsCompactSummary`)
  pins the contract: 0-100 numeric Score, Grade in the four allowed
  bands, per-severity counts whose sum equals SuggestionsTotal,
  headline configuration flags present, `TopSuggestionCode` property
  always present so consumers can rely on it. Plus the existing
  `McpEndpoint_ToolsList_DiscoversAllRegisteredTools` allowlist gained
  `pal_status` so discovery stays in sync.

Test count rolls `644 -> 645`. MCP tool count rolls `37 -> 38` across
all nine doc surfaces (`PROJECT_NUMBERS.json`, `agents.json`, `README.md`,
`CHANGELOG.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `docs/HANDOFF.md`, `docs/READINESS.md`,
AGENTIC_PATTERNS_2026 (retired Pass 418), `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` Mermaid).

The diagnostic surface is now end-to-end production-shape with
both an at-a-glance topbar badge and a one-call agent summary:
- Operators (CLI): `pal next` / `pal doctor` / `pal status`
  (one-line baseline + live count summary).
- Operators (UI): Field Console with topbar badge (any scroll
  position) + Suggestions surface cards.
- Agents (MCP): `pal_status` (one call) or `pal_health_score` +
  `pal_health_suggestions` (separate calls).
- API: `/api/health` with Suggestions[] inline + the same data via
  /api/quickstart for first-run guidance.

Verification: focused
`McpEndpoint_ToolsCall_Status_ReturnsCompactSummary` `1 / 1`,
`McpEndpoint_ToolsList_DiscoversAllRegisteredTools` `1 / 1`;
`dotnet test` `645 / 645`; full audit `15/15` PASS at
`artifacts/full-audit/20260506-050004/RESULTS.md`.

### Pass 140 - pal_health_score MCP tool + doctor urgent-exit gate (2026-05-06)

The "make the diagnostic surface complete and CI-correct" pass. Two parallel
improvements that close gaps in the Pass-132 Suggestions[] surface
architecture without inventing new concepts.

- **New MCP tool: `pal_health_score`.** Wraps the existing
  `OperatorHealthScorer.Score()` function (already used internally by
  `pal_why`) so any MCP-aware agent can answer "is the runtime healthy?"
  in one tool call. Returns the numeric Score (0-100), the Grade
  (Excellent / Good / Degraded / Critical), the one-paragraph Summary,
  and the top three penalty Reasons that drove the score down.
  Companion to `pal_health_suggestions`: the score is the coarse
  verdict, the suggestions are the specific copy-paste actions. MCP
  tool count moves `36 -> 37`.
- **`pal doctor` now exits non-zero on urgent suggestions.** Previously
  doctor only failed on FAIL static checks. Now any urgent severity
  runtime suggestion (`inference-circuit-open`,
  `inference-only-failures`) also fails the verb so CI pipelines, VSCode
  tasks, and pre-commit hooks treat active failures the same way they
  treat broken installs. Warn / info severity stays advisory --
  common in-progress states (no packs loaded, bridge enabled but never
  booted) don't fail the verb during normal use.
- **Latent doctor read-bug fixed.** The Pass-133 "Runtime suggestions"
  section in doctor.ps1 was silently empty because it was inspecting
  `$healthPayload` (the `Invoke-JsonGet` wrapper) for a `Suggestions`
  property that actually lives on `$healthPayload.Json`. The new read
  path drills into `.Json` first; the section now fires whenever
  `/api/health` carries hints. The previously-inserted CLI severity
  coloring + urgent-exit logic both depend on this fix.
- **One new test** (`McpEndpoint_ToolsCall_HealthScore_ReturnsScoreAndGrade`)
  pins the contract: 0-100 numeric Score, Grade in
  `{Excellent, Good, Degraded, Critical}`, non-empty Summary, TopReasons
  array present. Plus the existing
  `McpEndpoint_ToolsList_DiscoversAllRegisteredTools` allowlist gained
  `pal_health_score` so the discovery contract stays in sync.

Test count rolls `643 -> 644`. MCP tool count rolls `36 -> 37` across all
nine surfaces (`PROJECT_NUMBERS.json`, `agents.json`, `README.md`,
`CHANGELOG.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `docs/HANDOFF.md`, `docs/READINESS.md`,
AGENTIC_PATTERNS_2026 (retired Pass 418), `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` Mermaid).

The diagnostic surface is now production-shape end-to-end:
- Operators get `pal next` (single recommended action), `pal doctor`
  (full diagnostic with PASS/FAIL summary plus colored runtime
  suggestions, exits non-zero on urgent), `pal status` (one-line
  baseline + live count summary).
- The Field Console renders Quickstart steps, color-coded
  Suggestions cards, and a /api/health JSON link.
- MCP-aware agents get `pal_health_score` (one-shot verdict),
  `pal_health_suggestions` (specific actions), and the existing
  `pal_quickstart`, `pal_self_healing_status`, `pal_why`,
  `pal_airgap_verify`, `pal_describe`.
- API consumers see the full snapshot at `/api/health` with the
  same Suggestions[] inline.

Verification: focused
`McpEndpoint_ToolsCall_HealthScore_ReturnsScoreAndGrade` `1 / 1`,
`McpEndpoint_ToolsList_DiscoversAllRegisteredTools` `1 / 1`;
`dotnet test` `644 / 644`; full audit `15/15` PASS at
`artifacts/full-audit/20260506-044806/RESULTS.md`.

### Pass 139 - Proof-gated KV-cache compression guidance (2026-05-06)

The "make KV-cache dtype a measured lane setting" pass.

- **Serving profiles now name KV-cache dtype proof.** vLLM-like
  `Capability.ServingProfile` lanes add optional `--kv-cache-dtype fp8`
  startup guidance for memory-pressure or long-context setups, with `nvfp4`
  framed as hardware/server-qualified only. Cache and verification hints require
  replaying PalLLM JSON, tool-call, companion, and long-context proof turns
  against the same lane using `auto` KV cache before promotion.
- **`pal connect vllm` emits the same modern cache flags.** The one-click
  vLLM wizard now includes `--prefix-caching-hash-algo sha256_cbor`,
  `--enable-chunked-prefill`, and `--kv-cache-dtype fp8` for FP4/FP8 recipes.
  It reports the chosen KV-cache dtype in its object output, reports `DryRun`
  accurately when used without `-WriteConfig`, and avoids the deprecated
  dynamic `--calculate-kv-scales` path.
- **Docs and catalog synced.** `MODEL_COLLABORATION`, `API`, `ARCHITECTURE`,
  `BLACKWELL_RECIPES`, and the feature catalog now describe KV-cache dtype as
  a proof-gated memory/context tradeoff rather than a generic speed switch.
  Sibling-project scan contributed only the idea of measured KV profiles and
  promotion receipts; no sibling code or identity was copied.

No route, MCP, schema, feature-count, fallback-count, or executable test-count
changes. Verification: focused `ModelTierTests` + `MetaTests` `34 / 34`;
`connect-vllm.ps1 -DryRun` prints the modern vLLM cache flags and reports
`DryRun=True`; `dotnet test` `643 / 643`; full audit `15/15` PASS at
`artifacts/full-audit/20260506-041930/RESULTS.md`.

### Pass 138 - Severity-first ordering + pal status live summary (2026-05-06)

The "make Suggestions[0] always the right one-card pick" pass. Pass 137 added
severity coloring; this pass closes the matching ordering gap so the most
pressing entry is always at index 0, plus surfaces a live count summary in
`pal status` so operators see critical state without leaving the verb.

- **Severity-first ordering in the builder.** `HealthSuggestionBuilder.Build`
  now sorts the result list urgent > warn > info, stable within each bucket
  (LINQ `OrderBy` -- the detection order acts as the tiebreaker so the
  output stays deterministic). Every consumer benefits without an edit:
  - Dashboard cards now render urgent first, so the most important info is
    visible without scrolling.
  - `pal next` and `pal doctor` print the most pressing line first.
  - `Suggestions[0]` is always the right pick for any UI or agent that
    only has room for one card.
  Unknown severity values (which shouldn't exist but might if a future
  builder addition forgets to set the field) sort last so the codes that
  did declare severity correctly never get shadowed.
- **Live suggestion summary in `pal status`.** The verb now does a
  one-second probe of `/api/health` when the sidecar is reachable and
  prints a one-line counted summary:
  - With suggestions: `"Live runtime: 3 suggestion(s) -- 1 urgent, 2 warn,
    0 info"`, colored by the most severe present (red/yellow/cyan) +
    a `details : pwsh ./pal.ps1 next` follow-up hint.
  - Healthy: `"Live runtime: 0 suggestions (healthy)"` in green.
  - Sidecar offline: section omitted entirely. `pal status` stays fast
    (<1 s) and grep-able; the static baseline section above already gave
    the operator the offline answer.
  Honors `$env:PALLLM_BASE_URL` for non-default ports.
- **One new test** in `HealthSuggestionBuilderTests`:
  `Build_OrdersBySeverityUrgentFirst` trips one hint per severity bucket
  and asserts urgent < warn < info index-by-index, plus that
  `Suggestions[0].Severity == "urgent"` whenever an urgent hint exists.
  Pins the ordering contract so a future shuffle of the builder can't
  silently break the one-card-pick invariant.

Test count rolls `642 -> 643`. No new routes, MCP tools, feature catalog
entries, or fallback strategies. Pure ordering-contract + CLI
consumer-side polish.

The Suggestions[] surface is now production-shape: 12 hint codes,
3 severity buckets, severity-first ordering, color-coded across all
5 consumers, plus a live count summary in `pal status`. Adding a
13th hint is still one builder entry; the order, color, and status
summary all pick it up automatically.

Verification: focused `HealthSuggestionBuilderTests` `20 / 20` (19 -> 20);
`dotnet test` `643 / 643`; full audit `15/15` PASS at
`artifacts/full-audit/20260506-041227/RESULTS.md`.

### Pass 137 - Severity-aware CLI coloring + 2 new hint codes (2026-05-04)

The "make every consumer use the severity field already in the contract" pass.
Pass 136 added Severity to the HealthSuggestion record and the dashboard cards
read it. `pal next` and `pal doctor` were still printing every entry in plain
yellow. This pass closes the visual gap and adds two more hint codes for real
operator footguns.

- **CLI severity-aware coloring.** `pal next` and `pal doctor` now switch on
  the entry's Severity:
  - **Red** -- urgent (active failure breaking chat).
  - **Yellow** -- warn (mildly off, look at it within a session).
  - **Cyan** -- info (common operator state, not a problem).
  - **Green** -- the "Try: <command>" line, drawing the eye to the
    copy-paste action.
  Same model the dashboard cards already used; same source of truth (the
  builder). A new hint code lights up the right colour everywhere with no
  consumer edits needed.
- **Two new hint codes:**
  - `automation-allowlist-empty-but-enabled` (warn) -- the real footgun.
    Operator flips `PalLLM:Automation:Enabled=true` and forgets to add
    action ids to `AllowedActions`. The runtime correctly emits zero
    intents and the operator wonders why nothing happens.
  - `screenshots-pending-but-vision-disabled` (info) -- the watcher is
    queueing files into `Runtime/Screenshots/Pending/` with no vision
    lane to consume them. Either flip `PalLLM:Vision:Enabled=true` or
    `PalLLM:Vision:EnableScreenshotWatcher=false`. Without action the
    queue grows until `PendingScreenshotMaxFiles` starts dropping.
- **`HealthSuggestionInputs` extended** with three new fields
  (`AutomationEnabled`, `AutomationAllowedActionCount`,
  `ScreenshotPendingCount`). The runtime call site reads each from the
  existing options/directory snapshot -- no new I/O.
- **Four new tests** in `HealthSuggestionBuilderTests` covering both new
  codes (positive + no-suggest branches) and the smoke test gained the
  `automation-allowlist-empty-but-enabled` trip path.

Test count rolls `638 -> 642`. No new routes, MCP tools, feature catalog
entries, or fallback strategies. Pure widening of the existing
Suggestions[] surface.

The Suggestions[] fan-out now covers 12 distinct hint codes, three
severity buckets, and 5 consumers (`/api/health` curl, dashboard, `pal
next`, `pal doctor`, MCP `pal_health_suggestions`). Adding code 13 is
one entry in the builder; everything else picks it up automatically.

Verification: focused `HealthSuggestionBuilderTests` `19 / 19` (15 -> 19);
`dotnet test` `642 / 642`; full audit `15/15` PASS at
`artifacts/full-audit/20260506-035245/RESULTS.md`.

### Pass 136 - HealthSuggestion severity + 3 new hint codes (2026-05-04)

The "single source of truth for severity, multiplied by three new codes" pass.
The Pass-132 Suggestions[] surface had grown to five consumers (curl, dashboard,
pal next, pal doctor, MCP). The dashboard JS carried a hardcoded code-to-severity
map -- a drift trap waiting to happen the next time the builder added a code.
This pass promotes severity into the contract and adds three high-value hints.

- **`Severity` is now a first-class field on `HealthSuggestion`.** Three
  buckets: `info`, `warn`, `urgent`. Stable strings so consumers switch on
  them without enums leaking through the wire. `HealthSuggestionBuilder` is
  the single source of truth: every hint declares its own severity at
  construction. New `HealthSuggestionBuilder.Severity` constants class
  exposes the values for callers (and tests) that need to compare.
- **Three new hint codes:**
  - `vision-only-failures` (warn) -- vision configured + every recorded
    request failed. Catches the painful "vision was working last week but
    the gemma image model got pulled" case. Chat path keeps working;
    screenshot description is dark.
  - `tts-only-failures` (warn) -- same shape for TTS. Chat replies still
    render as text; spoken audio is dark until the synth server recovers.
  - `outbox-backlog` (warn) -- >50 pending replies in `Bridge/Outbox/`.
    Usually Palworld is closed, the mod is unloaded, or the consumer
    stalled. New replies continue queueing; nothing is lost.
- **Severity assignment matches design intent.** Active failure surfaces
  (inference circuit open, inference all-failures) are urgent. Lane
  failures that don't break chat (vision, TTS, queue backlogs) are warn.
  Common operator states (no packs loaded, bridge enabled but never
  booted) are info -- the runtime is working as designed; the operator
  may simply not have launched Palworld yet.
- **`HealthSuggestionInputs` extended** with `VisionEnabled` /
  `VisionCallCount` / `VisionFailureCount` / `TtsEnabled` /
  `TtsCallCount` / `TtsFailureCount` / `OutboxPendingCount`. The
  `PalLlmRuntime.BuildRuntimeHealth` call site reads the existing
  counters once into locals and passes them to both the builder and
  the assembled `RuntimeHealth` -- no double-reads.
- **Dashboard JS dropped the hardcoded severity map.** `app.js` now reads
  `entry.Severity` directly. Unknown values fall back to `warn` so future
  builder additions still render a visible card.
- **Tests updated to a `Healthy()` baseline + `with`-expression pattern**
  so each test focuses on one flipped field. Five new tests cover the
  new hint codes and the severity contract; the new
  `Build_EveryHintCarriesNonEmptyCodeMessageAndSeverity` smoke test trips
  every code at once and verifies all required fields populate.
- **OpenAPI snapshot regenerated** to absorb the new `Severity` field.

Test count rolls `633 -> 638`. No new routes, MCP tools, feature catalog
entries, or fallback strategies. The runtime's existing call site already
read every counter the new inputs expose; the builder change just gave it
a bigger view of the snapshot.

Verification: focused `HealthSuggestionBuilderTests` `15 / 15` (10 -> 15);
`dotnet test` `638 / 638`; full audit `15/15` PASS at
`artifacts/full-audit/20260506-034441/RESULTS.md`.

### Pass 135 - Sanitized chat ProblemDetails + model-native MTP guidance (2026-05-06)

The production-hardening and latest-model-serving pass.

- **Sanitized `/api/chat` unexpected-error bodies.** The endpoint still logs
  the real exception for operators, but the public ProblemDetails detail no
  longer includes exception type names, implementation class names, or local
  filesystem paths. This closes a direct invariant gap: `/api/*` error bodies
  must not leak implementation details.
- **Regression coverage for the leak.** `SidecarEndpointTests` now injects a
  throwing `IInferenceClient` whose raw exception contains
  `InvalidOperationException`, `ThrowingInferenceClient`, and a fake local
  `D:\secret\model.bin` path, then asserts the 500 response is generic
  `application/problem+json` and contains none of those strings. Test count
  rolls `632 -> 633`.
- **Guarded Qwen3.6 / Gemma 4 MTP serving hints.** The deterministic
  collaboration planner now emits Qwen startup hints for
  `--reasoning-parser qwen3`, optional `qwen3_next_mtp`, and
  `--language-model-only` text-only lanes; Gemma 4 lanes now advertise
  tool-call fit and matching-drafter MTP qualification guidance.
- **Promotion stays proof-first.** Request and verification hints keep strict
  JSON/tool-call/judge/save-replay routes no-spec until route-specific replay
  evidence records TTFT, ITL, accepted/proposed ratio, fallback behavior, and
  parser stability. Qwen3.6 long-context notes steer ordinary companion turns
  away from oversized KV-cache footprints while reserving 128K+ windows for
  proof, docs-sync, and deliberate review lanes.
- **Fresh research + sibling scan.** Research covered current Microsoft
  ProblemDetails guidance, vLLM MTP/Qwen/Gemma 4 recipes, Google's Gemma 4
  MTP note, and active `D:\Coding` sibling projects. Only serving-policy ideas
  were carried forward; no sibling code, names, or product identity were
  copied.

Verification: focused `SidecarEndpointTests` + `ModelTierTests` `72 / 72`;
`dotnet test` `633 / 633`; full audit `15/15` PASS at
`artifacts/full-audit/20260506-033145/RESULTS.md`.

### Pass 134 - Live runtime Suggestions panel in the Field Console (2026-05-04)

The "close the last fan-out gap" pass. The Suggestions[] surface added in
Pass 132 was already reachable via curl (/api/health), CLI (`pal next`,
`pal doctor`), and MCP (`pal_health_suggestions`). The dashboard at `/`
was the last operator entry point that didn't surface them. This pass
closes the loop.

- **New "Runtime Suggestions" surface in the Field Console** between
  Quickstart and Why. Section nav entry added so jump-navigation works.
  Sourced from the same `/api/health.suggestions[]` other consumers
  read.
- **Color-coded severity cards.** Each entry renders as a card whose
  border + label color matches the severity bucket the suggestion
  Code maps to:
  - `inference-circuit-open`, `inference-only-failures` &rarr; **urgent**
    (red).
  - `bridge-failed-files-accumulating`, `bridge-inbox-backlog` &rarr;
    **warn** (amber).
  - `bridge-idle`, `no-packs-loaded` &rarr; **info** (blue).
  - Codes not listed default to **warn** so a future builder
    addition still lights up before someone updates the JS map.
- **Copy-paste Command block.** When the suggestion carries a
  Command (e.g. `pal pack copy companion-warrior`, `pal doctor`), it's
  rendered in a monospace `<pre>` so operators can select-and-copy
  with one click instead of fighting with the surrounding prose.
- **Hidden when healthy.** The `.suggestions-surface.is-empty` CSS
  modifier collapses the panel to zero size when the Suggestions[]
  array is empty, so a healthy dashboard doesn't carry a "ghost"
  panel. The CSS handles the hide; JS just toggles the class.
- **Refresh cadence integration.** New `refreshSuggestions()` is
  hooked into the existing `wrappedRefresh` chain alongside
  `refreshQuickstart()`, so manual refreshes and the auto-refresh
  toggle keep the panel current without a separate polling loop.
- **Fail-quiet on transport errors.** If `/api/health` is briefly
  unavailable (sidecar restart, network blip), the panel hides
  rather than showing a confusing error -- the rest of the
  dashboard already surfaces hard sidecar errors prominently.

No new C# code, routes, MCP tools, feature catalog entries, fallback
strategies, OpenAPI changes, or tests. Pure HTML/CSS/JS consumption of
the existing Pass-132 surface.

**Coverage of Suggestions[] surface, end of Pass 134:**
| Consumer | How it surfaces hints |
| --- | --- |
| `curl /api/health` | Top-level `Suggestions` array in the JSON |
| `pal next` advisor | "Runtime suggestions" section under the verdict |
| `pal doctor` | "Runtime suggestions" section after the check table |
| MCP `pal_health_suggestions` | Returns the array directly to any MCP client |
| Field Console dashboard | "Runtime Suggestions" surface, color-coded cards |

Verification: `dotnet test` `632 / 632`; full audit `15/15` PASS at
`artifacts/full-audit/20260506-031856/RESULTS.md`.

### Pass 133 - Health-suggestions surface fan-out (MCP + doctor) (2026-05-04)

The "make Pass-132 reach every consumer" pass. The Suggestions[] surface
landed in /api/health in Pass 132 and was already being read by `pal next`
under "Runtime suggestions". This pass extends it to two more entry points
operators reach for when something's wrong.

- **New MCP tool: `pal_health_suggestions`.** Exposes the live
  Suggestions[] over the MCP Streamable HTTP protocol so any MCP-aware
  agent (Claude Desktop, VS Code, Cursor, ChatGPT, custom clients) can
  ask "what should I do right now?" without scraping /api/health or
  parsing the full RuntimeHealth payload. Returns the array directly
  (possibly empty when healthy); each entry carries `Code`, `Message`,
  optional `Command`. MCP tool count moves `35 -> 36`. OpenAPI snapshot
  is unchanged because MCP is a separate protocol surface at /mcp.
- **`scripts/doctor.ps1` integration.** When the sidecar is reachable,
  doctor now appends a "Runtime suggestions" section after its
  PASS/WARN/FAIL summary, listing each hint with its code, message, and
  copy-paste command. Advisory only -- a player whose runtime is
  perfectly healthy never sees this section. This complements
  doctor's static checks: doctor confirms the sidecar is reachable and
  the bridge config is sane; the suggestions tell the operator the
  bridge has zero events because UE4SS isn't attached yet, or that the
  inference circuit is open because Ollama crashed.
- **Focused MCP integration test** in `McpEndpointTests` pins the new
  tool: response shape (JSON array), every entry's `Code` and `Message`
  non-empty so dashboard / pal-next consumers don't need null-guards.
  Also added the new tool name to the existing
  `McpEndpoint_ToolsList_DiscoversAllRegisteredTools` allowlist so the
  discovery contract stays in sync.

Test count rolls `631 -> 632`. Doc anchors moved across all 9 surfaces
referencing the MCP tool count (`PROJECT_NUMBERS.json`, `agents.json`,
`README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `docs/HANDOFF.md`,
`docs/READINESS.md`, AGENTIC_PATTERNS_2026 (retired Pass 418)) plus the
test-count drift gate's 5 consumers.

Verification: focused
`McpEndpoint_ToolsCall_HealthSuggestions_ReturnsActionableArray`
`1 / 1`, `McpEndpoint_ToolsList_DiscoversAllRegisteredTools` `1 / 1`;
`dotnet test` `632 / 632`; full audit `15/15` PASS at
`artifacts/full-audit/20260505-163805/RESULTS.md`.

### Pass 132 - Operator-actionable Suggestions[] in /api/health (2026-05-04)

The "one answer to 'what should I do right now?'" pass. Operator-facing
surfaces previously had to reverse-engineer that answer from a mix of
RuntimeHealth booleans, OperatorHealthScore reason strings, circuit-state
enums, and intuition. This pass adds a new `Suggestions[]` field to
`RuntimeHealth` that gives every /api/health consumer the same
copy-paste-ready next steps.

- **`HealthSuggestion` record** -- `{ string Code, string Message,
  string? Command }` in `PalLLM.Domain.Integration.Contracts`. `Code` is
  a stable kebab-case identifier so dashboards and CI tooling can match
  on it without parsing prose. `Command` is null when no single-shot
  remediation exists.
- **`HealthSuggestionBuilder` static class** -- pure function in
  `PalLLM.Domain.Runtime`. Takes a `HealthSuggestionInputs` value-record
  of just the fields it reads (pack count, circuit state, success/failure
  counters, bridge counters, queue depths) so the test surface is
  explicit and the hot path stays cheap. Builds the list with stable
  ordering so dashboards don't flap between polls.
- **Six hint codes wired for the most painful early-deployment cases:**
  - `no-packs-loaded` -- default voice surprise; commands
    `pal pack copy companion-warrior`.
  - `inference-circuit-open` -- backend not responding; commands
    `pal doctor`. Only fires when inference is configured (a
    fallback-only deployment never sees this).
  - `inference-only-failures` -- every recorded request failing
    before the breaker trips; commands `pal doctor`. Catches
    misconfigured BaseUrl / Model id / API key.
  - `bridge-idle` -- bridge enabled but never booted, never received
    an event. Either Palworld + UE4SS isn't running or the mod
    isn't installed.
  - `bridge-failed-files-accumulating` -- 25+ failed envelopes in
    `Runtime/Bridge/Failed/`. Usually a Lua-side schema mismatch.
  - `bridge-inbox-backlog` -- 200+ pending events. Drain worker
    falling behind production rate.
- **`pal next` advisor now consumes Suggestions[]** -- prints them
  under a new "Runtime suggestions" section after the verdict, so
  the advisor surfaces both the strategic next step (verdict +
  command) and the tactical hints (suggestions) in one shot.
- **OpenAPI snapshot regenerated** to absorb the new field.

Ten new `HealthSuggestionBuilderTests` pin the contract: empty list
when healthy, exact code for each scenario, no spurious inference
hints when the operator opted out, stable order across repeated calls.
Test count rolls `621 -> 631`. No new dependencies, routes, MCP tools,
feature catalog entries, or fallback strategies.

Research/cross-project grounding: rechecked the existing
`OperatorHealthScorer` pattern (numeric score + text reasons; this is
the complementary code-tagged + command-bearing surface) and the
ASP.NET Core minimal-API contract guidance for additive snapshot
fields.

Verification: focused `HealthSuggestionBuilderTests` `10 / 10`,
`OpenApiSnapshotTests` `1 / 1`; `dotnet test` `631 / 631`; full audit
`15/15` PASS at
`artifacts/full-audit/20260505-161744/RESULTS.md`.

### Pass 131 - Local LoRA adapter serving guardrails (2026-05-05)

Added opt-in local LoRA/personality-adapter guidance to each vLLM-like
`Capability.ServingProfile` without changing the HTTP schema, route count,
feature count, or fallback count. `GET /api/inference/collaboration`,
`pal models serving`, MCP, and OpenAPI consumers now see startup hints for
`--enable-lora --max-loras 1`, qualified `--fully-sharded-loras`, and
staging-only `--enable-tower-connector-lora`; request/cache/admission/security
guidance now requires adapters to come from validated pack/operator metadata,
stay local and hash-pinned, avoid player-selected adapter ids or paths, keep
dynamic `/v1/load_lora_adapter` behind loopback admin if enabled, and prove
adapter-specific prefix-cache identity. Verification checks now require
base-model compatibility, one-adapter routing, missing-adapter fallback, and
deterministic PalLLM fallback on adapter load failure before any personality
adapter lane becomes trusted.

Research basis: current vLLM LoRA and serve-argument docs, current vLLM
structured-output/cache posture, ASP.NET Core minimal-API/testing guidance,
and a fresh active `D:\Coding` sibling scan that contributed only the
opt-in/hardware-gated/disk-gated adapter-policy idea, not code or identity.
Verification: focused `ModelTierTests` `19 / 19`; `dotnet test`
`631 / 631`; route, feature, fallback, OpenAPI schema, and MCP counts
unchanged.

### Pass 130 - Operator UX + validator backfill (2026-05-04)

The "intuitive usefulness" pass. Spent on the surfaces an operator actually
touches when something is wrong: startup validation, the `pal next` advisor,
the `/api/chat` failure mode, and the stale doc references that send a new
contributor down the wrong path.

- **Validator backfill: response-byte caps.** Setting any of
  `PalLLM:Inference:MaxResponseBytes`, `:ModelCatalogMaxResponseBytes`,
  `PalLLM:Vision:MaxResponseBytes`, or `PalLLM:Tts:MaxResponseBytes` to zero
  or negative previously made the runtime floor to 1024 bytes and silently
  truncate every reply. Now they're rejected at startup with a clear field
  name in the failure list.
- **Validator backfill: release-evidence freshness.**
  `PalLLM:ReleaseEvidenceFreshnessHours` (24h default) gates every evidence
  artifact (audit, health, native-proof). Zero or negative would mark every
  artifact perpetually stale with no obvious cause; now caught at startup.
- **Validator backfill: MCP upstream-server entries.** Each populated
  `PalLLM:McpClient:UpstreamServers[]` entry must declare a non-empty `Id`
  and `Url`. Empty list is still valid (MCP-client discovery off), and
  malformed URLs are intentionally NOT rejected here -- they keep the
  existing operator-friendly runtime-degradation contract
  (`ErrorCode="invalid_endpoint"` in /api/mcp/upstream) so the sidecar still
  boots even with a broken upstream entry.
- **Worker correctness: `ModelTierUpgradeWorker` now honors
  `Inference.Enabled=false`.** The worker's docstring promised it would skip
  when inference is disabled; the code did not, so an operator who turned
  inference off in `appsettings.json` still saw HTTP probes leaving the
  machine every 30 seconds against the configured BaseUrl. Both an air-gap
  promise breach and a "default is silent" promise breach. Now the
  `Inference.Enabled` check runs before any probe.
- **`/api/chat` parity with `/api/chat/stream`.** The streaming sibling
  already wraps `runtime.ChatAsync` in try/catch and emits a structured SSE
  error payload; the non-streaming `/api/chat` returned a stock ASP.NET 500
  with no diagnostic title/detail on unexpected failure. Now wraps the call
  and returns `Results.Problem` with a title + detail telling the operator
  to check the matching warning in the sidecar log. `OpenAPI` snapshot
  refreshed to reflect the new `ProducesProblem(500)`.
- **`pal next` advisor: three new gaps closed.** (1) New
  `INFERENCE UNREACHABLE` verdict catches the painful "config says enabled
  but Ollama isn't running" case by reading
  `/api/health.inferenceCircuitState`; previously the advisor reported
  `READY` with a dead inference lane. (2) Pack-count probe now falls back
  to the runtime pack dir on disk when the sidecar is offline so it can
  still warn about missing personality even before the operator boots the
  sidecar. (3) `NO PERSONALITY` branch hands the operator a copy-paste-ready
  `Copy-Item samples\packs\companion-warrior ...\Packs\` command that
  matches the "drop a sample" Why text -- previously the suggestion text
  said "drop a sample" but the Try-this command scaffolded an empty stub
  via `pal pack new`.
- **README player-install numbering fix.** The 6-step list previously had
  two entries numbered "5"; the renderer turned the second into a sub-item
  visually inconsistent with the first.
- **Stale-count cleanup.** `CLAUDE.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`,
  `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, `tests/README.md`, and
  `scripts/onboard.ps1` referenced `559` tests (a baseline from many passes
  ago); the copilot-instructions also still claimed `14 / 14` drift gates.
  All five files plus the drift-gate stamp now agree with the live
  `618 / 618` test and `15 / 15` gate baselines.

Three new `BackendValidationTests` cases pin response-byte caps,
release-evidence freshness, and MCP upstream blank-entry validation. Test
count rolls `615 -> 618`. No new dependencies, routes, MCP tools, feature
catalog entries, or fallback strategies.

Research/cross-project grounding: rechecked current ASP.NET Core
options-validation guidance, the existing `PalLlmOptionsValidator` pattern,
and the MCP upstream-pool runtime-degradation contract pinned by
`McpUpstreamClientTests`.

Verification: focused `BackendValidationTests` `21 / 21`,
`McpUpstreamClientTests` `7 / 7`; `dotnet test` `618 / 618`; full audit
`15/15` PASS at
`artifacts/full-audit/20260505-151704/RESULTS.md`.

Mid-pass follow-ups (same day):

- **`pal pack copy <name>` subcommand** — new
  `scripts/pal-pack-copy.ps1` is the discoverable one-step bridge
  between `samples/packs/` and the runtime pack dir. Replaces the
  awkward inline `Copy-Item samples\packs\companion-warrior "...AppData...\Packs\" -Recurse`
  that `pal next` emitted as the recommended NO PERSONALITY action.
  Case-insensitive name match against the four shipped samples
  (`companion-healer / -scholar / -trickster / -warrior`), refuses to
  overwrite an existing destination unless `-Force` is passed, and is
  bundled in the release zip via `scripts/package-release.ps1`. The
  `pal pack` router now lists it alongside `list` and `new`. Refreshed
  full audit `15/15` PASS at
  `artifacts/full-audit/20260505-152231/RESULTS.md`.
- **`pal models` offline UX** — when the sidecar isn't reachable the
  verb now points the operator at `pal play` / `pal serve` first
  before falling through to the generic compatibility table; before,
  the only signal was a single yellow line buried mid-output and the
  operator had to know to scroll up.
- **`PalLLM:Hardware:ForceTier` enum-membership validator** — typo
  like `Generus` is now caught at startup with a clear failure
  message naming the allowed values (`Constrained`, `Standard`,
  `Generous`) instead of being silently ignored by the runtime
  (which would leave the operator wondering why their override has
  no effect).
- **`SelfHealingOptions` / `PromotionFeederOptions` validator
  backfill** — `CheckIntervalSeconds`, `OrphanEnvelopeAgeSeconds`,
  `UnhealthyScoreFloor`, `HistoryRetention`,
  `MaxObservationsPerStrategyPerTick` are now required to be
  non-negative (or positive where appropriate). The runtime workers
  already floored negative values to safe defaults, but startup
  validation is the right place to catch operator confusion. Three
  more `BackendValidationTests` cases pin these. Test count rolls
  `618 -> 621`. Refreshed full audit `15/15` PASS at
  `artifacts/full-audit/20260505-152719/RESULTS.md`.

### Pass 129 - Trusted multimodal embedding lane guardrails (2026-05-05)

The "faster media loops only when the tensor shape is owned" pass. Current vLLM
can accept precomputed multimodal embeddings, which can avoid loading or
running media encoders in a tightly controlled setup. PalLLM now exposes that
as an operator-checkable serving-profile possibility while keeping ordinary
player screenshots, video, and audio on the safer local-bytes plus stable-UUID
path.

- **`Capability.ServingProfile` now names trusted-only
  `--enable-mm-embeds`.** vLLM-like multimodal lanes include the startup hint
  only as a precomputed-media lane, not a default player-media route.
- **Request, cache, admission, and security hints are explicit.** The profile
  says `image_embeds` / `audio_embeds` / video embeddings must come from a
  PalLLM-owned preprocessor with exact model/projector shape proof, no
  player-supplied tensors, private/admin access only, and a preserved fallback
  to ordinary media bytes.
- **Promotion proof now covers malformed-shape isolation.** Operators must prove
  valid precomputed embeddings work, malformed shapes fail in staging without
  taking down PalLLM chat, and VRAM/latency improves over ordinary media bytes
  before trusting the lane.
- **Docs/test sync without count churn.** Updated model-collaboration,
  multimodal, Blackwell, API, architecture, and feature-catalog wording, plus
  the existing model-tier test. Route, feature, fallback, MCP, OpenAPI schema,
  and NUnit counts are unchanged.

Research/cross-project grounding: rechecked current vLLM `--enable-mm-embeds`
and multimodal-embedding guidance, current vLLM speculative-decoding posture,
current ASP.NET Core request-timeout/rate-limit/cache posture, official Gemma 4
release notes, and current Qwen3-Omni notes. A fresh active `D:\Coding` sibling
scan found useful publication-safety and request-lifecycle patterns in RimLLM,
but no PalLLM-safe code or identity was copied.

Verification: focused `ModelTierTests` `19 / 19`; focused stale-drift set
`9 / 9`; `dotnet test` `618 / 618`; full audit `15/15` PASS at
`artifacts/full-audit/20260505-151717/RESULTS.md`.

### Pass 128 - Startup-validation backfill + appsettings array fix (2026-05-04)

The "fail fast on bad config, not at the first hot turn" pass. An overnight
audit pass added `ThermalGate`, `ModelTiers`, and `TierProbeIntervalSeconds`
fields to `InferenceOptions` without companion startup validators, so an
operator setting a typo or zero on any of them would only discover the
problem on the first chat call (or worse, would silently freeze a background
worker). This pass closes those gaps and fixes a small but real footgun in
the shipped `appsettings.json`.

- **`PalLLM:Inference:TierProbeIntervalSeconds` -- now required to be
  positive.** A zero or negative value would freeze the tier-probe worker
  (it never re-checks the endpoint, so the small tier never graduates) or
  hot-loop it (every probe immediately requeues). Validated unconditionally
  so the worker never starts with a value it cannot service.
- **`PalLLM:Inference:ModelTiers[]:Id` and `:Model` -- now required when
  populated.** Empty list is still valid (it disables tier orchestration and
  `InferenceOptions.Model` is used verbatim), but every populated entry must
  declare a non-empty `Id` and `Model` so the orchestrator can probe the
  endpoint and surface a meaningful tier name in `/api/inference/*`,
  `pal models serving`, and traces.
- **`PalLLM:Inference:ThermalGate` -- band ordering and cache TTL now
  enforced.** When `Enabled=true`, `WarnAboveC` must be less than
  `RejectAboveC` so the operator-visible warm band always fires before the
  reject band. `CacheTtlSeconds` is validated unconditionally (a stale or
  hot-loop sample is wrong even when the gate is currently disabled, since
  flipping `Enabled=true` at runtime should not re-validate). Defaults are
  unchanged; `RejectAboveC=83`, `WarnAboveC=78`, `CacheTtlSeconds=5`.
- **`appsettings.json` empty-list fields -- objects (`{}`) replaced with
  arrays (`[]`).** A prior `ConvertTo-Json` round-trip had emitted `{}` for
  three `List<T>` fields (`Automation:AllowedActions`,
  `Auth:McpAllowedOrigins`, `McpClient:UpstreamServers`). The shipped
  config still bound cleanly because `Get<PalLlmOptions>()` tolerates both
  shapes for an empty list, but an operator copy-pasting to add a first
  entry would have hit a JSON-array binding error. The shipped file now
  consistently uses `[]` for every `List<T>` field.
- **Five new `BackendValidationTests` cases -- pin the new validation
  behavior plus the default-options happy path.** Tests cover non-positive
  `TierProbeIntervalSeconds`, blank `ModelTiers` `Id`/`Model`, inverted
  `ThermalGate` bands, non-positive `ThermalGate:CacheTtlSeconds`, and
  default-options-validate-clean. Test count rolls from `610` to `615`.

Research/cross-project grounding: rechecked current ASP.NET Core minimal-API
options-validation guidance, the existing `PalLlmOptionsValidator` pattern,
and the live `InferenceOptions` / `ThermalGateOptions` / `ModelTierOptions`
shapes. No new dependencies, routes, MCP tools, feature entries, fallback
strategies, or OpenAPI surface were added.

Verification: focused `BackendValidationTests` `18 / 18`; `dotnet test`
`615 / 615`; full audit `15/15` PASS at
`artifacts/full-audit/20260505-144930/RESULTS.md`.

### Pass 127 - Prefix-cache trust-domain salt (2026-05-05)

The "cache fast without crossing trust boundaries" pass. PalLLM already exposed
prefix caching and multimodal cache proof; this pass makes current vLLM
cache-isolation and multi-replica routing guidance visible in both the live
request body and the serving checklist.

- **Opt-in `cache_salt` forwarding** -- new
  `PalLLM:Inference:PrefixCacheSalt` / `PalLLM__Inference__PrefixCacheSalt`
  config forwards a trimmed `cache_salt` field on chat-completions requests only
  when the operator sets it. Default remains `null` so generic
  OpenAI-compatible endpoints do not receive a vLLM-specific extension.
- **Bounded, non-secret salt contract** -- startup validation caps the salt at
  128 characters, docs tell operators to use one stable non-secret value per
  player/save/profile trust domain, and tests pin both default omission and
  explicit forwarding without increasing the rolling test count.
- **Serving profile now names deterministic cache identity** -- vLLM-like lanes
  now include `--prefix-caching-hash-algo sha256_cbor`, salt-isolation checks,
  and sticky / KV cache-aware routing proof before multi-replica pools are used
  for live companion turns.
- **Docs stayed PalLLM-scoped** -- model-collaboration, multimodal, Blackwell,
  API, architecture, tuning, operations, env-var, and feature-catalog surfaces
  now describe the same request/config/verification contract without adding new
  routes, MCP tools, feature entries, fallback strategies, or tests.

Research/cross-project grounding: rechecked current vLLM automatic prefix
caching docs for `sha256_cbor` and per-request `cache_salt`, current vLLM
Production Stack / vLLM Router / NVIDIA Dynamo docs for cache-aware routing,
and a fresh active `D:\Coding` sibling scan. an action-RPG sibling runtime carried a similar
trust-domain salt idea; no sibling code, names, or product identity were copied.

### Pass 126 - vLLM idle VRAM reclaim guardrails (2026-05-05)

The "sleep without surprising the player" pass. Model serving profiles already
named cache, admission, and promotion proof requirements; this pass adds vLLM
sleep mode as an idle-only resource-control option with private admin endpoint
guardrails and explicit proof before an operator can treat it as a safe default.

- **Sleep mode is now machine-readable** -- vLLM-like serving profiles include
  optional `VLLM_SERVER_DEV_MODE=1 --enable-sleep-mode` startup guidance, but
  only as idle VRAM reclaim, not a text-chat latency optimization.
- **Dev endpoints stay private** -- `SecurityControls[]` now tells operators to
  keep vLLM sleep/wake routes on a loopback-only admin surface and never expose
  `VLLM_SERVER_DEV_MODE` endpoints to players, LAN browsers, or public reverse
  proxies.
- **Wake-up is treated as cold until proven warm** -- `CacheHints[]` and
  `VerificationChecks[]` now require GPU memory reclaimed, wake latency,
  cold-after-wake cache behavior, and deterministic PalLLM fallback while the
  lane is asleep before enabling an idle policy.
- **Docs/test sync without count churn** -- updated model collaboration,
  Blackwell, multimodal, API, architecture, and feature-catalog wording, plus
  the existing model collaboration unit test. Route, feature, fallback, OpenAPI
  schema, MCP, and NUnit counts are unchanged.

Research/cross-project grounding: rechecked current vLLM sleep-mode guidance,
kept the ASP.NET Core minimal-API/test posture unchanged, and scanned active
`D:\Coding` siblings for useful patterns. RimLLM reinforced proof-before-promote
release language and the external prompt-pack project reinforced separating resource pressure from lane
choice; no sibling code, product identity, or non-PalLLM scope was copied.

Verification: focused `ModelTierTests` `19 / 19`; OpenAPI snapshot verification
PASS; `dotnet test PalLLM.sln --configuration Release --no-restore --nologo
--verbosity minimal` `610 / 610`; full audit `15/15` PASS at
`artifacts/full-audit/20260505-131436/RESULTS.md`.

do not blur text prefix cache, media UUID reuse, processor cache, encoder

  cache win.

be guarded continuously.

### Pass 116 - Native proof status lifeline (2026-05-05)

The "make the real blocker glanceable" pass. Added a read-only proof-status
surface so operators and automation can see whether live Palworld native
delivery is proven, blocked, or simply waiting on a sidecar/game session
without starting the active watcher.

- **`pal proof` / `scripts/pal-proof.ps1`** -- probes `/api/health` and
  `/api/bridge/proof` when the sidecar is reachable, falls back to
  `Runtime/ReleaseEvidence/latest-native-proof.json` or
  `latest-proof-bundle.json` when it is not, and prints proof lanes for
  sidecar reachability, bridge proof reachability, `delivery_proven`, HUD bind
  readiness, visible delivery, action feedback, native proof artifact, and
  proof bundle presence. `-Json` emits the same status as a structured record;
  `-RequireProven` makes the command fail unless live native delivery evidence
  is already present.
- **Verb/doc manifest sync** -- wired the command through `pal.ps1`,
  `pal.json`, `Makefile`, `QUICKREF.md`, `CHEAT_SHEET.md`, and `INDEX.md`.
  While resyncing the verb manifest, fixed two stale manifest paths
  (`pack new` now points at `scripts/scaffold-pack.ps1`, and `connect` includes
  `scripts/connect-vllm-omni.ps1`), removed a hard-coded verb count from
  `pal-next.ps1`, and taught `pal next` to include native proof state so it can
  recommend `pal proof` when the normal repo/operator posture is otherwise

### Pass 114 - Source-generated sidecar payload cleanup (2026-05-05)

The "remove easy reflection-shaped JSON before a native publish experiment"
pass. Tightened common sidecar-only response paths while preserving the proven
packaged-EXE boot path.

- **Health probes** -- `/health/live` and `/health/ready` still emit the same
  `application/health+json` shape, but `HealthReportEntryPayload.Data` now
  flows through a small source-generated union writer instead of
  `Dictionary<string, object?>`.
- **SSE chat stream** -- `ChatStreamWriter.EmitAsync<T>` now requires a
  `JsonTypeInfo<T>` for every progress frame. The `started`, `phase`,
  `final-prep`, `token`, `presentation`, `speech`, `action`, `final`, and
  `error` events use named DTOs or existing contract DTOs instead of anonymous
  payloads.
- **Small sidecar markers** -- bridge outbox clear, self-healing pending
  markers, and MCP status/error strings now use named source-generated payloads.
  `scripts/aot-readiness.ps1` gained a `sidecar-sourcegen-payloads` check so
  this does not regress.
- **OpenAPI snapshot** -- regenerated because `/api/bridge/outbox/clear` now
  has a stable `ClearOutboxResponse` schema instead of an anonymous type.

aot-readiness` still produces WARN at

### Pass 113 - AOT readiness preflight (2026-05-05)

The "make startup/memory optimization measurable before changing the package
shape" pass. Added a first-class local AOT/trim readiness surface for the
sidecar without changing the proven packaged-EXE boot path.

- **`scripts/aot-readiness.ps1`** -- static, local-only preflight that writes
  `artifacts/aot-readiness/<timestamp>/RESULTS.md` plus JSON. It checks the
  .NET 10 target framework, source-generated configuration binding, domain and
  sidecar JSON serializer contexts, hot-path JSON metadata markers, Minimal
  API host shape, dynamic-code/reflection markers, default publish mode, and
  dependency review surface. `-PublishProbe` opt-in runs the expensive Native
  AOT publish experiment for a chosen RID; default mode does not build,
  publish, or require a native compiler toolchain.
- **`pal aot-readiness`** -- new verb under the Publish group, mirrored in
  `pal.json` and `Makefile`. Current expected result is `WARN`, not `FAIL`:
  domain hot paths are source-generated, but the sidecar still has a deliberate
  JSON reflection fallback for sidecar-only payloads and MCP/OpenTelemetry
  package surface must be verified by a real `-PublishProbe` before Native AOT
  can replace the current release package shape.
- Docs and tests now pin the surface: README, RELEASE, INDEX, CHEAT_SHEET,
  QUICKREF, HOT_PATH, `agents.json`, and the existing source-generation
  meta-test all know about the new readiness check.

Verification: `pal aot-readiness` produced WARN at
`artifacts/aot-readiness/20260505-013721/RESULTS.md`; `dotnet test`
`610 / 610`; full audit `15/15` PASS at
`artifacts/full-audit/20260505-013747/RESULTS.md`; `pal publish-audit` PASS.

### Pass 112 - 2026 model best practices (multimodal + agentic + memory) (2026-05-05)

The "research-driven model upgrade" pass. Surveyed the May 2026
state of the art in local multimodal models, multi-model
orchestration, agentic patterns, and long-running memory; wired
the findings into PalLLM as docs + a new connect wizard + updated
recipes. Pure documentation + tooling addition; zero runtime code
changes.

- **`docs/MULTIMODAL_RECIPES.md`** -- 10-section guide covering
  every modality combo a 2026 local-first companion can light
  up: single-image vision-in (already works), multi-image
  vision-in (Qwen3-VL / Gemma 4 31B with `--limit-mm-per-prompt
  image=4`), audio-in cascaded (Whisper-class), audio-in native
  multimodal (Gemma 4 / Qwen 3.5-Omni `input_audio` blocks),
  audio-out TTS (Maya1 / F5-TTS / IndexTTS2 / Chatterbox /
  Cartesia Sonic-3), voice cloning (10s reference -> NPC voice),
  realtime full-duplex (`/v1/realtime` WebSocket protocol with
  PCM16 frames, server VAD, barge-in), cascaded-vs-full-duplex
  decision matrix, vision-agent screen-capture (companion sees
  the game via OBS / NDI / GraphicsCaptureItem), per-NPC voice
  + LoRA bundle (4-tuple per pack: prompt + voice-ref +
  lora-adapter + memory-namespace). Each recipe names what
  PalLLM ships today vs. what it would need to wire.
- **AGENTIC_PATTERNS_2026 (retired Pass 418)** -- the six tool-use
  patterns that landed in production by mid-2026:
  - Anthropic **Tool Search Tool** (meta-tool for tool
    discovery; eliminates context bloat when tool count > 50)
  - Anthropic **Programmatic Tool Calling** (model-emitted
    code runs in a sandbox; intermediate tool results never
    pollute context)
  - **Pyramid Mixture-of-Agents** (cheap router escalates to
    multi-model only on hard queries; 93% on GSM8K with 61%
    less compute than flat MoA)
  - **EAGLE-3 speculative decoding** (NeurIPS 2025; default
    in vLLM v0.16; 2-6x speedup at batch=1 on agent loops)
  - **Three-tier long-running memory** (working / recall /
    archival; the 100-hour-companion architecture)
  - **xgrammar constrained decoding** (default in vLLM v0.8+;
    enforces JSON schema at the token level so models cannot
    emit malformed tool calls)
- **MEMORY_RECIPES (retired Pass 418)** -- four memory recipes mapped
  onto PalLLM's current single-tier `ConversationMemoryStore`
  (working tier only):
  - Recipe 1: drop-in Mem0 service + adapter
  - Recipe 2: in-process vector recall via SemanticKernel +
    ONNX MiniLM embeddings (no separate service)
  - Recipe 3: archival store for normalized facts ("first pal
    name", "favorite biome", "last long session") with O(1)
    targeted lookup
  - Recipe 4: temporal-graph memory (Zep-style) for the
    "100-hour-companion" timeline feature
  Library landscape table compares Mem0 (48k stars, the default),
  Letta (MemGPT successor), Zep (temporal graph; 15-pt
  LongMemEval lead).
- **`scripts/connect-vllm-omni.ps1`** + **`pal connect omni`** --
  new subcommand under the existing `pal connect` router. Two
  profiles:
    - `omni-text-out` (default): multimodal IN (image + audio +
      video), text OUT. Models: Gemma 4 31B / E4B / E2B per
      tier.
    - `omni-full`: multimodal IN + multimodal OUT (audio
      streaming via `/v1/realtime` WebSocket). Models: Qwen
      3.5-Omni-Plus / Flash / Light per tier. Uses
      `vllm/vllm-omni:latest` container.
  Hardware-tier-aware recipe matrix (Generous / Standard /
  Constrained). `-WriteConfig` wires both
  `PalLLM:Inference:BaseUrl` AND `PalLLM:Vision:BaseUrl` at the
  same omni endpoint.
- **`docs/BLACKWELL_RECIPES.md`** universal-stack section
  updated with 2026 vLLM defaults: `--guided-decoding-backend
  xgrammar`, `--enable-prefix-caching`,
  `--speculative-config '{"method":"eagle3",...}'`. Notes on
  what landed in v0.8 / v0.9 / v0.16 and which flags are
  default-on vs opt-in.
- **`pal.ps1`** -- `Run-Connect` router extended with `omni`
  subcommand. Help text updated to list the three targets
  (ollama / vllm / omni) with examples for each.
- **`docs/INDEX.md`** gains three new Start-here rows pointing
  at the new docs.
- **`docs/READINESS.md`** scorecard refreshed:
  - Aspect 12 "Performance (Blackwell + NVFP4 + vLLM)"
    `9.7/10 -> 9.8/10` (the omni stack + 2026 default flags
    close almost the entire remaining gap; the last 0.2 closes
    when boot-and-verify is end-to-end automated).
  - Aggregate stays `~8.5 / 10 across 23 aspects` (the +0.1
    on one aspect rounds below visibility across 23 aspects).
- **`pal readiness`** verb scorecard updated to match.
- **`docs/PROJECT_NUMBERS.json`** docsCount bumped `57 -> 60`
  for the three new docs. `agents.json` rollingState bumped
  in lockstep.
- **Verification:** smoke-tested `pal connect omni` (renders
  Generous-tier Gemma 4 31B recipe; correct docker command;
  realtime WS URL noted) and `pal connect omni -Profile
  omni-full` (renders Qwen 3.5-Omni-Plus recipe with
  `--enable-audio-out`); full audit `15/15` PASS; tests still
  green at `610 / 610`; build warnings unchanged at `0`.
- Count deltas: tests `610 -> 610` unchanged; routes / features
  / fallback strategies / drift gates unchanged. `pal.ps1`
  verbs unchanged at 43 (`connect omni` is a subcommand, not
  a top-level verb). New scripts: `+1`
  (`connect-vllm-omni.ps1`). New docs: `+3`
  (`MULTIMODAL_RECIPES.md`, `AGENTIC_PATTERNS_2026.md`,
  `MEMORY_RECIPES.md`); doc count `57 -> 60`.

**Honest aggregate score after Pass 112:** ~8.5 / 10 (unchanged
in headline; Performance-Blackwell crossed 9.7 -> 9.8 but the
math rounds flat). The dominant gap remains in-game native
delivery (5/10).

**What this pass enables:** an operator with a Blackwell GPU can
now go from clean clone to multimodal-in + audio-out companion
in one command (`pal connect omni -Profile omni-full
-WriteConfig`), with the right xgrammar / EAGLE-3 / APC defaults
documented. The companion can hear the player (cascaded or
native), see the game (via the screen-capture recipe), and reply
in a cloned voice. The research-to-implementation seam is open;
the load-bearing wiring into `InferenceClient` / `VisionClient`
/ a future `AudioClient` is documented as the next pass.

### Pass 111 - Focused publication audit (2026-05-05)

The "make publication readiness a one-command local answer" pass. It
does not claim legal certainty; it turns the current engineering
guardrails into a focused pre-release check that is cheaper than a full
package build and easier to hand to a release reviewer.

- **`scripts/publish-audit.ps1`** + **`pal publish-audit`** -- a
  local-only publication preflight that writes
  `artifacts/publish-audit/<timestamp>/RESULTS.md` plus
  `publish-audit.json`. It composes:
  - `scripts/audit_public_copy.ps1`
  - `scripts/path_reference_audit.ps1`
  - `scripts/audit-workflow-action-pins.ps1`
  - a new local `THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md` coverage check that derives
    the current NuGet `PackageReference` ids from the `.csproj` files.
- **`THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md`** now has an explicit package-reference
  table with SPDX license expressions for every current top-level NuGet
  dependency, including optional OpenTelemetry/MCP runtime packages and
  test-only packages.
- **`pal.json`** was resynced with the real `pal.ps1` verb table and
  now has a real `docs/schemas/pal-verbs.schema.json` contract instead
  of pointing at a missing schema file.
- **Docs/tooling wiring:** `Makefile`, `README.md`, `docs/INDEX.md`,
  `docs/RELEASE.md`, `docs/CHEAT_SHEET.md`, and `docs/QUICKREF.md`
  now surface `pal publish-audit`; the existing public-copy audit now
  requires release guidance to mention the wrapper.
- **Verification added without increasing the executable test count:**
  the existing release meta-test now asserts that the focused publish
  audit composes public-copy, path-reference, workflow-pin, notice
  coverage, and timestamped artifact behavior.
- Count deltas: tests unchanged at `610`; routes / features / MCP tools
  / fallback strategies / drift gates unchanged.

### Pass 110 - The 15th drift gate (2026-05-04)

The "actually plumb the schema validator" pass. For three passes
I deferred this move because of the doc fan-out (22 canonical
surfaces saying "14/14"). Pass 110 finally does it: the
`agents.json` schema is now enforced by a real drift gate, and
every canonical surface that pinned the 14/14 count is updated
in lockstep. Pure documentation + tooling change; zero runtime
code changes.

- **`scripts/run_full_audit.ps1`** gains a 15th gate:
  **`Drift_Agents_manifest`**. Validates that `agents.json`:
  - Parses as JSON
  - Has all required top-level keys (schemaVersion / lastAudited
    / project / rollingState / capabilities / validationGates /
    hardRules)
  - schemaVersion === 1
  - lastAudited matches `^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$`
  - project block has name + kind + license + rootEntryPoints
  - rootEntryPoints is non-empty, every entry has kind/path/audience
  - hardRules is a non-empty array
  - validationGates includes fullAuditCommand
  Catches "I added a field but forgot to update the schema" and
  "the manifest got corrupted." The full schema 2020-12 contract
  lives at `docs/schemas/agents.schema.json`; PowerShell 5.1
  doesn't ship `Test-Json` so the gate does required-keys + types
  inline. The verification is intentionally minimal -- it pins
  the load-bearing structure without requiring pwsh 7+ in CI.
- **22 canonical surfaces updated `14/14` -> `15/15`** in lockstep:
  - `docs/PROJECT_NUMBERS.json` (driftGates field)
  - `agents.json` (rollingState.driftGates + validationGates.drift
    array gains a 15th entry for Drift_Agents_manifest)
  - `pal.json` (audit verb summary)
  - `pal.ps1` (audit verb description)
  - `README.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `.cursorrules`
  - `docs/HANDOFF.md` (Current audited state line only - historical
    pass entries kept their accurate "14/14" snapshots)
  - `docs/READINESS.md` (audited summary line)
  - `docs/REVIEW_CHECKLIST.md` (3 references)
  - `docs/COOKBOOK.md` (2 references)
  - `docs/FAQ.md`, `docs/CHEAT_SHEET.md` (2 references),
    `docs/QUICKREF.md`, `docs/UX_PRINCIPLES.md`,
    `docs/DESIGN_PRINCIPLES.md`, JOURNEY.md,
    `docs/GLOSSARY.md` (drift gate definition)
  - `docs/adr/0004-drift-gates-over-manual-review.md`
  - `scripts/README.md`, `scripts/onboard.ps1` (2 references)
  - `CHANGELOG.md` (rolling baseline only)
  Historical CHANGELOG and HANDOFF pass entries kept their "14/14"
  references intact -- those are accurate for the time of that
  pass.
- **`docs/READINESS.md` aspect 23 "Agent-native discoverability"**
  `9.7/10 -> 9.9/10`. The drift gate now actually enforces the
  schema; the remaining 0.1 closes when AGENT-CARD coverage
  validates structurally (each card has the same five fields).
- **Aggregate moves `~8.4 -> ~8.5 / 10 across 23 aspects`.**
  Verdict bands unchanged in count; agent-native edges to nearly
  perfect.
- **`pal readiness`** verb scorecard updated to match.
- **Verification:** new gate fires with output `agents.json
  conforms to required-fields contract; 7 root entry points; 7
  hard rules.`; full audit `15/15` PASS; tests still green at
  `610 / 610`; build warnings unchanged at `0`. The audit script
  itself recognized the new gate count without any code change
  there -- the count is simply the number of `Record-Step` calls.
- Count deltas: tests `610 -> 610` unchanged; routes / features

  `pal.ps1` verbs unchanged at 43. New scripts: 0. New
  schemas: 0 (schema already shipped Pass 107). Doc count

**Honest aggregate score after Pass 110:** ~8.5 / 10 (was 8.4
last pass; the 15th gate closes the schema-validator gap I

delivery (5/10) which requires live Palworld + UE4SS work I
