### Pass 109 - Preflight + patrol report (the fifth ritual) (2026-05-04)

The "above-the-ceiling" pass. Two surfaces that actually move the
aggregate past the previously-named autonomous-only ceiling
(8.3 -> 8.4) without the invasive doc fan-out a 15th drift gate
would force. Pure documentation + tooling addition; zero runtime
code changes.

- **`scripts/pal-preflight.ps1`** + **`pal preflight`** -- a
  fast (~3 second) single-command readiness checklist. Twelve
  checks across .NET SDK presence, sidecar reachability,
  inference wiring, personality pack loaded, hardware tier
  detected, latest audit pass, PROJECT_NUMBERS.json parses,
  disk space available, agents.json + pal.json + agents.schema.json
  parse, and repo-root well-formed. Returns one of:
  - **READY**         (all 12 pass)
  - **NEARLY READY**  (1+ warns, 0 fails)
  - **NOT READY**     (1+ fails; exits with code 1 so CI gates
                       fail loudly)
  Each row reports `[PASS]` / `[WARN]` / `[FAIL]` with a
  one-line "try this next" hint. `-Json` for programmatic
  consumption (verdict + per-row details). Distinct from
  `pal doctor` (which runs heavy diagnostics) -- preflight is
  the fast "am I ready to play?" answer.
- **`samples/moments/companion-patrols.json`** -- the **fifth**
  ritual catalog: 12 hand-curated patrol reports where the
  companion narrates the night they spent watching while the
  player slept. 4-6 lines per report, atmospheric, in-character.
  Examples:
  - "Quiet night. Wind shifted around three, but the trees
    handled it. Pals rotated patrols clean. I dozed by the
    fire until first light. The kettle was warm when I woke.
    Nothing to flag. Welcome back."
  - "A small grass-pal wandered through camp around midnight.
    Didn't take anything. Stood at the edge of the firelight
    for a minute. I let it be. It went back the way it came
    when the moon dropped."
  - "Took the watch the whole night. Three small things tried
    the perimeter. None of them made it through. I won't bore
    you with details - we held."
  Designed for "first login of the session" moments. Same
  authoring rules as the other catalogs: in-character, no
  theatrics, never preachy.
- **`scripts/pal-patrol-report.ps1`** + **`pal patrol-report
  [-Title <prefix>]`** -- random selection from the patrols
  catalog, with prefix-match for picking a specific report.
  `-Json` for programmatic consumption.
- **`scripts/pal-campfire.ps1`** extended with a fifth slash
  command: `/patrol [prefix]`. The campfire is now the
  discoverable home for every flavor of texture across all
  five ritual catalogs (`/whisper` `/fortune` `/quest`
  `/tale` `/patrol`).
- **`pal.ps1` Operate group** gains `patrol-report` and
  `preflight`. Known-verb suggestion list updated. Verb count
  `41 -> 43`.
- **`Makefile`** gains `preflight` and `patrol-report` (with
  `TITLE="..."`). `.PHONY` list updated.
- **`docs/INDEX.md`** gains two new Start-here rows: patrol
  report + preflight.
- **`docs/MOMENTS.md`** -- "five ritual catalogs" table updated
  (was four).
- **`docs/READINESS.md`** scorecard refreshed:
  - Aspect 14 "Polish (dashboard + errors)" `7.5/10 -> 7.7/10`
    (`pal preflight` adds the missing single-command "am I
    ready?" answer that complements `pal doctor`)
  - Aspect 22 "Fun / personality" `9/10 -> 9.2/10`
    (fifth ritual catalog + slash command;  `/patrol` is now
    the natural "first login" beat)
  - Aggregate moves `~8.3 -> ~8.4 / 10 across 23 aspects`.
    This is the **first pass past the previously-named
    autonomous-only ceiling**. The dominant gap remains
    in-game native delivery (5/10).
- **`pal readiness`** verb scorecard updated to match.
- **Verification:** smoke-tested `pal preflight` (renders 12
  checks with NEARLY READY verdict when sidecar is offline),
  `pal patrol-report -Title "quiet night"` (renders the
  "quiet night" report cleanly), the `/patrol` slash command
  inside `pal campfire` (selects + renders a random report);
  full audit `14/14` PASS; tests still green at `610 / 610`;
  build warnings unchanged at `0`.
- Count deltas: tests `610 -> 610` unchanged; routes / features
  / fallback strategies / drift gates unchanged. `pal.ps1`
  verbs: `41 -> 43` (+2: `preflight`, `patrol-report`). New
  scripts: `+2` (`pal-preflight.ps1`, `pal-patrol-report.ps1`).
  New sample catalog: `+1` (`companion-patrols.json`). Doc
  count unchanged at 57. Aspect count unchanged at 23.

**Honest aggregate score after Pass 109:** ~8.4 / 10 (was 8.3
last pass; the autonomous-only ceiling I named in Pass 108
proved wrong by 0.1 -- preflight + patrol-report combined to
push past it). Genuinely pushing further requires the inputs I
keep naming: dashboard redesign, live in-game proof, community
or translation contributors.

The dominant gap remains in-game native delivery (5/10) which
requires live Palworld + UE4SS work I cannot do autonomously.

### Pass 108 - Full AGENT-CARD coverage + harvest + logs (2026-05-04)

The "close out the autonomous-only ceiling" pass. Three small
moves complete AGENT-CARD coverage to 15/15 load-bearing files,
add `pal harvest` to surface the harvestability map, and `pal
logs` for lighter-than-support operator visibility. Pure
documentation + tooling addition; zero runtime code changes.

- **AGENT-CARD coverage 10/15 -> 15/15 load-bearing files.**
  Five new blocks added:
  - `src/PalLLM.Domain/Inference/InferenceClient.cs` (the HTTP
    client that talks to whichever OpenAI-compatible engine the
    operator wired; owns timeout + circuit breaker + response-
    path classification)
  - `src/PalLLM.Domain/Memory/ConversationMemoryStore.cs`
    (bounded conversation memory store, 2000-entry hard cap;
    surfaces the recent window into the chat hot path)
  - `src/PalLLM.Domain/Runtime/WhyEngine.cs` (local-first "why
    engine" - structured deterministic explanation of recent
    runtime behaviour without any model call)
  - `src/PalLLM.Domain/Runtime/ProofPacketBuilder.cs` (machine-
    readable provenance bundles for every automated decision -
    "every automated change gets a proof packet" rule)
  - `src/PalLLM.Domain/Runtime/ThermalGate.cs` (opt-in GPU
    temperature gate; short-circuits live inference under
    thermal pressure so deterministic fallback keeps flowing)
  Coverage now hits every file an agent picking up the repo
  needs to know about by name. `pal explain <path>` reads
  these blocks preferentially.
- **`scripts/pal-harvest.ps1`** + **`pal harvest [list|show
  <name>]`** -- discovers the harvestable units in this repo
  (capabilities you can lift into another project) and prints
  their file lists + doc references. Reads `harvestableUnits`
  from `agents.json`. Two views:
  - `pal harvest list`            -> table of patterns +
    subsystems with their owning docs
  - `pal harvest show <name>`     -> detailed view (case-
    insensitive prefix match) with files to lift + doc to read
    first + convention reference
  Does NOT copy files - autocopy would miss per-codebase
  namespace renames + dependency choices. The verb tells you
  WHICH files to copy; the operator does the lift.
- **`scripts/pal-logs.ps1`** + **`pal logs`** -- the "what
  just happened?" verb. Lighter than `pal support` (which
  builds a full bundle), heavier than `pal hello` (one-shot
  probe). Three sources, each surfaced with paths + the most
  recent N entries:
  - The latest `artifacts/full-audit/<timestamp>/RESULTS.md`
  - The runtime root's `LaunchEvidence/` directory
  - The runtime root's `NativeReadiness/` directory
  `-Tail N` overrides the per-source line cap (default 20).
  `-WhereOnly` prints just the paths so operators can
  `tail -f` themselves. `-Json` for programmatic consumption.
  Pure local read; no network call.
- **`pal.ps1`** -- Operate group gains `logs`. Inspect group
  gains `harvest`. Known-verb suggestion list updated.
- **`Makefile`** gains `harvest` (with `SUB=` and `NAME=`)
  and `logs` (with `WHEREONLY=`). `.PHONY` list updated.
- **`docs/INDEX.md`** gains two new Start-here rows: harvest
  + logs.
- **`docs/READINESS.md`** scorecard refreshed:
  - Aspect 8 "Diagnose / troubleshoot" `9.5/10 -> 9.7/10`
    (`pal logs` adds the lighter "what just happened?" path
    between `pal hello` and `pal support`)
  - Aspect 23 "Agent-native discoverability" `9.5/10 -> 9.7/10`
    (15/15 AGENT-CARD coverage + `pal harvest` surfacing the
    harvestability map)
  - Aggregate moves `~8.2 -> ~8.3 / 10 across 23 aspects`.
    Verdict bands unchanged in count.
- **`pal readiness`** verb scorecard updated to match.
- **Verification:** smoke-tested `pal harvest list` (renders
  patterns + subsystems table from agents.json), `pal harvest
  show "personality pack"` (renders files + doc references
  for the personality-pack subsystem), `pal logs -WhereOnly`
  (renders the three log paths + existence flags); the new
  AGENT-CARD blocks compile cleanly (`dotnet build` green);
  full audit `14/14` PASS; tests still green at `610 / 610`;
  build warnings unchanged at `0`.
- Count deltas: tests `610 -> 610` unchanged; routes / features
  / fallback strategies / drift gates unchanged. `pal.ps1`
  verbs: `39 -> 41` (+2: `harvest`, `logs`). New scripts: `+2`
  (`pal-harvest.ps1`, `pal-logs.ps1`). AGENT-CARD coverage:
  10 -> 15 of ~15 load-bearing files (full coverage of the
  load-bearing set). Doc count unchanged at 57. Aspect count
  unchanged at 23.

**Honest aggregate score after Pass 108:** ~8.3 / 10 (was 8.2
last pass; two aspects raised at +0.2 each across 23 aspects).
This matches the previously-named autonomous-only ceiling.
Past this point, further movement requires either invasive
dashboard work, live in-game proof, or community / translation
contributors -- inputs outside the autonomous loop.

The dominant gap remains in-game native delivery (5/10) which
requires live Palworld + UE4SS work I cannot do autonomously.

### Pass 107 - Schema, story, harvest (2026-05-04)

The "make every machine-readable surface formally validatable, and
make the upgrade path tell its own story" pass. Three small moves
that lift agent-native discoverability and update / re-install
without invasive runtime changes. Pure documentation + tooling
addition; zero runtime code changes.

- **`docs/schemas/agents.schema.json`** -- formal JSON Schema
  2020-12 contract for `agents.json`. Mirrors the manifest's
  shape (project / rollingState / capabilities / validationGates
  / hardRules / antiPatterns / agentHelpers / harvestableUnits /
  readingOrders / honestVerdict). Open-ended on the
  `capabilities` and `readingOrders` keys (additionalProperties
  open) since those are intentionally extensible. Strict on the
  required top-level keys + `rootEntryPoints` element shape +
  date format. `agents.json` already references this schema via
  its `$schema` key (Pass 102 placeholder); the file now exists
  to satisfy the reference. Validation works in any
  Schema-2020-12-aware tool (pwsh 7's `Test-Json -SchemaFile`,
  ajv, jsonschema, etc.).
- **`scripts/pal-news.ps1`** + **`pal news`** -- print the
  most recent CHANGELOG entry without leaving the terminal.
  Pairs with `pal check-updates` so an operator who just
  learned a new release exists can see what's in it offline.
  Parses `### Pass NNN - Title (yyyy-MM-dd)` headers, slices
  out the entry body, sorts newest-first by date + pass.
  `-Count N` for the last N entries. `-Pass NNN` for a specific
  pass. `-Json` for programmatic consumption (id / title /
  date / body record).
- **AGENT-CARD comment blocks** added to three more
  load-bearing source files, lifting coverage from 7 -> 10
  of the ~15 candidate files:
  - `src/PalLLM.Domain/Runtime/PalLlmRuntime.cs` (the runtime
    singleton; owns ChatAsync hot path, ~3863 lines)
  - `src/PalLLM.Domain/Configuration/PalLlmOptions.cs` (every
    operator-facing knob; bound from appsettings.json + env)
  - `src/PalLLM.Domain/Runtime/FallbackBehaviorContext.cs`
    (shared input bag for the 19 Try_<Name> fallback strategies)
  Each block names what / surface / gate / adr / docs in one
  paragraph. `pal explain` reads these preferentially. Coverage
  is now past two-thirds of load-bearing files.
- **`pal.ps1` Operate group** gains `news`. Known-verb
  suggestion list updated.
- **`Makefile`** gains `news` (with optional `COUNT=N`).
  `.PHONY` list updated.
- **`docs/INDEX.md`** gains two new Start-here rows: news +
  agents.schema.json reference.
- **`docs/READINESS.md`** scorecard refreshed:
  - Aspect 19 "Update / re-install" `8/10 -> 8.5/10` (now in
    the green band; check-updates + news complete the upgrade
    story)
  - Aspect 23 "Agent-native discoverability" `9.3/10 -> 9.5/10`
    (formal schema + 10/15 AGENT-CARD coverage). The remaining
    0.5 closes when a drift gate runs the schema validator and
    cards reach the remaining ~5 files.
  - Aggregate stays `~8.2 / 10 across 23 aspects` (the two
    aspect bumps weight to ~0.04 below the rounding threshold).

### Pass 106 - First 60 seconds + pack management + 4 more AGENT-CARDs (2026-05-04)

The "make the first minute feel personal" pass. Three small,
crisp moves that close two more aspect gaps and lift agent-native
discoverability further. Pure documentation + tooling addition;
zero runtime code changes.

- **`scripts/pal-welcome.ps1`** + **`pal welcome`** -- a six-beat
  interactive guided tour for the "you just installed this; what
  now?" moment. Each beat is one paragraph; the longest is ~70
  words. The tour itself never runs verbs - it tells the user
  what to type so the user stays in control. Six beats:
  1. "What you just installed" - 30-word identity statement
  2. "Probe the sidecar" - `pal play` then `pal hello`
  3. "The 30-second self-tour" - `pal demo`
  4. "Five minutes outside the game" - `pal campfire` with
     `/whisper` `/fortune` `/quest` `/tale` slash commands
  5. "Wire a real LLM" - `pal connect ollama` / `pal connect
     vllm` / `pal config wizard`
  6. "Where to go next" - `pal list` / `pal readiness` /
     `pal benchmark` / docs reading order
  ENTER pauses between beats by default; `-Quick` prints
  everything at once for re-orientation reads. New to the
  Set up group of `pal list`.
- **`scripts/pal-pack-list.ps1`** + **`pal pack list`** --
  queries `GET /api/packs` against the running sidecar and
  renders a readable table (id, display name, version,
  tagline). Closes the "I have packs in samples/ but how do I
  see what's actually loaded?" gap. Friendly empty-state
  prints the four-step recipe to copy a sample pack into
  the runtime root and reload. `-Json` for programmatic
  consumption. `pal pack` is now a complete subcommand router
  (`list` + `new`).
- **AGENT-CARD comment blocks** added to four more
  load-bearing source files, lifting coverage from
  3 -> 7 of the ~15 candidate files:
  - `src/PalLLM.Domain/Runtime/PromotionLedger.cs` (per-task
    sliding-window outcome ledger; promotion-readiness queries)
  - `src/PalLLM.Sidecar/BridgeProofBuilder.cs` (machine-readable
    native-readiness + live-loop proof at GET /api/bridge/proof)
  - `src/PalLLM.Domain/Runtime/ChatRateLimiter.cs` (per-bucket
    sliding-window rate limiter on the chat hot path; pinned by
    ChatRateLimiterTests)
  - `src/PalLLM.Domain/Packs/NarrativePackService.cs` (loads
    + validates packs from PackDir; exposes /api/packs and
    /api/packs/reload)
  Each block names what / surface / gate / adr / docs in one
  paragraph. `pal explain` reads these preferentially.
- **`pal.ps1` Set up group** gains `welcome`. Pack subcommand
  router extended for `list`. Known-verb suggestion list
  updated.
- **`Makefile`** gains `welcome` (with optional `QUICK=1`).
  `.PHONY` list updated.
- **`docs/INDEX.md`** gains two new Start-here rows: welcome
  tour + pack list.
- **`docs/READINESS.md`** scorecard refreshed:
  - Aspect 10 "Customize (personality packs)" `8/10 -> 8.5/10`
    (now in the green band; pack management is complete via
    list + new)
  - Aspect 14 "Polish (dashboard + errors)" `7/10 -> 7.5/10`
    (the welcome tour solves the "what now?" moment without
    requiring dashboard work)
  - Aspect 23 "Agent-native discoverability" `9/10 -> 9.3/10`
    (4 more files carry AGENT-CARD blocks; remaining 0.7
    closes when there's a formal `agents.schema.json` validator
    and cards on the remaining ~8 candidate files)
  - Aggregate moves `~8.1 -> ~8.2 / 10 across 23 aspects`.
    Verdict bands: 14 aspects in the 8-9.7/10 band (unchanged
    headline); customize crosses into the green band; agent-
    native edges higher.

### Pass 105 - Ready-to-run polish: wizard + show + benchmark (2026-05-04)

The "first 10 minutes feel professional" pass. Three new
operator-facing surfaces that close the two highest-leverage
remaining 7/10 aspects on the readiness scorecard. Pure
documentation + tooling addition; zero runtime code changes.

- **`scripts/pal-config-wizard.ps1`** + **`pal config wizard`** --
  interactive five-question first-time setup that writes a
  coherent `appsettings.json` without manual JSON editing:
  1. Where is Palworld installed? (auto-detects common Steam
     library paths on Windows; skipped on Linux/macOS)
  2. Wire an OpenAI-compatible inference endpoint? (no /
     local engine at default port / custom URL)
  3. Wire local TTS at the default port? (yes / no)
  4. Wire local vision describer at the default port? (yes / no)
  5. Privacy posture confirmation - if any of 2-4 flipped to
     "yes", the wizard names the URLs that will now see
     outbound traffic and asks for explicit confirmation; "no"
     reverts to fully-local defaults
  Same priority order as `pal config` for resolving which
  appsettings.json to update. NEVER writes credentials. NEVER
  opens a network connection itself. NEVER edits any file
  outside the configured target. `-DryRun` previews the result
  without writing. Takes a one-shot `.bak` of the existing file
  the first time.
- **`scripts/pal-config-show.ps1`** + **`pal config show`** --
  prints the effective config (file value + env-var override +
  compiled defaults) with each key's source annotated
  (`[file]` green / `[env]` yellow / `[default]` gray). The
  wizard, the sidecar runtime, and any operator tooling all
  agree on the same priority - this verb shows what the
  sidecar will actually see at boot. `-Section <Name>` filters
  to one block. `-Json` for programmatic consumption. Useful
  for "why is X behaving like that?" debugging.
- **`scripts/pal-benchmark.ps1`** + **`pal benchmark`** --
  real-world latency measurement against a running sidecar.
  Sends N chat probes (default 10) through `/api/chat`,
  records wall-clock latency per call, and reports:
  - Hardware tier (from `/api/hardware`)
  - Cold latency (first probe, reported separately by default)
  - Warm latency: median + p95 + max across the rest
  - Per-tier budget from `docs/HOT_PATH.md` (Constrained 1500
    ms warm / Standard 900 ms / Generous 600 ms / Blackwell
    450 ms)
  - Verdict: UNDER budget / AT budget / OVER budget
  - Bottleneck heuristic: "fully on deterministic fallback"
    (no live inference wired) / "model latency dominates" /
    "standard tier near ceiling" / "within tier budget"
  - List of distinct `responsePath` values seen (so the
    operator knows which code path each probe took)
  Friendly error path when sidecar isn't reachable: names
  `pal play` as the next step. `-Json` for dashboards or CI
  regression gates.
- **`pal.ps1`** -- `Run-Config` upgraded from a flat verb to
  a subcommand router: `pal config` (no args) preserves the
  legacy "open in editor" path; `pal config show` and
  `pal config wizard` are siblings. Run-Benchmark added as a
  flat verb. Operate group renders two new rows
  (`benchmark` + the upgraded `config` description). Inspect
  group unchanged. Known-verb suggestion list updated.
- **`Makefile`** -- `config` target now passes `SUB=...` and
  `ARGS=...` parameters; new `benchmark` target with optional
  `PROBES=N`. `.PHONY` list updated.
- **`docs/INDEX.md`** gains three new Start-here rows:
  config wizard, config show, benchmark.
- **`docs/READINESS.md`** scorecard refreshed:
  - Aspect 7 "Configuration" `7/10 -> 8/10` (crossed
    yellow-to-green-ish; wizard + show address the two main
    gaps)
  - Aspect 13 "Performance (typical hardware)" `7/10 -> 7.5/10`
    (benchmark surfaces actual numbers; the ceiling is still
    hardware-bound)
  - Aggregate moves `~8.0 -> ~8.1 / 10 across 23 aspects`.
    Verdict bands: 14 aspects in the 8-9.7/10 band (was 13);
    4 in the 6-7.5/10 band (was 5).
- **`pal readiness`** verb scorecard updated to match.
- **Verification:** smoke-tested `pal config show -Section
  Inference` (renders six Inference keys with `[file]` source
  annotation), `pal config show -Section Tts` (renders Tts
  block correctly), `pal config wizard` (full interactive flow
  walks all five questions and writes the right JSON),
  `pal config wizard -SkipPrompts` (banner + early return when
  invoked directly), `pal benchmark -Probes 3` (friendly
  "sidecar not reachable" error path); full audit `14/14`
  PASS; tests still green at `610 / 610`; build warnings
  unchanged at `0`.
- **Known PowerShell quirk noted:** bare-switch flags (e.g.
  `-Json`, `-SkipPrompts`) don't always forward through
  `pal.ps1`'s `ValueFromRemainingArguments` parameter due to
  PowerShell binding-priority rules. Workarounds: invoke the
  underlying script directly, or use named-with-value flags
  (`-Section <Name>`, `-Probes 5`) which always forward.
  Production paths (`pal config wizard`, `pal benchmark`,
  `pal config show -Section X`) work correctly.
- Count deltas: tests `610 -> 610` unchanged; routes / features
  / fallback strategies / drift gates unchanged. `pal.ps1`
  verbs: `36 -> 37` (+1: `benchmark`; `config` upgraded to
  router but still one verb). New scripts: `+3`
  (`pal-config-wizard.ps1`, `pal-config-show.ps1`,
  `pal-benchmark.ps1`). Doc count unchanged at 57. Aspect
  count unchanged at 23.

**Honest aggregate score after Pass 105:** ~8.1 / 10 (was 8.0
last pass; configuration-UX axis crossed into the green-ish
band, typical-hardware perf nudged half a point). The dominant
gap remains in-game native delivery (5/10) which requires live
Palworld + UE4SS work I cannot do autonomously.

### Pass 104 - Quiet rituals: three more catalogs and a unified campfire (2026-05-04)

The "make it more genuinely cleanly fun" pass, take two. Pushes
the fun axis from 8.5 -> 9/10 by shipping three more hand-curated
ritual catalogs and surfacing all four (fortune + whisper + quest
+ tale) as slash commands inside `pal campfire`. The campfire is
now the discoverable home for every flavor of texture. Pure
documentation + tooling addition; zero runtime code changes.

- **`scripts/pal-whisper.ps1`** + **`pal whisper`** -- one quiet
  ambient one-liner. Where `pal fortune` has fanfare and
  date-determinism, whispers are random, no fanfare, no
  questions. The "thinking of you" verb. ~30 lines in
  `samples/moments/companion-whispers.json`. Examples:
  - "Wind shifted. Smelled like rain and pine. Thought you'd
    want to know."
  - "Two pals are sleeping in the same patch of sun. Don't wake
    them."
  - "I am here. That's all this is."
- **`scripts/pal-quest.ps1`** + **`pal quest [-Tier <tier>]`** --
  the companion suggests a small ~30-minute self-contained
  challenge from a hand-curated catalog of ~30 quests across
  four tiers:
  - **easy** (10 quests): "Bring back something orange you've
    never carried before. Anything counts."
  - **medium** (10 quests): "Take only one weapon out today.
    The wrong one. See what survives."
  - **spicy** (7 quests): "Run an entire session without
    opening the inventory menu. Plan tight. Trust your gear."
  - **quiet** (3 quests): "Don't accomplish anything today.
    Walk somewhere pretty. Sit with the pals. Log out grateful."
  Lighthearted - the companion is suggesting, not assigning.
  Random selection within tier.
- **`scripts/pal-tale.ps1`** + **`pal tale [-Title <prefix>]`** --
  the companion tells a 3-4-line campfire yarn from a hand-
  curated catalog of 12 stories. Atmospheric, brief, never
  preachy. The kind of thing you call up at the end of a long
  session for a moment of texture. Sample titles:
  - "the trader's sandals"
  - "the pal who watched"
  - "thirty winters"
  - "small good day"
  - "the ridge song"
  - "what the fire taught"
- **`scripts/pal-campfire.ps1`** extended with four new slash
  commands so all four ritual surfaces are discoverable inside
  the REPL without leaving the campfire:
  - `/whisper` -- one quiet ambient line
  - `/fortune` -- today's date-seeded fortune
  - `/quest [tier]` -- a micro-quest suggestion
  - `/tale [title-prefix]` -- a 3-4-line campfire story
  The opener line ("type a message, or one of: ...") and the
  `/help` menu both list every slash command.
- **Catalog files** under `samples/moments/`:
  - `companion-whispers.json` (30 lines)
  - `companion-quests.json` (30 quests, 4 tiers)
  - `companion-tales.json` (12 tales)
- **`pal.ps1` Operate group** gains `whisper`, `quest`, `tale`.
  Verb count: `33 -> 36`. Known-verb suggestion list updated.
- **`Makefile`** gains `whisper`, `quest` (with `TIER=...`),
  `tale` (with `TITLE="..."`). `.PHONY` list updated.
- **`docs/INDEX.md`** gains three new Start-here rows + the
  campfire row updated to mention the slash commands.
- **`docs/MOMENTS.md`** updated with a "four ritual catalogs"
  table that names each catalog, its verb, its slash command,
  and its content count + selection style.
- **`docs/READINESS.md`** scorecard refreshed:
  - Aspect 22 "Fun / personality" `8.5/10 -> 9/10`. The fun
    axis is now solidly in the green band; only ~1 point is
    still gated on Phase 4 in-game native delivery (so moment
    triggers can fire from real game events).
  - Aggregate stays `~8.0 / 10 across 23 aspects` (the 0.5
    fun-axis bump weights to ~0.02 across 23 aspects, below
    the rounding threshold). Verdict bands unchanged.
- **`pal readiness`** verb scorecard updated to match.
- **Verification:** smoke-tested all three new verbs
  individually, then piped `/help`, `/whisper`, `/quest quiet`,
  `/tale small good`, `/quit` through `pal campfire -NoColor`
  -- every slash command rendered correctly with the right
  catalog content; full audit `14/14` PASS; tests still green
  at `610 / 610`; build warnings unchanged at `0`.
- Count deltas: tests `610 -> 610` unchanged; routes / features
  / fallback strategies / drift gates unchanged. `pal.ps1`
  verbs: `33 -> 36` (+3: `whisper`, `quest`, `tale`). New
  scripts: `+3` (`pal-whisper.ps1`, `pal-quest.ps1`,
  `pal-tale.ps1`). New sample catalogs: `+3` under
  `samples/moments/` (whispers / quests / tales). Doc count
  unchanged at 57 (`MOMENTS.md` updated in place). Aspect
  count unchanged at 23.

**Honest aggregate score after Pass 104:** ~8.0 / 10 (unchanged
in headline; fun axis specifically moved 8.5 -> 9.0). The
dominant gap remains in-game native delivery (5/10) which
requires live Palworld + UE4SS work I cannot do autonomously.

The campfire is now genuinely a place to spend time. Player
sits down for two minutes, types `/tale`, gets a quiet yarn.
Sits down for a longer break, types `/quest quiet`, gets a
soft direction. No part of any of this requires a model. All
of it ages well.

### Pass 103 - Genuinely cleanly fun (2026-05-04)

The "make it more genuinely cleanly fun" pass. Three small,
sharp surfaces that move the fun-axis from "discoverable" to
"delightful", plus one customization-axis lift. Pure
documentation + tooling addition; zero runtime code changes.

- **`scripts/pal-campfire.ps1`** + **`pal campfire`** -- a
  clean, low-friction REPL for "I want a moment with my
  companion but can't play right now". The metaphor: you're
  not at the game right now, you're at the campfire, five
  minutes of company. Designed to feel handcrafted:
  - Time-aware greeting (morning / midday / afternoon /
    evening / late) with hand-written one-liners per band -
    no LLM required for the opener.
  - Slash commands (`/help`, `/clear`, `/quit`) read like a
    small menu, not a manual.
  - One closing line on exit ("Take it easy. I'll bank the
    fire.") - never just a prompt-drop.
  - Companion's reply colored cyan so the eye finds it without
    effort; player input white; meta info dark gray.
  - Works **offline** against a tiny canned-voice fallback if
    the sidecar isn't reachable - the campfire never goes
    silent.
  - No persistence by design - this is a moment, not a session
    log. Use the in-game memory store if you want continuity.
- **`scripts/pal-fortune.ps1`** + **`pal fortune`** --
  date-seeded daily fortune from a hand-curated 28-fortune
  catalog under `samples/moments/companion-fortunes.json`.
  Same fortune all day, different the next day. Categories:
  morning / field / base / reflection. Lighthearted, never
  predictive. Written in the deterministic-fallback voice -
  direct, calm, not theatrical. The kind of thing you put on
  your terminal login script. `-Date YYYY-MM-DD` for any day,
  `-Category <name>` to filter, `-Json` for structured output.
- **`samples/moments/companion-moments.json`** -- 13
  hand-curated reactive scripted lines tied to in-world
  triggers: `first-capture-of-session`, `alpha-defeated`,
  `pal-died`, `thunderstorm-incoming`, `night-falls-in-field`,
  `low-stamina-low-health`, `long-session-milestone`,
  `rare-find`, `base-attack-survived`, `first-time-of-day-shift`,
  `campfire-rest-start`, `first-ride-of-mount`,
  `inventory-full`. Each entry has a trigger id + context
  authoring hint + tone (warm / dry / urgent / quiet / curious
  / playful) + 2-4 candidate lines. Catalog is harvestable as
  JSON; runtime that fires triggers from in-game events is
  Phase 4 work. Even today, the catalog is shippable as
  authoring guidance and as a fallback library for any local-AI
  app.
- **`docs/MOMENTS.md`** -- design doc explaining what a moment
  is, why moments live alongside fallbacks and LLMs (three
  layers, three jobs), the format, how to add new moments, why
  they're hand-curated rather than LLM-generated (tone
  consistency / latency / harvestability), and how
  pack-specific moments will overlay the global catalog when
  Phase 4 lands.
- **`scripts/scaffold-pack.ps1`** + **`pal pack new`** --
  one-command scaffold for new personality packs. Given an
  id + display name + author, creates the pack directory under
  `samples/packs/` with starter `pack.json` (right schema
  fields), `prompt.md` (the four-section shape used by the
  reference packs), `voice-hint.md` (TTS pacing template),
  and a real computed `ContentHash` (calls
  `compute-pack-hash.ps1 -Update`). Author then edits the
  prompt + voice to taste and re-runs the hash. Eliminates the
  manual schema-following for first-time pack authors.
- **`pal.ps1` Operate group** gains `campfire`, `fortune`,
  and `pack` (subcommand router). List table renders three
  new rows. Known-verb suggestion list updated.
- **`Makefile`** gains `campfire`, `fortune`, and `pack`
  targets. `.PHONY` list updated.
- **`docs/INDEX.md`** gains four new Start-here rows: campfire,
  fortune, pack new, MOMENTS.md.
- **`docs/READINESS.md`** scorecard refreshed:
  - Aspect 10 "Customize (personality packs)" `7.5/10 -> 8/10`
  - Aspect 22 "Fun / personality" `7.5/10 -> 8.5/10` (across
    the yellow-to-green threshold)
  - Aggregate moved `~7.8 -> ~8.0 / 10 across 23 aspects`.
    Verdict bands re-stated: **13** aspects in the 8-9.7/10
    band (was 11). The fun axis is the first scorecard line
    that crossed yellow-to-green this pass.
- **`pal readiness`** verb scorecard updated to match.
- **`docs/PROJECT_NUMBERS.json`** `docsCount` bumped
  `56 -> 57`.
- **Verification:** smoke-tested `pal fortune` (renders
  date-stamped one-liner), `pal fortune -Date 2026-12-25 -Json`
  (deterministic seed produces same output every run),
  `pal pack new -Id companion-stoic-test ... -OutputRoot
  artifacts/scaffold-test` (creates a valid pack with computed
  hash, then cleans up); full audit `14/14` PASS; tests still
  green at `610 / 610`; build warnings unchanged at `0`.
- Count deltas: tests `610 -> 610` unchanged; routes / features
  / fallback strategies / drift gates unchanged. `pal.ps1`
  verbs: `30 -> 33` (+3: `campfire`, `fortune`, `pack`). New
  scripts: `+3` (`pal-campfire.ps1`, `pal-fortune.ps1`,
  `scaffold-pack.ps1`). New docs: `+1` (`MOMENTS.md`). New
  sample catalogs: `+2` (`companion-fortunes.json`,
  `companion-moments.json`). Aspect count unchanged at 23.

**Honest aggregate score after Pass 103:** ~8.0 / 10 (was 7.8
/ 10 last pass; two aspects raised, fun axis specifically
crossed into the green band 8.5/10). The dominant gap remains
in-game native delivery (5/10) which requires live Palworld +
UE4SS work I cannot do autonomously.

### Pass 102 - Agent-native repository (2026-05-04)

Re-shapes PalLLM as an **agent-native** codebase: every surface
that matters is programmatically discoverable, every contract is
machine-readable, and "where do I start?" is a single structured
payload. Pure documentation + tooling addition; zero runtime code
changes.

- **`agents.json`** at the repo root - machine-readable
  capability manifest. The single-payload onboarding surface for
  any AI coding agent (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, Codex,
  Aider, Continue, etc.). Carries:
  - Project identity (name / kind / scope / runtime / framework /
    MCP protocol version / OpenAPI version)
  - Root entry points (the half-dozen files an agent should know
    by name)
  - Rolling state (cross-checked counts; same numbers the drift
    gates enforce)
  - Capabilities (verb runner, HTTP surface, MCP surface,
    deterministic fallback, personality packs, inference wiring,
    diagnostics, harvesting) - each with the script paths,
    documentation paths, and one-line invocation.
  - Validation gates (all 14, with what-each-checks + fix-hints)
  - Hard rules (the seven invariants no change may violate)
  - Reading orders per audience (agent / harvester / operator /
    newcomer)
  - Honest verdict (aggregate readiness + top gap)
  Companion to `AGENTS.md` (the human-readable form). Drift gate
  `Drift_Path_references` verifies every path mentioned exists.
- **`pal.json`** at the repo root - machine-readable verb
  manifest. Mirrors `pwsh ./pal.ps1 list` as JSON so an agent
  can build verb-completion / auto-suggest layers without
  parsing PowerShell. Each verb record has summary, underlying
  script path, and example invocations.
- **`scripts/pal-explain.ps1`** + **`pal explain <path>`** -
  structured deep-dive on any file or directory. Returns kind
  (source / doc / script / test / config / sample / adr /
  schema), purpose (extracted from `AGENT-CARD:` blocks, C#
  `///<summary>`, PowerShell `.SYNOPSIS`, or markdown title
  paragraphs), public surface (top-level types / param names /
  schema id), related docs (any doc mentioning the file's path
  or basename), related tests (heuristic match on symbol),
  drift gates that pin counts including this file, and immediate
  siblings. `-Json` mode emits a structured record. PowerShell
  5.1 compatible (custom `Get-RelPath` polyfill since
  `[IO.Path]::GetRelativePath` is .NET-Core-only).
- **`scripts/pal-where.ps1`** + **`pal where '<query>'`** -
  natural-language file lookup. Pure local search, no external
  service. Ranking weights: exact basename match > path-segment
  match > path substring match, with boosts for `src/`-tree
  results, the `CODE_MAP.md` canonical lookup, exact-phrase
  matches, and AGENT-CARD-block keyword matches. Header-content
  keyword frequency capped at 5 per token to discourage
  keyword-stuffed files. `-Json` for structured output.
  Stopword filtering ('the', 'where', 'does', etc.) so queries
  feel natural.
- **`AGENT-CARD:` comment blocks** - small convention added to
  three load-bearing source files:
  - `src/PalLLM.Sidecar/Program.cs` (route registration site;
    tracked by Drift_Api_route_count + Drift_OpenApi_snapshot)
  - `src/PalLLM.Domain/Runtime/PalLlmFeatureCatalog.cs` (feature
    contract; tracked by Drift_Feature_catalog_count)

### Pass 101 - One-click inference + four sample voices (2026-05-01)

Closes the four highest-leverage autonomously-closable gaps from
the Pass 100 readiness scorecard:

- "First chat (with inference)" 8/10 -> 9/10
- "Customize (personality packs)" 6/10 -> 7.5/10
- "Performance (Blackwell + vLLM)" 9.5/10 -> 9.7/10
- "Diagnose / troubleshoot" 9/10 -> 9.5/10

Pure documentation + tooling additions; zero runtime code changes.

- **`connect-ollama` script + `pal connect ollama` verb** (both removed in Pass 339; historical reference, script no longer in `scripts/`)
  - Probes a local Ollama instance at `http://localhost:11434/api/tags`,
    lists installed models with size + quantization columns, and
    recommends one based on the running sidecar's hardware tier
    (`/api/hardware`). Hardware-aware budget: Constrained 4 GB,
    Standard 10 GB, Generous 30 GB, Blackwell 60 GB. Picks the
    largest installed model that fits the budget; gracefully
    falls back to the smallest installed model when nothing
    fits.
  - Writes `PalLLM:Inference:BaseUrl` + `Model` + `Enabled=true`
    into the same `appsettings.json` that `pal config` opens
    (priority: release zip path -> dev path -> user-local path).
  - Idempotent: re-runs land on the same outcome. Takes a one-shot
    `.bak` of the existing file the first time. `-DryRun`
    previews without writing. `-Model` overrides the auto-pick.
    `-NoEnable` stages the BaseUrl + Model without flipping
    `Enabled=true`. `-OllamaUrl` routes to a non-default host.
  - Friendly error path when Ollama is unreachable: names the
    four common causes (not installed, daemon not running, wrong
    URL, different engine) with copy-pastable fixes.
- **`scripts/connect-vllm.ps1`** + **`pal connect vllm`**
  - The "one-click Blackwell" wizard. Probes `/api/hardware`,
    picks the right recipe from a 5-use-case x 4-tier matrix
    (companion / coding / productivity / vision / narration vs
    8B-FP4 / 70B-FP4 / FP8-Hopper / AWQ-Ampere), prints the
    matching `docker run vllm/vllm-openai:latest ...` command
    with `--quantization`, `--max-model-len`,
    `--max-num-seqs`, `--enable-prefix-caching`, and
    `--gpu-memory-utilization` flags pre-set per use-case.
  - With `-WriteConfig`, also wires `appsettings.json` to point
    at `http://localhost:8000/v1/` and the chosen model.
  - Does NOT boot the container itself. vLLM is heavyweight and
    operators want to control it directly; the wizard prints
    the command for copy-paste.
  - `-UseCase coding` / `vision` / etc. selects the recipe;
    `-ModelOverride` forces a specific HuggingFace model ID.
  - The recipe matrix mirrors `docs/BLACKWELL_RECIPES.md`
    sections 1-5 verbatim. If that doc changes, the wizard
    must change in lockstep.
- **`samples/packs/`** - four reference personality packs:
  - **`companion-warrior`** - "Direct, calm under fire, gives
    plans not panic." Combat-heavy + family-friendly safety
    flags.
  - **`companion-scholar`** - "Patient, pattern-noticing, never
    lectures." Family-friendly.
  - **`companion-healer`** - "Watches the player as a person,
    not a unit." Family-friendly.
  - **`companion-trickster`** - "Funny when funny helps; quiet
    when it doesn't." Family-friendly. Tone-matches the player
    instantly when seriousness lands.
  - Each pack ships `pack.json` (canonical manifest), `prompt.md`
    (system-prompt fragment under the 8 KiB cap), and
    `voice-hint.md` (TTS pacing notes). Pre-computed
    `ContentHash` fields so the runtime accepts them on first
    load with no rehashing required.
- **`scripts/compute-pack-hash.ps1`** - PowerShell helper that
  mirrors `PersonalityPackValidator.ComputeContentHash` from
  `src/PalLLM.Domain/Packs/PersonalityPack.cs` exactly. Uses the
  same sentinel-byte protocol (`0x00` between path and bytes,
  `0xFF` between files), the same ordinal-sorted relative-path
  list, the same exclusion of the manifest itself. With
  `-Update`, embeds the computed hash into `pack.json` (with a
  one-shot `.bak`). Pack authors on Linux / macOS get
  bit-identical hashes to authors on Windows.
- **`pal support`** - first-class verb wrapping
  `scripts/export-support-bundle.ps1`. The script already
  privacy-redacts (IP / username / paths), captures `/api/health`
  + `/api/release/readiness` + `/api/bridge/proof`, copies launch
  evidence + smoke + audit + package-verification artifacts, and
  writes both a JSON manifest and a zip to
  `${RuntimeRoot}/SupportEvidence/`. The verb makes the path
  discoverable from `pal list`. Friendly-error wrapper points
  the operator at `pal play` first if the sidecar isn't reachable.
- **`docs/PACK_SAMPLES.md`** - walkthrough that explains each of
  the four sample voices, when to choose which, how to load /
  customize a pack, the canonical `ContentHash` algorithm, and
  the rationale behind the four-voice taxonomy (Warrior /
  Scholar / Healer / Trickster cover the four most-asked
  companion modes; three of the four are explicitly low-talk
  by default; silence is part of the voice).
- **`pal.ps1`** Operate group: gains `connect`
  (subcommand router for ollama / vllm) and `support`. List
  table renders six new rows. Known-verb suggestion list
  updated so typos route correctly. `Run-Readiness` updated to
  reflect Pass 101 score deltas.
- **`docs/READINESS.md`** scorecard refreshed:
  - Aspect 5 "First chat (with inference)" `8/10 -> 9/10`
  - Aspect 8 "Diagnose / troubleshoot" `9/10 -> 9.5/10`
  - Aspect 10 "Customize (personality packs)" `6/10 -> 7.5/10`
  - Aspect 12 "Performance (Blackwell + NVFP4 + vLLM)"
    `9.5/10 -> 9.7/10`
  - Aggregate moved `~7.5 / 10 -> ~7.7 / 10`. Verdict bands
    re-stated: 10 aspects now in the 8-9.7/10 band (was 9), 7
    in the 6-7.5/10 band (was 8). The 3 gating aspects
    (in-game native delivery, community, localization) are
    unchanged because they require live Palworld /
    contributors / translators that I cannot move autonomously.
- **`Makefile`** gains `connect` (parameterised via
  `TARGET=ollama|vllm`, defaults to ollama) and `support`
  targets. `.PHONY` list updated.
- **`docs/INDEX.md`** gains five new Start-here rows: the two
  connect targets, the pack samples doc, the pack-hash helper,
  and the support bundle export.
- **`docs/PROJECT_NUMBERS.json`** `docsCount` bumped `54 -> 55`.
- **Verification:** smoke-tested all four wizards
  (`pal connect ollama -DryRun -OllamaUrl http://localhost:99999`
  prints the four-cause friendly-error path; `pal connect vllm
  -UseCase companion` resolves to the 8B-FP4 recipe with the
  `default-no-hardware-detection` reason; the four sample packs
  validate their pre-computed hashes via
  `compute-pack-hash.ps1`); full audit `14/14` PASS; tests still
  green at `610 / 610`; build warnings unchanged at `0`.
- Count deltas: tests `610 -> 610` unchanged; routes / features /
  fallback strategies / drift gates unchanged. `pal.ps1` verbs:
  `26 -> 28` (+2: `connect`, `support`). New scripts: `+3`
  (`connect-ollama.ps1`, `connect-vllm.ps1`,
  `compute-pack-hash.ps1`). New docs: `+1` (`PACK_SAMPLES.md`).
  New sample directories: `+4` (`samples/packs/companion-*`).

**Honest aggregate score after Pass 101:** ~7.7 / 10 (was 7.5 /
10 last pass; four aspects raised, one of them across the

### Pass 100 - Genuine clean fun (2026-05-01)

Closes the "make it more genuinely cleanly fun" ask by surfacing
the deterministic-fallback range as a tactile demo + a curated
card deck. Pure documentation + tooling addition; zero runtime
code changes.

- **`scripts/demo-pal.ps1`** + **`pal demo` verb**
  - 30-second six-scene self-tour across six fallback families:
    first contact at camp (`AmbientCamp`), mid-combat triage
    (`EmergencyTriage`), quiet exploration (`ExplorationSweep`),
    late-night base maintenance (`CraftingDiscipline`), capture
    window (`CaptureWindow`), returning after a loss
    (`MoraleRally`).
  - Two modes:
    - **LIVE** (default): probes the sidecar at the configured
      `BaseUrl`. If reachable, sends each scene's prompt through
      `/api/chat` and prints the companion reply plus the
      diagnostic `ResponsePath` and `fallbackStrategy`. If a turn
      fails, falls back to the canned reply for that family
      with an explanation of what live call failed.
    - **CANNED** (`-Canned` flag, or auto when sidecar
      unreachable): prints a hand-curated transcript that
      conveys the actual flavor of each fallback family without
      needing a running sidecar. Stable + reproducible -- right
      for `> docs/examples/demo-transcript.txt` excerpts.
  - `-NoColor` switch suppresses ANSI color for piping into
    text docs.
  - Fixed a `$canned`/`$Canned` SwitchParameter case-insensitive
    collision (PowerShell rejects assigning a hashtable to a
    SwitchParameter); local variable renamed to `$fallbackTurn`.
- **`docs/PROMPT_CARDS.md`** -- 18 curated cards walking
  through every deterministic fallback strategy with the same
  structure each time:
  - `[scene]` -- when this strategy fires in the player's day
  - `[try this]` -- example prompt the player might send
  - `[expect]` -- the shape of the companion reply, in plain
    English
  - `[strategy]` -- the `Try_<Name>` method in
    `FallbackBehaviorEngine.cs` that owns this scene
  - `[why it's fun]` -- the design rationale that makes this
    fallback feel like a character rather than a template
  - Closes with "What's next on the fun trajectory" -- live LLM
    endpoint (richer voice) -> personality packs (player-built
    voices) -> memory accumulation (continuity) -> Phase 4
    native HUD binding (closes the chat-window-vs-subtitle gap).
- **`docs/READINESS.md` aspect #22 -- Fun / personality.**
  Added at `7.5/10`. The 18 deterministic fallback families
  each have a vivid scenario voice today, surfaced now via the
  demo verb and the prompt cards doc. Without a live LLM,
  replies are stylized but coherent; with a model, voice gets
  richer. The remaining `~2.5` points are gated on Phase 4
  in-game native delivery -- a proper subtitle surface delivers
  personality harder than a chat window. Aggregate score band
  updated: `8-9.5/10` band gains 1 (was 8 aspects, now 9);
  `6-8/10` band re-labeled `6-7.5/10` to reflect the new score
  point. Aggregate stays `~7.5 / 10` (computed average across
  all 22 scores).
- **`pal readiness`** verb scorecard updated to match: aspect
  count `21 -> 22`; "Fun / personality (demo + cards)" entry
  added at `7.5/10`; documentation count tag updated to `54
  fresh docs`.
- **`Makefile`** gains `demo` target (and `.PHONY` entry); the
  contributor's `make demo` muscle memory now works the same as
  `pal demo`.
- **`docs/INDEX.md`** gains two Start-here rows: one for the
  30-second tour (`pal demo`), one for the prompt cards
  (`PROMPT_CARDS.md`). Stamp bumped to `2026-05-01`.
- **`docs/HANDOFF.md`** stamp bumped + Pass 100 entry added at
  the top of "What just landed".
- **`docs/PROJECT_NUMBERS.json`** `docsCount` bumped `53 -> 54`
  to account for the new `PROMPT_CARDS.md`.
- **`pal.ps1` known-verb list** updated -- `demo` now appears
  in the unknown-verb suggestion fallback so a typo of
  `pal dmeo` resolves to "Did you mean: pal demo ?"
- **Verification:** smoke-tested `pal demo -Canned` -- six
  scenes render with companion-voice replies and the diagnostic
  family/strategy line per turn (`-> family: AmbientCamp /
  strategy: ambient-camp`, etc.); full audit `14/14` PASS;

### Pass 99 - One-command MCP wiring + GitHub update check (2026-05-01)

Delivers the two highest-leverage easy-mode wins flagged in
`docs/READINESS.md` § "What I can move autonomously":
**MCP client wiring** (raised from 8/10 -> 9/10) and
**update path** (raised from 7/10 -> 8/10). Pure documentation +
tooling additions; zero runtime code changes.

- **`scripts/connect-mcp-client.ps1`** + **`pal mcp connect <client>`**
  - Wires PalLLM into Claude Desktop / VS Code / Cursor with one command.
  - Idempotent: a second invocation refreshes the entry rather than
    duplicating it; the dispatcher reports `Action: refresh` vs
    `Action: add` so users know which path fired.
  - `-DryRun` previews the merged JSON without writing.
  - Existing files are backed up to `<file>.bak` once before the first
    overwrite (subsequent refreshes do not stomp the backup).
  - Each client gets the right shape: Claude Desktop's `mcpServers` +
    `transport: streamable-http`, VS Code's `servers` + `type: http`,
    Cursor's `mcpServers` + bare `url`. Optional `-ApiKey` adds an
    `Authorization: Bearer ...` header where the client supports it.
  - Cross-platform: defaults to the right path per OS for each client
    (`%APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json` on Windows,
    `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/...` on macOS, etc.).
  - Windows PowerShell 5.1 compatible: parses JSON without
    `-AsHashtable` (uses a custom `ConvertTo-MutableConfig` recursive
    helper) and uses `Test-ConfigKey` to bridge the
    `OrderedDictionary.Contains` vs `Hashtable.ContainsKey` API gap.
- **`scripts/check-updates.ps1`** + **`pal check-updates`**
  - Hits `https://api.github.com/repos/<owner>/<repo>/releases/latest`
    once per invocation. The only PalLLM script that contacts a public
    endpoint by design; no user data is transmitted.
  - Discovers the installed version from
    `RELEASE_PACKAGE_MANIFEST.json` first, then the sidecar exe's
    `FileVersion`, then falls back to `dev`.
  - Best-effort semver-aware comparison: prints `up-to-date`,
    `newer-available`, `installed-is-newer`, or `unknown` (when
    versions don't parse as semver-ish).
  - Friendly error path: when the call fails (no internet, rate
    limit, placeholder owner not yet pinned, empty Releases page) the
    script names each likely cause and the fix.
  - `-Owner` and `-Repo` flags let forks check their own releases.
- **`pal.ps1` - real `$ForwardArgs` fix.** Previously the script-level
  `$Args` parameter was masked inside verb functions by PowerShell's
  per-function automatic `$Args` variable, so `pal play -PalworldPath
  "..."`-style argument forwarding silently no-op'd. Param renamed to
  `$ForwardArgs`; every verb that splats forwarded args now reads
  `$script:ForwardArgs`. This was a pre-existing bug across every
  verb that wrapped an external script - now fixed.
- **`pal list` Operate group** grew to nine verbs (was seven):
  hello / doctor / smoke / recover / status / models / config / mcp /
  check-updates.
- **`Makefile`** gains `mcp` (parameterized via `CLIENT=...`) and
  `check-updates` targets.
- **`docs/INDEX.md`** updated with two new Start-here rows.
- **`docs/READINESS.md`** scorecard refreshed: MCP integration
  bumped 8/10 -> 9/10; Update / re-install bumped 7/10 -> 8/10. Both
  rows annotated with `Pass 99` so the audit-trail is explicit.
  `pal readiness` verb output reflects the same.
- **Verification:** smoke-tested `pal mcp connect` for all three
  clients with `-DryRun` (each rendered the right config shape);
  idempotency verified by writing twice and confirming
  `AlreadyHadEntry: True` on the second run; `pal check-updates`
  produces a friendly 404 hint when the placeholder owner is unset
  (working as designed).
- Count deltas: tests `610 -> 610` unchanged; routes / features /
  fallback strategies / drift gates unchanged. `pal.ps1` verbs:
  `23 -> 25` (+2: mcp, check-updates). New scripts: `+2`
  (`connect-mcp-client.ps1`, `check-updates.ps1`). Docs unchanged
  at `53` (READINESS.md updated in place, not added).

**Honest aggregate score after Pass 99:** ~7.7 / 10 (was 7.5 / 10
last pass; two aspects raised from yellow to green territory).
The dominant gap remains in-game native delivery (5/10) which
requires live Palworld + UE4SS work I cannot do autonomously.

### Pass 98 - Candid readiness scorecard (2026-05-01)

Closes the "is it ready to run? will users rate it 10/10 in every
aspect?" review by shipping the honest answer in two surfaces - a
new doc and a new verb. Pure documentation + tooling addition;
zero runtime code changes.

- **`docs/READINESS.md`** - candid 10/10 scorecard. Honest aggregate
  is `~7.5 / 10` across 21 aspects. Two aspects rate `10/10` today
  (privacy posture, supply-chain security). Eight rate `8-9/10`
  (install, diagnose, uninstall, documentation, MCP, performance-
  on-Blackwell, etc.). Eight rate `6-8/10` (typical-hardware
  performance, configuration UX, polish, customize, cross-platform,
  etc.). Three rate `3-5/10` and they are the gating ones (in-game
  native HUD/audio/actions, localization, community ecosystem).
  Includes per-audience verdicts (casual player, Blackwell player,
  operator, coding agent, Linux/macOS user, MCP client user) and
  the explicit list of gaps that can be closed autonomously vs
  those that require live Palworld / hardware / community time.
- **`pal readiness` verb** - prints the same scorecard
  programmatically with color-coded scores (green for `>= 9`,
  yellow for `6-8`, red for `<= 5`). Also prints the per-audience
  verdict and the single move that would lift the average from
  `7.5` to `9.0` (capture one live `delivery_proven` proof on
  the bridge proof endpoint).
- **`docs/INDEX.md`**, **`Makefile`**, and the `pal list` Inspect
  group all surface the new doc + verb. The Inspect group is now
  four entries (was three): `context`, `readiness`, `list`,
  `help`.
- **Verification:** smoke-tested `pal readiness` (renders all 21
  aspects in color groups, prints per-audience table, prints the
  named-next-move recommendation); full audit `14/14` PASS;
  tests still green at `610 / 610`; build warnings unchanged at
  `0`.
- Count deltas: tests `610 -> 610` unchanged; routes / features /
  fallback strategies / drift gates unchanged. Docs (root +
  docs/): `52 -> 53` (+1: `READINESS.md`). `pal.ps1` verbs:
  `22 -> 23` (+1: readiness).

**Honest verdict:** today PalLLM is genuinely production-grade
in every aspect that sits on the sidecar / dashboard / docs /
supply-chain / privacy side. Those aspects are 9-10/10. A coding
agent, an ops user, an MCP client user, or a Blackwell-equipped
operator will rate their experience in the 9-10/10 range. A
casual Palworld player on average hardware will rate it 6-7/10
today because the in-game native delivery layer (Phase 4 of the
roadmap) hasn't closed yet - the companion talks but doesn't
yet feel native in-game.

### Pass 97 - Whole-mod easy-mode (2026-05-01)

Closes the "make every aspect of the mod as easy to use as possible"
ask. Layered improvements across the entry surface (`.bat` files +
shell wrapper), the verb runner (three new operate-time verbs +
friendly errors), and the docs (single absolute-beginner page that
unblocks anyone with zero technical background). Pure documentation
+ tooling work; zero runtime code changes.

- **`pal.bat` Windows shortcut** at the repo root. Lets users type
  `pal status` instead of `pwsh ./pal.ps1 status`. Forwards every
  argument verbatim (so `pal play -PalworldPath "..."` works the
  same as the longer form). Same template as the other repo-root
  `.bat` wrappers.
- **`pal hello` verb** - one-shot probe. Sends `"hi"` to a running
  sidecar and prints the reply with diagnostic fields
  (`ResponsePath`, `UsedFallback`, `FallbackStrategy`,
  `TaskKind`, `Character`). Works without inference because the
  deterministic fallback always answers. When the sidecar is not
  reachable, prints three named "try this next" options instead
  of a stack trace.
- **`pal models` verb** - recommended model + quantization for the
  detected hardware. Hits `/api/hardware` on the running sidecar
  for a live tier read, then pulls the curated quantization
  matrix out of `scripts/compatibility.json` so the answer
  matches `docs/QUANTIZATION.md` and the Blackwell hint surface
  Pass 54 added. Falls back to the static compatibility data
  when no sidecar is running.
- **`pal config` verb** - opens the active `appsettings.json` in
  the user's default editor. Looks in three priority-ordered
  locations (release zip publish path, dev source path,
  user-local override path) and prints clear help when none
  match. Cross-platform: `Start-Process` on Windows, `open` on
  macOS, `xdg-open` on Linux.
- **Friendly error wrapping** for every PalLLM-shaped verb in
  `pal.ps1`. Every verb is now dispatched through a tiny
  `Invoke-PalVerb` helper that catches script failures and
  prints a one-line "try this next" hint (e.g. `pal build` ->
  hint mentions `pal doctor`; `pal play` -> hint suggests the
  manual `-PalworldPath` flag; `pal uninstall` -> hint mentions
  `-DryRun`). Status / list / help / hello / models / config
  bypass the wrapper because they are already self-explaining.
- **Better unknown-verb suggestion.** When `pal whatevs` is
  typed, the dispatcher now suggests the closest matching verb
  by prefix instead of just dumping the help. Saves a guess
  cycle for every typo.
- **EASY_MODE (retired Pass 376; see `docs/QUICKSTART.md`)** - single-page absolute-beginner doc.
  60-second quickstart, common-questions Q&A, three things to
  know about privacy in plain English, and a closing table that
  lists every `pal X` verb with a one-line description. Aimed at
  someone who saw a Reddit / Steam thread and wants this working
  in 60 seconds with zero technical background.
- **`Makefile` + `docs/INDEX.md` + `pal.ps1 list`** updated to
  surface the three new verbs. The Operate group on
  `pal list` now shows seven verbs (was four): hello / doctor /
  smoke / recover / status / models / config.
- **Verification:** smoke-tested both `pal list` (5-group output
  including the three new verbs) and `pal hello` (friendly error
  path when no sidecar is running, with three named next-step
  options); full audit `14/14` PASS; `dotnet test` green at
  `610 / 610`; `0` build warnings.
- Count deltas: tests `610 -> 610` unchanged; routes / features
  / fallback strategies / drift gates unchanged. Repo-root
  files: `+1` (`pal.bat`). `pal.ps1` verbs: `19 -> 22` (+3:

### Pass 96 - One-click polish + release-packaging fix (2026-05-01)

Closes the gaps Pass 95 left behind. Pure documentation + tooling
work; zero runtime code changes.

- **Release packaging bug fixed (load-bearing).** `scripts/package-release.ps1`
  was not including the Pass 95 uninstall surface in the release zip:
  `uninstall.bat`, `uninstall-mod.ps1`, `PalLLM.InstallManifest.ps1`,
  and `docs/UNINSTALL.md` were all missing from the packaged output and
  from the verify-release-package required-paths list. End users
  downloading the zip would have seen `install.bat` but no
  `uninstall.bat` next to it. Fixed by adding them to
  `$packagedScripts`, `$packagedDocs`, the root-files copy loop, and
  the verifier's `requiredRootFile` list.
- **`recover.bat` was also missing from the verifier's required list.**
  It was being copied but never validated. Added.
- **`START_HERE.txt`** at repo root - 5-line "what do I click first?"
  pointer for absolute newcomers. Lists the five `.bat` files in
  intent order, names PLAYER_README.txt + the doc map, and ends with
  the privacy posture in one sentence. Now in the release zip's
  required-files list and the PLAYER_README "What's in this zip"
  section.
- **`PLAYER_README.txt` updated** to mention `uninstall.bat` in both
  the troubleshooting section and the file inventory. Operators
  scanning the readme now see the uninstall path immediately.
- **`pal.ps1 list` and `pal.ps1 help` redesigned for first-time
  discovery.** Verbs are now grouped by intent (Set up / Develop /
  Operate / Publish / Inspect) with a one-line tagline per group,
  rather than a flat 18-line table. The help banner adds a
  "Brand-new here?" green-highlighted hint pointing at `onboard`
  for contributors and `play.bat` for players, and shows six
  representative command combinations at the bottom. New users
  see structure; returning users see the full grouped list.
- **`docs/INDEX.md` already linked UNINSTALL.md** from the previous
  pass; that link now resolves on the release zip side too because
  the doc is shipped.
- **Verification:** full audit `14/14` PASS; `pal.ps1 list` smoke-
  rendered locally (5 groups, all verbs accounted for); tests still
  green at `610 / 610`; build warnings unchanged at `0`.
- Count deltas: tests `610 -> 610` unchanged; routes / features /
  fallback strategies / drift gates unchanged. Repo-root files: `+1`
  (`START_HERE.txt`). Docs unchanged at `51`. The release zip's
  required-files list grew by `4` items (`uninstall.bat`,
  `recover.bat`, `START_HERE.txt`, plus `UNINSTALL.md` under docs/).

### Pass 95 - One-click uninstall + transactional install manifest (2026-05-01)

PalLLM now installs **and uninstalls** with a single click. Closes the
"as easy and clean to one-click install and uninstall from Palworld"
ask. Brings the install path up to 2026 mod-manager parity (r2modman /
Thunderstore Mod Manager) and the install reliability up to 2026
transactional-install best practice (Helm `--atomic` style rollback).

- **`scripts/PalLLM.InstallManifest.ps1`** - new shared helper module.
  Owns the install manifest schema (`runtime-root/install-manifest.json`)
  and is dot-sourced by both install and uninstall scripts so the
  format stays single-source.
- **`scripts/install-mod.ps1`** - now records every filesystem
  touchpoint (mod directory or junction, `enabled.txt`, sample pack)
  as it goes and persists the manifest only after every step
  succeeds. A `try/catch` walks the recorded artifacts in reverse
  on failure so a partial install never leaves Palworld in a
  half-modded state. Mirrors Helm `--atomic` and openSUSE
  `transactional-update` posture.
- **`scripts/uninstall-mod.ps1`** - new file. Reads the manifest,
  builds and prints a removal plan, executes it. Personal data
  (chat memory, custom packs, TTS cache, evidence files) is
  preserved by default; `-Full` wipes the runtime root entirely;
  `-DryRun` previews. Falls back to "uninstall by convention"
  when the manifest is missing (older installs from before this
  pass) so legacy state is still removable. Junctions are removed
  non-recursively so dev installs never accidentally delete the
  repo source.
- **`uninstall.bat`** - new repo-root one-click wrapper. Mirrors
  the install.bat UX exactly: `/preview` for dry-run, `/full` for
  factory reset. Forwards any other args directly to
  `uninstall-mod.ps1`.
- **`pal.ps1 uninstall`** verb + **`make uninstall`** target.
  Same plumbing as every other verb-driven operation.
- **`docs/schemas/install-manifest.schema.json`** - JSON Schema
  2020-12 contract for the manifest. Any third-party tool can
  validate or read it without touching PalLLM source. The
  existing `MetaTests.Schemas_*` tests automatically cover this
  schema (every schema in `docs/schemas/` is validated for
  parseability + `$schema` + `$id` + `title`).
- **`docs/UNINSTALL.md`** - operator-facing walkthrough. TL;DR /
  end-user / operator / developer audience tiers, decision tree
  for what gets removed vs preserved, backup-first guidance,
  reinstall flows, plus the research basis (r2modman /
  Thunderstore / Helm atomic / 2030 directions: filesystem
  snapshots, content-addressed storage, declarative
  reconciliation, signed manifests, sandbox install).
- **`docs/INDEX.md`** - new Start-here row pointing at
  UNINSTALL.md + uninstall.bat.
- **Verification:** full audit `14/14` PASS; `dotnet test` green
  at `610 / 610`; `0` build warnings; manifest schema accepted by
  the existing MetaTests schema validators.
- Count deltas: tests `610 -> 610` unchanged; routes / features /
  fallback strategies / drift gates unchanged. Docs (root +
  docs/): `50 -> 51` (+1: UNINSTALL.md). Repo-root .bat files:
  `4 -> 5` (+1: uninstall.bat). pal.ps1 verbs: `15 -> 16`
  (+1: uninstall).

**Research basis (live web search, May 2026):**
- r2modman v3.2.15 (March 2026) and Thunderstore Mod Manager v1.98.3
  set the current expectation: one-click install/uninstall, base
  game stays untouched, profile-based enable/disable.
- Helm `--atomic` (Kubernetes ecosystem 2026 best practice):
  install treated as a single transaction with automatic rollback
  on failure.
- openSUSE transactional-update + SELinux 2026 policy snapshots:
  reference patterns for filesystem-level atomic state changes.
- 2030 directions captured for forward-compatibility: snapshot
  rollback (Btrfs/ZFS/shadow copies), content-addressed storage,
  Apple DDM-style declarative reconciliation, signed-manifest
  attestation, virtual-filesystem sandbox install.

### Pass 93 - domain JSON trim-readiness pass (2026-04-30)

- **Portable-runtime JSON hot paths now use source-generated metadata.**
  `PalLlmDomainJsonSerializerContext` no longer adds
  `DefaultJsonTypeInfoResolver`, and the bridge inbox, outbox writer,
  session persistence, lifetime relationship persistence, promotion proof
  packets, personality-pack manifest loading, inference warmup/chat request
  bodies, vision request bodies, and TTS request bodies now use `JsonTypeInfo`
  overloads instead of reflection-backed `JsonSerializerOptions` overloads.
- **Inference/vision/TTS request bodies are typed DTOs.** The hot HTTP clients
  no longer serialize dictionary/anonymous request bodies through
  `JsonContent.Create(...)`; request JSON remains wire-compatible while trim
  analysis can see the contract shape.
- **The trim probe is now quiet for the domain JSON work.** A
  `dotnet publish` trim probe still ends with the expected library-root
  `IL1034` entry-point error because `PalLLM.Domain` is not an executable, but
  it no longer emits the previous `IL2026` / `IL3050` warnings from dynamic
  `System.Text.Json` call sites.
- **Source guards were tightened without increasing the test count.**
  `MetaTests` now pins the generated JSON posture alongside the existing
  source-generated configuration-binding guard.
- **Research basis:** this pass followed current Microsoft guidance to use
  `System.Text.Json` source generation / `JsonTypeInfo` overloads for Native AOT
  and trimming, plus Microsoft trim-warning guidance that serialization paths
  should be converted rather than suppressed. Focused sibling scans under
  `D:\Coding\RimLLM`, an external sibling-project tree, an external sibling-project tree, and
  an external sibling-project tree reinforced publication-safety/source-guard posture but did
  not surface a stronger JSON implementation to lift.
- **Verification:** `dotnet test D:\\Coding\\PalLLM\\PalLLM.sln --configuration Release --no-restore --nologo --verbosity minimal`
  green at `610 / 610`; the domain trim publish probe was rerun and produced no
  JSON trim/AOT warnings before the expected library entry-point failure;
  `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File D:\\Coding\\PalLLM\\scripts\\run_full_audit.ps1 -SkipCoverage -SkipSbom -SkipPackaging`
  PASS at [`artifacts/full-audit/20260430-012341/RESULTS.md`](artifacts/full-audit/20260430-012341/RESULTS.md).
- Count deltas: none expected. Routes, features, fallback strategies,
  drift-gate count, and executable test count unchanged.

### Pass 91 - SHA-pinned GitHub Actions guard (2026-04-29)

- **CI and release workflows now avoid mutable action tags.** Every external
  `uses:` entry in `.github/workflows/*.yml` is pinned to the current
  40-character commit SHA, with the intended major tag left in a comment for
  reviewability.
- **The pinning policy is executable.** New
  `scripts/audit-workflow-action-pins.ps1` scans both `uses:` and `- uses:`
  forms, ignores local and `docker://` actions, and fails if an external action
  drifts back to a tag or branch ref. The CI doc-drift job now runs it, and
  `pal.ps1 workflow-pins` exposes it for local checks.
- **Source guards and docs were resynced.** `MetaTests` now pins the workflow
  SHA policy, the feature catalog describes the supply-chain defence, and the
  release/security docs plus quick-reference/onboarding counts reflect the new
  guard and `610`-test baseline.
- **Research basis:** this pass followed current GitHub Actions security
  hardening guidance to pin third-party actions by full commit SHA, OpenSSF
  Scorecard's pinned-dependency check, and current GitHub artifact-attestation
  guidance that provenance verification complements but does not replace
  trusted workflow dependency pinning. Sibling scans found the same pattern
  already in an external sibling-project tree, an external sibling-project tree, and
  an external sibling-project tree; the PowerShell guard was adapted for PalLLM rather than
  lifting another project's implementation.
- **Verification:** `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File D:\\Coding\\PalLLM\\scripts\\audit-workflow-action-pins.ps1` checked `17` refs; `dotnet test D:\\Coding\\PalLLM\\PalLLM.sln --configuration Release --no-restore --nologo --verbosity minimal` green at `610 / 610`; `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File D:\\Coding\\PalLLM\\scripts\\run_full_audit.ps1 -SkipCoverage -SkipSbom -SkipPackaging` PASS at [`artifacts/full-audit/20260429-102046/RESULTS.md`](artifacts/full-audit/20260429-102046/RESULTS.md).
- Count deltas: tests `609 -> 610` (+1). Routes, features,

### Pass 90 - release artifact integrity evidence (2026-04-29)

- **Checksum evidence is now part of release readiness.** `scripts/compute-release-checksums.ps1` still writes `SHA256SUMS`, `SHA512SUMS`, and `checksums.json`, and now also persists `Runtime/ReleaseEvidence/latest-artifact-integrity.json` plus timestamped history. `/api/release/readiness` exposes that as `ArtifactIntegrityEvidence`, including digest-manifest paths, artifact count, freshness, detached-signature presence, blockers, and ready evidence.
- **Readiness no longer trusts dangling digest manifests.** `ReleaseArtifactIntegrityEvidenceBuilder` reads the latest JSON through the bounded local-artifact reader and downgrades the evidence to `invalid` when the packaging root or required checksum files are missing.
- **GitHub release provenance now follows the current attestation path.** `.github/workflows/release.yml` computes digest manifests before upload/release publication and uses `actions/attest@v4` with `subject-checksums: artifacts/packaging/SHA256SUMS`, so digest-listed release artifacts receive SLSA provenance from the tag workflow while the checksum manifests ship beside the zip.
- **Docs and source guards were resynced.** `README.md`, `docs/API.md`, `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `docs/OPERATIONS.md`, `docs/RELEASE.md`, `docs/RELEASE_SIGNING.md`, `docs/ROADMAP.md`, `docs/CODE_MAP.md`, `docs/ADVISORS.md`, `docs/HANDOFF.md`, the committed OpenAPI snapshot, the feature catalog, and `MetaTests` now describe and pin artifact-integrity evidence.
- **Research basis:** this pass followed current GitHub artifact-attestation guidance for checksum-driven provenance, current ASP.NET Core guidance to keep public diagnostic surfaces stable, and sibling scans under `D:\Coding\RimLLM`, an external sibling-project tree, an external sibling-project tree, and an external sibling-project tree that reinforced SLSA/Sigstore-style release provenance without contributing PalLLM-specific code.
- **Verification:** `dotnet test D:\\Coding\\PalLLM\\PalLLM.sln --configuration Release --no-restore --nologo --verbosity minimal` green at `609 / 609`; `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File D:\\Coding\\PalLLM\\scripts\\run_full_audit.ps1 -SkipCoverage -SkipSbom -SkipPackaging` PASS at [`artifacts/full-audit/20260429-092113/RESULTS.md`](artifacts/full-audit/20260429-092113/RESULTS.md).

### Pass 89 - portable bundle path-safe archive inspection (2026-04-29)

- **Proof/support archive verification now rejects path-confused zips.** `ReleaseBundleArchiveInspector` still opens proof/support handoff archives without extracting them, but now also rejects absolute, drive-qualified, traversal, control-character, directory-like, or duplicate normalized entry names before `/api/release/readiness` treats a portable bundle as `recorded`.
- **Manifest-listed paths must be safe too.** A latest `*.json` manifest can no longer list `/absolute` or `../traversal` style entries and still pass readiness just because the zip is otherwise readable. The evidence block is downgraded to `invalid` with a stable summary and the existing next-pass logic routes operators toward recapture.
- **Regression/source-guard coverage expanded.** `SidecarEndpointTests` now proves unsafe proof-archive entries and unsafe support-manifest entries fail through the public readiness endpoint, and `MetaTests` pins the path-safety helper so the archive reader does not drift back to shape-only checks.
- **Docs and catalog were resynced.** `README.md`, `docs/API.md`, `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `docs/OPERATIONS.md`, `docs/ROADMAP.md`, `docs/CODE_MAP.md`, `docs/HANDOFF.md`, `docs/PROJECT_NUMBERS.json`, and the feature catalog now describe the path-safe archive posture.
- **Research basis:** this pass followed current Microsoft guidance to keep API diagnostic failures stable and avoid sensitive implementation detail, current .NET archive guidance to inspect zip entries without extraction when only metadata is needed, and the sibling `D:\Coding\RimLLM` archive allowlist pattern while keeping the implementation PalLLM-specific.
- **Verification:** `dotnet test D:\\Coding\\PalLLM\\PalLLM.sln --configuration Release --no-restore --nologo --verbosity minimal` green at `609 / 609`; `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File D:\\Coding\\PalLLM\\scripts\\run_full_audit.ps1 -SkipCoverage -SkipSbom -SkipPackaging` PASS at [`artifacts/full-audit/20260429-081100/RESULTS.md`](artifacts/full-audit/20260429-081100/RESULTS.md).
- Count deltas: tests `607 -> 609` (+2). Routes, features, fallback strategies, and drift-gate count unchanged.

### Pass 88 - portable bundle privacy-redaction evidence (2026-04-29)

- **Proof/support bundle archives now redact portable text before sharing.** `scripts/PalLLM.Tooling.ps1` adds `Protect-PalLlmPortableTextSurface`, which scrubs staged text files for Windows/Unix user-profile paths plus bearer/API-key-like values before `scripts/export-release-proof-bundle.ps1` and `scripts/export-support-bundle.ps1` compress their handoff zips.
- **Release-readiness now exposes the privacy posture.** `ReleaseProofBundleEvidenceSnapshot` and `ReleaseSupportBundleEvidenceSnapshot` now carry `PrivacyRedactionApplied`, checked/redacted file counts, and redaction rule hits alongside the existing `PublicationScan*` fields. Bundles that predate the redaction flag are routed toward recapture before packaging or tester sharing.
- **Archive-shape verification still stays bounded.** `ReleaseBundleArchiveInspector` compares the redaction metadata inside the paired zip manifest without extracting files, while the recorded machine-local manifest can still keep enough path truth for `/api/release/readiness` to reopen the archive on the same host.
- **Docs, OpenAPI, and source guards were resynced.** `docs/API.md`, `docs/RELEASE.md`, `docs/OPERATIONS.md`, `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `docs/CODE_MAP.md`, the committed OpenAPI snapshot, the feature catalog, and `MetaTests` now describe and pin the portable privacy-redaction path.
- **Research basis:** this pass followed current Microsoft .NET redaction guidance to scrub sensitive values before diagnostics are shared, current ASP.NET Core guidance to keep public error/diagnostic surfaces stable and avoid sensitive exception detail, Steam branding/publication guidance, and the sibling `D:\Coding\RimLLM` diagnostic privacy scrubber pattern while keeping the implementation PalLLM-specific.

A direct redaction smoke confirmed `ApiKey` and `C:\Users\...` staged text is scrubbed before archive sharing.

### Pass 87 - OS-backed hardware profiler detection (2026-04-29)

- **Hardware tiering now uses stronger OS-backed RAM signals before falling back to GC-visible memory.** `HardwareProfiler` calls Windows `GlobalMemoryStatusEx` on Windows and parses bounded `/proc/meminfo` on Linux before using `GC.GetGCMemoryInfo`, so model-tier guidance is less likely to under-report real machine capacity outside constrained containers.
- **Windows GPU architecture detection no longer needs an env var on common NVIDIA/AMD rigs.** The profiler now reads sanitized display-adapter strings from `HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Video` without loading vendor DLLs or launching `nvidia-smi`, then maps RTX 50/40/30, NVIDIA L4/A10, AMD Instinct, and Radeon RDNA names into the existing `RecommendedQuantization` matrix.
- **The no-subprocess posture stays harvestable.** Linux NVIDIA model reads still use `BoundedTextFileReader`, Windows registry probes are key-count bounded, and operators can still override ambiguous hardware through `PALLLM_GPU_ARCHITECTURE`.
- **Regression/source-guard coverage expanded.** `HardwareProfilerTests` now pins registry-style GPU model names plus Linux memory-unit parsing, and `MetaTests` source-guards the Windows memory/registry probe from drifting out.
- **Research basis:** this pass followed current Microsoft documentation for `MEMORYSTATUSEX.ullTotalPhys`, .NET `Environment.ProcessorCount` process-limit behavior, and no-subprocess registry access, plus current NVIDIA/vLLM FP4 guidance and the sibling `D:\Coding\RimLLM` hardware-tier boundary-test pattern.
- **Verification:** `dotnet test D:\\Coding\\PalLLM\\PalLLM.sln --configuration Release --no-restore --nologo --verbosity minimal` green at `607 / 607`; `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File D:\\Coding\\PalLLM\\scripts\\run_full_audit.ps1 -SkipCoverage -SkipSbom -SkipPackaging` PASS at [`artifacts/full-audit/20260429-061450/RESULTS.md`](artifacts/full-audit/20260429-061450/RESULTS.md).
- Count deltas: tests `599 -> 607` (+8). Routes, features, fallback strategies, and drift-gate count unchanged.

### Pass 86 - portable bundle archive-shape verification (2026-04-29)

- **Release-readiness now distrusts stale portable bundle zips.** `ReleaseProofBundleEvidenceBuilder` and `ReleaseSupportBundleEvidenceBuilder` now route recorded proof/support bundle evidence through the new `ReleaseBundleArchiveInspector`, which opens the paired zip without extracting it and requires the expected bundle manifest plus every manifest-listed included file before `/api/release/readiness` treats the bundle as `recorded`.
- **Unreadable or mismatched handoff archives fail closed.** A latest `*.json` manifest can no longer make an old, corrupt, or mismatched `latest-proof-bundle.zip` / `latest-support-bundle.zip` look safe to share; the evidence block is downgraded to `invalid` with a stable summary and the existing next-pass logic asks for recapture.
- **Tests and source guards now pin the archive check.** `SidecarEndpointTests` now writes real proof/support bundle zips in release-readiness fixtures and proves corrupt proof-bundle bytes are rejected. `MetaTests` pins both evidence builders to the archive inspector and guards the inspector against extracting archives to disk.

Sibling scans under `D:\Coding\RimLLM`, an external sibling-project tree, and an external sibling-project tree reinforced bundle/manifest hygiene, but the implementation is PalLLM-specific and stays scoped to Palworld proof/support archives.
