### Pass 85 - portable bundle publication-scan evidence (2026-04-29)

- **Proof and support bundles now carry publication-scan evidence.** `scripts/export-release-proof-bundle.ps1` and `scripts/export-support-bundle.ps1` scan their staged text files before writing manifests and now persist `PublicationScanPassed`, `PublicationScanCheckedFileCount`, and `PublicationScanViolations`.
- **Bundle scans share the release-package policy.** `scripts/PalLLM.Tooling.ps1` now owns the reusable text-surface scanner, and `scripts/verify-release-package.ps1` delegates to it instead of carrying a private copy of the denylist logic. Release packages keep their stricter root player-copy provider-neutrality check.
- **Release-readiness can block bad handoff archives.** `ReleaseProofBundleEvidenceSnapshot` and `ReleaseSupportBundleEvidenceSnapshot` expose the scan fields through `/api/release/readiness`; failed proof/support scans mark the bundle `invalid` and make the next recommended pass recapture the affected archive before tester handoff.
- **Docs, OpenAPI, and source guards were resynced.** `docs/API.md`, `docs/RELEASE.md`, `docs/OPERATIONS.md`, `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `docs/CODE_MAP.md`, the committed OpenAPI snapshot, and `tests/PalLLM.Tests/MetaTests.cs` now describe and pin the portable-bundle scan posture.
- **Research basis:** this pass followed current ASP.NET Core production error/public-surface guidance, current ASP.NET Core hot-path guidance to keep checks bounded and tooling-side, Steam branding guidance around not implying sponsorship or affiliation, and the sibling an action-RPG sibling runtime staged release-bundle hygiene pattern while keeping PalLLM scoped to Palworld.

### Pass 83 - source-generated configuration binding (2026-04-29)

- **A source guard pins the startup posture.** `tests/PalLLM.Tests/MetaTests.cs` now fails if the sidecar project drops the generated binding setting, keeping the low-reflection/trimming-friendly config path visible to small future agents.

### Pass 82 - release package readme/doc drift guard (2026-04-29)

- **Release packages now bundle every root-level doc named by `PLAYER_README.txt`.** `scripts/package-release.ps1` now includes `docs/PITCH.md`, `docs/FAQ.md`, and `docs/PRIVACY.md` alongside the existing deployment docs, so the generated "What's in this zip" section no longer points players at files that are absent from the release archive.
- **Root player copy is now narrower and generated.** The package script now writes a compact player-facing `CHANGELOG.md` through `Write-PlayerChangelog` instead of copying the source repository's developer changelog, and the generated `PLAYER_README.txt` now uses protocol/provider-neutral wording for MCP clients and local inference endpoints.
- **The release-packaging script now boots cleanly under Windows PowerShell.** `scripts/package-release.ps1` resolves its default `OutputRoot` after `$PSScriptRoot` is bound, avoiding the empty-`$PSScriptRoot` parameter-default failure hit by direct `-File` execution.
- **Package publication-surface verification is source-guarded and runtime-visible.** `scripts/verify-release-package.ps1` scans expanded release packages for sibling-project bleed and unnecessary root player-copy brands, then records `PublicationScanPassed`, `PublicationScanCheckedFileCount`, and `PublicationScanViolations` in the durable verification artifact. `ReleasePackageVerificationEvidenceSnapshot` now carries those fields through `/api/release/readiness`, and the committed OpenAPI snapshot was refreshed.
- **The package/readme contract is source-guarded.** `tests/PalLLM.Tests/MetaTests.cs` now parses the generated `$playerReadme` body and the `$packagedDocs` array, checks root player-copy wording, source-guards the generated changelog and package-publication scan, and fails the suite if release copy references a `docs\*.md` file that the package script does not ship.
- **Release docs and counts were resynced.** `docs/RELEASE.md` now documents the package/readme and package-publication-scan invariants, and count-bearing docs were bumped to the `598`-test baseline.
- **Research basis:** this pass kept the release shape aligned with current Microsoft .NET deployment guidance for self-contained single-file distribution and ReadyToRun startup tuning, current ASP.NET Core API error/public-copy hygiene, and the sibling RimLLM public-copy scope-guardrail ADR.

### Pass 81 - pack publication-safety validation (2026-04-29)

### Pass 80 - lazy bounded directory retention (2026-04-29)

- **Retention sweeps no longer materialize and sort full runtime directories.** `src/PalLLM.Domain/Runtime/DirectoryRetention.cs` now uses `DirectoryInfo.EnumerateFiles(...)` with duplicate-path tracking across overlapping patterns, deletes age-expired files as they are encountered, and keeps only a bounded `PriorityQueue<FileInfo, long>` of the newest retained candidates for `maxFiles` pruning.
- **Long unattended sessions now prune with bounded memory.** Outbox, archive, failed, diagnostics, TTS, and pending screenshot cleanup still enforces the same age/count contract, but count pruning no longer needs an array of every matching file plus a full sort.
- **Regression/source-guard coverage was tightened.** `tests/PalLLM.Tests/RuntimeTests.cs` now proves overlapping retention patterns do not double-count or double-delete while preserving the newest survivors, and `tests/PalLLM.Tests/MetaTests.cs` guards `DirectoryRetention.cs` against drifting back to `GetFiles(...)` or full-list sorting.
- **Docs were resynced to the live retention posture.** `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `docs/CODE_MAP.md`, `docs/HOT_PATH.md`, `docs/INVARIANTS.md`, `docs/OPERATIONS.md`, `docs/QUICKREF.md`, and `docs/HANDOFF.md` now describe retention as lazy and bounded; current count-bearing docs were bumped to `595` passing tests.
- **Research basis:** this pass followed current Microsoft guidance that `EnumerateFiles` can begin returning entries before the whole directory is collected, that `GetFiles` waits for the full array, that `PriorityQueue<TElement,TPriority>` removes the lowest-priority element, and that ASP.NET Core hot paths should minimize allocation pressure. A sibling scan under `D:\Coding\RimLLM`, an external sibling-project tree, an external sibling-project tree, and an external sibling-project tree also surfaced publication-safety ideas for a later pack-validation pass, but did not produce a better retention helper to lift directly.
- **Verification:** `dotnet test D:\\Coding\\PalLLM\\PalLLM.sln --configuration Release --no-restore --nologo --verbosity minimal` green at `595 / 595`; `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File D:\\Coding\\PalLLM\\scripts\\run_full_audit.ps1 -SkipCoverage -SkipSbom -SkipPackaging` PASS at [`artifacts/full-audit/20260429-014603/RESULTS.md`](artifacts/full-audit/20260429-014603/RESULTS.md).
- Count deltas: tests `594 -> 595` (+1). Routes, features, fallback strategies, and drift-gate count unchanged.

### Pass 79 - streamed bounded HTTP text-body reader (2026-04-29)

- **Bounded HTTP text bodies no longer take a second buffering hop before decode.** `src/PalLLM.Domain/Inference/HttpContentReadLimiter.cs` now keeps `ReadTextAsync(...)` on the original `ReadAsStreamAsync(...)` stream behind the existing bounded wrapper instead of copying the body through an intermediate `MemoryStream` and then decoding it.
- **The body phase is now cancellation-aware on current .NET.** The shared text-reader path now uses `StreamReader.ReadToEndAsync(CancellationToken)`, so inference, vision, and TTS status-body drains keep honoring the explicit content-phase timeout/cancellation budget that PalLLM already applies after `HttpCompletionOption.ResponseHeadersRead` returns.
- **Regression coverage was tightened without increasing the executable suite count.** `tests/PalLLM.Tests/InferenceClientTests.cs` now proves unknown-length inference error bodies still drain through the shared bounded text-reader while the public `HTTP 502` status remains sanitized, and `tests/PalLLM.Tests/MetaTests.cs` source-guards `HttpContentReadLimiter.cs` against drifting back to `new MemoryStream(...)` or a non-cancellable `ReadToEndAsync` call.
- **Transport docs were resynced to the live helper.** `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `docs/CODE_MAP.md`, and `docs/HANDOFF.md` now describe the transport seam honestly as shared bounded JSON/text/byte readers rather than implying the text-body path still buffers through a separate in-memory copy.
- **Research basis:** this pass followed current Microsoft guidance that `HttpCompletionOption.ResponseHeadersRead` leaves content-phase timeout enforcement to the caller, that `HttpContent.ReadAsStreamAsync(...)` stays stream-oriented on that path, that `StreamReader.ReadToEndAsync(CancellationToken)` is available on current .NET, and that reusable/pool-backed buffers remain the right fit for transient body handling. A sibling scan under `D:\Coding\RimLLM`, an external sibling-project tree, and an external sibling-project tree reinforced the streaming/body-timeout posture but did not surface a more efficient text-body helper to lift directly.
- **Verification:** `dotnet test D:\\Coding\\PalLLM\\PalLLM.sln --configuration Release --no-restore --nologo --verbosity minimal` green at `594 / 594`; `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File D:\\Coding\\PalLLM\\scripts\\run_full_audit.ps1 -SkipCoverage -SkipSbom -SkipPackaging` PASS at [`artifacts/full-audit/20260429-010943/RESULTS.md`](artifacts/full-audit/20260429-010943/RESULTS.md).
- Count deltas: none expected. Routes, features, fallback strategies, drift-gate count, and executable test count unchanged.

### Pass 78 - stable adapter warning summaries + publication-doc guard (2026-04-28)

- **Adapter warning logs no longer echo raw exception text on the remaining support-bundle-facing bridge/file-operation seams.** `src/PalLLM.Domain/Runtime/PalLlmRuntime.cs` now routes screenshot processing, outbox clear, outbox write, and bridge event processing failures through explicit helpers such as `DescribeScreenshotProcessingFailure(...)`, `DescribeOutboxWriteFailure(...)`, and `DescribeBridgeProcessingFailure(...)` instead of interpolating `ex.Message`.
- **The new warning summaries keep operational signal while dropping unstable path/serializer wording.** Representative outcomes now stay on stable local contracts such as `vision output could not be applied`, `the file could not be deleted`, `reply envelope directory was missing`, and `bridge event payload was invalid for its declared type`, which keeps `/api/logs`, the dashboard, and support bundles readable without copying machine-local failure prose into the shipped support surface.
- **Regression coverage now pins both runtime behavior and source shape.** `tests/PalLLM.Tests/RuntimeTests.cs` now proves representative screenshot, outbox, and bridge failures still fail gracefully while logging only the new stable summaries, and `tests/PalLLM.Tests/MetaTests.cs` source-guards `PalLlmRuntime.cs` against drifting back to the five raw warning patterns that used `ex.Message`.
- **Publication-facing docs now have a mechanical sibling-project guard.** New `tests/PalLLM.Tests/MetaTests.cs` coverage scans `README.md`, `docs/PITCH.md`, `docs/FAQ.md`, `docs/API.md`, `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `docs/ROADMAP.md`, `docs/RELEASE.md`, and `docs/PRIVACY.md` for sibling-project bleed (`RimLLM`, `an external asset-generation sibling`, `an action-RPG sibling runtime`, and the audited the external prompt-pack project pack identifiers) so release-facing copy stays PalLLM-specific even when sibling-project audits continue in development docs.

- **Research basis:** this pass followed current Microsoft ASP.NET Core guidance not to serve sensitive exception information in production, current Microsoft logging guidance that detailed log levels may contain sensitive app data, and current .NET redaction guidance that sensitive information should not be written to logs in plain text. A sibling audit under an external sibling-project tree also surfaced a useful publish-surface guard pattern that was adapted into the PalLLM meta-test layer.

### Pass 77 - stable narrative-pack parse-location messaging (2026-04-28)

- **Narrative-pack validation no longer forwards serializer exception prose.** `src/PalLLM.Domain/Packs/NarrativePackValidator.cs` now maps malformed JSON through `DescribeParseFailure(JsonException)` instead of returning `Pack JSON could not be parsed: {ex.Message}`.
- **Pack authors still get actionable location help.** The validator now returns an explicit location-based contract (`Pack JSON could not be parsed near line X, byte Y.`) when `System.Text.Json` provides parse coordinates, preserving usefulness without coupling the public message to serializer wording.

### Pass 76 - explicit oversized inference/vision status contract (2026-04-28)

- **Inference and vision no longer depend on exception text for oversized success-body failures.** `src/PalLLM.Domain/Inference/InferenceClient.cs` and `src/PalLLM.Domain/Inference/VisionClient.cs` now route those `response_too_large` statuses through `HttpContentReadLimiter.BuildExceededLimitMessage(...)` directly instead of forwarding `InvalidDataException.Message`.
- **The public wording is now intentionally stable and shared.** Both lanes use one explicit local contract (`Inference response exceeds the configured cap of N bytes.` / `Vision response exceeds the configured cap of N bytes.`), which keeps the publication-facing API seam predictable even if the underlying limiter implementation changes later.
- **Regression coverage now pins both behavior and source shape.** `tests/PalLLM.Tests/InferenceClientTests.cs` asserts the exact oversized status text for inference and vision, and `tests/PalLLM.Tests/MetaTests.cs` source-guards both clients against drifting back to `catch (InvalidDataException ex)` or `ex.Message`-derived public status strings.
- **Operator docs were resynced to the live contract.** `docs/API.md`, `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, and `docs/HANDOFF.md` now describe the explicit oversized-body wording instead of only the broader bounded-response posture.
- **Research basis:** this pass followed current Microsoft guidance that `HttpCompletionOption.ResponseHeadersRead` stops `HttpClient.Timeout` at the end of headers and leaves content-phase enforcement to the caller, plus current ASP.NET Core guidance not to serve sensitive exception detail to clients in production.
- **Verification:** `dotnet test D:\\Coding\\PalLLM\\PalLLM.sln --configuration Release --no-restore --nologo --verbosity minimal` green at `591 / 591`; `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File D:\\Coding\\PalLLM\\scripts\\run_full_audit.ps1 -SkipCoverage -SkipSbom -SkipPackaging` PASS at [`artifacts/full-audit/20260428-230640/RESULTS.md`](artifacts/full-audit/20260428-230640/RESULTS.md).
- Count deltas: none expected. Routes, features, fallback strategies, drift-gate count, and executable test count unchanged.

### Pass 75 - pooled bounded TTS audio-body reader (2026-04-28)

- **The shared HTTP body limiter now covers binary transport bodies too.** `src/PalLLM.Domain/Inference/HttpContentReadLimiter.cs` now exposes `ReadBytesAsync(...)`, extending the existing bounded JSON/text transport seam to successful binary responses with the same header-first size checks, explicit byte cap, and small initial pooled buffer for unknown-length streams.
- **`HttpTtsClient` no longer grows a dedicated `MemoryStream` just to copy it again into the final `byte[]`.** `src/PalLLM.Domain/Inference/TtsClient.cs` now reads successful audio through `HttpContentReadLimiter.ReadBytesAsync(...)`, so unknown-length TTS bodies start from a small rented buffer, grow only as needed, and still fail with the same stable oversized-audio status when they cross `PalLLM:Tts:MaxResponseBytes`.
- **The public TTS failure path is now source-guarded against raw exception-message passthrough.** `tests/PalLLM.Tests/MetaTests.cs` now requires `TtsClient` to stay on the shared bounded byte-reader seam and blocks regressions back to `new MemoryStream(...)` growth or `TtsResult.Failed(ex.Message)` on the public path.
- **Operator docs were resynced to the live transport posture.** `docs/API.md`, `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, and `docs/HANDOFF.md` now describe TTS as using the shared bounded HTTP byte-reader before audio is materialized, written, or attached to a response.
- **Research basis:** this pass followed current Microsoft guidance that `HttpCompletionOption.ResponseHeadersRead` leaves content-phase timeout/stream handling to the caller, that `HttpContent.ReadAsStreamAsync(...)` stays stream-oriented on that path, and that `ArrayPool<T>` improves performance when transient buffers are created and destroyed frequently.
- **Verification:** `dotnet test D:\\Coding\\PalLLM\\PalLLM.sln --configuration Release --no-restore --nologo --verbosity minimal` green at `591 / 591`; `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File D:\\Coding\\PalLLM\\scripts\\run_full_audit.ps1 -SkipCoverage -SkipSbom -SkipPackaging` PASS at [`artifacts/full-audit/20260428-220735/RESULTS.md`](artifacts/full-audit/20260428-220735/RESULTS.md).
- Count deltas: none expected. Routes, features, fallback strategies, and drift-gate count unchanged.

### Pass 74 - companion-intelligence design pass from external research prompt-pack audit (2026-04-28)

- **Added a PalLLM-specific companion-intelligence design note instead of smuggling speculative AGI work into the ship-critical queue.** The retired COMPANION_INTELLIGENCE design note (Pass 376) captured the best PalLLM fits from an external sibling-project tree audit: visible memory, replay/rationale traces, confidence-calibrated escalation, advisory world-model planning, idle reflection/compaction, anomaly detection, and batching/quant-aware routing.
- **The external prompt-pack audit was broad, but the conclusions were intentionally narrow.** The scan covered the extracted prompt-pack directories, the older `_archive` material, and the `12` top-level zip archives. The zips were confirmed to be mirrors of the extracted prompt packs rather than hidden extra content. Highest-signal packs for PalLLM were `external-pack`, `external-pack`, `external-pack`, `external-pack`, `external-pack`, and the older `external-pack` archive.
- **The design note keeps PalLLM scoped as a Palworld mod.** Browser/computer-use agents, sandboxed code execution, webcam/mic-first features, social/viral generators, and broad gameplay autopilot were explicitly rejected as poor fits. The approved direction is "AGI-lite" through better memory, world modeling, self-critique, confidence, replay, and low-latency scheduling on top of the current deterministic local-first runtime.
- **Roadmap and queue docs now explain where this work belongs.** `docs/INDEX.md` now links the new note directly, `docs/IMPLEMENTATION_QUEUE.md` now says these ideas are post-foundation and must not preempt the native-proof/HUD/audio/action queue, and `docs/ROADMAP.md` now calls broad AGI-style expansion a non-goal until the native Palworld proof chain is finished.
- **Research basis:** local sibling prompt-pack audit only for this pass. The most relevant prompt IDs were `G20`, `G21`, `G24`, `G50`, `F01`, `F04`, `F31`, `F32`, `F35`, `F36`, `F42`, `S30`, `T01`, `T02`, `T04`, `T05`, `T09`, `U05`, `U06`, `U07`, `P05`, `P29`, `O07`, and `O08`.
- **Verification:** `dotnet test D:\\Coding\\PalLLM\\PalLLM.sln --configuration Release --no-restore --nologo --verbosity minimal` green at `591 / 591`; `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File D:\\Coding\\PalLLM\\scripts\\run_full_audit.ps1 -SkipCoverage -SkipSbom -SkipPackaging` PASS at [`artifacts/full-audit/20260428-220112/RESULTS.md`](artifacts/full-audit/20260428-220112/RESULTS.md).

### Pass 73 - relative pack listings + MCP rejection hygiene (2026-04-28)

- **Loaded pack listings no longer disclose absolute local filesystem paths.** `src/PalLLM.Domain/Packs/NarrativePackService.cs` now normalizes `PackSummary.FilePath` through a `BuildPackDisplayPath(...)` helper before exposing it, so `GET /api/packs` returns pack-root-relative `/`-separated manifest paths such as `companions/alias-pack.json` instead of absolute machine-local paths.
- **The contract now says what the runtime actually guarantees.** `src/PalLLM.Domain/Integration/Contracts.cs` now documents `PackSummary.FilePath` as a pack-root-relative public path chosen to stay stable across machines without exposing the operator's local layout.
- **`pal_promotion_record` now rejects bad inputs with the same stable shape as the hardened HTTP surface.** `src/PalLLM.Sidecar/Mcp/PalLlmMcpTools.cs` no longer serializes raw `ArgumentException` text from `ledger.Record(...)`; it now validates `taskClass`, `patternId`, and `outcome` up front and returns stable `status=\"rejected\"` payloads such as `taskClass is required.` or `Outcome must be one of: ...`.
- **Regression coverage and source guards tightened without changing the executable suite count.** Existing coverage in `tests/PalLLM.Tests/RuntimeTests.cs` now proves loaded pack paths come back as pack-root-relative paths and do not echo the absolute pack root, while `tests/PalLLM.Tests/MetaTests.cs` now source-guards both `NarrativePackService` and `PalLlmMcpTools.cs` against drifting back to absolute pack-path exposure or raw MCP rejection text.
- **Operator docs were resynced to the live behavior.** `docs/API.md` and `docs/PACK_AUTHORING.md` now describe `GET /api/packs` as returning pack-root-relative manifest paths, `docs/OPERATIONS.md` now states the honest MCP safety posture (`no guarded gameplay actions`, but bounded local operator surfaces like the promotion ledger can still be updated), and `docs/HANDOFF.md` records the new pass.
- **Research basis:** this pass followed current Microsoft ASP.NET Core production error-handling guidance not to serve sensitive error information to clients, plus a sibling audit under `D:\Coding\RimLLM` that reinforced relative-path normalization for publication-facing surfaces without contributing a directly liftable pack-list implementation.
- **Verification:** `dotnet test D:\\Coding\\PalLLM\\PalLLM.sln --configuration Release --no-restore --nologo --verbosity minimal` green at `591 / 591`; `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File D:\\Coding\\PalLLM\\scripts\\export-openapi.ps1 -Verify` matched the committed snapshot; `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File D:\\Coding\\PalLLM\\scripts\\run_full_audit.ps1 -SkipCoverage -SkipSbom -SkipPackaging` PASS at [`artifacts/full-audit/20260428-211028/RESULTS.md`](artifacts/full-audit/20260428-211028/RESULTS.md).

### Pass 72 - blank failed session file paths (2026-04-28)

- **Failed session save/load responses no longer disclose the local session path.** `src/PalLLM.Domain/Runtime/SessionPersistence.cs` now routes every failure result through a shared `CreateFailureResult(...)` helper that keeps `Success=false`, preserves the stable operator-facing `StatusMessage`, and blanks `SessionPersistenceResult.FilePath` instead of returning the absolute `runtime-root/session.json` path on public failures.
- **Successful save/load behavior is unchanged, but the contract is now explicit.** `src/PalLLM.Domain/Integration/Contracts.cs` now documents that `SessionPersistenceResult.FilePath` is populated only for successful save/load operations and remains blank on failure so callers can depend on one stable rule.
- **Regression coverage was tightened without changing the executable suite count.** Existing runtime tests in `tests/PalLLM.Tests/RuntimeTests.cs` now prove both failed save and failed load paths keep `FilePath` blank, and `tests/PalLLM.Tests/MetaTests.cs` source-guards `SessionPersistence.cs` against drifting back to `Success=false` plus `FilePath = path`.
- **The public docs and handoff now describe the live session contract honestly.** `docs/API.md`, `docs/OPERATIONS.md`, and `docs/HANDOFF.md` now state that failed session save/reload responses keep `FilePath` blank alongside the already-sanitized `StatusMessage`.
- **Research basis:** this pass followed current Microsoft ASP.NET Core guidance not to serve sensitive error information to clients in production and to standardize API error shaping through stable public contracts where possible.
- **Verification:** `dotnet test D:\\Coding\\PalLLM\\PalLLM.sln --configuration Release --no-restore --nologo --verbosity minimal` green at `591 / 591`; `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/export-openapi.ps1 -Verify` matched the committed snapshot; `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/run_full_audit.ps1 -SkipCoverage -SkipSbom -SkipPackaging` PASS at [`artifacts/full-audit/20260428-204849/RESULTS.md`](artifacts/full-audit/20260428-204849/RESULTS.md).

### Pass 71 - sanitized session persistence failure statuses (2026-04-28)

- **Session save failures no longer echo raw local exception text through the public API contract.** `src/PalLLM.Domain/Runtime/SessionPersistence.cs` now routes save-path exceptions through `DescribeSessionSaveFailure(Exception)` so `POST /api/session/save` returns stable operator-facing status categories instead of filesystem-dependent `ex.Message` text.
- **The direct load-exception fallback stays on the same sanitized status posture.** The exception-based `DescribeSessionLoadFailure(Exception)` helper now remains the single public mapping path for load exceptions, keeping `POST /api/session/reload` aligned with the repo's "no sensitive error detail in public API responses" posture even when failure happens outside the bounded-reader failure-code path.
- **Regression coverage was tightened without changing the executable suite count.** `tests/PalLLM.Tests/RuntimeTests.cs` now proves a blocked save path returns the stable write-failure status and does not echo the attempted local path, while `tests/PalLLM.Tests/MetaTests.cs` source-guards `SessionPersistence` against drifting back to interpolated `ex.Message` save/load text.
- **The operator docs and handoff now describe the live session contract honestly.** `docs/API.md` and `docs/OPERATIONS.md` now state that session save/load failures return `SessionPersistenceResult.Success=false` with a stable `StatusMessage`, and `docs/HANDOFF.md` records this pass plus the fresh audit artifact.
- **Research basis:** this pass followed current Microsoft ASP.NET Core guidance not to serve sensitive error information to clients in production and to use standardized error-shaping patterns for API responses where possible.
- **Verification:** `dotnet test D:\\Coding\\PalLLM\\PalLLM.sln --configuration Release --no-restore --nologo --verbosity minimal` green at `591 / 591`; `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/export-openapi.ps1 -Verify` matched the committed snapshot; `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/run_full_audit.ps1 -SkipCoverage -SkipSbom -SkipPackaging` PASS at [`artifacts/full-audit/20260428-201300/RESULTS.md`](artifacts/full-audit/20260428-201300/RESULTS.md).
- Count deltas: tests unchanged at `591`. Routes, features, fallback strategies, and drift-gate count unchanged.

### Pass 70 - bounded persisted-session ingress + stable reload failures (2026-04-28)

- **Persisted session reload is now bounded before startup deserialization starts.** `src/PalLLM.Domain/Runtime/SessionPersistence.cs` no longer opens `session.json` with a raw `FileStream` and feeds that straight into `JsonSerializer.Deserialize(...)`. Primary and backup loads now go through the shared `BoundedJsonFileReader.TryRead(...)` path via `TryReadSessionFile(...)`, backed by the new `PalLLM:Session:MaxPersistedBytes` option in `src/PalLLM.Domain/Configuration/PalLlmOptions.cs` and the shipped `src/PalLLM.Sidecar/appsettings.json` default of `8388608` bytes.
- **Reload failures now degrade to stable categories instead of arbitrary local exception text.** Oversized, malformed, or unreadable primary files now report stable status strings and fall back to `session.json.bak` without partially populating memory or relationships.
- **Startup validation and appsettings now expose the cap honestly.** `src/PalLLM.Sidecar/PalLlmOptionsValidator.cs` fails fast when `PalLLM:Session:MaxPersistedBytes <= 0`, and `tests/PalLLM.Tests/BackendValidationTests.cs` now requires the shipped config file to document that knob explicitly.
- **Regression coverage and source guards tightened.** `tests/PalLLM.Tests/RuntimeTests.cs` now proves oversized primary session files recover through `.bak` when available and return a stable oversized failure when not, while `tests/PalLLM.Tests/MetaTests.cs` source-guards session reload against drifting back to a direct `FileStream` deserialize path.
- **Docs were resynced to the live session contract.** `README.md`, `docs/API.md`, `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `docs/OPERATIONS.md`, `docs/TUNING.md`, `docs/HANDOFF.md`, `docs/ROADMAP.md`, `docs/CODE_MAP.md`, and `docs/PROJECT_NUMBERS.json` now describe the bounded session-ingress posture and the new `591`-test baseline.
- **Research basis:** this pass followed current Microsoft guidance that `JsonSerializer.Deserialize(Stream, ...)` reads the stream to completion, that `FileOptions.SequentialScan` is the appropriate hint for sequential local reads, and that ASP.NET Core hot paths should avoid large object allocations where practical. The sibling scan across `D:\Coding\RimLLM`, an external sibling-project tree, an external sibling-project tree, and an external sibling-project tree did not surface a directly liftable session-persistence reader; the useful pattern remained PalLLM's own bounded JSON seam.
- **Verification:** `dotnet test PalLLM.sln --no-restore --nologo --verbosity minimal` green at `591 / 591`; `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/run_full_audit.ps1 -SkipCoverage -SkipSbom -SkipPackaging` PASS at [`artifacts/full-audit/20260428-191441/RESULTS.md`](artifacts/full-audit/20260428-191441/RESULTS.md).
- Count deltas: tests `589 -> 591` (+2). Routes, features, fallback strategies, and drift-gate count unchanged.

### Pass 69 - pooled screenshot ingress + lazy outbox clear (2026-04-28)

- **Screenshot ingest no longer allocates a fresh `byte[]` per bridge image.** New harvestable `src/PalLLM.Domain/Runtime/BoundedBase64FileReader.cs` reads local files through `FileStreamOptions`, `FileOptions.SequentialScan`, and `ArrayPool<byte>` growth, then hands `ProcessScreenshotsAsync(...)` a bounded base64 payload without going through `File.ReadAllBytesAsync(...)`.
- **The screenshot watcher now stays on the same bounded-ingress posture as the other file-backed runtime paths.** `src/PalLLM.Domain/Runtime/PalLlmRuntime.cs` now routes `Bridge/Screenshots/*` through the new helper and degrades empty, oversized, or unreadable files to `Bridge/Failed/` with stable log text before any vision call is attempted.
- **Outbox clearing now walks the directory lazily instead of pre-materializing the full file list.** `ClearOutbox()` now uses `Directory.EnumerateFiles(...)` and tolerates enumeration races without first allocating a `string[]` for the whole outbox.
- **Regression coverage and source guards tightened without changing the rolling suite size.** `tests/PalLLM.Tests/RuntimeTests.cs` now proves screenshot ingest still forwards the expected base64 payload and MIME type to the vision path, while `tests/PalLLM.Tests/MetaTests.cs` now blocks screenshot ingress from drifting back to `File.ReadAllBytesAsync(...)` and outbox clearing from drifting back to `Directory.GetFiles(...)`.
- **The harvest/navigation docs now describe the new helper honestly.** `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `docs/DATAFLOW.md`, `docs/CODE_MAP.md`, and `docs/HARVEST.md` now call out the pooled screenshot-ingress reader and the low-allocation local-file posture it enforces.
- **Research basis:** this pass followed current Microsoft guidance around `Convert.ToBase64String(ReadOnlySpan<byte>)`, `ArrayPool<T>` for frequent transient buffers, `FileStreamOptions` / `FileOptions.SequentialScan` for sequential local reads, and `Directory.EnumerateFiles(...)` for lazy filesystem walks. The sibling scan across `D:\Coding\RimLLM`, an external sibling-project tree, an external sibling-project tree, and an external sibling-project tree again reinforced bounded local ingress patterns but did not surface a directly liftable base64 reader.
- **Verification:** `dotnet test PalLLM.sln --configuration Release --no-restore --nologo --verbosity minimal` green at `589 / 589`; `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/run_full_audit.ps1 -SkipCoverage -SkipSbom -SkipPackaging` PASS at [`artifacts/full-audit/20260428-190446/RESULTS.md`](artifacts/full-audit/20260428-190446/RESULTS.md).
- Count deltas: tests unchanged at `589`. Routes, features, fallback strategies, and drift-gate count unchanged.

### Pass 68 - bounded bridge inbox event ingress + live bridge docs (2026-04-28)

- **Bridge inbox drains now stay on the same bounded local-JSON contract as the other file-backed ingress surfaces.** `src/PalLLM.Domain/Runtime/PalLlmRuntime.cs` no longer deserializes `Bridge/Inbox/*.json` envelopes straight from `File.OpenRead(...)`. The drain now routes each file through `BoundedJsonFileReader.TryRead(...)`, backed by the new `PalLLM:Bridge:MaxInboxEventBytes` option in `src/PalLLM.Domain/Configuration/PalLlmOptions.cs` and the shipped `src/PalLLM.Sidecar/appsettings.json` default of `65536` bytes.
- **Oversized, malformed, and unreadable bridge envelopes now degrade deterministically.** Instead of letting a runaway local producer force unbounded bridge-event deserialization, `DrainInbox(...)` now quarantines those files to `Bridge/Failed/` with stable operator-readable reason text while preserving the existing happy path for valid events.
- **Regression coverage and source guards tightened without changing the rolling suite count.** `tests/PalLLM.Tests/RuntimeTests.cs` now proves a normal `chat_message` still drains while an oversized sibling envelope is rejected before it mutates memory, `tests/PalLLM.Tests/BackendValidationTests.cs` now startup-validates `PalLLM:Bridge:MaxInboxEventBytes` and requires the shipped `appsettings.json` to document it explicitly, and `tests/PalLLM.Tests/MetaTests.cs` now blocks bridge drain from drifting back to raw `File.OpenRead(...)` ingress.
- **Bridge-facing docs and schema were resynced to the live runtime.** `docs/API.md`, `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `docs/DATAFLOW.md`, `docs/EVENTS.md`, `docs/EXTENSION_POINTS.md`, and `docs/schemas/bridge-event-envelope.schema.json` now describe the current bridge event vocabulary (`bridge_boot`, `chat_message`, `snapshot`, `base_discovered`, `combat_*`, `pal_status`, `production`, `travel`, `weather_change`, `raid`, `ui_probe`, `reply_delivery`) instead of the stale pre-pass names.
- **Research basis:** this pass followed current Microsoft guidance around stream-based `JsonSerializer.Deserialize(Stream, ...)`, `Directory.EnumerateFiles(...)` versus array-returning enumeration APIs, and `FileOptions.SequentialScan` as the right low-allocation posture for sequential local reads. The sibling scan across `D:\Coding\RimLLM`, an external sibling-project tree, an external sibling-project tree, and an external sibling-project tree did not surface a directly liftable bridge-ingress reader, but the capped-event posture visible in the Unity input-system code cached under `an action-RPG sibling runtime` reinforced the value of explicit per-event byte ceilings.
- **Verification:** `dotnet test PalLLM.sln --configuration Release --no-restore --nologo --verbosity minimal` green at `589 / 589`; `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/run_full_audit.ps1 -SkipCoverage -SkipSbom -SkipPackaging` PASS at [`artifacts/full-audit/20260428-182651/RESULTS.md`](artifacts/full-audit/20260428-182651/RESULTS.md).

### Pass 67 - bounded root-contained personality-pack validation (2026-04-28)

- **Personality-pack validation now stays inside the pack root and off the unbounded-read path.** `src/PalLLM.Domain/Packs/PersonalityPack.cs` no longer deserializes `pack.json` through `File.ReadAllText(...)` or hashes tracked files through `File.ReadAllBytes(...)` plus a concatenating `MemoryStream`. The validator now reads `pack.json` through `BoundedJsonFileReader.TryRead(...)` with a new `MaxManifestBytes = 65536` cap, rejects any `PromptPath` / `VoiceHintPath` / `PortraitPath` / `AudioSamples[]` entry that escapes the declared pack directory, and recomputes `ContentHash` through a streaming `IncrementalHash` pass over sequential file reads.
- **The validator now matches its documented contract more honestly.** `ContentHash` is now required and shape-checked as 64 lowercase hex characters, and malformed manifests or unreadable tracked files now come back as structured validation issues instead of surfacing raw JSON exceptions or file-read failures.
- **The schema/story docs now describe the live pack constraints.** `docs/FAQ.md`, `docs/GLOSSARY.md`, `docs/EXTENSION_POINTS.md`, and `docs/schemas/personality-pack.schema.json` now call out that tracked paths must remain inside the pack root and that content-hash recomputation is streaming rather than full-buffer.
- **Regression coverage and source guards tightened with one new executable case.** `tests/PalLLM.Tests/PersonalityPackValidatorTests.cs` now proves escaped prompt paths, blank content hashes, and oversized manifests are rejected, and `tests/PalLLM.Tests/MetaTests.cs` now blocks `PersonalityPackValidator` from drifting back to `File.ReadAllText(manifestPath)`, `File.ReadAllBytes(abs)`, or `new MemoryStream()` for content hashing. Rolling suite baseline is now `589`.
- **Research basis:** this pass followed current Microsoft guidance around stream-based `JsonSerializer.Deserialize(Stream, ...)`, `FileStreamOptions` with `FileOptions.SequentialScan` for sequential local reads, and the current .NET cryptography APIs for streaming SHA-256/incremental hashing without building a full concatenation buffer in memory. The sibling scan across `D:\Coding\RimLLM`, an external sibling-project tree, an external sibling-project tree, and an external sibling-project tree reinforced the existing source-guard style but did not surface a directly liftable personality-pack validator.
- **Verification:** `dotnet test PalLLM.sln --configuration Release --no-restore --nologo --verbosity minimal` green at `589 / 589`; `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/run_full_audit.ps1 -SkipCoverage -SkipSbom -SkipPackaging` PASS at [`artifacts/full-audit/20260428-171351/RESULTS.md`](artifacts/full-audit/20260428-171351/RESULTS.md); `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/export-openapi.ps1 -Verify` confirmed the committed snapshot still matches.
- Count deltas: tests `588 -> 589` (+1). Routes, features, fallback strategies, and drift-gate count unchanged.

### Pass 66 - bounded narrative-pack reload ingress (2026-04-28)

- **Narrative-pack reload now stays on the bounded-ingress path.** `src/PalLLM.Domain/Packs/NarrativePackService.cs` no longer pre-materializes the recursive pack file list with `Directory.GetFiles(...)` or deserializes on-disk pack JSON from an unbounded stream. Reload now walks the pack tree lazily with `Directory.EnumerateFiles(...)` plus `EnumerationOptions.IgnoreInaccessible`, and each candidate file is read through `BoundedJsonFileReader.TryRead(...)`.
- **The on-disk reload cap is now explicitly shared with the validation endpoint.** `src/PalLLM.Domain/Packs/NarrativePackValidator.cs` now exposes `MaxPackBytes = 1_000_000`, and `src/PalLLM.Sidecar/Program.cs` uses that same constant for `POST /api/packs/validate`. The runtime and the HTTP validator are now on one documented narrative-pack size contract instead of two implicit ones that could drift.
- **Malformed or oversized content files no longer block a good reload.** A single broken or too-large narrative-pack JSON file is skipped while valid packs still load and index normally, keeping startup and `POST /api/packs/reload` resilient under bad authored content.
- **Regression coverage and source guards were tightened without changing the suite size.** `tests/PalLLM.Tests/RuntimeTests.cs` now proves a valid alias pack still loads when a malformed sibling pack and an oversized sibling pack are present in the same directory, and `tests/PalLLM.Tests/MetaTests.cs` now pins `NarrativePackService` to the bounded JSON reader plus lazy file enumeration so this path cannot drift back to `Directory.GetFiles(...)` or `File.ReadAllText(...)`.
- **Docs were resynced to the live pack contract.** `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `docs/API.md`, `docs/PACK_AUTHORING.md`, and `docs/HANDOFF.md` now describe the bounded reload behavior and the shared `1,000,000` byte narrative-pack ceiling.
- **Research basis:** this pass followed current Microsoft guidance that file enumeration APIs returning enumerable collections are better for large trees than array-returning APIs, that `FileOptions.SequentialScan` can improve large sequential reads, and that `JsonSerializer.Deserialize(Stream, ...)` keeps JSON ingress on the stream path instead of buffering text first. The sibling audit across `D:\Coding\RimLLM`, an external sibling-project tree, an external sibling-project tree, and an external sibling-project tree did not surface a directly liftable narrative-pack loader, so the implementation remains PalLLM-native.
- **Verification:** `dotnet test PalLLM.sln --configuration Release --no-restore --nologo --verbosity minimal` green at `588 / 588`; `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/run_full_audit.ps1 -SkipCoverage -SkipSbom -SkipPackaging` PASS at [`artifacts/full-audit/20260428-161118/RESULTS.md`](artifacts/full-audit/20260428-161118/RESULTS.md); `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/export-openapi.ps1 -Verify` confirmed the committed snapshot still matches.
- Count deltas: tests unchanged at `588`. Routes, features, fallback strategies, and drift-gate count unchanged.

### Pass 65 - bounded hardware GPU probe + AMD MXFP4 guidance (2026-04-28)

- **The last unbounded local GPU-info read on the hardware-profiler path is gone.** New harvestable `src/PalLLM.Domain/Runtime/BoundedTextFileReader.cs` centralizes bounded sequential local text reads with `FileStreamOptions`, `FileOptions.SequentialScan`, and stable `missing` / `oversized` / `unreadable` outcomes.
- **`HardwareProfiler` now uses the bounded text helper for the Linux NVIDIA `/proc` probe.** `src/PalLLM.Domain/Inference/HardwareProfiler.cs` no longer buffers `/proc/driver/nvidia/gpus/*/information` through `File.ReadAllText(...)`, preserving the no-subprocess detection path while avoiding an unbounded local file read on that hot posture surface.
- **AMD Instinct hints now surface a real MXFP4 recommendation instead of stale "theory" docs.** `PALLLM_GPU_ARCHITECTURE` now normalizes `mi300` / `gfx942` to `cdna3` and `mi350` / `gfx950` to `cdna4`, `HardwareProfiler` returns `RecommendedQuantization = "mxfp4"` for those lanes, and `src/PalLLM.Sidecar/QuickstartGuideBuilder.cs` adds an AMD-specific MXFP4 validation step to `/api/quickstart`.
- **The quantization docs are now aligned with current upstream reality.** `docs/QUANTIZATION.md`, `docs/MODEL_COLLABORATION.md`, and the architecture/handoff surfaces now reflect current official vLLM and AMD ROCm support for MXFP4 on current Instinct-class ROCm lanes, while still calling out that exact backend/model coverage remains something operators must validate on their chosen serving stack.
- **The test-count drift source is now honest.** `tests/PalLLM.Tests/MetaTests.cs`, `scripts/run_full_audit.ps1`, `docs/PROJECT_NUMBERS.json`, and the audited count-bearing docs now count executable NUnit cases (`[Test]` + declared `[TestCase]`) so the recorded baseline matches the real `dotnet test` total instead of undercounting parameterized cases.
- **Regression coverage:** new AMD MXFP4 and quickstart cases landed in `HardwareProfilerTests` and `QuickstartGuideEndpointTests`, the source-guard meta-test now blocks `HardwareProfiler` from drifting back to `File.ReadAllText(infoPath)`, and the rolling suite baseline is now `588`.
- **Research basis:** this pass followed current Microsoft guidance around bounded sequential file reads with `FileStreamOptions` / `FileOptions.SequentialScan`, plus the current official vLLM and AMD ROCm documentation confirming MXFP4 support on current ROCm-backed Instinct lanes. The sibling scan under `D:\Coding` reinforced the Blackwell/MXFP4 direction but did not surface directly liftable code; other Claude chats in this window remained inaccessible from this environment.
- **Verification:** `dotnet test PalLLM.sln --configuration Release --no-restore --nologo --verbosity minimal` green at `588 / 588`; full audit PASS at [`artifacts/full-audit/20260428-152427/RESULTS.md`](artifacts/full-audit/20260428-152427/RESULTS.md).
- Count deltas: tests `582 -> 588` (+6 executable cases). Routes, features, fallback strategies, and drift-gate count unchanged.

- **The local-artifact reader seam is now shared across runtime and sidecar.** `src/PalLLM.Sidecar/ArtifactJsonFileReader.cs` became a thin wrapper over the shared domain helper, so release-readiness, self-healing, lifetime aggregates, and UI-probe diagnostics all stay on one bounded JSON-ingress contract.
