### Pass 63 - bounded local release/self-healing artifact ingress (2026-04-28)

- **Release-readiness and self-healing artifact reads are now bounded before they enter public surfaces.** The new harvestable `src/PalLLM.Sidecar/ArtifactJsonFileReader.cs` replaces ad-hoc `File.ReadAllText(...)` calls across the release-readiness evidence builders and `SelfHealingStatusReader`, so oversized or malformed local JSON artifacts can no longer force unbounded string allocation on `/api/release/readiness`, `/api/self-healing/status`, or their MCP mirrors.
- **Public summaries stay sanitized even when local evidence is broken.** Oversized, malformed, or unreadable local artifacts now degrade to stable `invalid` / `unreadable` summaries instead of reflecting raw exception text back through operator-facing API or MCP payloads.
- **A new startup-validated operator knob landed under `PalLLM:Http`.** `LocalArtifactMaxBytes` was added to `src/PalLLM.Domain/Configuration/PalLlmOptions.cs`, validated in `src/PalLLM.Sidecar/PalLlmOptionsValidator.cs`, and surfaced in `src/PalLLM.Sidecar/appsettings.json` with the default `65536` byte cap.
- **Regression coverage now pins both behavior and source shape.** `tests/PalLLM.Tests/SidecarEndpointTests.cs` proves oversized release evidence degrades safely, `tests/PalLLM.Tests/McpEndpointTests.cs` proves the MCP self-healing tool does the same, `tests/PalLLM.Tests/BackendValidationTests.cs` fails startup when `LocalArtifactMaxBytes <= 0`, and `tests/PalLLM.Tests/MetaTests.cs` now blocks these readers from drifting back to `File.ReadAllText(...)` or raw `ex.Message` exposure.
- **Docs were resynced to the live bounded-ingress contract.** `docs/API.md`, `docs/TUNING.md`, and `docs/HANDOFF.md` now describe the new local-artifact cap and the sanitized degradation behavior for release-readiness and self-healing evidence.
- **Research basis:** this pass followed current Microsoft guidance to keep hot ASP.NET paths away from large body allocations, to prefer stream-based `System.Text.Json` parsing, and to use `FileStreamOptions` / `FileOptions.SequentialScan` for sequential file reads. The sibling-project scan under `D:\Coding\RimLLM` reinforced the existing PalLLM direction of enforcing source guards on hot-path ingress.
- **Verification:** `dotnet test PalLLM.sln` green at `581 / 581`; full audit PASS at [`artifacts/full-audit/20260428-142458/RESULTS.md`](artifacts/full-audit/20260428-142458/RESULTS.md).
- Count deltas: tests unchanged at `581`. Routes, features, fallback strategies, and drift-gate count unchanged.

### Pass 62 - bounded thermal sensor probe + thermal doc truth (2026-04-28)

- **The thermal gate's `nvidia-smi` probe no longer risks a redirected-process deadlock or unbounded read.** `ThermalGate` now drains both redirected pipes through the new harvestable `src/PalLLM.Domain/Runtime/ProcessTextReadLimiter.cs`, retains only a bounded stdout prefix, and gives the drain phase its own short timeout before falling back to the gate's documented permissive mode.
- **The parser is now explicit and testable.** `ThermalGate.ParseHottestTemperature(...)` isolates the "pick the hottest numeric line" rule, while truncated or malformed `nvidia-smi` output now safely degrades to `unavailable` instead of trusting a partial capture.
- **The source guard now covers the last remaining redirected-process hot path.** `tests/PalLLM.Tests/MetaTests.cs` now fails if `ThermalGate.cs` ever reintroduces `ReadToEnd(...)`, and new `ProcessTextReadLimiterTests` + `ThermalGateTests` pin bounded-prefix retention, drain-only mode, and numeric parsing.
- **Docs were resynced to the real thermal-gate implementation.** `docs/ADVISORS.md`, `docs/GLOSSARY.md`, and `docs/HARVEST.md` no longer claim the gate is Windows/WMI-only; they now describe the real `nvidia-smi` + env-override best-effort contract. Count-bearing docs were also bumped to the live `581`-test baseline.
- **Research basis:** this pass followed current Microsoft guidance on avoiding redirected-process deadlocks by draining output asynchronously rather than calling `ReadToEnd()` after `WaitForExit()`, and it reused the same bounded process-text pattern already proven in the sibling `D:\Coding\RimLLM` runtime.
- **Verification:** `dotnet test PalLLM.sln` green at `581 / 581`; full audit PASS at [`artifacts/full-audit/20260428-131303/RESULTS.md`](artifacts/full-audit/20260428-131303/RESULTS.md).
- Count deltas: tests `577 -> 581` (+4). Routes, features, fallback strategies, and drift-gate count unchanged.

### Pass 61 - bounded upstream MCP discovery metadata (2026-04-28)

- **Upstream MCP discovery snapshots are now bounded before they enter PalLLM state.** `McpUpstreamClientPool` now caps the tool/resource/prompt metadata it accepts from each configured upstream server, preventing a noisy, buggy, or hostile upstream from bloating the cached snapshot or the `/api/mcp/upstream` and `pal_list_upstream_mcp` surfaces with arbitrarily large catalogs.
- **The metadata is sanitized too, not just truncated.** Discovery labels now normalize whitespace, strip control characters, sort deterministically, and truncate long entries to a startup-validated maximum length so the snapshot stays compact, stable, and safe to surface in operator-facing APIs and dashboards.
- **New operator knobs landed under `PalLLM:McpClient`.** `MaxToolsPerServer`, `MaxResourcesPerServer`, `MaxPromptsPerServer`, and `MaxMetadataEntryLength` were added to `PalLlmOptions`, validated by `PalLlmOptionsValidator`, and exposed in the shipped `src/PalLLM.Sidecar/appsettings.json` defaults.
- **Regression coverage:** 2 new tests landed. `McpUpstreamClientTests` now boots a fake MCP upstream with oversized catalogs and proves the cached snapshot is bounded and trimmed, and `BackendValidationTests` now fails startup validation when any of the new MCP metadata caps are non-positive.
- **Docs were resynced to the live contract.** The architecture, API, operations, tuning, handoff, and rolling-count docs now describe the new upstream discovery bounds and the current `577`-test baseline.
- **Research basis:** this pass followed current Microsoft ASP.NET Core performance guidance around keeping hot-path allocations bounded and the official MCP C# transport guidance for current upstream transport patterns. A sibling-project audit under `D:\Coding` did not surface a directly liftable MCP metadata-bounding implementation, so the discovery-shaping logic remains PalLLM-specific and harvestable.
- Count deltas: tests `575 -> 577` (+2). Routes, features, fallback strategies, and drift-gate count unchanged.

### Pass 60 - outbound transport source guards (2026-04-28)

- **The test suite now fails if PalLLM's hot outbound HTTP paths drift back to buffered body reads.** `tests/PalLLM.Tests/MetaTests.cs` now inspects `InferenceClient.cs`, `VisionClient.cs`, `TtsClient.cs`, and `ModelAvailabilityProbe.cs` and requires each hot transport file to keep `HttpCompletionOption.ResponseHeadersRead` while forbidding `ReadAsStringAsync(...)` and `ReadAsByteArrayAsync(...)` on those outbound bodies.
- **The DI registration pattern is now pinned too.** The same meta-test now inspects `src/PalLLM.Sidecar/Program.cs` and requires every named hot client registration (`IModelAvailabilityProbe`, `IInferenceClient`, `IVisionClient`, `ITtsClient`, and the upstream MCP discovery client) to keep the pooled `SocketsHttpHandler` pattern with `PooledConnectionLifetime`, `PooledConnectionIdleTimeout`, and `SetHandlerLifetime(Timeout.InfiniteTimeSpan)`.
- **Research basis:** this pass followed current Microsoft guidance that hot HTTP clients should either be long-lived with `PooledConnectionLifetime` or factory-created with pooled handlers, and that streamed content behavior depends on `HttpCompletionOption.ResponseHeadersRead` plus `ReadAsStream*`-style consumption. The sibling-repo audit under `D:\Coding\RimLLM` also surfaced a useful source-guard pattern that PalLLM now adopts in its own test layer.
- **Test count stays flat on purpose.** The two schema meta-tests were consolidated into one broader schema-contract test, which offset the new outbound-transport meta-test and preserved the rolling `575`-test baseline.
- Count deltas: tests unchanged at `575`. Routes, features, fallback strategies, and drift-gate count unchanged.

### Pass 59 - sanitized public transport failures + promotion-record contract hardening (2026-04-28)

- **Public transport failures no longer echo raw upstream detail.** `HttpJsonInferenceClient`, `HttpVisionClient`, and `HttpTtsClient` now return high-level status strings (`HTTP 502`, timed out, unreachable, malformed JSON, etc.) instead of reflecting raw upstream bodies or exception text back through `ChatResponse.StatusMessage`, `VisionDescribeResponse.StatusMessage`, `VisionWorldStateResponse.StatusMessage`, or `TtsSynthesizeResponse.StatusMessage`.
- **Promotion-record caller errors now stay on a stable `ProblemDetails` contract.** `POST /api/promotion/record` validates `TaskClass`, `PatternId`, and `Outcome` explicitly before writing to the ledger, so missing fields or invalid outcomes return deterministic 400 payloads rather than whatever `ArgumentException.Message` happened to say that day.
- **The outcome-validation seam is now reusable and harvest-friendly.** `PromotionLedger` exposes `AllowedOutcomeValues` and `TryNormalizeOutcome(...)`, which keeps the HTTP contract, the in-memory ledger, and any future MCP/tool surfaces on one shared normalization path.
- **Docs were resynced to the publishable error-handling policy.** `docs/API.md`, `docs/OPERATIONS.md`, and `docs/INVARIANTS.md` now state that public status/error surfaces stay high-level and do not echo raw exception text or upstream response bodies.
- **Research basis:** this pass followed current Microsoft guidance for ASP.NET Core production error handling and Problem Details plus the OWASP Error Handling guidance on avoiding implementation-detail leaks in public responses.
- **Regression coverage:** 3 new tests landed for sanitized inference/vision/TTS transport failures and stable `promotion/record` ProblemDetails.
- Count deltas: tests `572 -> 575` (+3). Routes, features, fallback strategies, and drift-gate count unchanged.

### Pass 58 - MCP origin guard + stable upstream probe failures (2026-04-28)

- **`/mcp` now validates browser `Origin` headers before the MCP transport runs.** Requests without `Origin` still work (the common desktop-host case), loopback browser origins are allowed automatically, and non-loopback browser origins must be explicitly allowlisted via `PalLLM:Auth:McpAllowedOrigins[]`. Disallowed origins now receive a standard `403 ProblemDetails` body instead of reaching the transport layer.
- **Upstream MCP discovery now reuses a pooled HTTP client.** `McpUpstreamClientPool` now probes through a named `HttpClient` with `ConnectionTimeout` set on the Streamable HTTP transport, matching current .NET guidance for long-lived client reuse on periodic background discovery work.
- **Upstream failure snapshots no longer leak raw exception text.** `GET /api/mcp/upstream` and the `pal_list_upstream_mcp` tool now expose a stable `ErrorCode` (`timeout`, `network`, `invalid_endpoint`, `http_401`, `http_403`, `http_4xx`, `http_5xx`, `protocol`, `unexpected`, etc.) plus a sanitized `Error` message so automation can branch reliably without parsing host-specific socket strings.
- **The shipped config surface is now explicit.** `src/PalLLM.Sidecar/appsettings.json` now carries the default `Auth` and `McpClient` blocks, including the empty `McpAllowedOrigins` and `UpstreamServers` arrays, so the operator-visible config file matches the live options surface.
- **Docs were resynced to the live MCP contract.** The README, architecture/API/operations references, tuning/environment docs, MCP quickstart, handoff packet, project numbers, and the count-bearing contributor docs now all reflect the current MCP behavior and the current test baseline.
- **Regression coverage:** 4 new MCP tests landed across `tests/PalLLM.Tests/McpEndpointTests.cs` and `tests/PalLLM.Tests/McpUpstreamClientTests.cs`.
- Count deltas: tests `568 -> 572` (+4). Routes, features, fallback strategies, and drift-gate count unchanged.

### Pass 57 - bounded pack-validation ingress + sanitized SSE failures (2026-04-28)

- **`/api/packs/validate` now enforces its byte cap even when `Content-Length` is absent.** The endpoint now sets `IHttpMaxRequestBodySizeFeature` when available and reads the body through the new `HttpRequestBodyReadLimiter`, so chunked or otherwise unknown-length uploads cannot stream past the documented `1,000,000` byte cap before validation starts.
- **Request-body reading follows current ASP.NET Core guidance more closely.** The new limiter uses `HttpRequest.BodyReader` instead of `StreamReader.ReadToEndAsync`, matching Microsoft's current pipelines-first guidance for request-body work and the minimal-API sample pattern of explicitly detecting oversized chunked bodies.
- **`/api/chat/stream` no longer leaks exception text in SSE error events.** Internal failures are now logged server-side and surfaced to the client as a generic `{ request_id, message, retryable, reason }` payload, removing the previous `detail = ex.Message` wire leak from the `/api/*` surface.
- **Docs were resynced to the live behavior.** `docs/DATAFLOW.md` now matches the real SSE sequence (`started` / `phase` / `token` / `presentation` / optional `speech` / optional `action` / `final`, with `error` on failure), and the API/pack-authoring docs now call out the pack-validation `413` contract and `1,000,000` byte limit.
- **Regression coverage:** 2 new endpoint tests landed for unknown-length pack validation bodies and sanitized streaming-error payloads.
- Count deltas: tests `566 -> 568` (+2). Routes, features, fallback strategies, and drift-gate count unchanged.

### Pass 56 - bounded model discovery + config/doc drift cleanup (2026-04-28)

- **Model-catalog probing is now bounded and header-first.** `HttpModelAvailabilityProbe` now issues discovery calls with `ResponseHeadersRead`, applies an explicit linked body-phase timeout, and parses `/v1/models` or `/api/tags` through `HttpContentReadLimiter` so a misbehaving local endpoint cannot stream an arbitrarily large catalog into the sidecar.
- **New operator knob:** `PalLLM:Inference:ModelCatalogMaxResponseBytes` was added to `PalLlmOptions` and to the shipped `src/PalLLM.Sidecar/appsettings.json` defaults. It ships at `262144` bytes (256 KB) so model discovery stays roomier than chat replies while remaining bounded.
- **TTS failure-path reads are now bounded too.** `TtsClient` no longer calls `ReadAsStringAsync` on oversized error responses; it reads a capped diagnostic preview through the same limiter, preserving operator visibility without letting error bodies grow without bound.
- **Handler pooling was normalized on the remaining probe client.** The model-availability probe now uses the same `SocketsHttpHandler` pooling pattern as the other hot HTTP clients: pooled connections with `SetHandlerLifetime(Timeout.InfiniteTimeSpan)` and bounded connection lifetimes.
- **Docs and config references were resynced.** The rolling test baseline is now `566`, `docs/ENV_VARS.md` matches the live `Automation.Enabled`, `Automation.EmitToOutbox`, `Vision.EnableScreenshotWatcher`, and `McpClient.UpstreamServers[].{Id,Url}` shapes, and the tuning docs now cover `Inference.ModelCatalogMaxResponseBytes`.
- **Regression coverage:** 3 new tests landed across `ModelTierTests` and `InferenceClientTests` for declared oversized model catalogs, streaming oversized model catalogs, and oversized TTS error bodies.
- Count deltas: tests `563 -> 566` (+3). Routes, features, fallback strategies, and drift-gate count unchanged.

### Pass 55 - bounded inference/vision transports (2026-04-28)

- **Inference + vision hot paths are now bounded after headers.** `HttpJsonInferenceClient` and `HttpVisionClient` still use `HttpCompletionOption.ResponseHeadersRead`, but now pair it with explicit linked body-phase timeouts, header-first size checks, bounded error-body reads, and a shared `HttpContentReadLimiter` so successful chat-completions JSON payloads cannot grow without bound after headers are received.
- **New operator knobs:** `PalLLM:Inference:MaxResponseBytes` and `PalLLM:Vision:MaxResponseBytes` were added to `PalLlmOptions` and to the shipped `src/PalLLM.Sidecar/appsettings.json` defaults. Both ship at `65536` bytes (64 KB).
- **Shared response parser hardening.** `ChatCompletionsResponseReader` now parses through the bounded stream helper instead of reading an unbounded body to EOF. The sibling-project audit that informed this pass surfaced the same bounded-body pattern in `D:\Coding\RimLLM\Source\RimLLM\Core\BackendHttp.cs`; PalLLM now carries its own harvestable variant in `src/PalLLM.Domain/Inference/HttpContentReadLimiter.cs`.
- **Timeout semantics are now consistent.** `Program.cs` and `HttpTtsClient` were normalized to honor any positive configured timeout so the validator, docs, and runtime behaviour all agree on what `TimeoutSeconds` means.
- **Regression coverage:** 4 new tests in `tests/PalLLM.Tests/InferenceClientTests.cs` pin oversized declared inference bodies, oversized streaming inference bodies, oversized inference error bodies, and oversized declared vision bodies.
- **Docs resynced:** `README.md`, `docs/ROADMAP.md`, `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `docs/API.md`, `docs/OPERATIONS.md`, `docs/TUNING.md`, `docs/ENV_VARS.md`, `docs/CODE_MAP.md`, `docs/HANDOFF.md`, and `docs/PROJECT_NUMBERS.json`.
- Count deltas: tests `559 -> 563` (+4). Routes, features, fallback strategies, and drift-gate count unchanged.

### Pass 54 — NVFP4 / Blackwell awareness (2026-04-25)

The chat-path code is unchanged — PalLLM is an HTTP client and
quantization is the inference server's concern. What changed is
that PalLLM now **detects Blackwell hardware** and **recommends
the right quantization format per architecture**, surfacing the
recommendation in `/api/hardware`, `/api/quickstart`, the
dashboard, and `pal context`.

- **`HardwareProfiler` extended.** New fields on the
  `HardwareProfile` record:
  - `GpuArchitecture` — coarse classifier
    (`blackwell` / `hopper` / `ada` / `ampere` /
    `turing-or-older` / `none` / `unknown`).
  - `GpuArchitectureDetail` — provenance of the decision (env
    var name, `/proc` model line, or
    `architecture-not-determinable-without-hint`).
  - `Fp4TensorCoresLikely` — true only for Blackwell and
    successors.
  - `RecommendedQuantization` — `nvfp4` / `fp8` / `q4_k_m`
    based on the architecture matrix in
    `docs/QUANTIZATION.md`.
- **Detection sources** (no subprocess): operator
  `PALLLM_GPU_ARCHITECTURE` env-var hint;
  `/proc/driver/nvidia/gpus/<id>/information` on Linux when
  available; `unknown` (lowest-risk default) otherwise. Stays
  hot-path-safe and embeddable.
- **Recommendation engine** maps architecture → format:
  Blackwell → NVFP4 (2× FP8 throughput, ~37 GB for 70B);
  Hopper / Ada → FP8; Ampere and earlier → Q4_K_M;
  no GPU → Q4_K_M.
- **`HardwareProfiler.BuildRecommendation`** now appends an
  NVFP4 nudge to the human-readable line when Blackwell is
  detected.
- **`QuickstartGuideBuilder`** gains a new optional step that
  fires when `Fp4TensorCoresLikely=true` — points the operator
  at vLLM + an NVFP4 model with a verify command and a link to
  `docs/QUANTIZATION.md`.
- **`docs/QUANTIZATION.md`** — comprehensive operator-facing
  primer covering NVFP4, MXFP4, FP8 (E4M3), Q4_K_M, Q8_0,
  AWQ-INT4, GPTQ-INT4. Includes per-architecture
  recommendation matrix, community sentiment summary
  (r/LocalLLaMA + HuggingFace forum themes through late
  2025), agentic / tool-use considerations,
  cross-app guidance, and 2027 / 2035 outlook (FP6, MXFP6,
  trained-scale FP4, future 3-bit formats).
- **`docs/MODEL_COLLABORATION.md`** updated with the
  per-architecture quantization defaults table and a
  recommended Blackwell stack (vLLM 0.6+ with NVFP4 model).
- **`scripts/compatibility.json`** schema extended:
  `inferenceEndpoints` now includes `tensorrt-llm` and
  `sglang` rows; new `quantizationFormats` block listing
  NVFP4, MXFP4, FP8, Q4_K_M, Q8_0, AWQ-INT4 with their
  hardware requirements + supported engines.
- **5 new tests** in `HardwareProfilerTests.cs` covering the
  Blackwell hint, Hopper hint, Ampere hint, no-GPU branch,
  and the always-populated architecture fields. The existing
  `GracefulDegradationAdvisorTests` was updated to construct
  `HardwareProfile` with the new fields.
- Test count: `554 → 559` (+5). Drift gates `14 / 14`
  unchanged. Build warnings `0`. Routes / features / MCP
  tools unchanged.
- Docs (root + docs/): `48 → 49` (+1: `QUANTIZATION.md`).

**Honest scope note.** PalLLM does not quantize models or run
inference. The actual ~2× speedup and ~50% VRAM savings come
from the operator switching their inference server to vLLM /
TensorRT-LLM with an NVFP4-quantized model. PalLLM works with
that immediately, no PalLLM code change required — the
detection + recommendation + docs are what we added.

### Pass 53 — Meta-tests + scaffolder + first-hour tour (2026-04-25)

Closes the "verify documentation health from inside the test
suite" + "scaffold a new extension in 30 seconds" + "guided
on-ramp for a brand-new contributor" gaps. Pure documentation +
tooling additions; zero runtime code changes.

- **`tests/PalLLM.Tests/MetaTests.cs`** — 10 NUnit meta-tests
  asserting documentation + agent-readiness invariants:
  every agent doorway file (AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md /
  llms.txt / .cursorrules / .github/copilot-instructions.md)
  exists; PROJECT_NUMBERS.json is parseable + has every
  required key; the recorded test count agrees with the live
  `[Test]` count; every ADR has Status metadata; every
  schema is valid JSON + declares `$schema` + `$id` +
  title; every long-form doc has a Last audited stamp;
  ADR numbering is sequential with no gaps; AGENTS.md
  references resolve to existing files; Directory.Build.props
  exists and suppresses CS1591. The test layer adds
  belt-and-suspenders to the drift gates.
- **`scripts/scaffold.ps1`** + **`pal.ps1 scaffold`** verb +
  **`make scaffold`** target — interactive scaffolder for the
  six most-common change types (advisor / builder / validator
  / feeder / fallback / mcp-tool). Generates placeholder
  files with TODO markers naming the exact follow-up steps;
  refuses to overwrite existing files; never edits existing
  files (so multiple invocations stay safe). Prints a
  next-step checklist tailored to the kind.
- **FIRST_HOUR (retired Pass 376; see `docs/QUICKSTART.md`)** — guided 60-minute tour from
  fresh clone to first shipped change. Five 10-15 minute
  slices: one-command setup → rolling baseline → mental
  model → poke at the running runtime → poke at the code →
  ship a tiny change. Designed for a brand-new contributor
  who's already past PITCH and wants the *feel* of the
  codebase.
- **`docs/UX_PRINCIPLES.md`** — the seven principles shaping
  every operator + contributor surface (defaults / one-command
  ops / honest diagnostics / inspectable shapes / audit-as-
  safety-net / layered docs / automatic provenance) plus
  four explicit anti-principles. The tie-breaker doc when a
  surface choice is ambiguous.
- Test count: `544 → 554` (+10 meta-tests). Every count
  reference in README, ROADMAP, ARCHITECTURE, CODE_MAP,
  HANDOFF, CONTRIBUTING, CLAUDE, AGENTS, CHEAT_SHEET, FAQ,
  TESTING, REVIEW_CHECKLIST, INVARIANTS, COOKBOOK, JOURNEY
  scorecard, PROJECT_NUMBERS.json, pal.ps1, onboard.ps1,
  ADR-0004 bumped in lockstep.
- Drift gates: `14 / 14` unchanged. Build warnings: `0`
  unchanged. Routes / features / MCP tools unchanged.
- Docs (root + docs/): `46 → 48` (`+2` new docs:
  FIRST_HOUR, UX_PRINCIPLES; plus the new
  `tests/PalLLM.Tests/MetaTests.cs` and
  `scripts/scaffold.ps1` which aren't doc-stamp-bearing).

### Pass 52 — Mental models + invariants + agent context JSON (2026-04-25)

Closes the "I want to *think* about this codebase" gap. Pure
documentation + tooling additions; zero runtime code changes.

- **`docs/MENTAL_MODEL.md`** — ten short paragraphs covering the
  core concepts (companion-not-chatbot / bridge-is-a-mailbox /
  runtime-is-core-sidecar-is-host / inference-is-enrichment /
  posture-surfaces / declarative-role-mesh / observe-suggest-stage
  / opt-in-everything / drift-gates-as-type-system / shaped-to-
  pick-up-cold), each with an analogy that makes it click. Plus a
  Mermaid graph showing how the concepts connect. Designed to be
  the "build the right mental model in 5 minutes" door for new
  contributors and agents.
- **`docs/INVARIANTS.md`** — 26 numbered invariants the runtime
  upholds, each naming where it's enforced (code path or drift
  gate). Positive complement to `ANTI_PATTERNS.md`. Covers reply
  pipeline, bridge, configuration, persistence, observability,
  documentation, build, and code shape. If a reviewer sees one
  violated, treat it as a bug.
- **`docs/REVIEW_CHECKLIST.md`** — what a reviewer (human or
  agent) checks for in a PR. Quick green-light path (4 items) +
  conceptual review (invariants + anti-patterns + patterns) +
  per-area dives (documentation, tests, security, performance,
  bridge contract). Designed to be skimmed in 60 seconds.
- **`scripts/agent-context.ps1`** — single-shot JSON document
  for AI agents picking up a session. Includes the rolling
  counts (from `PROJECT_NUMBERS.json`), the latest audit
  result, the ADR inventory with status, the schema inventory
  with title and `$id`, the doc-freshness map (every doc with
  a `Last audited:` stamp + age in days + stale flag), the
  recommended reading order, and the quick-command map. Cheap
  to compute, side-effect-free.
- **`pal.ps1 context`** verb (and `make context`) — invokes
  `agent-context.ps1`. The recommended first thing for an
  agent to run after `pal status`.
- **README.md "AI agent reading this in 2026+?" preamble** —
  inline pointer right at the top of the README directing
  agents to AGENTS.md / MENTAL_MODEL.md / HANDOFF.md and the
  `pal context` / `pal status` / `pal onboard` shortcuts.
  Pairs with the existing `llms.txt`, `AGENTS.md`,
  `CLAUDE.md`, `.cursorrules`, and
  `.github/copilot-instructions.md` files — different agents
  read different doors, all roads lead to AGENTS.md.
- **Mermaid `erDiagram`** added to `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` —
  visual data-model showing PalLlmRuntime + ConversationMemoryStore +
  RelationshipTracker + FallbackBehaviorEngine +
  PresentationCuePlanner + ChatDispatchPlanner + opt-in HTTP
  clients + PromotionLedger + IGameAdapter and how the entities
  relate. Pairs with the existing system-shell diagram + the
  DATAFLOW sequence diagrams + the STATE_MACHINES state diagrams
  for a complete visual cross-section.
- `docs/INDEX.md` updated: 4 new "Start here" rows
  (MENTAL_MODEL / INVARIANTS / REVIEW_CHECKLIST /
  `pal.ps1 context`); 3 new Explanation-quadrant entries.
- Count deltas: tests `544 → 544`, drift gates `14 / 14`,
  build warnings `0`. Docs (root + docs/): `43 → 46`
  (`+3` new docs).

### Pass 51 — Reference indexes + IDE one-click + machine-readable state (2026-04-25)

Closes the "I want to look something up" / "I want to start coding
in 30 seconds" / "I want to consume the rolling state from a
script" gaps. Pure documentation + tooling additions; zero runtime
code changes.

- **`docs/QUICKREF.md`** — sortable / grep-able reference for
  every surface: pal.ps1 verbs, drift gates, hot-path budgets,
  OpenTelemetry spans, ResponsePath values, bridge directories,
  runtime root layout, health endpoints, configuration root keys,
  environment variables, doc map. Companion to the narrative
  CHEAT_SHEET.
- **`docs/EVENTS.md`** — every event PalLLM emits or consumes:
  bridge inbox event types (chat_player_message,
  world_snapshot, event_combat, event_milestone,
  event_screenshot_ready, event_widget_probe), bridge outbox
  events (chat_reply), OpenTelemetry spans + tags grouped by
  subsystem (Chat / Bridge / Memory / Vision / TTS / Posture),
  Prometheus metrics (10 counters, 5 gauges, 4 histograms),
  cross-event correlation walk-through.
- **`docs/ENV_VARS.md`** — every environment variable that
  affects the runtime: ASP.NET Core, OpenTelemetry, PalLLM-
  specific instrumentation toggles, and the
  `PalLLM__<Section>__<Field>` pattern for overriding any
  `PallmOptions` field. Includes array-field syntax and config
  precedence order.
- **`docs/PROJECT_NUMBERS.json`** — machine-readable rolling
  state. Single JSON document with tests / drift gates / build
  warnings / routes / features / MCP tools / fallback
  strategies / ADRs / honest roadmap. Has a
  `linksToSourceOfTruth` block naming the underlying code file
  for each number. Drift gates verify that docs match this
  file's counts; consumers (CI scripts, dashboards, the new
  `pal.ps1 status` verb) read it directly.
- **Per-folder `README.md`** files for `src/`, `tests/`,
  `scripts/`, `mod/`, `docs/`. Each one is a 30-second
  orientation to that directory: what's inside, where to look
  next, what the conventions are.
- **`.vscode/launch.json`** — F5-runnable sidecar with three
  profiles: Release, Debug, and "Sidecar with OTLP traces"
  (env-vars preset for a local Jaeger). `preLaunchTask` wires
  to the build target so debug-from-cold-clone works in one
  press.
- **`.vscode/tasks.json`** — every `pal.ps1` verb wired as a VS
  Code task. `Ctrl+Shift+B` runs build, `Ctrl+Shift+P → Run
  Test Task` runs the suite, `audit (full)` / `audit (fast)` /
  `play (boot sidecar + open dashboard)` / `onboard` /
  `regenerate OpenAPI snapshot` / `doctor` / `status` are all
  one-click from the command palette.
- **`.vscode/settings.json`** — workspace-level settings:
  EOL conventions matching `.editorconfig`, file-explorer
  exclusions for generated bin/obj/TestResults/CoverageReport,
  search exclusions for the same plus the OpenAPI snapshot.
- **`.vscode/extensions.json`** — recommended extensions
  (C# Dev Kit, PowerShell, EditorConfig, Markdown Mermaid,
  Markdown All-in-One). Opening the repo prompts the user to
  install if they're missing.
- **`Makefile`** — thin cross-platform wrapper around
  `pal.ps1`. `make build / test / audit / play / onboard /
  status / ...` for contributors with `make` muscle memory.
  Every target delegates to `pwsh ./pal.ps1 <verb>` so the
  source of truth stays single.
- **`pal.ps1 status`** — new verb. Reads
  `docs/PROJECT_NUMBERS.json` and the latest audit report and
  prints a one-screen summary: every count, every label, the
  latest audit overall pass/fail status. Designed to be the
  fast (<1 s) "is everything OK?" check between sessions.
- **`docs/INDEX.md`** updated: 8 new "Start here" rows
  (QUICKREF / EVENTS / ENV_VARS / PROJECT_NUMBERS / `pal.ps1
  status` / `.vscode/launch.json` / `pwsh ../pal.ps1 list` /
  `make` parity); new Reference-quadrant entries for the four
  new docs.
- Count deltas: tests `544 → 544`, drift gates `14 / 14`,
  build warnings `0 → 0`. Docs (root + docs/): `39 → 43`
  (`+4` new docs: QUICKREF, EVENTS, ENV_VARS,
  PROJECT_NUMBERS.json; plus 5 per-folder READMEs and 4
  `.vscode/` config files and a Makefile that aren't doc-stamp-bearing).

### Pass 50 — Recipes + extension map + verb-driven task runner (2026-04-25)

Adds the next layer of "easy to continue coding" infrastructure:
recipes for common changes, an explicit extension-point map,
visual data-flow + state-machine diagrams, a testing cookbook, a
one-page cheat sheet, a verb-driven task runner, and tool-specific
agent pointer files. Pure documentation + tooling additions; zero
runtime code changes.

- **`pal.ps1`** at the repo root — verb-driven task runner.
  `pwsh ./pal.ps1 build / test / audit / fast-audit / run / play
  / onboard / doctor / smoke / openapi / package / recover / list
  / help`. Wraps every common operation behind one memorable
  command. Inspired by `make`, `just`, and `npm run`. Forwards
  remaining arguments to the underlying script for power-user
  flag passthrough.
- **`docs/COOKBOOK.md`** — step-by-step recipes for the nine
  most-common changes: new HTTP endpoint, new fallback strategy,
  new MCP tool, new config flag, new advisor / builder, new
  bridge event type, new guarded action type, new feature-catalog
  entry, new ADR. Each recipe names the exact files, the drift
  gates that fire if you skip a step, and the verify command.
- **`docs/EXTENSION_POINTS.md`** — "where do I add code for X?"
  map. One-line-per-surface-area table covering 30+ extension
  points with the file path and a named existing example to
  copy. Pairs with the cookbook: cookbook walks through every
  step, extension-points gives the file path at a glance.
- **`docs/DATAFLOW.md`** — Mermaid sequence diagrams for the
  major flows: player chat → reply (with all the inference /
  fallback / breaker / rate-limit branches), bridge inbox drain,
  memory persistence (with the mutation-version skip-write
  optimization shown explicitly), vision describe, promotion
  ledger lifecycle, chat streaming SSE channels.
- **`docs/STATE_MACHINES.md`** — Mermaid stateDiagrams for the
  explicit subsystems: inference circuit breaker (Closed →
  Open → HalfOpen → Closed), bridge inbox worker (Polling /
  Draining / Stopped), promotion ledger (Empty → Observed →
  Suggested → Staged), TTL cache (Cold / Warm with signature
  invalidation), and the chat reply path decision tree showing
  every `ResponsePath` outcome.
- **`docs/TESTING.md`** — "how to write a test for X" cookbook.
  Pure-logic class, TTL-cached surface, HTTP endpoint, MCP tool,
  streaming endpoint, bridge event handler, fallback strategy.
  Each pattern shows the canonical fixture wiring + a real
  reference test from the existing 544.
- **`docs/CHEAT_SHEET.md`** — one-page summary of everything.
  Every `pal.ps1` verb, every key file, every drift gate, the
  hard rules, the directory layout, the "I want to add X" quick
  map, the health endpoints, and the runbook entry points.
  Designed to be the single page a returning contributor reads
  first.
- **`.github/copilot-instructions.md`** — GitHub Copilot's
  per-repo convention. Pointer to `AGENTS.md` plus a one-screen
  TL;DR (test count, drift gates, hot files, run loop).
- **`.cursorrules`** — Cursor's per-repo convention. Same shape
  as the Copilot file. Both pointer files keep `AGENTS.md` as
  the universal source of truth — tool-specific files just
  wrap it.
- **`docs/INDEX.md`** updated with eight new "Start here" rows
  + new Reference-quadrant entries for COOKBOOK / EXTENSION_POINTS
  / DATAFLOW / STATE_MACHINES / TESTING / CHEAT_SHEET.
- Count deltas: tests `544 → 544` unchanged, drift gates
  `14 / 14` unchanged, build warnings `0 → 0` unchanged.
  Docs (root + docs/): `33 → 39` (`+6` new docs:
  COOKBOOK, EXTENSION_POINTS, DATAFLOW, STATE_MACHINES,
  TESTING, CHEAT_SHEET; plus `pal.ps1` at root, plus the
  two tool-specific pointer files).

### Pass 49 — Handoff-ready foundations: ADRs + RUNBOOK + schemas + onboarding (2026-04-25)

Adds the load-bearing reference docs that a coding agent (or human
contributor) needs to pick up the repo cold and continue work
without rediscovering decisions or guessing at wire-level shapes.
No code behaviour changes; every feature preserved.

- **`docs/adr/`** — Architecture Decision Records. Six accepted ADRs
  covering the foundational choices (deterministic-first replies,
  the portable adapter seam, the one-way advisory bridge, drift
  gates over manual review, the TTL-cache pattern, opt-in
  defaults). Each ADR uses the Michael-Nygard 2011 template plus
  a "Harvest hint" section unique to PalLLM that names the file
  a downstream harvester would copy. Cross-linked via `Depends
  on:` / `Supports:` headers so the dependency graph between
  decisions is explicit.
- **`docs/RUNBOOK.md`** — incident response playbook. Triage in
  five minutes, then per-symptom sections for "sidecar won't
  start", "chat returns deterministic replies even though I have
  inference enabled", "outbox isn't emptying", "drift audit
  failing in CI", "memory store is corrupt", "GPU memory
  pressure", "disk filling up", and the last-resort recovery
  path. Each section names the specific diagnostic command and
  the smallest fix.
- **`docs/ANTI_PATTERNS.md`** — what's been deliberately
  rejected and why. Saves a new agent from re-proposing
  considered-and-rejected ideas. Six categories (runtime / chat
  path, bridge / game integration, configuration / defaults,
  documentation discipline, code style / refactoring,
  performance, promotion / automation, testing) plus a "things
  that look like anti-patterns but aren't" section explaining
  the deliberate-but-surprising choices (3863-line
  `PalLlmRuntime.cs`, multiple `/api/describe` + `/api/features`
  + `/api/health` surfaces).
- **`docs/OBSERVABILITY.md`** — single-stop OpenTelemetry
  primer. 30-second TL;DR for wiring up Jaeger; full span
  inventory (`Chat.Turn`, `Chat.Plan`, `Chat.Inference`,
  `Bridge.Drain`, `Memory.Recall`, `Posture.Capture` and their
  tags); how to disable specific instrumentation when
  benchmarking PalLLM-only spans; cloud-collector pairings
  verified vendor-agnostic.
- **`docs/HOT_PATH.md`** — explicit per-method performance
  budgets. Cold and warm targets per hardware tier
  (Constrained / Standard / Generous) for the chat path
  (`ChatAsync`, `ChatDispatchPlanner.Plan`, fallback director,
  presentation cue planner), world snapshot, health / posture
  builders, bridge worker, and memory store. Names the
  TTL-caches and the hard upper bounds set by the network
  timeout configuration. Verifiable via the OpenTelemetry
  spans documented in `OBSERVABILITY.md`.
- **`docs/schemas/`** — JSON Schema 2020-12 contracts for the
  off-HTTP wire shapes. Three schemas:
  `outbox-envelope.schema.json` (sidecar-to-Lua reply),
  `bridge-event-envelope.schema.json` (Lua-to-sidecar event),
  `personality-pack.schema.json` (`pack.json` manifest with
  required fields and content-hash invariant). Each schema
  references its source-of-truth code file. `Test-Json` and
  `ajv` workflows shown in the index README.
- **Mermaid system diagram** added to `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` —
  shows the four shells (Game / Bridge filesystem / Sidecar /
  Domain) plus the opt-in dependencies, with solid lines for
  always-on local data flows and dashed lines for opt-in
  network calls. Each ADR cross-linked from the diagram.
- **`scripts/onboard.ps1`** — one-command first-time
  contributor onboarding. Verifies the .NET 10 SDK, builds in
  Release, runs the 544-test suite, runs the fast drift audit,
  boots the sidecar in a new window, and opens the Field
  Console dashboard. `-SkipDashboard` and `-SkipAudit` flags
  for CI / iteration variants. Stops at the first failure with
  a focused diagnostic.
- **`SECURITY.txt`** at repo root — RFC 9116 machine-readable
  security-discovery file. Pointer to `SECURITY.md` for the
  full policy. Maintainer-fork instructions for filling in the
  GitHub-coordinates placeholders before pushing publicly.
- **`docs/INDEX.md`** updated with five new "Start here" rows
  + new "Performance investigator" / "First-time builder"
  audience shortcuts. ADR / ANTI_PATTERNS / OBSERVABILITY /
  HOT_PATH / RUNBOOK / schemas all linked from the
  Diataxis-quadrant sections too.
- **JOURNEY.md** scorecard column bumped to "After
  Pass 49"; Pass 49 added to the Phase 3 pass list.
- Count deltas: tests `544 → 544` unchanged, drift gates
  `14 / 14` unchanged, build warnings `0 → 0` unchanged,
  routes / features / MCP tools all unchanged. Pure
  documentation + tooling additions; zero runtime code
  changes. Docs (root + docs/): `25 → 33` (`+8` —

### Pass 48 — Zero-warning build + centralized MSBuild props (2026-04-25)

- Added a root-level `Directory.Build.props` that every project inherits.
  First use is a repo-wide `NoWarn=$(NoWarn);CS1591` with an inline
  rationale — XML docs are still **generated** for IntelliSense and
  harvesters (via each csproj's `GenerateDocumentationFile=true`), but
  the build is no longer drowned in `3_840` missing-doc warnings from
  positional-parameter records whose fields are self-documenting.
- Converted every record type that used inline positional-parameter
  `/// <summary>` comments to the canonical C# pattern of class-level
  `<param>` tags. Nine files touched (`ChatDispatchPlanner`,
  `HardwareProfiler`, `PartyChatContracts`, `DirectiveIntentTranslator`,
  `GracefulDegradationAdvisor`, `MoodWeatherAdvisor`,
  `PrivacyPostureBuilder`, `PromotionApplier`, `WorldNarrationAdvisor`);
  150 CS1587 "XML on invalid element" warnings eliminated.
- Fixed 5 unresolved-cref warnings (CS1574) by either fully qualifying
  the target or swapping `<see cref="X"/>` for `<c>X</c>` where the
  symbol lived in a cross-project or BCL namespace. Also moved an
  orphan `SelfHealingOptions` `<summary>` block onto its actual class
  declaration.
- Fixed the `HardwareProfiler.Capture` XML that referenced a
  non-existent `cacheTtl` parameter (CS1734) and a stale
  `ActivitySource.StartActivity` cref (CS0419).
- Fixed the real CS8604 possible-null warning in
  `PromotionApplyPreviewBuilder.Build` — coalesces `suggestion.TaskClass`
  to `string.Empty` instead of passing `null` to a non-nullable record
  parameter.
- Silenced 5 CS8625 null-literal warnings in
  `ProofPacketBuilderTests.cs` by adding the `null!` suppression that
  the other deliberate-null lines in the same file already use.
- Net result: `dotnet build` now reports **zero warnings**, closing
  the "cleanup debt" explicitly called out in `docs/HANDOFF.md`.
- Count deltas: tests `544 → 544` unchanged, drift gates `14 / 14`
  unchanged, routes / features / MCP tools all unchanged, build
  warnings **`3_840 → 0`**.

### Self-description cache tuning + auth ProblemDetails contract (2026-04-24)

- Added `PalLLM:Http:SelfDescriptionCacheSeconds` (default `15`) so
  `GET /api/describe` now uses its own short cache window instead of
  inheriting the much longer feature-catalog TTL. The route now
  participates in both private-cache `ETag` revalidation and
  server-side output caching, which reduces repeated AI/MCP handshake
  cost without holding stale runtime posture for long.
- Fixed the `ShouldDisableCaching` allowlist drift so `/api/describe`
  is treated consistently with the other deliberate conditional-cache
  inspection surfaces. The API, architecture, operations, and tuning
  docs now reflect that contract explicitly.
- Protected `/api/*` and `/mcp` auth failures now return standard
  `ProblemDetails` bodies while preserving `WWW-Authenticate: Bearer`,
  bringing the auth path into line with the repo's "no ad-hoc error
  bodies on `/api/*`" invariant. Added regression coverage for the
  `401` response shape plus the new self-description cache option.
- Count deltas: tests `542 → 544` (+2), drift gates `14 / 14`
  unchanged, routes / features / MCP tools all unchanged.

### TTS transport hardening + documentation truth pass (2026-04-24)

- `HttpTtsClient` now issues TTS requests with
  `HttpCompletionOption.ResponseHeadersRead`, enforces a linked
  end-to-end timeout budget across both headers and body reads, and
  streams the response through a bounded reader instead of relying on
  the default fully-buffered success path.
- Added `PalLLM:Tts:MaxResponseBytes` (default `16777216`, 16 MB) to
  cap successful audio payloads before the sidecar materializes or
  writes them. Two regression tests pin both the declared
  `Content-Length` short-circuit path and the streaming no-length path.
- Refreshed the sidecar's opt-in OpenTelemetry package set to the
  current April 2026 patch line (`OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting`
  `1.15.3`, OTLP exporter `1.15.3`, HTTP instrumentation `1.15.1`) so
  `dotnet list package --vulnerable` no longer reports known
  advisories for the production build.
- Reconciled the stale runtime-path and config-key copy that had drifted
  across docs and public-facing strings:
  `runtime-root/session.json` replaced the old `Runtime/SessionState/`
  wording; `runtime-root/Packs/` replaced `PalLLM:PackRoot`;
  `ASPNETCORE_URLS / --urls` replaced the nonexistent
  `PalLLM:Http:BindAddress`; and
  `PalLLM:McpClient:UpstreamServers[]` replaced the older MCP key shape.
- Extended `scripts/run_full_audit.ps1` so `docs/CODE_MAP.md` and
  `docs/HANDOFF.md` are now checked alongside `README.md`,
  `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `docs/API.md`, and `docs/ROADMAP.md` for
  route, feature, feature-split, and test-count drift.
- Count deltas: tests `540 → 542` (+2 — both TTS transport paths),
  drift gates `14 / 14` unchanged, routes / features / MCP tools all
  unchanged.

### Handoff-readiness + end-user polish (2026-04-24)

Multi-round refactor arc (Passes 43-47). Zero features removed; every
existing endpoint, MCP tool, feature-catalog entry, advisor, and
builder preserved. The arc focused on three axes the stated goals
named: *understandable* (new layperson + contributor docs),
*harvestable* (symbol-to-file navigation + single-file capability
recipes), and *lower-latency* (TTL-cache pattern applied uniformly to
every read-heavy deterministic posture surface).

**New root-level files**

- **`AGENTS.md`** — root-level agent briefing. Essential reading
  order, non-negotiable invariants, working loop, anti-patterns,
  handoff protocol. Matches the emerging 2024-25
  coding-agent-onboarding convention.
- **`CLAUDE.md`** — Claude-Code-specific shortcut. Project facts,
  working loop, drift-gate list, anti-patterns.
- **`llms.txt`** — machine-readable AI-discoverability index per
  Jeremy Howard's (answer.ai) emerging standard. "For humans / For
  coding agents / Reference / Machine-readable contracts" sections.

**New docs under `docs/`**

- **`PITCH.md`** — plain-English tour for laypeople. Problem +
  local-by-default + zero-inference fallback + hardware scaling +
  MCP + privacy + 5-step install.
- **`FAQ.md`** — Q&A for first-timers: cost, GPU, privacy, safety,
  uninstall, what-happens-if-internet-drops, what-if-it-crashes.
- **`GLOSSARY.md`** — 40+ PalLLM-specific terms defined in plain
  English (advisor, director, duo, role, posture, proof packet,
  etc.) with cross-links back to the conceptual docs.
- **`CODE_MAP.md`** — symbol-to-file navigation: project layout,
  the three hot files, where every capability lives, by concept
  (chat pipeline, fallback, memory, inference, role mesh, promotion
  loop, posture advisors, HTTP/MCP machinery, release).
- **`ADVISORS.md`** — one-page consolidated catalog of every advisor
  / builder / validator / feeder / tracker / store. Each row:
  file path, kind (Pure / Stateful / Cached), public surface, where
  it's surfaced (HTTP + MCP + dashboard).
- **`HARVEST.md`** — recipe-style guide to lifting individual
  capabilities. Four concrete recipes (Duo planner only, proof
  packets only, full promotion loop, privacy posture) plus the
  exhaustive harvestable-capability table.
- **`CONVENTIONS.md`** — the 4 patterns (advisor / builder /
  validator / feeder), the 3 hard rules (deterministic-first,
  observer-only, every automated change gets a proof packet),
  type-naming cheatsheet, error handling, async, JSON, testing,
  feature catalog, HTTP, MCP, dashboard, scripts.
- **`DESIGN_PRINCIPLES.md`** — the 10 numbered principles holding
  the codebase together, with industry-practice context (Simple Made
  Easy, 12-Factor, Diátaxis, Hexagonal, llms.txt, AGENTS.md).

**Cross-linking**

- `docs/INDEX.md` — added all new docs to both the "Start here"
  quickref and Diátaxis-quadrant lists; added Curious-layperson /
  Coding-agent / Harvester audience shortcuts.
- `docs/HANDOFF.md` — explicit reading-order callout pointing to
  AGENTS.md / CODE_MAP.md / CONVENTIONS.md / HARVEST.md.
- `README.md` — layman-first opener (5 bullets before the technical
  stats block); explicit pointer to `docs/PITCH.md` for newcomers.
- `CONTRIBUTING.md` — agent-guidance block at the top pointing at
  AGENTS.md + CLAUDE.md.

**Code refactor — TTL-cache pattern across four read-heavy builders**

A uniform signature-based TTL-cache wrapper now avoids repeated
composition cost on every hot-path endpoint poll. Pattern documented
in `DESIGN_PRINCIPLES.md § 8`; every wrapper follows the same shape
(volatile slot, atomic swap, signature-keyed invalidation, no locks
on the happy path, cold-start double-compute harmless because the
result is deterministic).

| Surface | File | TTL | Signature invalidator |
|---|---|---|---|
| Hardware profile | `HardwareProfiler.CaptureCached` | 5 min | `forceTier` change |
| Privacy posture | `PrivacyPostureBuilder.CaptureCached` | 30 s | Inference/Vision/TTS enable+BaseUrl, OTLP env-var |
| Resource-budget posture | `ResourceBudgetPostureBuilder.CaptureCached` | 15 s | Options fields + metrics + 75% fallback-share boundary |
| Airgap report | `AirGapVerifier.VerifyCached` | 30 s | Options outbound fields + OTLP env-var + MCP upstream list |

All four original `.Capture()` / `.Verify()` APIs preserved — every
existing test passes unchanged. Total: `+12` new regression tests
pinning the cache contracts.

**Count deltas**

- Tests: `528 → 540` (+12)
- Feature catalog: `116 → 120` (+4 — one per new TTL-cache entry)
- Docs (root + docs/): `19 → 25` (+6 new, +1 preserved `INDEX.md`
  updates, full cross-linking)
- Routes: `55 → 55` (unchanged — pattern is purely additive)
- MCP tools: `35 → 35` (unchanged)
- Drift gates: `14 / 14` green throughout

**Honest end-user status**

The sidecar runtime is production-ready: self-contained
`PalLLM.Sidecar.exe` boots cleanly, 540 tests + 14 drift gates
enforce quality, one-click `install.bat` / `play.bat` / `support.bat`
/ `recover.bat` cover the full operator life cycle. The roadmap
number holds at **`76.2%`** because the remaining work is strictly
in-game Palworld-native delivery (HUD binding, audio playback,
native action executor coverage) that requires a live in-game
session to close — and those items are tracked in
`docs/ROADMAP.md` § "Missing for 100%" for the next in-game work
session.

### Companion depth + distribution quality (2026-04-24)

Passes 38 through 42 — companion-facing depth (mood weather,
personality packs, lifetime memory) plus distribution-quality
infrastructure (release checksums + signing docs + compatibility
matrix). `+27` tests, `+5` feature-catalog entries, `+2` HTTP
endpoints, `+1` MCP tool, `+3` docs.

- **Pass 38 — mood weather advisor (C10).** New
  `MoodWeatherAdvisor.Forecast` blends a character's
  `CharacterRelationship` (affinity + last tone) with the world
  snapshot (threat, player HP, time-of-day) into a short mood
  bucket (content/uneasy/agitated/affectionate/weary/wary), weather
  metaphor (clear-sky/partly-cloudy/cold-front/storm-front/
  cloudburst/golden-hour/twilight), tone word, and plain-English
  summary. Combat/low-HP override affinity; tone nudges happen
  before night-softening so night always wins. Surfaced via
  `GET /api/characters/{id}/mood` + `pal_mood_weather` MCP tool.
  7 new regression tests.
- **Pass 39 — personality pack v1 format (C6).** New
  `PersonalityPackManifest` record + `PersonalityPackValidator`
  under `src/PalLLM.Domain/Packs/`. A pack is a directory with
  `pack.json` + `prompt.md` + optional `voice-hint.md` / portrait /
  audio samples. The validator enforces schema version, required
  files, bounded prompt length (8 KB cap), allowlisted audio
  extensions (`.ogg` / `.opus`), and a content-hash invariant:
  loaders reject a pack whose on-disk content hash doesn't match
  the manifest's declared `ContentHash`. Pure local — no signature
  verification, no download path. 6 new regression tests.
- **Pass 40 — cross-session lifetime memory (C8).** New
  `LifetimeRelationshipAggregator` rolls per-session
  `CharacterRelationship` records into a cross-session aggregate
  (first-seen, last-seen, session-count, peak/floor affinity,
  cumulative average, mood-tally). `Summarise` renders each record
  as a life-story sentence. Persistence is caller's concern: aggregate
  writes to `{PalSavedRoot}/Runtime/LifetimeRelationships/latest.json`
  and loads through `Deserialize` (empty-fallback on malformed JSON).
  Surfaced via `GET /api/relationships/lifetime`. 5 new tests.
- **Pass 41 — release checksums + signing docs (E2).** New
  `scripts/compute-release-checksums.ps1` walks
  `artifacts/packaging/` and writes canonical `SHA256SUMS` +
  `SHA512SUMS` + `checksums.json` (LF line endings, sort-stable
  ordering, structured JSON with size bytes + capture time). New
  `docs/RELEASE_SIGNING.md` documents the full policy: digests
  always published, minisign/GPG signatures opt-in, code-signing
  separately, key rotation workflow, verification commands for
  Windows + Linux + macOS.
- **Pass 42 — doctor compatibility matrix (D9).** New
  `docs/COMPATIBILITY.md` with supported Palworld / UE4SS / .NET /
  OS versions, known-good UE4SS mod pairs, known conflicts (mod +
  tooling), known-safe inference endpoints. Mirrored by
  `scripts/compatibility.json` (schema v1) so `scripts/doctor.ps1`
  can consume it at run time and new entries land by JSON patch
  rather than code release.
- **Verification.** `dotnet test` reports `528 passing / 0 failing`,
  `scripts/run_full_audit.ps1` reports `14/14` drift gates green.
  Test count moved `510 → 528` (+18), feature catalog `111 → 116`
  (+5), HTTP routes `53 → 55` (+2), MCP tools `34 → 35` (+1).

### Mesh surfaces + advisories + party chat (2026-04-24)

Passes 30 through 36 — a focused overnight arc adding MCP vision describe,
a deterministic player-intent translator, doc-freshness audit gate,
graceful-degradation advisor, party chat endpoint, resource-budget
posture surface, and world-narration advisor. `+29` tests, `+6`
feature-catalog entries, `+5` HTTP endpoints, `+5` MCP tools, `+1`
drift gate.

- **Pass 30 — `pal_vision_describe` MCP tool (C5).** Wrapper over the
  existing `/api/vision/describe` endpoint so an MCP client can push
  a base64 image and get a plain-text scene description in one call.
  Returns status='disabled' when vision is off, status='failed' on
  upstream failure, or the full `VisionDescribeResponse` on success.
  Pure wrapper — privacy posture for vision traffic stays honest.
- **Pass 31 — directive intent translator (C3).** New
  `DirectiveIntentTranslator` turns natural-language player chat
  ("hey SpeciesAlpha, stop mining and help me fight") into an ordered
  `PalDirective[]` the UE4SS mod can forward to the native pal-AI
  controller. Recognises 8 intents: `stop_current_task`,
  `recall_pals`, `help_in_combat`, `gather_resources`,
  `request_craft_queue`, `mark_waypoint`, `follow_player`,
  `guard_position`. Never emits above
  `PalLLM:Automation:AllowedActions` — candidates that fire a cue
  but aren't allowlisted land in `RejectedCandidates[]` with a
  diagnostic. Surfaced via `POST /api/directives/plan` +
  `pal_directives_plan` MCP tool. 7 new regression tests.
- **Pass 32 — doc-freshness drift gate (E8).** Added
  `Drift_Doc_freshness` step to `scripts/run_full_audit.ps1`. Fails
  when any doc's canonical `Last audited: \`YYYY-MM-DD\`` stamp is
  older than 45 days. Skips docs that don't carry the stamp by
  design (NOTICE, SECURITY, LICENSE, CHANGELOG, issue templates).
  Tightens the docs↔code truth gap so stale content can't hide.
- **Pass 33 — graceful-degradation advisor (D2).** New
  `GracefulDegradationAdvisor` inspects `HardwareProfile` +
  `PalLlmOptions` and returns a posture classification
  (`CpuOnlyConstrained` / `CpuOnlyCapable` / `GpuEntry` / `Standard` /
  `NoDegradation`) with ordered recommendations
  (`keep` / `disable` / `review` / `opt-in` / `leave-off`).
  Answers "my laptop has no GPU, can I still play?" Surfaced via
  `GET /api/degradation/advisory` + `pal_degradation_advisory`
  MCP tool. 4 new tests.
- **Pass 34 — party chat endpoint (C1).** New
  `POST /api/chat/party` fans a single utterance across multiple
  character ids in order. Each per-character turn runs through the
  full `ChatAsync` pipeline (task-aware execution profile, Pass-8
  planner, rate limiting, deterministic fallback all apply
  per-turn). Threaded mode seeds each reply with a summary of
  earlier replies so a conversation forms. Deliberately NOT a
  breaking change on `ChatRequest` — party chat is a sibling
  endpoint. Returns `PartyChatResponse { PartyId, Turns[],
  Threaded, TotalLatencyMs, FallbackTurnCount, CapturedAtUtc }`.
  4 new regression tests.
- **Pass 35 — resource-budget posture (D10).** New
  `ResourceBudgetPostureBuilder` surfaces every tracked budget
  (inference rate, circuit breaker, vision queue, TTS caps, memory
  window, bridge retention, chat fallback share) with
  `ok` / `review` / `exhausted` / `unknown` bucketing and a
  plain-English recommendation. High fallback share on a
  live-configured install flips the chat-fallback-share row to
  `review`. Surfaced via `GET /api/budgets` +
  `pal_resource_budgets` MCP tool. 5 new tests.
- **Pass 36 — world-narration advisor (C2).** New
  `WorldNarrationAdvisor` decides whether the current world
  snapshot warrants a companion's one-line narration quip.
  Triggers: `combat-start`, `threat-spike`, `night-fall`,
  `weather-change`, `low-health`, `objective-update`. Rate-limited
  at 90s minimum gap (enforced by caller, surfaced as
  `MinimumGapSeconds` in the cue). Surfaced via
  `GET /api/narration/cue` + `pal_narration_cue` MCP tool. 9 new
  tests pin every trigger + rate-limit + null/unloaded-snapshot
  edge case.
- **Verification.** `dotnet test` reports `510 passing / 0 failing`,
  `scripts/run_full_audit.ps1` reports `14/14` drift gates green
  (including the new Pass-32 freshness gate). Test count moved
  `481 → 510` (+29), feature catalog `105 → 111` (+6), HTTP
  routes `48 → 53` (+5), MCP tools `27 → 34` (+7). Three of the MCP
  tools are sibling-read wrappers over existing endpoints
  (`pal_vision_describe`, `pal_degradation_advisory`,
  `pal_resource_budgets`) so the underlying route surface is smaller
  by count.

### 2035 mesh + privacy + infrastructure consolidation (2026-04-24)

Passes 21 through 28 — a tight sleep-session arc that lands the
executable role-aware routing observability, the promotion-apply verb,
hardware auto-detection, per-channel SSE events, and first-class
privacy posture. `+14` tests, `+7` feature-catalog entries, `+3`
MCP tools, `+3` HTTP endpoints, `+3` docs.

- **Pass 21 — `CooperationPattern` populated on every chat turn.**
  `PalLlmRuntime.ChatAsync` now calls the Pass-8
  `DuoOrchestratorPlanner` to pick a cooperation pattern based on the
  inferred `DuoTaskKind` + configured role bindings, and writes the
  pattern id onto `ChatResponse.CooperationPattern`. Three runtime
  tests pin `DeterministicOnly` / `SingleRoleFallback` /
  `ParallelDisagreement` for unbound / Worker-only / both-bound
  configurations. Still advisory — actual dispatch stays single-lane —
  but the field is no longer always null.
- **Pass 22 — `ChatDispatchPlanner` + `DispatchedRoleChain` +
  `DispatchMode`.** New
  `src/PalLLM.Domain/Inference/ChatDispatchPlanner.cs` turns a
  `(DuoCooperationPattern, ModelRoleCoverage)` pair into a concrete
  `ChatDispatchDecision` with ordered role chain ("Worker" or
  "Judge→Worker→Judge" etc.), primary/reviewer binding ids, mode
  bucket ("deterministic-only", "single-role", "duo-sequential",
  "duo-parallel", "duo-fanout", "duo-tournament", "duo-background",
  "duo-watchdog", "duo-appeal"), and a plain-English reason. Surfaced
  on every `ChatResponse` as `DispatchedRoleChain` +
  `DispatchMode`, and on every `/api/chat/plan` as `Dispatch`. Eight
  new regression tests including an exhaustive enum-coverage safety
  net so a future `DuoCooperationPattern` addition can't silently
  skip the mapping.
- **Pass 23 — per-channel SSE events on `/api/chat/stream`.** In
  addition to the existing `started` / `phase` / `final` events,
  `/api/chat/stream` now emits word-level `token` events (each
  carrying `{index, total, text}` from `AssistantMessage`), plus
  dedicated `presentation`, `speech`, and `action` events so
  streaming clients can render incrementally instead of parsing the
  full `ChatResponse`. Concatenated `token` text exactly equals the
  final `AssistantMessage` (pinned by a regression test). Field
  Console Chat panel now renders live tokens as they arrive and
  swaps to the final structured reply once the `final` event lands.
- **Pass 24 — `POST /api/promotion/apply` + `pal_promotion_apply`.**
  New `PromotionApplier` class + `PromotionApplyOptions` config
  block close the full 2035 hard-code promotion loop. The apply verb
  takes a Pass-14 preview and persists a durable staging triple —
  `template-{timestamp}-{task-class}.md` +
  `rollback-{timestamp}-{task-class}.txt` +
  `packet-{timestamp}-{task-class}.json` — under the configured
  `PromotionApply:StagingRoot`. Gated by
  `PalLLM:PromotionApply:AllowApply=false` by default (returns `403`
  until flipped on). NEVER mutates source code; the staged template
  is meant to be cherry-picked by a human reviewer. Retention-bounded
  by `MaxStagedArtifacts` — oldest triple pruned on overflow. Four
  regression tests pin refusal / stage / retention / packet
  provenance.
- **Pass 25 — hardware auto-detection (D1).** New
  `HardwareProfiler` captures OS, logical core count, rounded RAM GiB,
  and GPU-likelihood signal (via `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES` /
  `NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES` / `HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES` env-vars + driver
  markers under `System32/` on Windows and `/dev/nvidia*` + `/dev/kfd`
  on Linux), derives a `DuoHardwareTier` recommendation, and
  honours a `PalLLM:Hardware:ForceTier` override. Surfaced via
  `GET /api/hardware` + `pal_hardware_profile` MCP tool. Zero
  subprocess launches, zero network, safe on hot paths. Five
  regression tests cover default detection + three-tier override +
  case-insensitive parsing + unparsable fallback.
- **Pass 26 — support-export issue template.** Added
  `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/support_export.md` asking reporters to
  attach `support.bat`'s zip output, which makes the single highest-
  signal bug report path obvious. (CI workflow + bug-report /
  feature-request / compat-report templates already existed.)
- **Pass 27 — privacy posture surface (E3).** New
  `PrivacyPostureBuilder` enumerates every data-emitting surface
  PalLLM ships (conversation memory, dashboard, health probes, proof
  packets, promotion staging, live inference, vision, TTS, OTLP
  telemetry, upstream MCP, narrative packs, crash reports, update
  checks, analytics — 16 total) and classifies each as
  `never-leaves` / `only-with-opt-in` / `leaves-by-default`.
  Surfaced via `GET /api/privacy/posture` + `pal_privacy_posture`
  MCP tool. New `docs/PRIVACY.md` mirrors the same inventory in
  human-readable form; five regression tests pin the core
  guarantees (default install = zero outbound, enabled inference
  flips to `leaves-by-default`, every surface has a valid status,
  bucket counts partition the full list, ship-critical
  `never-leaves` surfaces stay in the inventory).
- **Pass 28 — server operator guide (E6).** New
  `docs/SERVER_OPERATOR.md` covers Topology A (sidecar on the
  dedicated-server box) vs Topology B (separate AI host), systemd
  unit file, per-player rate-limit tuning, monitoring metric
  inventory, backup/upgrade flow, and the privacy disclosure
  obligations for server admins who enable remote inference.
  Wired into `docs/INDEX.md`.
- **Verification.** `dotnet test PalLLM.sln --configuration Release`
  reports `481 passing / 0 failing` and
  `scripts/run_full_audit.ps1 -SkipCoverage -SkipSbom -SkipPackaging`
  reports `13/13` drift gates green. Test count moved
  `452 → 481` (+29), feature catalog `101 → 105` (+4),
  HTTP routes `45 → 48` (+3), MCP tools `25 → 27` (+2).

### Dashboard chat panel + per-turn inferred-task-kind advisory (2026-04-24)

Passes 18 and 19 of the 2035-mesh arc — operator-facing chat surface in
the Field Console plus per-turn planner advisory metadata on every
`ChatResponse`.

- **Pass 18 — Field Console Chat panel.** The dashboard
  (`src/PalLLM.Sidecar/wwwroot/{index.html,app.js,styles.css}`) gained a
  `#chat` panel with textarea input, SSE toggle, optional character-id
  override, submit button, rolling conversation history, and per-turn
  metadata chips (reply path, latency, phases, emotional cues). The
  panel calls `/api/chat/plan` for an advisory Duo-cooperation chip
  before the request, then dispatches to either `/api/chat` (sync) or
  `/api/chat/stream` (SSE) depending on the toggle. Added
  `dashboard-chat-panel` feature-catalog entry so feature counts reflect
  the new surface. Dashboard is no longer read-only — operators can now
  converse with the companion from the browser without leaving the
  Field Console.
- **Pass 19 — per-turn planner advisory on `ChatResponse`.**
  `ChatResponse` (`src/PalLLM.Domain/Integration/Contracts.cs`) gained
  two nullable advisory fields: `InferredTaskKind` (stringified
  `DuoTaskKind` produced by the Pass-16 `ChatTaskKindInferer` keyword
  scan) and `CooperationPattern` (reserved for the future executable
  routing step described in Pass 21+). `PalLlmRuntime.ChatAsync` now
  populates `InferredTaskKind` inside a defensive try/catch so inference
  failure can never fail a chat turn. `CooperationPattern` is
  intentionally `null` today because chat traffic still dispatches
  through the single-model lane — it will switch on once the planner's
  `DuoCooperationPattern` is actually driving
  `PalLlmRuntime.ChatAsync`'s dispatch. Added two regression tests
  (`ChatAsync_PopulatesInferredTaskKindAdvisory` and
  `ChatAsync_InferredTaskKindRespectsExplicitTaskTagOverride`) so the
  advisory contract is pinned. The committed OpenAPI snapshot at
  `docs/openapi/palllm-sidecar-v1.json` was regenerated to pick up the
  two new fields on the `ChatResponse` schema. Test count moved
  `450 -> 452`, feature-catalog moved `100 -> 101`, route + MCP-tool
  surface unchanged at `45` + `25`.
- **Doc + audit resync.** `README.md`, `docs/ROADMAP.md`,
  `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `docs/HANDOFF.md`, `docs/API.md`,
  `docs/MCP_QUICKSTART.md`, and JOURNEY.md all refreshed to the
  Pass-19 numbers. JOURNEY.md scorecard now shows the full
  19-pass arc with the `+64%` tests / `+63%` feature-catalog / `+50%`
  routes / `+127%` MCP-tools deltas from the pre-Pass-2 baseline.
- **Verification.** `dotnet test PalLLM.sln --configuration Release`
  reports `452 passing / 0 failing`, and
  `scripts/run_full_audit.ps1 -SkipCoverage -SkipSbom -SkipPackaging`
  reports `13 / 13` drift gates green. Packaged `PalLLM-v1.0.0.zip`
  rebuilt via `scripts/package-release.ps1 -Version v1.0.0` and
  live-probed against the extracted EXE.

### Recent-window readiness alerting surfaces (2026-04-24)

- **`/health/ready` now carries a dedicated recent-window inference check.**
  Added `InferencePerformanceReadinessHealthCheck`, so the health payload now
  reports both `results.readiness` for core runtime invariants and
  `results.inference_recent_window` for the bounded live-lane budget posture.
  When the recent live window is `degraded` or `critical`, the top-level health
  payload now reports `status=Degraded` while still returning HTTP `200`, and
  the result data includes a bounded `alerting_lanes[]` preview for the active
  offending lanes.
- **`/metrics` now exports first-class recent-window readiness gauges.**
  `PrometheusExporter` gained bounded recent-window and per-lane readiness
  families: `palllm_inference_recent_window_status`,
  `palllm_inference_recent_window_*_ratio_percent`,
  `palllm_inference_recent_window_{degraded,critical}_lanes`,
  `palllm_inference_lane_status`, and `palllm_inference_lane_sample_count`.
  The lane labels are bounded to the active recent-window lane set
  (`operation`, `provider`, `model`, `budget`) rather than per-request ids.
- **OTLP metrics now carry the same readiness truth.**
  Added `PalLlmOperationalTelemetry`, which registers observable gauges on the
  existing `PalLlmTelemetry.Meter` when `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` is set.
  Opt-in OTLP backends now receive the same bounded recent-window readiness
  state under `palllm.inference.recent_window.*` and `palllm.inference.lane.*`
  that Prometheus sees locally.
- **Docs and regression coverage moved together.**
  Updated `README.md`, `docs/API.md`, `docs/OPERATIONS.md`, and
  `docs/TUNING.md` to describe the new health/metrics/OTLP contract, and added
  regression coverage for the new health-check result plus the recent-window
  Prometheus metric families. Audited passing test count moved `428 -> 431`.

### Single-file packaged sidecar startup fix (2026-04-23)

- **Fixed `InvalidOperationException` on packaged-sidecar startup.** The
  `PublishSingleFile=true` self-extracting bundle produced by
  `scripts/package-release.ps1` left `PalLLM.Sidecar.staticwebassets.endpoints.json`
  next to the EXE but never copied it into the per-launch
  `AppContext.BaseDirectory` extraction temp directory, so
  `MapStaticAssets()`'s default manifest lookup threw on the first request
  through the static-asset endpoint pipeline. `IncludeAllContentForSelfExtract=true`
  did not catch the manifest because the endpoints JSON is generated by an
  SDK target that runs after the single-file bundle is composed.
- **Resolved without reverting the endpoint-routed static-asset fast path.**
  `src/PalLLM.Sidecar/Program.cs` now resolves the manifest beside
  `Environment.ProcessPath` when one is present and hands the explicit
  path to `MapStaticAssets(manifestPath)`. `dotnet run`, the framework-
  dependent publish path, and the test host all keep falling through to the
  default `AppContext.BaseDirectory` lookup, so no behaviour changes for the
  287-test suite or developer-loop scenarios. The pre-compressed `.br`/`.gz`
  artifacts in `wwwroot/` keep flowing through the endpoint-routed pipeline.

### v1.0.0 consolidation (2026-04-23)

Final three-pass consolidation that caps the Pass-2 → Pass-14 arc with
a shippable production package, a chat-plan advisor, and a full
journey doc.

- **Pass 15 — production package rebuild.** `PalLLM-v1.0.0.zip`
  (58.6 MB) extracts to `D:/PalLLM-1.0.0-prod/`, bundles a
  self-contained 64 MB single-file EXE, and boots cleanly (Pass 2's
  `staticwebassets` extraction bug is resolved). Live probe confirmed
  every surface: `/health/live` healthy, `/api/describe`
  OperatorHealth 80/Good, `/api/quickstart` needs-attention headline,
  `/api/airgap/verify` strict-airgapped, `/api/roles` pairing-pattern
  text, `/api/features` 98 entries, MCP tools/list returns 24 pal_*
  tools, `/api/why` FallbackTriggered, `/api/duo/plan` Pattern=11,
  `/api/disagreement/check` verdict=agree, `/api/proof/packet` emits
  stable SHA, `/api/chat/stream` emits SSE started → phase → final.
- **JOURNEY.md** — new executive summary of the 14-pass
  architectural arc. Scoreboard (tests +55%, catalog +58%, routes
  +47%, MCP tools +118%), five fallback tiers, HTTP+MCP+dashboard
  parity table, the "2035 local-first AI mesh loop" wired end-to-end.
  Linked from `docs/INDEX.md`.
- **Pass 16 — role-aware chat planning.** New
  `ChatTaskKindInferer` maps raw user messages to one of the 10
  `DuoTaskKind` values via a deterministic keyword classifier. New
  `POST /api/chat/plan` and `pal_chat_plan` MCP tool (tool #25) feed
  the inferred kind through the Pass-8 planner and return the full
  DuoPlan the instance would use — without running inference. High-risk
  phrases (delete / wipe / api key / production deploy) always route
  to `DuoTaskKind.HighRisk`; explicit `TaskTag` overrides message-
  keyword inference. **19 new tests**: 14 inferer classifier tests
  (every task kind + edge cases + tag override) and 5 endpoint tests
  (defaults / architecture / high-risk / tag override / full shape).
- **Feature-catalog growth `98 -> 100`**: `chat-task-kind-inferer`,
  `chat-plan-advisor`. Route count `44 -> 45`. MCP tool count
  `24 -> 25`. Test count `428 -> 450`. README / ROADMAP / ARCHITECTURE
  / HANDOFF / API / MCP-quickstart / JOURNEY all resynced. Committed
  OpenAPI snapshot regenerated.

### Promotion apply previews (2026-04-23)

Closes the final step of the 2035 hard-code promotion loop. After
Pass 12 surfaces *which* pattern to promote and Pass 13 automates the
feeding, Pass 14 now shows operators the **concrete change they'd
hand-apply** — file path, before-context anchor, after-code template,
safety warnings, and a single-line git rollback command.

- **`PromotionApplyPreviewBuilder`** in `PalLLM.Domain.Runtime`.
  Deterministic — no file reads, no inference call. Per-task-class
  templates (fallback-director → `FallbackBehaviorEngine.cs` priority
  bump; duo-\* → `DuoOrchestratorPlanner.cs` SelectPattern pin;
  live-inference → tighter circuit-breaker window; disagreement →
  tighter agree threshold; rate-limiter → MaxCharacterRequestsPerMinute
  tweak; tier-transition → default `ModelTiers` list). Unknown task
  classes fall through to a generic template so the builder never
  fails to produce output. Every preview carries a ProofPacket tagged
  `promotion-apply-preview` with `HumanReviewRequired=true`.
- **`POST /api/promotion/apply/preview`** — accepts
  `{TaskClass, PatternId?}` and returns `PromotionApplyPreview{TaskClass,
  PatternId, TargetFile, DiffPreview, SafetyWarnings[],
  RollbackCommand, Provenance}`. Status codes: 400 missing task /
  404 unobserved / 409 not-yet-candidate / 200 preview. If
  `PatternId` is omitted the server defaults to the candidate's
  most-common pattern.
- **`pal_promotion_apply_preview` MCP tool** — MCP tool count now
  `24` (up from `23`). AI agents can now ask "what would the actual
  change look like if I promoted this?" and get a copy-pasteable
  template back.
- **14 new tests** (`414 -> 428`): 10 builder tests covering every
  recognised task class + unknown fallthrough + provenance/safety
  invariants, 4 endpoint tests covering 400 / 404 / 409 / 200.
- **Feature-catalog growth `96 -> 98`**:
  `promotion-apply-preview-builder`,
  `promotion-apply-preview-endpoint`. Route count `43 -> 44`.
  README / ROADMAP / ARCHITECTURE / HANDOFF / API / MCP-quickstart
  all resynced. Committed OpenAPI snapshot regenerated.

### Full-surface promotion feeder (2026-04-23)

Extends the Pass 11 `PromotionLedgerFeeder` from fallback-only to the
full observable runtime. The feeder now also diffs `RuntimeHealth`'s
inference-success, inference-failure, rate-limit, and model-tier
transition counters on each tick, emitting observations against four
new task classes. The dashboard Promotion panel and `/api/promotion/*`
surfaces pick up the new signals automatically.

- **`PromotionLedgerFeeder`** now takes `PalLlmRuntime` in addition to
  `PalLlmMetrics` so it can read health-counter deltas. Still pure
  observer — no mutation of metrics, runtime, or ChatAsync. Reads are
  bounded (same `MaxObservationsPerStrategyPerTick` cap) and baselines
  are seeded on startup so pre-existing counters never retroactively
  flood the ledger.
- **Three new task classes** recorded automatically:
  - `live-inference` — inference-success counter delta → `success`;
    inference-failure counter delta → `validator-fail`. Pattern id is
    the live `InferenceActiveModel` from `RuntimeHealth` so different
    models populate separate observation streams and promotion
    candidates surface per-model.
  - `rate-limiter` — rate-limit-engagement counter delta → `success`
    (the limiter itself working is a positive signal; excess traffic
    still lands in the fallback).
  - `model-tier-transition` — tier-graduation counter delta →
    `success` with pattern id `"from->to"` so the dashboard can
    distinguish graduations from demotions.
- **Three new opt-out toggles** under `PalLLM:PromotionFeeder`:
  `TrackLiveInference` / `TrackRateLimiter` / `TrackTierTransitions`
  (all default `true`). Each has a matching `*TaskClass` string for
  operators who want custom slot names.
- **5 new tests** (`409 -> 414`): tier-transition records with
  `"from->to"` pattern key / disabling tier transitions records nothing
  / fresh runtime leaves live-inference + rate-limiter at zero /
  every-signal-disabled still protects the always-on fallback path /
  configurable task-class names flow through to observations.
- **Feature-catalog entry updated** (not added): the existing
  `promotion-ledger-auto-feeder` entry now documents the full
  five-surface coverage. Route / feature / MCP-tool counts unchanged.

### Promotion suggestions (2026-04-23)

Closes the final 2035 loop: a stable pattern no longer just surfaces
as "candidate" on the dashboard — the system also proposes the exact
hard-code change that would lock it in, with target file, rollback
path, and a ProofPacket tagging the suggestion itself.

- **`PromotionSuggestionBuilder`** in `PalLLM.Domain.Runtime`.
  Pure deterministic function: given a `PromotionSummary`, emits one
  `PromotionSuggestion` per candidate with `{TaskClass, PatternId,
  TargetFile, SuggestedChange, EvidenceSummary, RollbackPath,
  Provenance (full ProofPacket)}`. Recognised task classes
  (fallback-director → FallbackBehaviorEngine.cs, duo-\* →
  DuoOrchestratorPlanner.cs, live-inference → InferenceClient.cs,
  duo-disagreement-detector → DisagreementDetector.cs) get tailored
  file targets and one-sentence recipes; unknown classes fall through
  to a generic suggestion so the builder never fails to produce
  output. Every suggestion carries `HumanReviewRequired=true` and
  `Confidence=high` — hard-coding is always human-gated.
- **`GET /api/promotion/suggestions`** + **`pal_promotion_suggestions`
  MCP tool** — brings MCP tool count to `23` (up from `22`). Skips
  non-candidate tasks automatically.
- **Dashboard Promotion panel** — candidate cards now expand to show
  the inline suggestion: target file (as code), suggested change,
  evidence summary, rollback path. Parallel fetch of summary +
  suggestions; if the suggestions endpoint fails, falls back
  gracefully to the Pass 11 summary-only render. Uses a standard
  `<details>` element (open by default) so keyboard + screen-reader
  users get the same detail view as mouse users.
- **11 new tests** (`398 -> 409`): empty-summary → empty set / skips
  non-candidates / fallback-director → engine file / duo-\* →
  planner file / live-inference → inference client / disagreement
  detector → detector file / unknown → generic / every suggestion
  carries full ProofPacket provenance / evidence summary format is
  stable / multi-candidate set / BuildForTask ↔ Build equivalence.
- **Feature-catalog growth `93 -> 96`**:
  `promotion-suggestion-builder`, `promotion-suggestions-endpoint`,
  `dashboard-promotion-suggestion-inline`. Route count `42 -> 43`.
  README / ROADMAP / ARCHITECTURE / HANDOFF / API / MCP-quickstart
  all resynced. Committed OpenAPI snapshot regenerated.

### Auto-feeder + dashboard promotion panel (2026-04-23)

Closes the Pass 10 loop. The ledger no longer needs manual `POST` calls
to populate — a background worker converts live fallback-strategy
metric deltas into observations automatically, and a new dashboard
panel visualises which patterns are stable enough to hard-code.

- **`PromotionLedgerFeeder` background worker** in `PalLLM.Sidecar`.
  Every 30s (configurable) reads `PalLlmMetrics.Snapshot()`, diffs the
  fallback-strategy counts against the prior tick, and records one
  `OutcomeSuccess` observation per increment into the ledger.
  Observer-only — never mutates metrics, ChatAsync, or the
  deterministic always-available layer. Opt-in (defaults ON) via new
