  `PalLlmOptions.PromotionFeeder` (`Enabled` / `CheckIntervalSeconds`
  / `MaxObservationsPerStrategyPerTick` / `FallbackTaskClass`). Seeds a
  baseline at boot so pre-existing counters don't retroactively flood
  the ledger. Caps per-tick emission so a burst window is bounded but
  still advances the prior count past the full delta (no double-record
  next tick).
- **Dashboard Promotion panel** reads `/api/promotion/summary` on
  every refresh and renders per-task cards:
  - Green "candidate" cards glow when a pattern crosses the stability
    gate (≥20 obs / ≥95% success / no recent block or override).
  - Amber "collecting" cards show patterns still gathering evidence.
  - Red "blocked" cards surface patterns with recent disagreement-block
    or human-override outcomes so operators can investigate the root
    cause before promoting.
  - Empty / loading / error states stay neutral so fresh sidecars
    don't look broken.
  Section anchor `<a href="#promotion">Promotion</a>` added to the top
  nav. Rides the existing dashboard refresh cadence — no separate
  polling loop.
- **6 new tests** (`392 -> 398`): disabled feeder records zero / no-delta
  returns zero / new strategy fires record one per increment / burst
  beyond cap advances baseline anyway / custom task-class respected /
  multi-strategy recording.
- **Feature-catalog growth `91 -> 93`**:
  `promotion-ledger-auto-feeder`, `dashboard-promotion-panel`. Route
  count unchanged at 42. README / ROADMAP / ARCHITECTURE / HANDOFF /
  API / MCP-quickstart all resynced.

### Hard-code promotion ledger (2026-04-23)

Implements the 2035 doc's headline prediction that *"successful AI
behavior becomes hard-coded, safe, fast product logic"* as a concrete,
machine-readable surface: record per-task-class outcomes, read back
which patterns are stable enough to promote.

- **`PromotionLedger`** in `PalLLM.Domain.Runtime`. Bounded in-memory
  deque (200 observations per task class, oldest-first eviction) with
  per-task lock so observations across different task classes don't
  contend. Outcomes are constrained to `success` / `disagreement-block`
  / `validator-fail` / `human-override`; anything else rejects with
  `ArgumentException` so the wire surface can't silently record
  garbage. Promotion criterion is deliberately conservative:
  ≥20 observations AND ≥95% success rate AND zero `disagreement-block`
  / `human-override` outcomes in the most recent 10.
- **`POST /api/promotion/record`** — records one observation; invalid
  outcomes return ProblemDetails 400 with a helpful error instead of
  silently succeeding or 500-ing.
- **`GET /api/promotion/summary`** — returns the per-task summary with
  counts, success rate, most-common pattern (ordinal-alphabetical
  tie-break so dashboards don't flap), `IsPromotionCandidate` flag,
  and a human-readable recommendation sentence for every state
  ("not enough data", "recent block", "success rate below floor",
  "stable — consider hard-coding").
- **`pal_promotion_record` + `pal_promotion_summary` MCP tools** —
  brings the MCP tool count to `22` (up from `20`). AI agents can now
  feed observations into the ledger and ask "has this pattern been
  stable enough to trust?" before relying on it.
- **16 new tests** (`376 -> 392`): 12 `PromotionLedgerTests` pinning
  fresh-ledger empty / invalid-outcome reject / below-minimum no-promote
  / high-success-clean promote / recent-block blocks / recent-override
  blocks / low-success blocks / beyond-window eviction /
  tie-break determinism / sort order, plus 4 `PromotionEndpointTests`
  covering fresh summary, valid record, invalid-outcome ProblemDetails,
  and post-record reflection.
- **Feature-catalog growth `89 -> 91`**: `promotion-ledger`,
  `promotion-endpoints`. Route count `40 -> 42`. MCP tool-list test
  bumped to 22 tools. README / ROADMAP / ARCHITECTURE / HANDOFF / API /
  MCP-quickstart all resynced. Committed OpenAPI snapshot regenerated.

### Disagreement detector + proof packet builder (2026-04-23)

Completes Pass 8's `ParallelDisagreement` cooperation pattern by adding
a deterministic structural comparison of two model outputs, and lands
the 2035 doc's *"provenance becomes a normal product artifact"*
prediction with a machine-readable proof-packet bundle for any
automated decision.

- **`DisagreementDetector`** in `PalLLM.Domain.Runtime`. Blends three
  independent similarity signals — semantic cosine (via the portable
  `SemanticEmbedder`), token-overlap Jaccard index, and length ratio —
  into a single `CombinedScore` with conservative thresholds
  (≥0.85 agree, ≥0.60 minor-drift, otherwise major-disagreement).
  Emits `{Verdict, SafetySignal (proceed/review/block),
  Recommendation, KeyEntityAgreement[]}`. Never calls inference. Empty
  or null inputs are treated as structural failures and always block.
- **`ProofPacketBuilder`** in `PalLLM.Domain.Runtime`. One-shot builder
  that wraps any automated PalLLM decision as a `ProofPacket { Version,
  Id (stable SHA-256), Subsystem, Decision, PrimaryReason,
  CapturedAtUtc, Evidence[], ModelArtifacts[], ValidatorResults[],
  RollbackPath, Confidence, HumanReviewRequired }`. Convenience
  builders `FromFallbackDecision` (turns a `FallbackBehaviorDecision`
  into a proof packet) and `FromDisagreement` (turns a
  `DisagreementAnalysis` into a proof packet with the right
  `HumanReviewRequired` flag) so existing runtime paths can attach
  provenance in one line.
- **`POST /api/disagreement/check`** and **`POST /api/proof/packet`** —
  new HTTP endpoints exposing both primitives with stable PascalCase
  JSON shapes.
- **`pal_disagreement_check` + `pal_proof_packet` MCP tools** —
  brings the MCP tool count to `20` (up from `18`). AI agents can now
  compare two model outputs structurally and attach provenance bundles
  to their decisions without calling out to a REST client.
- **19 new tests** (`357 -> 376`): 10 `DisagreementDetectorTests`
  (identical / paraphrase / opposite / one-empty / both-empty /
  null-inputs / shared-entities / length-ratio / stable-output /
  invariant-culture summary), 9 `ProofPacketBuilderTests` (basic
  populate / stable id / time-variant id / missing evidence /
  unknown confidence / missing subsystem / FromDisagreement
  block+agree / FromFallbackDecision).
- **Feature-catalog growth `87 -> 89`**: `duo-disagreement-detector`,
  `proof-packet-builder`. Route count `38 -> 40`. MCP tool-list test
  bumped to `20` tools. README / ROADMAP / ARCHITECTURE / HANDOFF /
  API / MCP-quickstart all resynced. Committed OpenAPI snapshot
  regenerated.

### Qwen Duo Mesh orchestrator (2026-04-23)

Layers the ten Qwen Duo cooperation patterns from the 2035 architecture
brief onto the Pass 7 role registry. Where `/api/roles` tells you WHAT
is bound, `/api/duo/plan` now tells you HOW to use it — and both AI
clients and dashboards get the same recommendation shape.

- **`DuoCooperationPattern` enum + `DuoOrchestratorPlanner` service**
  in `PalLLM.Domain.Inference`. Deterministic — no inference call, no
  external I/O. Given a task kind (CommandRouting / ImplementDraft /
  ArchitecturePlan / Audit / ParallelCandidates / FinalSynthesis /
  LongContextSynthesis / ToolExecution / HighRisk / MediaPrompting),
  a risk level, a hardware tier, and the live role coverage, returns
  a structured `DuoPlan` carrying one of the ten patterns (Scout →
  Judge, Architect → Implementer → Auditor, Fan-out → Synthesis,
  Parallel Disagreement, Branch Tournament, Sequential Swap,
  Worker-live/Judge-background, Draft → Finalize, Duo Watchdog, Dense
  Appeal Court) plus step-by-step role assignments, thinking-mode
  hints, context-budget hints, and an escalation path.
- **High-risk tasks always take `ParallelDisagreement`** so
  disagreement between Worker and Judge becomes a first-class safety
  signal.
- **Constrained-hardware tasks always take `SequentialSwap`** so
  operators on 8–20GB VRAM / CPU-only laptops get a plan that only
  needs one model resident at a time.
- **Missing-role degradation** — the planner falls through to
  `SingleRoleFallback` when only Worker or only Judge is bound, and
  to `DeterministicOnly` when neither is bound, each with a clear
  nudge to declare the missing role.
- **`POST /api/duo/plan`** — new HTTP endpoint exposing the planner.
  Accepts numeric enum values for `Kind` / `Risk` / `Hardware`
  (default `ImplementDraft` / `Low` / `Standard`) and returns the
  `DuoPlan` shape the dashboard and MCP tool both consume.
- **`pal_duo_plan` MCP tool** — brings the MCP tool count to `18` (up
  from `17`). Claude Desktop / Cursor / VS Code users can ask "which
  cooperation pattern should I use for this?" and get a structured
  plan back without reading any docs. Accepts `kind` / `risk` /
  `hardware` as string enum names (case-insensitive) so AI callers can
  be sloppy.
- **Dashboard Mesh panel** — now appends a small "default Duo pattern"
  hint line below the pairing text, with friendly labels (Scout →
  Judge, Branch Tournament, etc.) mapped client-side from the server's
  numeric enum output. Fire-and-forget fetch — if `/api/duo/plan`
  fails the hint is silently omitted.
- **15 new tests** (`342 -> 357`): 13 `DuoOrchestratorPlannerTests`
  pinning pattern selection across every task kind + the missing-role
  + constrained-hardware + high-risk escape hatches, 2
  `DuoPlanEndpointTests` proving the HTTP contract. MCP tool-list
  test bumped to 18 tools.
- **Feature-catalog growth `83 -> 87`**: `duo-orchestrator-planner`,
  `duo-plan-endpoint`, `pal-duo-plan-mcp-tool`,
  `dashboard-mesh-duo-hint`. Route count `37 -> 38`. README /
  ROADMAP / ARCHITECTURE / HANDOFF / API / MCP-quickstart all
  resynced. Committed OpenAPI snapshot regenerated.

### Local-first AI mesh role registry (2026-04-23)

Lands the 2035 doc's core architectural recommendation: declarative
Edge / Worker / Judge / Media / Validator role bindings so operators
can map their available local models to mesh roles and AI clients can
see which brain handles what. Metadata-only today — no existing
inference behaviour changed — but sets the foundation for future
role-aware routing (fast worker draft → dense judge audit → media
generator → validator gate).

- **`ModelRole` enum + `ModelRoleBinding` options list + `ModelRoleRegistry`
  service** in `PalLLM.Domain.Inference`. Each binding declares
  `{ Role, Id, ModelId, BaseUrl, Description, Enabled }`; the registry
  reports coverage, active bindings, critical gaps (Edge + Worker), and
  a `PairingPattern` message tuned to the operator's current topology
  ("Full local mesh", "Worker + Judge only", "Edge + Worker only", etc.).
- **`GET /api/roles`** — new HTTP endpoint returning
  `ModelRoleCoverage { Slots[], ActiveBindings, TotalBindings,
  CriticalGaps[], PairingPattern }`. Shared with the MCP tool and the
  dashboard panel so every consumer sees the same shape.
- **`pal_model_roles` MCP tool** — brings the MCP tool count to `17`
  (up from `16`). Claude Desktop / Cursor / VS Code users can ask
  "what local models has this operator configured?" and get structured
  coverage back.
- **Quickstart integration** — `/api/quickstart` + `pal_quickstart` now
  consume the registry and emit an optional-tier step when the
  operator has no role bindings, or a recommended upgrade when a
  critical role (Edge / Worker) is still unbound. The nudge always
  stays in the 'optional' tier — role binding is metadata-only and
  never blocks the chat path.
- **Dashboard Mesh panel** — new Field Console section with a 5-slot
  grid showing per-role state (active / configured / missing),
  active-binding id + model tag, role description, and per-slot
  recommendation for unbound roles. Pairing-pattern string becomes the
  surface-copy headline. Section anchor `<a href="#mesh">Mesh</a>`
  added to the top nav.
- **5 new unit tests** pinning: empty-list coverage, Worker+Judge
  pairing pattern, full-mesh pattern, disabled bindings don't count as
  active, multi-binding-per-role first-enabled-wins. MCP tool-list
  test bumped to 17 tools.
- **Feature-catalog growth `78 -> 83`**: `model-role-registry`,
  `model-roles-endpoint`, `pal-model-roles-mcp-tool`,
  `dashboard-mesh-panel`, `quickstart-role-coverage-nudge`. Route count
  `36 -> 37`. Test count `337 -> 342`. README / ROADMAP / ARCHITECTURE
  / HANDOFF / API / MCP-quickstart all resynced. Committed OpenAPI
  snapshot regenerated.

### Local "why engine" (2026-04-23)

Implements the 2026+ local-first AI mesh "why engine" pattern: any
operator, player, or AI agent can ask a natural-language causal
question about PalLLM's recent behaviour and get a structured,
deterministic, evidence-grounded answer — even when every external
dependency is down.

- **`WhyEngine` in `PalLLM.Domain.Runtime`** — keyword-classifies the
  question into one of `FallbackTriggered` / `InferenceBypassed` /
  `CircuitBreaker` / `BridgeNotReady` / `LowHealthScore` /
  `RateLimited` / `ThermalGate` / `Unknown`, then returns a structured
  `WhyAnswer { Question, Intent, PrimaryReason, CausalChain[],
  EvidenceReferences[], Confidence }`. Unmatched questions fall
  through to a grounded generic-posture explanation so the engine
  never fails to produce useful output. **Portable, BCL-only,
  harvestable** by any other local-first LLM runtime.
- **`POST /api/why`** — one-shot HTTP endpoint wrapping
  `WhyEngine.Answer`. Accepts `{Question: string}`, returns the same
  `WhyAnswer` shape the dashboard and MCP tool render. Deterministic:
  no live inference call, so the endpoint is always available.
- **`pal_why` MCP tool** — brings the MCP tool count to `16` (up from
  `15`). Claude Desktop / Cursor / VS Code users can now ask "why did
  my companion say that?" or "why is the bridge not ready?" and get
  the same structured answer a REST client would.
- **Dashboard Why panel** — new Field Console section with a single
  input box plus five preset chips (Fallback? / Bridge? / Health? /
  Bypass? / Circuit?). Submit triggers `POST /api/why` and renders
  the causal chain as a numbered list with evidence references and
  intent-coloured border. Section anchor `<a href="#why">Why</a>`
  added to the top nav.
- **13 new tests** (`324 -> 337`): 10 `WhyEngineTests` pinning intent
  classification + grounded-fallback behaviour, 2 `WhyEndpointTests`
  proving the HTTP contract, 1 MCP test proving `pal_why` works
  through the MCP pipeline. MCP tool-list test bumped to 16 tools.
- **Feature-catalog growth `74 -> 78`**: `why-engine`, `why-endpoint`,
  `dashboard-why-panel`, `pal-why-mcp-tool`. Route count `35 -> 36`.
  README / ROADMAP / ARCHITECTURE / HANDOFF / API / MCP-quickstart
  all resynced. Committed OpenAPI snapshot regenerated.

### AI-first MCP tools + self-healing status chip (2026-04-23)

Exposes the self-awareness surfaces (identity, live state, next-step
guidance, air-gap posture, watchdog status) to MCP clients so Claude
Desktop / Cursor / VS Code users can ask natural-language questions and
get the same machine-readable payloads REST clients already see.

- **4 new MCP tools** in `PalLlmMcpTools`, bringing the MCP tool count
  to `15` (up from `11`):
  - `pal_describe` mirrors `GET /api/describe` — one-shot self-description
    manifest (identity, OperatorHealth, surface counts, posture
    guarantees, common-ask shortcuts, safety notes).
  - `pal_quickstart` mirrors `GET /api/quickstart` — live critical /
    recommended / optional steps with label / why / action / verify
    fields.
  - `pal_airgap_verify` mirrors `GET /api/airgap/verify` — classifies
    every enabled outbound surface without opening a TCP connection or
    emitting a live request.
  - `pal_self_healing_status` reads `Runtime/SelfHealingEvidence/latest-self-healing.json`
    via the shared `SelfHealingStatusReader`, returning the raw evidence
    or a structured pending marker.
- **`GET /api/self-healing/status`** — new HTTP endpoint exposing the
  same reader so the dashboard chip + the MCP tool see the exact same
  payload contract. Pending-marker shape is
  `{ status: "pending" | "unreadable", detail: "..." }` so clients can
  always distinguish "worker has not ticked yet" from "worker tick
  landed with these results".
- **Dashboard self-healing chip** in the top ribbon — compact pill that
  reads `/api/self-healing/status` on every refresh and shows "Watchdog:
  42s ago · no orphans" (quiet green), "archived N" (amber), or
  "pending first tick" (grey). Hover shows the exact operator-health
  score the last tick observed.
- **Shared `SelfHealingStatusReader`** keeps the reading + pending-marker
  shape single-sourced across HTTP / MCP / dashboard surfaces so future
  watchdog payload changes propagate in one edit.
- **4 new MCP test cases** pin the new tools' discoverability and
  payload contracts (Describe / Quickstart / AirgapVerify returns a
  valid verdict / SelfHealingStatus returns evidence or a pending
  marker). Route count `34 -> 35`, feature catalog `71 -> 74`
  (`ai-first-mcp-tools`, `self-healing-status-endpoint`,
  `dashboard-self-healing-chip`), test count `320 -> 324`.
- README, ROADMAP, ARCHITECTURE, HANDOFF, API, and MCP-quickstart
  resynced. Committed OpenAPI snapshot regenerated.

### Self-healing watchdog + dashboard Quickstart panel (2026-04-23)

Two additions that make PalLLM "sure to work" over long sessions and
"obviously intuitive" to a first-time human operator, without changing
any existing behaviour.

- **`SelfHealingWorker`** — conservative background watchdog that ticks
  every 60s (configurable), sweeps orphan outbox envelopes older than
  the configured threshold into
  `Runtime/SelfHealingEvidence/recovered-<UTC>/` (nothing is deleted —
  an operator can recover them if needed), logs a structured warning
  when the operator-health score drops at or below the unhealthy floor,
  and writes `Runtime/SelfHealingEvidence/latest-self-healing.json`
  plus a rotating history for audit. Defaults ON because behaviour is
  strictly additive — the watchdog never resets the circuit breaker,
  never restarts the sidecar, and never deletes files. Destructive
  recovery stays with the human-driven `recover.bat` flow. Options
  under `PalLLM:SelfHealing` (`Enabled` / `CheckIntervalSeconds` /
  `OrphanEnvelopeAgeSeconds` / `UnhealthyScoreFloor` /
  `HistoryRetention`). **4 new unit tests** pin: no-op tick still
  writes evidence, stale envelopes archived but fresh ones untouched,
  `OrphanEnvelopeAgeSeconds=0` disables sweep, evidence JSON matches
  the returned report.
- **Dashboard Quickstart panel** at the top of the Field Console,
  reading live from `GET /api/quickstart` and rendering the same
  state-aware critical / recommended / optional steps AI callers see,
  with priority-coloured cards (red / amber / blue / green) and a
  top-border that matches the overall status. New operators see
  exactly what to do next without reading any docs; the panel
  collapses to a quiet "ready" banner when nothing is pending. No new
  endpoints, no new polling loop — rides the existing dashboard
  refresh cadence. Section anchor added to the top-of-page nav.
- **Feature-catalog growth `69 -> 71`**: `self-healing-watchdog`,
  `dashboard-quickstart-panel`. Test count `316 -> 320`. Route count
  unchanged at 34. Docs resynced across README, ROADMAP, ARCHITECTURE,
  HANDOFF, API, and MCP-quickstart.

### Intuitive + lawyer-proof + streaming pass (2026-04-23)

Three additions to make PalLLM *even more* obvious to consume for humans and
AI agents, and to close the "is this truly local?" question for operators
before publication. All three are additive — nothing existing changed.

- **`GET /api/quickstart`** — live state-aware "do this next" guidance.
  Where `/api/describe` is the static manifest, `/api/quickstart` is
  dynamic: it reads the live `RuntimeHealth` + options and returns ordered
  critical / recommended / optional steps with `label / why / action /
  verify` fields on each. Both human operators and AI assistants can hit
  it once to know exactly what to do next (flip inference on? set an API
  key? enable warmup?) without scraping the opt-in matrix. Overall status
  is `ready` / `needs-setup` / `needs-attention`. Derived on every call
  so the answer always matches the runtime.
- **`GET /api/airgap/verify`** — classifies every enabled outbound
  surface (inference, vision, TTS, OTLP, MCP upstreams) as
  `loopback` / `private` / `public` / `disabled` / `unknown` so a
  compliance auditor, an operator, or an AI caller can prove PalLLM's
  air-gap posture in one machine-readable call. **Never opens a TCP
  connection or emits a single live request** — pure host-string
  inspection plus bounded DNS resolution. Overall verdict is one of
  `strict-airgapped` / `lan-airgapped` / `not-airgapped` /
  `indeterminate`. Answers "is this lawyer-proof local-only right now?"
  without a packet capture.
- **`POST /api/chat/stream`** — Server-Sent-Events variant of
  `/api/chat`. Emits `started` / `phase` / `final` (or `error`) events
  so web UIs and AI clients see progress before the final answer lands.
  The `final` event always carries the complete `ChatResponse` JSON, so
  callers that only care about the final answer can consume this endpoint
  too and ignore intermediate phases. Uses standard
  `event: <name>\ndata: <json>\n\n` framing that every browser
  `EventSource` and every MCP SSE client already speaks. Sets
  `X-Accel-Buffering: no` so Caddy / nginx pass through without
  buffering. Errors never leak implementation details: an `error` event
  carries a `retryable` flag + short `detail`. `/api/chat` itself stays
  unchanged for callers that prefer one-shot sync responses.
- **11 new tests** (`305 -> 316`): 2 for `/api/quickstart`, 8 for
  `AirGapVerifier` (disabled / loopback / localhost / RFC1918 / public /
  unresolvable / disabled-but-configured / mixed surfaces), 1 for
  `/api/chat/stream`. Existing readiness endpoint test pinned to the
  updated `34` route count; committed OpenAPI snapshot regenerated.
- **Feature-catalog growth `66 -> 69`**: `quickstart-guide-endpoint`,
  `air-gap-verifier`, `chat-stream-endpoint`. Route count `31 -> 34`;
  ARCHITECTURE / ROADMAP / README / HANDOFF / API / MCP-quickstart all
  resynced.

### One-click UX + robustness + AI-readability pass (2026-04-23)

Four cohesive additions as the "easy to use, easy to recover, easy for AI"
pass. All feature-flag-style opt-ins stay off by default; the additions
here affect only failure paths and an AI-consumer-facing surface.

- **Third-tier `EmergencyFallback` safety net** in
  [`src/PalLLM.Domain/Runtime/EmergencyFallback.cs`](src/PalLLM.Domain/Runtime/EmergencyFallback.cs).
  When the deterministic fallback director itself throws, returns
  `null`, or returns a whitespace-only message, the caller gets a
  rotating canned neutral acknowledgement instead of a crashed chat
  turn. Wrapped around every `_fallbackBehaviorEngine.Generate()` call
  site in `PalLlmRuntime.ChatAsync`. Tagged with
  `StrategyId="emergency-recovery"` so operators see the tier in the
  existing `palllm_fallback_strategy_total{strategy="..."}` metric with
  no new plumbing. Portable, zero dependencies beyond the BCL.
- **`GET /api/describe`** — one-shot self-description manifest for AI
  and MCP consumers. A single payload returns identity, operator
  happiness score + grade + top reasons, version, current state,
  surface counts, posture guarantees, common-ask shortcuts, and safety
  notes. Participates in the same strong-ETag private-cache protocol as
  `/api/features` so repeat callers cheap-revalidate with 304.
- **Operator happiness score** — single-number 0-100 health score
  (Excellent / Good / Degraded / Critical) rolled up from
  `RuntimeHealth`, with up to three top subtraction reasons for
  dashboards. Inference-side penalties only apply when inference is
  configured, so fallback-only operators never see a lower score just
  for not flipping inference on. Surfaced under
  `SelfDescription.OperatorHealth` on `/api/describe`.
- **`recover.bat` / `scripts/recover-palllm.ps1`** — fourth one-click
  verb next to `install.bat` / `play.bat` / `support.bat`. Stops any
  running sidecar, archives stuck bridge envelopes to a timestamped
  `Bridge/RecoveryArchive/<UTC>/` folder (nothing is lost), prunes
  durable evidence older than `-RetainEvidenceDays` (default 14),
  restarts the sidecar, and reports the operator happiness score.
  Every step is best-effort so idempotent re-runs are safe. Shipped in
  the release zip via `scripts/package-release.ps1`.
- **18 new tests** (`287 -> 305`): 9 for EmergencyFallback, 2 for
  `/api/describe` + 304 revalidation, 7 for the happiness scorer.
- **Feature-catalog growth `62 -> 66`**: `emergency-fallback-tier`,
  `self-description-endpoint`, `operator-happiness-score`, `recover-bat`.
  Route count `30 -> 31`; ARCHITECTURE, ROADMAP, README, HANDOFF, API,
  and MCP-quickstart all resynced. Committed OpenAPI snapshot
  regenerated to match the live route set.

### Opt-in GPU thermal gate (2026-04-23)

- **New `ThermalGate` in `PalLLM.Domain.Runtime`.** Opt-in, best-effort
  GPU-temperature gate that routes live inference to the deterministic
  fallback director when the primary GPU is already throttling, so
  player-visible chat latency stays predictable under thermal pressure
  instead of absorbing the throttled round-trip time. Sampler uses
  `nvidia-smi` when on PATH and falls back to permissive when no sensor
  is reachable, so the gate is always safe to leave enabled.
- **Configured under `PalLLM:Inference:ThermalGate`** with `Enabled`
  (default `false`), `RejectAboveC` (default `83.0`), `WarnAboveC`
  (default `78.0`), and `CacheTtlSeconds` (default `5`). The opt-in
  defaults-off posture matches every other PalLLM subsystem that can
  change behaviour.
- **Wired into `HttpJsonInferenceClient`** right next to the existing
  circuit-breaker check — rejecting decisions short-circuit to a
  structured `InferenceResult.Failed(...)` tagged `ErrorType=thermal_gated`
  with `LatencyMs=0`, so downstream telemetry can count gated turns
  distinctly from circuit-open turns.
- **Portable + harvestable.** Lives in a single file in `PalLLM.Domain/Runtime/`,
  depends only on the BCL, and is free to copy-paste into other local-first
  LLM companion runtimes alongside `SemanticEmbedder` and `ResponseCleanup`.
  No NVML / pynvml / vendor-SDK dependency is taken. The
  `PALLLM_FAKE_GPU_TEMP_C` env var lets tests and operators simulate a
  hot GPU on machines without real sensors.
- **11 new tests** covering: sensor unavailable -> permissive,
  under warn -> allow, between warn & reject -> warn,
  at/above reject -> reject, cache TTL reuses, cache TTL expiry
  resamples, sensor exception -> unavailable, `InvalidateCache` forces
  resample, env-override honored, plus two InferenceClient integration
  tests that confirm rejected decisions skip the HTTP round-trip and
  that an allowing gate lets the normal path through.
- `TUNING.md`, `OPERATIONS.md` opt-in matrix, README badges +
  "ready/scaffolded/deferred" counts, ARCHITECTURE feature snapshot,
  HANDOFF handoff packet, and the MCP-quickstart feature blurb all
  updated to reflect the new `62`/`59 ready` catalog numbers and
  `287` passing tests. Feature-catalog addition: `thermal-gate`.
- Origin note: the pattern was identified in a sibling
  an external sibling-project tree during the
  cross-project audit and re-implemented from scratch in C# against
  PalLLM's shape — the C# surface is independently authored, depends
  only on the BCL, and is MIT-licensed under this repo.

### Inference budget readiness surface + contract resync (2026-04-23)

- `GET /api/inference/performance` now carries a machine-readable
  `Assessment` for the overall recent window and each provider/model lane,
  including status, budget name, success ratio, target-hit ratio, ceiling-hit
  ratio, and the built-in chat (`3.0 s` / `8.0 s`) and vision (`2.5 s` /
  `6.0 s`) latency budgets.
- The Field Console now surfaces that readiness posture directly in the
  runtime ribbon, overview, and per-lane cards so operators can tell at a
  glance whether the active recent window is healthy, degraded, critical, or
  still gathering enough evidence.
- `GET /api/dashboard` now measures server assembly latency after collecting
  the inference-performance snapshot, fixing an undercount where the dashboard
  timing previously excluded part of its own payload construction.
- API, tuning, operations, and release-facing docs were resynced to the live
  contract so the published cache semantics and `InferencePerformanceSnapshot`
  shape match the implementation again.

### Release-readiness support-bundle evidence (2026-04-23)

- `GET /api/release/readiness` now exposes `SupportBundleEvidence` sourced from
  `Runtime/SupportEvidence/latest-support-bundle.json` plus `.zip`, so the
  API-level release surface can report whether a portable support archive
  exists, whether it is fresh enough to trust, and which proof/package/audit
  artifacts it captured.
- Added `ReleaseSupportBundleEvidenceBuilder` and expanded the release
  readiness `ETag` fingerprint so support-bundle captures invalidate cached
  readiness responses immediately instead of waiting on a server cache window.
- `ReleaseReadinessBuilder` now advertises `scripts/export-support-bundle.ps1`
  as a first-class hardening/support command and recommends a support-bundle
  refresh once smoke/native-proof/proof-bundle/package/full-audit evidence are
  already current.
- Endpoint coverage now verifies missing, fresh, stale, and refresh-invalidates
  behavior for `SupportBundleEvidence`, and the release/readiness docs were
  resynced to match the expanded contract.

### Claude handoff packet + doc hygiene sweep (2026-04-23)

- Added [`docs/HANDOFF.md`](docs/HANDOFF.md) as the temporary coding handoff
  packet, with the current audited state, real remaining blockers, next
  recommended coding pass, and the high-signal files and verification commands
  a replacement agent should use first.
- `README.md`, `docs/INDEX.md`, and `docs/IMPLEMENTATION_QUEUE.md` now point to
  that handoff doc so the next session can resume without re-deriving the whole
  repo state from scratch.
- Refreshed stale `Last audited` stamps across the remaining top-level docs
  reviewed in this pass.
- Repaired mojibake in `docs/MCP_QUICKSTART.md` and `docs/OPERATIONS.md` so the
  Claude Desktop and MCP guidance stays readable in release-facing surfaces.

### One-click launcher warm-lane priming (2026-04-22)

- `scripts/play-palllm.ps1` now issues a best-effort
  `POST /api/inference/warmup` after the sidecar is healthy and doctor has
  passed, so the packaged player path hides first-turn cold-start latency when
  live inference is enabled without turning warmup into a launch blocker.
- The same launcher now persists
  `Runtime/LaunchEvidence/latest-player-launch.json` plus `.md`, alongside
  timestamped history files, capturing the install target, sidecar status,
  doctor/warmup outcome, and latest bridge-proof/release-readiness posture
  from the most recent packaged launch.
- Added `scripts/export-support-bundle.ps1` plus package-root `support.bat`.
  They capture the latest launch evidence, health snapshot, bridge proof, and
  release-readiness artifacts into `Runtime/SupportEvidence/latest-support-bundle.zip`
  plus `.json`, giving packaged installs a one-click support/export path.
- `scripts/doctor.ps1` now reports `Inference warm lane` separately from
  generic sidecar health, so operators can distinguish "the sidecar is up" from
  "the active live-inference lane is actually warm."
- README, quickstart, operations, release notes, and the feature catalog were
  resynced to reflect the updated one-click behavior.

### Recent inference-window surface + dashboard/doc sync (2026-04-22)

- **PalLLM now publishes a bounded recent inference-performance surface.**
  `GET /api/inference/performance` returns a `15` minute
  `InferencePerformanceSnapshot` grouped by provider/model lane, with
  success/failure counts, average latency, p95 latency, token totals, and the
  latest error marker for live chat and vision work.
- **The Field Console now surfaces live lane health instead of making operators
  infer it from raw metrics.** The runtime dashboard gained an `Inference
  Lanes` section backed by the same snapshot, and the runtime summary text now
  exposes the current recent-window success rate directly.
- **Conditional reads now cover the new performance surface.** The endpoint
  participates in the same strong-`ETag` / private-cache revalidation contract
  as the feature catalog and proof surfaces, without fingerprint drift from the
  generated-at timestamp.
- **Docs and regression coverage were resynced.** README, API, architecture,
  operations, tuning, roadmap, and index docs now describe the new surface and
  current audited counts correctly; audited test count moved `265 -> 269`.

### One-click player launch + packaged sidecar fallback hardening (2026-04-22)

- Added `play.bat` plus `scripts/play-palllm.ps1` as the primary packaged
  player-launch flow. The new entry path installs or refreshes the mod, starts
  or reuses the sidecar, runs doctor, opens the dashboard, and launches
  Palworld from one command while keeping the lower-level scripts available for
  support.
- Hardened packaged sidecar fallbacks so `scripts/start-sidecar.ps1` and
  `scripts/doctor.ps1` now treat a self-contained `sidecar\publish\PalLLM.Sidecar.exe`
  as a first-class packaged runtime before falling back to the packaged DLL +
  `dotnet` or the repo project.
- Updated release packaging so `play.bat` and `scripts/play-palllm.ps1` ship in
  the zip, `PLAYER_README.txt` advertises the one-click flow, and the release
  docs plus roadmap now reflect the new primary launch path.
- Flipped `scripts/package-release.ps1` to player-first defaults: release zips
  now bundle a self-contained sidecar by default, while
  `-SkipSidecarPublish` and `-FrameworkDependentSidecar` remain available for
  lean or internal packaging lanes.
- Honest roadmap update: the official player-experience-weighted completion
  moved from `75.5%` to `76.2%` because the packaged player flow is now much
  closer to true one-click operation, even though live native HUD/audio/action
  proof still blocks 100%.

### Durable full-audit evidence + release-readiness sync (2026-04-22)

- **`scripts/run_full_audit.ps1` now leaves behind a durable latest-full-audit artifact.**
  Every full-audit pass now writes
  `Runtime/ReleaseEvidence/latest-full-audit.json` plus a timestamped history
  artifact, carrying the overall pass/fail state, enabled audit lanes,
  step counts, blockers, ready evidence, and pointers back to the
  timestamped `artifacts/full-audit/<stamp>/` bundle.
- **Release-readiness now exposes full source-tree audit truth, not just runtime proof.**
  `ReleaseReadinessSnapshot` gained `FullAuditEvidence`, backed by the new
  durable artifact, and the next recommended release pass now tells operators
  to rerun the full audit when build/test/drift/package truth is missing,
  stale, or failing even if smoke/native proof already exists.
- **The proof bundle now carries the latest full-audit artifact when present.**
  `scripts/export-release-proof-bundle.ps1` now copies
  `latest-full-audit.json` into the bundle alongside smoke/native proof when
  it is available, so one archive can travel with both source and runtime
  validation state.

### OTLP GenAI telemetry + doc/runtime resync (2026-04-22)

- **Inference and vision now emit standard GenAI client telemetry when OTLP is enabled.**
  The live model lanes now create `CLIENT` spans tagged with current
  `gen_ai.*` attributes, and PalLLM exports the matching
  `gen_ai.client.operation.duration` and `gen_ai.client.token.usage`
  histograms through the same opt-in OTLP pipeline that already carried
  traces and logs.
- **The streamed chat-completions reader now keeps response metadata instead of
  throwing it away.** Parsed response model ids, completion ids, finish
  reasons, and token usage now feed both telemetry and future runtime audits
  without buffering a second copy of the response body.
- **Docs and regression coverage were resynced.** README and operations/tuning
  docs now describe the OTLP metrics posture accurately, and the audited test
  count moved `263 -> 265`.

### Full audit now verifies a candidate release package (2026-04-22)

- **`scripts/run_full_audit.ps1` now checks the release zip, not just the repo.**
  The full audit now calls `scripts/package-release.ps1` by default, which in
  turn validates the candidate artifact against `RELEASE_PACKAGE_MANIFEST.json`
  and refreshes `latest-package-verification.json`.
- **Docs were tightened around the stricter audit contract.**
  Release and operations docs now explain that the default full-audit lane
  covers candidate-package verification too, and how to opt out with
  `-SkipPackaging` when you only want a fast source/doc pass.
- **Release-facing docs were cleaned up.**
  Fixed stale heading links and Diataxis wording drift in README/QUICKSTART/API
  so the publication surface stays easier to navigate and harvest.

### Release package manifest + verification evidence (2026-04-22)

- **Candidate packages are now manifest-backed and structurally verifiable.**
  `scripts/package-release.ps1` now writes `RELEASE_PACKAGE_MANIFEST.json`
  into the package root, ships the native-proof / proof-bundle / HUD helper
  scripts players and operators actually need, and can verify the finished zip
  immediately after packaging.
- **Release-readiness now exposes concrete package verification, not just
  runtime proof.** `ReleaseReadinessSnapshot` gained
  `PackageVerificationEvidence`, backed by
  `Runtime/ReleaseEvidence/latest-package-verification.json`, and
  `scripts/doctor.ps1` now reports the latest package-verification artifact
  alongside smoke/native-proof/proof-bundle evidence.
- **Docs, committed OpenAPI, and regression coverage were resynced.**
  Added release-readiness endpoint coverage for the new package-verification
  block, regenerated `docs/openapi/palllm-sidecar-v1.json`, and moved the
  audited passing test count `262 -> 263`.

### Snapshot ingest normalization closes the smoke-path crash (2026-04-22)

- **Partial bridge snapshots no longer crash the sidecar.**
  `SnapshotCloneExtensions` now normalizes `null` world collections, character
  collections, dictionaries, and missing `Position` objects while deep-cloning
  the published `GameWorldSnapshot` contract, so `POST /api/snapshot` accepts
  lean bridge payloads instead of throwing `ArgumentNullException`.
- **The exact runtime-only smoke failure is now pinned in tests.**
  Added runtime and HTTP regression coverage for `null` snapshot collections so
  the synthetic smoke loop and real Palworld bridge posts stay aligned.
- **Docs were resynced and the audited test count moved `259 -> 262`.**

### Release evidence freshness + doc-count resync (2026-04-22)

- **Release-readiness now distinguishes fresh proof from stale proof.**
  `SmokeEvidence`, `NativeProofEvidence`, and `ProofBundleEvidence` now carry
  freshness metadata, and `/api/release/readiness` recommends rerunning the
  relevant capture/export script when proof exists but has aged beyond the
  configured freshness window.
- **Release proof stays machine-friendly instead of timestamp-guessy.**
  The freshness contract is stable enough for ETag revalidation because it
  exposes `FreshnessStatus` plus `FreshUntilUtc`, instead of emitting a
  constantly changing relative age on every request.
- **Release docs were resynced to the current audited test count.**
  `README.md`, `docs/ROADMAP.md`, and `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` now reflect
  `262` passing tests.

### Provider-aware inference residency control + OpenAPI doc sync (2026-04-22)

- **Warmup now uses provider-aware residency controls for compatible local
  runtimes instead of only issuing a generic chat-completions ping.**
  `InferenceOptions` gained `ResidencyProvider` and `ResidencyTtlSeconds`,
  `HttpJsonInferenceClient` now resolves the active host into `none`,
  `ollama`, or `lmstudio`, and bounded warmup can route through an
  Ollama-native `/api/chat` preload with `keep_alive` while LM
  Studio-compatible chat-completions requests can carry `ttl`.
- **The warmup snapshot exposes what the runtime actually did.**
  `InferenceWarmupSnapshot` gained `ResidencyProvider`,
  `ResidencyTtlSeconds`, `WarmupTransport`, and
  `LastWarmupUsedResidencyHint`, and `PalLlmRuntime` now reports whether the
  last warmup used generic chat-completions or the native Ollama preload path.
- **Docs, committed OpenAPI, and regression coverage were resynced.**
  Added focused inference-client coverage for LM Studio `ttl`, provider
  disablement, and Ollama native warmup, regenerated
  `docs/openapi/palllm-sidecar-v1.json`, and moved the audited test count
  `255 -> 258`.

### Release proof bundle export + readiness sync (2026-04-22)

- **PalLLM now packages release evidence into one durable proof bundle.**
  `scripts/export-release-proof-bundle.ps1` captures the current
  `/api/release/readiness`, `/api/bridge/proof`, and `/api/health` snapshots,
  copies the latest smoke/native-proof artifacts plus HUD config when present,
  and writes `Runtime/ReleaseEvidence/latest-proof-bundle.json` plus
  `latest-proof-bundle.zip` with timestamped history artifacts.
- **The release surface now reports the latest proof bundle explicitly.**
  `ReleaseReadinessSnapshot` gained `ProofBundleEvidence`, backed by the new
  proof-bundle manifest/archive, and `/api/release/readiness` now uses that
  state to recommend `scripts/export-release-proof-bundle.ps1` only after the
  native-proof and smoke prerequisites are already satisfied.
- **Operator tooling and regression coverage were updated together.**
  `scripts/doctor.ps1` now reports the latest proof-bundle artifact separately,
  endpoint coverage was extended for the new evidence shape and ETag refresh
  path, and the audited passing test count moved `258 -> 259`.

### Live native-proof artifacts + release-readiness sync (2026-04-22)

- **PalLLM now distinguishes synthetic smoke from real Palworld-native proof
  in its release surface.** `ReleaseReadinessSnapshot` gained
  `NativeProofEvidence`, backed by `Runtime/ReleaseEvidence/latest-native-proof.json`,
  so `/api/release/readiness` can now say whether a real in-game HUD delivery
  proof has been captured instead of only whether the synthetic sidecar smoke
  loop passed.
- **Operators now have a dedicated live-proof helper.**
  `scripts/run-native-proof.ps1` polls `/api/bridge/proof`, can optionally
  apply the current HUD-bind recommendation first, and persists a timestamped
  native-proof artifact once the live bridge reaches `delivery_proven`.
- **Doctor now reports the latest persisted native proof separately from
  runtime health and synthetic smoke.** That keeps "the sidecar is fine" from
  being confused with "the Palworld-native HUD path was actually proven on the
  current build."

### Native HUD override workflow + bridge-side config proof (2026-04-22)

- **The Palworld bridge can now load native HUD overrides from a dedicated
  config file instead of forcing source edits in `main.lua`.** The UE4SS mod
  now looks for `config/native-hud.lua` beside the installed mod first, then
  falls back to a runtime-root override path, and keeps inline defaults as the
  final safety net.
- **Bridge proof now exposes which native-hud config source/path was actually
  active.** `bridge_boot`, `/api/health`, `/api/bridge/proof`, and durable
  smoke artifacts now report the live config source/path so HUD-bind proof is
  about the real Palworld configuration, not guesswork.
- **Operators now have a one-command HUD bind apply step.**
  `scripts/apply-hud-bind-recommendation.ps1` exports the ranked HUD target
  recommendation into `config/native-hud.lua` using ASCII output, which avoids
  Lua/BOM friction on Windows PowerShell and removes a manual copy-edit step
  from the native-HUD workflow.

### Warmup keepalive suppression for active lanes (2026-04-22)

- **Periodic inference keepalives now get out of the way when real traffic has
  already kept the lane warm.** `PalLlmRuntime` records the latest successful
  live inference hit per active model and skips `periodic_keepalive` warmup
  POSTs when that same model was just exercised inside the configured
  keepalive window.
- **The warmup snapshot exposes why a keepalive was skipped.**
  `InferenceWarmupSnapshot` now carries `LastLiveInferenceAtUtc` and
  `LastLiveInferenceModel`, so `/api/health`, `/health/ready`,
  `POST /api/inference/warmup`, and the MCP active-tier surfaces can explain
  whether the lane stayed warm from background warmup or from real player
  turns.
- **Regression coverage and docs moved with the runtime change.** Added tests
  covering both the skip path and the "model changed, so warm anyway" path,
  and updated the API/operations/architecture/tuning docs to describe the new
  behavior.

### Streamed inference + vision response parsing (2026-04-22)

- **Successful inference and vision calls no longer pay an avoidable full-body
  string allocation before JSON parsing.** `HttpJsonInferenceClient` and
  `HttpVisionClient` now send requests with `HttpCompletionOption.ResponseHeadersRead`
  and parse the JSON directly from the response stream on the success path,
  which follows current .NET guidance for lower-allocation HTTP reads.
- **Current chat-completions response shapes are accepted more robustly.**
  PalLLM now accepts both plain `message.content` strings and array-shaped text
  parts in successful inference/vision replies, which keeps newer
  OpenAI-compatible servers from tripping the fallback path just because they
  return segmented content.
- **Docs and regression coverage moved with the code.** Added direct coverage
  for array-shaped inference and vision responses, and the audited test count
  moved `249 -> 251`.

### Durable smoke evidence + fresh proof revalidation (2026-04-22)

- **PalLLM now persists release smoke evidence instead of treating smoke as
  a one-shot console check.** `scripts/run-sidecar-smoke.ps1` now writes
  `Runtime/ReleaseEvidence/latest-smoke.json` plus a timestamped history
  artifact under `Runtime/ReleaseEvidence/History`.
- **`GET /api/release/readiness` now carries that durable proof directly.**
  The `ReleaseReadinessSnapshot` contract gained `SmokeEvidence`, and the
  release surface now advertises the smoke-capture command alongside the
  existing audit commands.
- **Proof endpoints now revalidate correctly without stale server-side cache
  state.** `/api/bridge/proof` and `/api/release/readiness` keep strong
  `ETag` support, but their fingerprints now ignore volatile generation
  timestamps so clients can get `304 Not Modified` when the underlying proof
  has not changed and still see fresh smoke artifacts immediately after a new
  capture.
- **Regression coverage and docs were resynced.** Audited test count moved
  `247 -> 249`, and the README, API, architecture, operations, roadmap, and
  release guide now document the durable smoke-evidence path.

### HUD bind recommendation proof (2026-04-22)

- **PalLLM now turns raw `ui_probe` evidence into an actionable HUD-bind
  recommendation.** `RuntimeHealth.NativeReadiness` now carries
  `ConfiguredHudTargets` plus a `HudBindRecommendation` block with a compact
  status, exact recommended target, ranked shortlist, and suggested next
  steps.
- **The UE4SS bridge heartbeat now reports the real configured HUD target
  names.** `bridge_boot` no longer only reports a target count; it also emits
  `NativeHudWidgetTargets`, which lets the sidecar detect when the live
  Palworld config has drifted away from the top-ranked `ui_probe` candidate.
- **Operator tooling now surfaces the recommendation directly.**
  `scripts/doctor.ps1` gained a dedicated `HUD target recommendation` check,
  and `/api/bridge/proof` now includes enough data to answer "what widget
  should I bind next?" without manual comparison.
- **Regression coverage and docs were resynced.** Audited test count moved
  `246 -> 247`, and the API/operations/architecture docs now describe the HUD
  recommendation contract and the bridge-reported target list.

### Bridge proof endpoint + inventory sync (2026-04-22)

- **PalLLM now publishes a dedicated machine-readable bridge proof surface.**
  `GET /api/bridge/proof` returns the live native-readiness state, widget-seam
  evidence, and request/outbox/delivery/action loop proof in one ETagged
  snapshot instead of forcing operators to reconstruct that truth from
  `/api/health`, `ui_probe`, and recent bridge events.
- **The audit also corrected the live runtime inventory.** This pass exposed a
  pre-existing but undocumented `POST /api/inference/warmup` route and the
  missing `InferenceWarmupWorker` implementation behind the service
  registration, then wired the active-tier MCP payloads to include the warmup
  snapshot.
- **Docs, OpenAPI, and regression coverage were resynced to the real shipped
  surface.** Audited counts moved `243 -> 246` passing tests, `57 -> 58`
  feature-catalog entries, and `27 -> 29` `/api` routes after adding
  `/api/bridge/proof` and correcting the previously under-documented warmup
  route.

### Bridge loop proof + smoke closure (2026-04-22)

- **PalLLM now distinguishes "sidecar responded" from "Palworld actually
  rendered the turn."** `RuntimeHealth` and `BridgeActivitySnapshot` gained a
  machine-readable `BridgeLoop` / `LoopProof` snapshot that tracks the latest
  request ingress, outbox write, `reply_delivery`, and matching action
  feedback.
- **The UE4SS bridge now emits explicit delivery proof.**
  `mod/ue4ss/Mods/PalLLM/Scripts/main.lua` annotates rendered cards with the
  request metadata and writes `reply_delivery` events when a card actually
  renders, including the chosen surface and whether rendering was suppressed.
- **Operator tooling now closes and checks the synthetic loop.**
  `scripts/run-sidecar-smoke.ps1` injects synthetic delivery plus matching
  feedback and now requires `BridgeLoop.LoopClosed` before passing, while
  `scripts/doctor.ps1` reports reply-delivery proof separately from native seam
  readiness.
- **Docs and regression coverage moved with the runtime.**
  `/api/health` endpoint coverage now includes the closed-loop JSON contract,
  the committed OpenAPI snapshot includes the new bridge-proof schemas, and the
  audited counts moved `241 -> 243` tests and `56 -> 57` feature-catalog
  entries.

### Palworld-scope collaboration prune (2026-04-22)

- **The model-collaboration surfaces were pruned back to PalLLM's real scope.**
  `docs/MODEL_COLLABORATION.md`, `docs/API.md`, the MCP collaboration prompt
  and tools, and the live planner strings now describe Palworld-mod work only:
  runtime, bridge, HUD, screenshot, docs-sync, and release-hardening tasks.
- **Generic "AI studio" drift was removed without changing the actual runtime
  execution behavior.** The collaboration APIs still emit the same kind of
  hardware-aware fast-lane versus dense-lane plan, but their task labels,
  validators, and examples now stay grounded in PalLLM's bridge, HUD, and
  release workflows instead of asset/video/product-style framing.

### Per-lane prompt budget enforcement (2026-04-22)

- **Role-aware execution profiles now enforce Palworld-specific prompt and
  evidence budgets in the live chat path.** Fast lanes keep tighter caps on
  known bases, nearby hostiles, recent events, character detail, memory
  snippets, visual-context length, and total prompt size; deliberate lanes
  keep larger review windows.
- **Vision augmentation now follows the same lane budget.** Chat-time image
  summaries use the execution profile's chat-vision token budget, and the
  resulting visual context is trimmed to the lane's prompt budget before it is
  spliced into the system prompt.
- **Docs moved with the runtime.** README, API, tuning, architecture, and the
  feature catalog now describe prompt/evidence budgets as a real runtime
  behavior instead of a planning note.

### Neutral public snapshot schemas + richer OpenAPI docs (2026-04-22)

- **The publication-facing world-state contract no longer leaks the current
  bridge target's CLR type names.** The .NET 10 OpenAPI pipeline now maps the
  bridge-owned snapshot family to neutral component ids:
  `GameWorldSnapshot`, `GameBaseSnapshot`, and `GameCharacterSnapshot`.
- **OpenAPI generation now consumes XML docs from the shared domain project.**
  Both projects emit XML documentation files, and the sidecar feeds the domain
  assembly's XML into the built-in OpenAPI generator so DTO summaries travel
  into the committed spec instead of living only in source comments.
- **MCP and human docs were reworded to match the neutral contract.**
  The MCP world/character resource descriptions, `README.md`, `docs/API.md`,
  `docs/CORE_LIBRARY.md`, and `docs/RELEASE.md` now describe the snapshot
  family as a generic game-world contract while still being explicit that the
  live shipped bridge remains Palworld-specific internally.
- **Regression coverage now pins the neutral schema seam.**
  Endpoint tests verify that `/openapi/v1.json` exposes the neutral schema ids
  and that `/api/snapshot` references `#/components/schemas/GameWorldSnapshot`
  instead of the old target-specific component names.

### Release-readiness surface + static-asset fast path (2026-04-22)

- **PalLLM now publishes a machine-readable release posture.**
  `GET /api/release/readiness` returns the live route counts, featured
  operational surfaces, feature-catalog summary, canonical audit commands,
  canonical doc pointers, and current publication blockers so release
  automation does not have to scrape markdown.
- **The release/readiness surface follows the existing conditional-read
  contract.** The endpoint emits a strong `ETag`, private cache headers, and
  `304 Not Modified` responses on matching `If-None-Match`, using the same
  cache TTL as the feature catalog when output caching is enabled.
- **Static dashboard assets now use the endpoint-routed ASP.NET Core fast
  path.** `src/PalLLM.Sidecar/Program.cs` switched from `UseStaticFiles()` to
  `MapStaticAssets().ShortCircuit()`, which matches current Microsoft
  guidance for skipping the rest of the middleware pipeline after a static
  asset match.
- **The new release surface is wired into the contract and audit layers.**
  New release-readiness DTOs were added to the shared contracts and JSON
  source-generation context, endpoint tests now pin both the `200 OK` and
  `304 Not Modified` flows, and `scripts/run_full_audit.ps1` now recognises
  `MapStaticAssets().ShortCircuit()` when validating operational route
  counts.
- **Docs moved with the runtime.** `README.md`, `docs/API.md`,
  `docs/INDEX.md`, and `docs/RELEASE.md` now point at the release/readiness
  surface. Audited route count moved `26 -> 27` and audited test count moved
  `236 -> 238`.

### Roadmap audit + foundation-first queue sync (2026-04-22)

- **Roadmap and implementation queue were reconciled against the live code.**
  `docs/ROADMAP.md` now clearly separates what is implemented, what is merely
  scaffolded/default-off, and what is still missing for a real `100%` ship
  state.
- **The official project position stays `75.5%`, but the remaining work is now
  ordered by dependency safety instead of feature flash.**
  `docs/IMPLEMENTATION_QUEUE.md` now starts with compatibility proof and real
  Palworld smoke, then bridge truth and seam confirmation, then native
  delivery, native audio, guarded native actions, and finally clean-machine
  release proof.
- **Cross-doc navigation was cleaned up.** `docs/OPERATIONS.md` no longer points
  TTS readers at the stale old queue numbering.

### Native-readiness health audit path (2026-04-22)

- **`bridge_boot` now carries machine-readable native-readiness metadata.**
  The UE4SS bridge heartbeat includes structured compat signals plus the live
  bridge-side config gates for `ui_probe`, native HUD delivery, native action
  execution, production sampling, and native waypoint markers.
- **`GET /api/health` now distinguishes sidecar health from Palworld-native
  readiness.** `RuntimeHealth.NativeReadiness` records whether a real bridge
  boot has been seen, whether the HUD seam is genuinely bind-ready, and whether
  production / waypoint native paths are only configured versus actually proven
  on the running build.
- **`scripts/doctor.ps1` now reports the same distinction.** A healthy sidecar
  no longer implies the in-game seams are confirmed; doctor now emits separate
  PASS/WARN checks for bridge boot, HUD seam, production sampler, waypoint
  marker readiness, and the action-executor gate.

### Role-aware execution profiles + speech voice routing (2026-04-22)

- **`/api/chat` now applies a per-turn execution planner instead of treating
  the global inference settings as the whole policy.**
  `src/PalLLM.Domain/Inference/InferenceExecutionPlanner.cs` classifies the
  active model lane and the task/request shape into shipped profiles
  (`fast-reactive`, `fast-interactive`, `fast-deliberate`,
  `fast-creative`, `dense-interactive`, `dense-deliberate`,
  `dense-creative`), then chooses temperature, max-token budget, `top_p`,
  presence penalty, thinking mode, and whether live vision augmentation is
  allowed for that turn.
- **The live inference client now accepts per-request sampling and thinking
  overrides.** `InferencePrompt` carries `TopP`, `PresencePenalty`, and
  `EnableThinking`, and `HttpJsonInferenceClient` prefers those values over
  the global defaults when it shapes the outgoing chat-completions payload.
  Qwen-family lanes now also forward `preserve_thinking`, using
  `chat_template_kwargs` on generic OpenAI-compatible servers and root-level
  fields on DashScope-style hosts, which matches the current Qwen3.6 docs.
- **`ChatResponse` now reports how a turn was actually routed.** The response
  contract gained `InferenceModel`, `InferenceProfileId`, `InferenceLane`,
  `ThinkingRequested`, and `VisualContextSource`, giving dashboards, replay
  tools, and external clients the same per-turn execution truth the runtime
  already uses internally.
- **Role-aware speech voice routing now exists for normal chat replies.**
  `TtsOptions` gained optional `WarmVoice`, `SteadyVoice`, `UrgentVoice`, and
  `WhisperVoice` fields, and `PalLlmRuntime` now chooses among them from the
  presentation audio cue before falling back to `DefaultVoice`.
- **Docs and tests moved with the code.** The API and tuning references now
  describe the execution-profile bridge and the optional role-voice map, and
  the audited test count moved `230 -> 236`.

### Memory-store hot-path trim + doc sync (2026-04-22)

- **Conversation memory reads now stay off the full-sort path.**
  `src/PalLLM.Domain/Memory/ConversationMemoryStore.cs` no longer snapshots
  and re-sorts the full store for every `Recall`, `GetRecent`, and
  `AccumulatedImportance` read. Ranked recall now copies the bounded store
  into a pooled snapshot and keeps only the fixed top-N matches, while recent
  and importance windows scan the in-memory list in reverse chronological
  order under the existing lock discipline.
- **The optimization follows current .NET allocation guidance.**
  Temporary recall snapshots now use `ArrayPool<T>` and the rent/return stays
  within the same method, which cuts repeated short-lived array churn on the
  hot chat-memory path without leaking pooled references across call sites.
- **Focused regression coverage was added at the store layer.** New
  `ConversationMemoryStoreTests` pin recent-entry ordering, importance-window
  summation, and top-N recall semantics directly instead of relying only on
  higher-level runtime tests.
- **Docs were re-synced where they had drifted from code.**
  `README.md`, `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `docs/OPERATIONS.md`,
  `docs/TUNING.md`, and `docs/ROADMAP.md` now reflect the new `227` passing
  tests, the fact that `EnsureDirectories()` does not auto-create `ModelsDir`,
  and the fact that reflection consolidation is deterministic rather than
  model-backed.

### Hardware-aware model collaboration planner (2026-04-22)

- **PalLLM now ships a machine-readable local-model collaboration planner.**
  New `src/PalLLM.Domain/Inference/ModelCollaborationPlanner.cs` classifies
  configured model lanes into fast-iterative vs deliberate roles, emits staged
  recipes such as `fast-draft-dense-judge` and `watchdog-and-repair`, and
  tailors the plan to hardware hints instead of assuming one model fits every
  machine tier.
- **The collaboration plan is exposed on every control plane PalLLM already
  ships.** New `GET /api/inference/collaboration` snapshot route and
  `POST /api/inference/collaboration/plan` task-planning route on the REST
  side, plus the `pal_model_collaboration` and
  `pal_plan_model_collaboration_task` MCP tools and the
  `palllm://model/collaboration` resource on the MCP side.
- **The collaboration contract is now more actionable for real local stacks.**
  The snapshot now includes per-lane authority boundaries, risk-aware routing
  policies, a deterministic qualification suite for fresh quants, a
  machine-readable hardware playbook, and a new MCP prompt template
  `palllm_model_collaboration_orchestrator` that turns the live snapshot into a
  JSON-returning duo-orchestration scaffold.
- **This pass explicitly codifies the dense-plus-MoE local stack pattern.**
  The planner is generic, but the shipped heuristics now recognize the current
  Qwen Duo shape: a fast sparse-MoE worker lane and a slower dense reviewer
  lane.
- **The docs now include an operator-facing strategy guide.** New
  `docs/MODEL_COLLABORATION.md` captures the Qwen Duo Mesh, hardware tiers,
  self-healing loops, and the 2035 direction of model committees rather than
  single-model routing.
- **Counts moved again in lockstep with the code.** The audited surface is now
  `26` `/api` routes, `11` MCP tools, `6` direct MCP resources + `1` template,
  `4` MCP prompts, `56` feature-catalog entries, and `230` passing tests.

### OpenAPI snapshot gate + sidecar build repairs (2026-04-22)

- **The HTTP contract is now pinned as a committed artifact, not just a live
  endpoint.** `src/PalLLM.Sidecar/PalLLM.Sidecar.csproj` now carries
  `Microsoft.Extensions.ApiDescription.Server` and a stable build-time output
  name for the `v1` document. The checked-in snapshot lives at
  `docs/openapi/palllm-sidecar-v1.json`.
- **Snapshot export and verification are first-class tooling.**
  `scripts/export-openapi.ps1` regenerates the committed OpenAPI file or fails
  on drift with `-Verify`. It runs the build-time generator in a dedicated
  `OpenApiBuild` environment so hosted background workers stay off while the
  document is produced.
- **CI and the local full-audit pipeline now enforce the snapshot.**
  `.github/workflows/ci.yml` and `scripts/run_full_audit.ps1` both call
  `scripts/export-openapi.ps1 -Verify`, turning route-surface drift into a
  hard failure before publication.
- **Runtime-vs-snapshot drift is also covered in tests.** New
  `OpenApiSnapshotTests.OpenApiEndpoint_MatchesCommittedSnapshot` compares the
  live `/openapi/v1.json` output with the committed snapshot after canonical
  JSON normalization.
- **This pass also repaired several latent build blockers that the new contract
  tooling surfaced.** The sidecar serializer context now imports
  `PalLLM.Domain.Runtime` correctly, the MCP active-tier payload builders now
  use valid named constructor arguments, and the health payload mapper no
  longer emits the nullability warning that had broken the clean release build.
- **Docs synced to the new contract posture.** `README.md`, `docs/API.md`,
  `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `docs/OPERATIONS.md`, `docs/RELEASE.md`, and
  `CONTRIBUTING.md` now point at the committed snapshot and the regeneration
  command. Audited test count moved `215 -> 216`.

### Publication hardening + release audits (2026-04-22)

- **Release-surface guardrails are now codified.** New
  `scripts/audit_public_copy.ps1` and `scripts/public_copy_policy.ps1`
  define a publication-facing doc set, fail on unnecessary third-party
  client/model branding there, and verify that `README.md`, `SECURITY.md`,
  `CONTRIBUTING.md`, and the new `docs/RELEASE.md` all carry the expected
  release and security guidance.
- **Broken repo-local doc references now have a dedicated audit.** New
  `scripts/path_reference_audit.ps1` scans public docs, support docs,
  issue templates, and scripts for markdown links or quoted repo-local paths
  that no longer resolve, then emits a JSON report fit for automation.
- **Local release hygiene is easier to run before CI.** The repo now ships
  `.gitleaks.toml` and `.pre-commit-config.yaml`, wiring together baseline
  hygiene hooks, gitleaks, the public-copy audit, and the path-reference
  audit into one local pre-push loop.
- **The full audit pipeline and CI now enforce the release surface too.**
  `scripts/run_full_audit.ps1` and `.github/workflows/ci.yml` both run the
  new publication audits in addition to the existing route-count, feature,
  strategy, test-count, and markdown-link drift gates.
- **Top-level docs were refreshed with the live test count and less
  brand-heavy release copy.** `README.md`, `docs/INDEX.md`,
  `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `docs/ROADMAP.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`, and
  `SECURITY.md` now reflect `215` passing tests, link to the release guide,
  and use more neutral MCP/inference wording on the highest-visibility
  publication surfaces.

### Low-allocation JSON contract path (2026-04-22)

- **Hot HTTP and bridge JSON paths now use source-generated contract
  metadata.** New `PalLlmJsonSerializerContext` and
  `PalLlmDomainJsonSerializerContext` partial contexts wire
  `System.Text.Json` source generation into the sidecar and domain layers,
  covering the repeated API, MCP, bridge, pack, and vision shapes instead of
  paying reflection-based metadata discovery on every hot path.
- **ASP.NET Core JSON responses now see the generated metadata too.**
  The sidecar's global `JsonOptions` resolver chain now includes the
  generated context, so `/api/health`, `/api/dashboard`, `/api/features`,
  `/api/world`, and the other typed JSON endpoints benefit without any
  contract changes.
- **ETag fingerprints and health probes now stay on typed payloads.**
  Dashboard ETags and `application/health+json` probe bodies now serialize
  through named payload records/classes rather than anonymous objects,
  keeping the cache/probe path fast and explicit.
- **MCP read surfaces keep the same contract with less overhead.**
  Tool/resource payload shaping moved onto small named records for the active
  model-tier, recent bridge activity, chat result, and character lookup
  surfaces so MCP callers keep getting stable JSON without the reflective
  anonymous-object path.
- **Regression coverage expanded.** `McpEndpointTests` now pin
  `pal_active_model_tier` and the `palllm://runtime/health` resource as
  structured JSON bodies in addition to the existing MCP discovery and world
  snapshot coverage.

### Inspection-surface revalidation + browser hardening (2026-04-22)

- **Dashboard, feature-catalog, and upstream-MCP reads now revalidate instead
  of always redownloading.** `GET /api/dashboard`, `GET /api/features`, and
  `GET /api/mcp/upstream` now emit strong `ETag` fingerprints. The dashboard
  surface uses `Cache-Control: private, no-cache, must-revalidate`, while the
  feature catalog and upstream MCP snapshot use explicit private TTLs
  controlled by `PalLLM:Http:FeatureCatalogCacheMinutes` and
  `PalLLM:Http:UpstreamSnapshotCacheSeconds`. Matching `If-None-Match`
  requests return `304 Not Modified`, cutting repeated JSON transfer for the
  field console and other operator clients.
- **Field Console refresh loop now reuses unchanged dashboard state.**
  `wwwroot/app.js` remembers the last dashboard `ETag`, revalidates with
  `If-None-Match`, and on `304` re-renders from the last payload so relative
  freshness UI still advances without paying another full payload download.
- **Publishable default browser posture tightened.** All sidecar responses now
  ship baseline browser security headers:
  `Content-Security-Policy`, `Permissions-Policy`, `Referrer-Policy`,
  `X-Content-Type-Options`, and `X-Frame-Options`. This hardens the static
  dashboard and keeps the local-first publish surface closer to production
  expectations without changing any route contracts.
- **Runtime health assembly stops snapshotting metrics three times per read.**
  `PalLlmRuntime.BuildRuntimeHealth()` now captures one
  `PalLlmMetricsSnapshot` and reuses it for the health/dashboard response,
  shaving redundant work off the hottest inspection path.
- **Regression coverage + docs updated together.** New endpoint tests pin the
  `304` behavior for dashboard, feature catalog, and upstream MCP routes plus
  the baseline browser security headers; `docs/API.md`,
  `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, and `docs/TUNING.md` now document the conditional
  caching and hardening behavior instead of the old blanket `no-store` story.

### HTTP contract hardening + drift audit (2026-04-22)

- **Structured health probes**. `/health/live` and `/health/ready` now use a
  custom `HealthCheckOptions.ResponseWriter` that emits
  `application/health+json` with `status`, `totalDurationMs`, and per-check
  `results.*.data` payloads. Operators and probe dashboards no longer need to
  scrape plaintext plus a separate JSON endpoint to understand readiness.
- **First-party OpenAPI contract upgraded from "exists" to "publishable".**
  The sidecar now serves both `GET /openapi/v1.json` and
  `GET /openapi/v1.yaml`, and the route registrations now carry stable
  `operationId`s, summaries, tags, request-body metadata, and explicit
  `429` response metadata using the current ASP.NET Core minimal-API OpenAPI
  extensions (`WithName`, `WithSummary`, `WithTags`, `Accepts`, `Produces`,
  `ProducesProblem`).
- **Low-latency overload shedding for heavy lanes.** `PalLLM:Http:*` is a new
  shipped config block that controls a small output cache for the OpenAPI docs
  plus dedicated concurrency limiters for chat, vision, and TTS. Saturated
  endpoints now return `429 Too Many Requests` `ProblemDetails` instead of
  queueing unbounded local work and spiking interactive latency.
- **Read-mostly JSON surfaces now document their cache contract clearly.**
  `GET /api/features` and `GET /api/mcp/upstream` already emit strong `ETag`
  values plus private cache headers; this pass added explicit regression tests
  for their `304 Not Modified` flow and updated the API/ops/tuning docs so the
  cacheability of those endpoints is no longer tribal knowledge.
- **Regression coverage expanded**:
  `HealthEndpoints_ReturnStructuredJsonProbePayloads`,
  `OpenApiYamlEndpoint_ReturnsYamlVariantOfTheDocument`, the OpenAPI metadata
  assertions for `/api/chat`, and
  the conditional-cache tests for `/api/features` and `/api/mcp/upstream`
  now pin the new contract end to end.
- **Docs and drift tooling updated together**. `README.md`, `docs/API.md`,
  `docs/OPERATIONS.md`, `docs/TUNING.md`, `docs/INDEX.md`, and
  `docs/ROADMAP.md` now reflect the JSON+YAML OpenAPI surface, the separate
  `/mcp` protocol route, the structured probe payloads, and the new `Http`
  tuning block. The local full-audit script and CI doc-drift gate now verify
  the operational/protocol route counts and the API reference's health/OpenAPI
  contract markers instead of only checking README math.

### Honesty pass + self-contained decoupling (2026-04-22)

- **Roadmap re-scored from `89.6%` to the honest `75.5%`.** The previous
  weighting counted two scaffolded features (`native-hud-attachment`,
  `production-sampler`) as `ready` even though both default to OFF
  in ship configuration and are therefore not player-facing. Scaffolded
  now counts at 40% of a ready feature's roadmap credit. Phase 3
  recalculated `91% → 60%`, Phase 4 `78% → 55%`, Phase 5 `86% → 72%`.
  Phases 1 and 2 unchanged (sidecar runtime genuinely ~95% complete).
- **New `scaffolded` status in the feature catalog.** Two existing
  entries retagged: `native-hud-attachment` (Lua UMG widget attachment
  exists but `native_hud_widget_targets` is empty by default) and
  `production-sampler` (bounded BaseCampManager poll exists but
  `production_sampler_enabled=false` by default pending hook-signature
  validation on a live build). `advisory-action-intents` notes updated
  to clarify that native game-state mutation is wired only for
  `waypoint_suggest`; the other two allowlisted types emit bridge
  feedback events only. New `FeaturesEndpoint_ScaffoldedEntriesMatchRoadmapCommitment`
  test locks in the scaffolded set so anyone changing a status has to
  update the roadmap math in the same PR.
- **Self-contained portable-adapter surface.** Removed the
  `ProjectReference` to the old sibling core project. The six
  portable interfaces (`IGameAdapter`, `ICharacter`, `IWorldClock`,
  `IPathProvider`, `ILogger`, `Vec3`) plus the `SemanticEmbedder` and
  `ResponseCleanup` helpers now live in a single ~250-line file
  [`src/PalLLM.Domain/Portable/PortableAdapterContracts.cs`](src/PalLLM.Domain/Portable/PortableAdapterContracts.cs).
  PalLLM no longer depends on any external project at build time,
  insulating the published binary from sibling-repo refactoring
  churn. `docs/CORE_LIBRARY.md` rewritten to document the new
  self-contained posture. `portable-core-adapter` catalog entry
  renamed to `portable-adapter-surface`.
- **Docs updated**: `docs/ROADMAP.md` gains a "2026-04-22 honesty
  pass" section and per-phase "why it's lower than claimed" notes;
  `README.md` status line reports `75.5%` and the new `51 ready /
  2 scaffolded / 1 deferred` split; `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`
  feature-catalog snapshot updated and "sibling library" language
  removed; `CONTRIBUTING.md` test count synced to 208.
- **Lawyer-proof sweep.** Purged all remaining "sibling library"
  framing from currently-authoritative docs and configs:
  `THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md` now describes the portable adapter
  surface as inlined + MIT-licensed rather than as an external
  dependency; `SECURITY.md` out-of-scope list rewritten to drop
  "sibling Core adapter library" (the dependency no longer exists);
  `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `release.yml`, and `codeql.yml`
  simplified to single-repo checkouts (no more sibling-repo-checkout
  steps that referenced an old sibling repository);
  `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/feature_request.md` scope-fit checklist
  item reworded generically; `docs/examples/compose.yaml` build
  instructions simplified to `docker build -t palllm:local .` from
  repo root; `docs/INDEX.md` entry for `CORE_LIBRARY.md` reframed as
  "portable adapter surface" rather than "reuse boundary with the
  sibling adapter library"; `autopilot-port` and `container-image`
  feature-catalog entries reworded to remove sibling-library
  references. The CHANGELOG / historical `ROADMAP.md` / `CORE_LIBRARY.md`
  retain the term only in past-tense rationale sections explaining
  why the decoupling happened.
- Test count `207 → 208` (one new roadmap-commitment test).
  Feature-catalog counts unchanged at 54 total.
- **New `scripts/run_full_audit.ps1` pipeline.** A single command
  reproduces what CI runs (build, tests, 6 drift gates) plus
  optional extras (coverage via ReportGenerator, CycloneDX SBOM
  generation) and writes a self-contained
  `artifacts/full-audit/<UTC-ts>/RESULTS.md` with a pass/fail table,
  per-step logs under `steps/`, and environment metadata. Lets
  contributors pre-flight exactly what CI will fail on, and gives
  auditors a pinned "here's where we stood on date X" snapshot.
  `CONTRIBUTING.md` pre-flight checklist bumped to point at it.
  the external prompt-pack project-level mojibake detector avoids the Windows-1252 false-positive
  that a naive `Get-Content` would hit on PS 5.1.

### Business Prometheus metrics (labeled counters + latency histogram)

- **Three new metric families** at `/metrics` complement the existing
  process-level counters with the dimensionality operators actually
  want for dashboards:
  - `palllm_fallback_strategy_total{strategy="..."}` — which of the
    19 deterministic strategies is hitting. Bounded cardinality
    (19 values), so breakdown panels work cleanly.
  - `palllm_model_tier_transition_total{from="...",to="..."}` — tier
    orchestrator graduation counter. Typical pattern: one
    `<none>→small` on startup, one `small→large` when the larger
    model finishes downloading. Persistent flapping between tiers
    would show up as an anomaly on this metric.
  - `palllm_chat_duration_seconds` — cumulative Prometheus histogram
    of end-to-end `/api/chat` latency. 13 buckets from 5 ms to 60 s
    covering fallback-only paths through large-model inference.
    Paired with `_count` + `_sum` so Grafana's `histogram_quantile()`
    reads the shape natively.
- **Backed by a new `PalLlmMetrics` type** in `PalLLM.Domain`. Pure
  C# — thread-safe counters via `ConcurrentDictionary<string,long>`
  and `Interlocked` on a fixed-bucket `long[]`. No external SDK
  dependency (no `System.Diagnostics.Metrics`, no OpenTelemetry SDK
  registration, no `prometheus-net`) — Domain stays portable. The
  existing `PrometheusExporter` renders the snapshot to exposition
  format without any new NuGet reference.
- **DI wiring**: registered as a singleton in `Program.cs` and
  injected into both `PalLlmRuntime` (optional constructor parameter,
  defaulting to a fresh instance for test scenarios) and
  `ModelTierOrchestrator`. Recording happens at the exact moments
  the events fire — chat latency via `Stopwatch.GetElapsedTime()`
  around the full `ChatAsync` call, fallback strategy when the
  director's decision is finalised, tier transitions inside the
  orchestrator's `RefreshAsync` only when `Changed` is true.
- **8 new unit tests** in `tests/PalLLM.Tests/PalLlmMetricsTests.cs`
  lock in: empty-state snapshots, per-strategy accumulation,
  null/whitespace defensive paths, the `<none>` tier sentinel,
  cumulative bucket semantics for 4 representative latencies
  (3 ms, 80 ms, 450 ms, 3.5 s), negative/NaN rejection, the full
  Prometheus exposition format (verifying label escaping, `+Inf`
  bucket, `_count`/`_sum` suffixes), and the empty-state
  "no labeled blocks" output cleanliness.
- **Docs**: `OPERATIONS.md` § Metrics scraping gained a "Labeled
  counters (bounded cardinality)" subsection, a "Chat latency
  histogram" subsection, and three example PromQL queries (p95
  chat latency, fallback-strategy mix, tier-graduation anomaly
  detection).
- Feature catalog grew `53 → 54` with a `business-prometheus-metrics`
  entry. Test count `199 → 207`.

### TLS deployment guide + Caddyfile + compose.yaml bug fix

- **New [`docs/TLS.md`](docs/TLS.md)**: full playbook for exposing
  PalLLM beyond localhost with TLS termination. Covers Caddy
  (recommended, auto-HTTPS), nginx (traditional, manual cert), and
  Traefik (container orchestration). Each config block pre-tuned for
  MCP Streamable HTTP / SSE compatibility — response buffering off,
  X-Forwarded-* headers preserved, security headers (HSTS, nosniff,
  Referrer-Policy) baseline. Also ships a hardening checklist paired
  with `PalLLM:Auth:ApiKey` + `ProtectMetrics` / `ProtectHealth` so
  operators know what to tighten before going public.
- **New [`docs/examples/Caddyfile`](docs/examples/Caddyfile)**:
