  ready-to-adapt Caddy config. Replace one hostname token, run
  `caddy run --config Caddyfile`, and you have TLS-terminated PalLLM
  with automatic Let's Encrypt provisioning. Ships in the release
  ZIP alongside `claude-desktop-config.json` / `vscode-mcp.json` /
  `compose.yaml` — single directory holds every integration stencil
  a new operator needs.
- **Bug fix in [`docs/examples/compose.yaml`](docs/examples/compose.yaml)**:
  the previous version had `build: context: ..` + `dockerfile:
  ../Dockerfile` which was wrong in both contexts — the Dockerfile
  previously expected the parent of multiple sibling repos as its build
  context (not `docs/`), and the shipped release ZIP has no
  `Dockerfile` at all. Replaced the build block with an `image:`
  reference to a placeholder registry URL + a comment block
  explaining the three supported image-source paths (local build,
  registry push, self-contained EXE). Fails fast with a clear error
  until the operator picks one instead of silently half-working.
- **Cross-linking**: `docs/INDEX.md` quick-navigation table + How-to
  Diátaxis quadrant gained the TLS row; `OPERATIONS.md` auth
  section now links to `TLS.md` where it mentions reverse proxies;
  `MCP_QUICKSTART.md` "Where to go from here" section includes the
  TLS pointer for cross-machine MCP deployments.
- Feature catalog grew `52 → 53` with a `tls-deployment-guide` entry.

### MCP onboarding — ready-to-paste examples + 5-minute quickstart

- **New [`docs/MCP_QUICKSTART.md`](docs/MCP_QUICKSTART.md)**: 5-minute
  walk-through from downloaded release ZIP to working companion chat
  inside Claude Desktop. Covers small-first model pull, sidecar
  launch (self-contained exe, dotnet run, or Docker Compose), config
  file paths per-OS, troubleshooting for the most common first-run
  issues, plus hand-off links to VS Code / Cursor / custom client
  setups.
- **New [`docs/examples/claude-desktop-config.json`](docs/examples/claude-desktop-config.json)**:
  ready-to-paste `mcpServers` entry with inline comments covering
  config-file paths per-OS, optional auth header placement, and
  remote-deployment notes. Zero copy-paste thinking for a first-time
  user.
- **New [`docs/examples/vscode-mcp.json`](docs/examples/vscode-mcp.json)**:
  same but for VS Code's `.vscode/mcp.json` GitHub Copilot Chat MCP
  schema.
- **New [`docs/examples/compose.yaml`](docs/examples/compose.yaml)**:
  Docker Compose stack with Ollama + PalLLM on a shared network,
  named volume for persisted model weights, health-checked services,
  and commented env-var overrides for auth / OTel / tier config.
  Operators who want "one command and it's up" have it.
- **Package script upgrade** — `scripts/package-release.ps1` now
  sweeps the entire `docs/examples/` directory into the release ZIP
  instead of copying a single named file. New example files become
  shipping assets automatically with no script edit. Also copies
  `docs/MCP_QUICKSTART.md` to the release root so the end-user ZIP
  carries it next to the starter README.
- **PLAYER_README inside the package** now calls out the MCP
  connection path with a dedicated section ("Connect PalLLM to
  Claude Desktop / VS Code / Cursor via MCP") so a user who unzips
  the release sees the MCP option immediately, not buried in docs.
- Cross-links: [`README.md`](README.md) and
  [`docs/INDEX.md`](docs/INDEX.md) quick-navigation tables get an
  MCP-quickstart row; the Diátaxis tutorial quadrant in INDEX lists
  both QUICKSTART (learning the runtime) and MCP_QUICKSTART
  (integrating it into an MCP host).

### MCP upstream client (PalLLM as MCP client too)

- **PalLLM is now an MCP server AND an MCP client.** Operators
  declare external MCP servers in
  `PalLLM:McpClient:UpstreamServers[]` with `{ Id, Url, BearerToken?,
  Enabled }` entries, and the sidecar probes each on startup and
  periodically (default every 5 minutes) to discover their tools,
  resources, and prompts. Turns the sidecar into a lightweight MCP
  hub: one endpoint reflects what every connected upstream can do.
- **Read-only by design**: PalLLM does NOT auto-proxy `tools/call`
  or `resources/read` to upstreams. V1 fetches catalog metadata
  only (`tools/list`, `resources/list`, `prompts/list`). This keeps
  the security model simple — operators explicitly opt into each
  upstream URL + auth, and bearer tokens are never surfaced on any
  API response. Future revisions can layer selective proxying on top
  once a per-upstream allowlist model is designed.
- **New `McpUpstreamClientPool` singleton** caches discovered
  primitives behind a volatile map replacement — readers (the REST
  endpoint, the MCP tool) get a lock-free consistent view, and
  probe failures record `Connected=false` with the error message
  rather than throwing. Concurrent refresh calls serialise behind a
  semaphore so duplicate ticks just wait.
- **New `McpUpstreamDiscoveryWorker` IHostedService** fires an
  immediate probe on startup (so `/api/mcp/upstream` has data within
  seconds) and re-probes every `DiscoveryIntervalSeconds`. Runs
  probes in parallel with a short `DiscoveryTimeoutSeconds`
  per-probe cap so one hung upstream can't stall the worker.
- **New `GET /api/mcp/upstream` REST endpoint** returns the current
  snapshot array — one entry per configured upstream, sorted by Id,
  with `Connected` / `Error` / `ServerName` / `ServerVersion` /
  `Tools[]` / `Resources[]` / `Prompts[]` / `LastProbedAtUtc`. Route
  count now `23 → 24`.
- **New MCP tool `pal_list_upstream_mcp`** exposes the same data
  through the MCP server surface — so MCP hosts (Claude Desktop,
  Cursor, VS Code) can discover the upstream fleet via the same
  JSON-RPC transport they use for everything else. Total MCP tools
  `8 → 9`.
- Built on the `HttpClientTransport` + `McpClient` client API from
  `ModelContextProtocol.Core`. Auth bearer tokens forwarded via
  `AdditionalHeaders` on the transport options, never logged.
- **4 new integration tests** in `tests/PalLLM.Tests/McpUpstreamClientTests.cs`:
  empty-config → empty array response; unreachable URL → graceful
  `Connected=false` snapshot (not an exception); disabled upstream →
  skipped entirely; MCP-tool and REST-endpoint return identical
  data.
- `TUNING.md` gained a "McpClient block (upstream MCP servers)"
  section with the full tuning table; `OPERATIONS.md` § "Exposing
  PalLLM via MCP" gained a "PalLLM as MCP client (upstream
  discovery)" subsection with example config, inspection commands,
  and the safety posture.
- Feature catalog grew `51 → 52` with an `mcp-upstream-client`
  entry. Test count `195 → 199`.

### Tuning reference + runtime personality doc

- **New [`docs/TUNING.md`](docs/TUNING.md)** consolidates every
  configurable PalLLM parameter into one operator-facing reference.
  Each of the 50+ parameters across all eight option blocks
  (`Inference`, `ModelTiers`, `Fallback`, `Vision`, `TTS`, `Bridge`,
  `Session`, `Automation`, `Auth`) lists: default, min, max,
  too-low symptoms, too-high symptoms, how to test, owner (operator
  / designer / programmer), and whether it can be changed at
  runtime. Fills a real gap — tuning knowledge was previously
  scattered across XML comments in `PalLlmOptions.cs` and had no
  guidance on symptom-spotting.
- **Runtime personality section** at the top of TUNING.md
  classifies every response-producing path as `Real`,
  `Deterministic`, or `Optional-real-with-deterministic-fallback`.
  Makes the "prioritise felt companionship over simulation fidelity"
  design principle explicit and operator-visible for the first
  time.
- **Performance budget section** pins per-stage targets for a
  `/api/chat` turn: `< 1 ms` validation, `< 2 ms` memory recall,
  `< 5 ms` fallback decision, `< 3 ms` outbox write. Anything over
  `30 ms` on the fallback-only path is "something is wrong" and
  points at the OTel tracing backend for diagnosis.
- **Tuning workflow** section documents the safe-change recipe:
  one variable at a time, fixed-seed reproduction, watch the
  `pal.chat` span tags for which route a turn took.
- **Cross-links**: [`docs/INDEX.md`](docs/INDEX.md) quick-navigation
  table gets a "Tune a specific parameter" row;
  [`docs/OPERATIONS.md`](docs/OPERATIONS.md) front matter points
  readers at TUNING.md for parameter-level work so the ops doc
  itself stays task-focused.

### MCP: resources + prompts (complete primitive surface)

- **PalLLM's MCP server now ships all three MCP primitives** — the
  initial cut (previous entry) shipped tools only; this cut adds
  resources and prompts so the server is feature-complete per the
  `2025-06-18` spec.
- **5 direct resources + 1 templated resource** via
  `[McpServerResource]` attributes in
  [`src/PalLLM.Sidecar/Mcp/PalLlmMcpResources.cs`](src/PalLLM.Sidecar/Mcp/PalLlmMcpResources.cs):
  - `palllm://world/snapshot`
  - `palllm://features`
  - `palllm://runtime/health`
  - `palllm://characters`
  - `palllm://model/tier/active`
  - `palllm://character/{characterId}` — templated URI, characterId
    parameter extracted automatically and passed to the method
- **3 user-controlled prompts** via `[McpServerPrompt]` attributes in
  [`src/PalLLM.Sidecar/Mcp/PalLlmMcpPrompts.cs`](src/PalLLM.Sidecar/Mcp/PalLlmMcpPrompts.cs):
  - `palllm_companion_chat` — opens a companion chat with the live
    scene + character profile baked into an assistant-role opening
    turn, then seeds the user turn with whatever the player typed
  - `palllm_threat_analysis` — tactical advisor framing with live
    threat/alert levels, player vitals, and nearby hostiles injected
  - `palllm_base_status` — base-network strategist framing with
    active/known base inventory injected
- **MCP role semantics**: prompts emit scene-setting context as an
  `assistant` role message (not `system`, which MCP spec does not
  allow) so the wire format round-trips without coercion. Documented
  inline in the prompt class.
- **Registration**: `AddMcpServer()` chain extended with
  `.WithResourcesFromAssembly().WithPromptsFromAssembly()` — adding a
  new primitive is now a one-file change with no central registration
  list to maintain.
- **7 new integration tests** cover:
  - `resources/list` returns every declared direct resource
  - `resources/templates/list` surfaces the per-character template
  - `resources/read` on `palllm://world/snapshot` returns a parseable
    `GameWorldSnapshot` in the body
  - `resources/read` on a missing `palllm://character/{id}` returns
    the graceful `not-found` sentinel (not an exception)
  - `prompts/list` returns every declared prompt
  - `prompts/get` on `palllm_companion_chat` returns assistant + user
    messages with the supplied userMessage argument echoed
  - `prompts/get` on `palllm_threat_analysis` injects the live threat
    level from the snapshot into the system framing
- **Feature catalog entry** for `mcp-server` rewritten to document
  all three primitives (was tools-only).
- **Docs**: `docs/OPERATIONS.md` § "Exposing PalLLM via MCP" now
  lists every resource URI and prompt name with purpose tables.
- Test count `188 → 195`.

### MCP server (Model Context Protocol)

- **PalLLM is now an MCP server** exposed at `POST /mcp` over
  Streamable HTTP, protocol version `2025-06-18`. Claude Desktop,
  Visual Studio Code, Cursor, ChatGPT, and any other MCP-aware agent
  can discover and invoke PalLLM's runtime through the standardised
  JSON-RPC 2.0 protocol — no custom integrations, no per-client
  plugin code.
- **8 tools ship in v1**, all read-only by design:
  - `pal_world_snapshot` — full world JSON
  - `pal_scene_description` — terse deterministic summary (via the
    snapshot fallback shipped last cycle)
  - `pal_chat` — send a message to a companion, get reply + response
    path metadata
  - `pal_recall_memory` — semantic memory recall
  - `pal_list_characters` — all known Pal companions
  - `pal_list_features` — feature catalog
  - `pal_list_recent_bridge_events` — recent Lua bridge activity
  - `pal_active_model_tier` — current tier + last probe result
- **Attribute-based registration**:
  `[McpServerToolType]` + `[McpServerTool]` attributes in
  `src/PalLLM.Sidecar/Mcp/PalLlmMcpTools.cs`, discovered via
  `WithToolsFromAssembly()`. Tool JSON Schemas are auto-generated
  from the C# method signatures — MCP client UIs render the correct
  form fields without any manual schema authoring.
- **DI-injected services**: `PalLlmRuntime`, `PalLlmOptions`, and
  `ModelTierOrchestrator` resolve directly as method parameters
  thanks to `ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore` 1.2.0's parameter
  injection.
- **Stateless transport**: `options.Stateless = true` — each
  JSON-RPC call is independent, so a fresh MCP client connects
  without resumption state. Ideal for ephemeral CLI / test callers.
- **Auth uniformity**: the bearer-token middleware now covers `/mcp`
  identically to `/api/*`. When `PalLLM:Auth:ApiKey` is set,
  unauthenticated MCP requests get `401` — MCP is not a back door.
- **Safety posture**: MCP tools are **read-only**. The guarded-action
  executor stays gated behind the existing
  `PalLLM:Automation:AllowedActions` allowlist so MCP clients can
  observe but cannot produce unchecked side effects. The two
  surfaces (MCP discovery and action execution) remain separate.
- **6 new integration tests** in `tests/PalLLM.Tests/McpEndpointTests.cs`
  cover: protocol version negotiation, tool discovery (every tool
  registered), per-tool shape invariants (non-empty description +
  object-typed inputSchema), `tools/call` execution (feature catalog
  + scene description sentinel), and auth enforcement on `/mcp`
  when `ApiKey` is configured.
- **`SidecarTestFixture` extracted** from `SidecarEndpointTests.cs`
  to its own file so cross-file integration tests can share the
  baseline sidecar host. Same behaviour, same `extraConfig` override
  surface.
- `docs/OPERATIONS.md` § "Exposing PalLLM via MCP" documents the
  endpoint, tool catalog, Claude Desktop / VS Code config examples,
  raw curl invocation, auth posture, and safety guarantees.
- Feature catalog grew `50 → 51` with an `mcp-server` entry.
  Test count `182 → 188`.

### Snapshot-derived vision fallback

- **Chat visual augmentation now has a deterministic fallback**: when
  the player attaches a screenshot but the multimodal model is off or
  the describe call fails, PalLLM composes a terse 1-3 sentence scene
  summary from the live `GameWorldSnapshot` (time of day, biome,
  weather, base-vs-wild location, nearby pals, hostiles, current
  objective) and splices it into the system prompt instead of dropping
  visual context. Companions stay situationally-aware even without a
  running multimodal model.
- **Source transparency in the prompt**: the system prompt labels the
  visual context as `(from vision model)` or `(from snapshot fallback)`
  so the model can weight it appropriately.
- **OpenTelemetry tag**: the `pal.chat` span gains a
  `pal.visual_context_source` tag (`vision_model`, `snapshot_fallback`,
  or `none`). Operators can query fallback-activation ratios directly
  from their tracing backend.
- **Scope-controlled**: the fallback is wired into the *chat
  augmentation* path only. The standalone `/api/vision/describe`
  endpoint stays strict-failure on purpose so external tools calling
  that endpoint get ground-truth vision output, never a synthesised
  stand-in that would mask a real outage.
- **18 new tests**: 15 composer unit tests cover every clause
  (location, company, threat, objective) across null/unloaded/rich
  snapshots + stability across invocations. 3 `ChatAsync` integration
  tests cover vision-disabled-but-world-loaded, vision-enabled-but-
  describe-fails, and vision-succeeds (fallback must NOT activate)
  paths.
- **Fallback coverage matrix** in `docs/OPERATIONS.md` updated to
  reflect the new capability. The "chat visual augmentation" row now
  shows `SnapshotVisionFallback.Compose` as the diverse fallback.
- Feature catalog grew `49 → 50` with a `snapshot-vision-fallback`
  entry. Test count `164 → 182`.

### Tiered local-model orchestration

- **New `PalLLM:Inference:ModelTiers[]` config** lets operators
  declare an ordered cascade of local models. The sidecar probes the
  inference endpoint to see which tier models are currently
  available, uses the highest-priority available tier on every
  request, and **automatically graduates** from a small fast-start
  tier (`gemma3:4b`) to a large quality tier (Unsloth dynamic quant
  of a 35B Qwen MoE) the moment the heavy one finishes downloading
  or warming — no manual config edit, no restart, no lost uptime.
- **Instant-start first request**: on startup the orchestrator seeds
  the active tier with the lowest-priority entry in list order so
  the very first `/api/chat` call works before any probe has run.
  Typical pairing: `gemma3:4b` (~3 GB, loads in seconds) is the seed
  while the large model is still being pulled.
- **`ModelTierUpgradeWorker`** (new `IHostedService`) re-probes every
  `TierProbeIntervalSeconds` (default `30`). Each graduation logs
  `Model tier graduated <prev> -> <new> (<model>)` at Information
  severity and emits a `pal.model_tier.transition` span on the
  `PalLLM.Runtime` ActivitySource — visible in any OTel tracing
  backend with `previous_tier`, `current_tier`, `model`, and
  `available_count` tags.
- **Probe targets both wire formats**: OpenAI-compat `/v1/models`
  first (works with Ollama ≥ 0.1.30, vLLM, LM Studio, llama.cpp
  server), falls back to Ollama-native `/api/tags`, merges results.
  Transient probe failures keep the current tier — no thrashing.
- **Default shipping tiers** (opinionated, editable in `appsettings.json`):
  - `small` → `gemma3:4b` (priority 1, ~3 GB)
  - `large` → `hf.co/unsloth/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-Instruct-UD-Q4_K_XL-GGUF`
    (priority 10, Unsloth dynamic 4-bit quant of a 35B MoE)
- **Backwards compatible**: empty `ModelTiers` list disables tier
  orchestration entirely and `Inference.Model` is used verbatim for
  every request. Existing configs keep working unchanged.
- **Fallback-coverage audit**: new `docs/OPERATIONS.md` §
  "Fallback coverage matrix" documents every response-producing path
  and its fallback posture. Net finding: chat has 19 diverse
  hard-coded strategies, vision / TTS return structured failure
  results with clear status messages, action intents / memory recall
  / presentation / session / relationships all have graceful no-op
  or deterministic-reconstruction paths. No gaps that risk a thrown
  exception reaching the player.
- **14 new regression tests** in `tests/PalLLM.Tests/ModelTierTests.cs`
  cover: probe parsing of both wire formats and their merge; empty
  BaseUrl short-circuit; network-failure swallowing; no-tiers static
  fallback; seed with lowest priority; no-graduation when only small
  is available; graduation when large appears; no-thrash when probe
  returns empty; priority-wins-over-list-order; and end-to-end
  `HttpJsonInferenceClient` integration showing the active tier's
  model tag in the outgoing request body before and after graduation.
- Feature catalog grew `48 → 49` with a `tiered-model-orchestration`
  entry. Test count `150 → 164`.

### Coverage backfill (test count 120 → 150, line 81.1% → 88.1%)

- **Paid off the coverage infrastructure**: the day-one baseline flagged
  three high-value gaps — `HttpTtsClient` at 8.5%, the entire
  `PalLlmFeatureCatalog` at 0% (no test ever hit `/api/features`),
  and `PalApiRequestValidator` at 45.7%. 30 targeted tests closed the
  gaps.
- **`HttpTtsClient` 8.5% → 94.2%**: 8 new tests cover disabled path,
  missing/oversized text guards, happy-path audio parse, bearer auth
  header when `Tts:ApiKey` is set, server-error surfacing,
  network-exception handling, and cancellation propagation. Three
  reusable fake `HttpMessageHandler`s (`RecordingTtsHandler`,
  `ThrowingTtsHandler`, `CancellingTtsHandler`) are defined in
  `tests/PalLLM.Tests/InferenceClientTests.cs` next to the existing
  `RecordingHandler` / `ScriptedHandler` pair.
- **`PalLlmFeatureCatalog` 0% → 100%**: 3 new endpoint-level tests
  in `SidecarEndpointTests.cs` — `FeaturesEndpoint_ReturnsEveryCatalogEntry...`
  pins shape and uniqueness, `FeaturesEndpoint_EveryStatusIsFromTheKnownVocabulary`
  guards against typo drift (`reaedy` silently breaking dashboard
  filters), and `FeaturesEndpoint_ExactlyOneDeferredEntryMatchesRoadmapCommitment`
  keeps `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` honest about the lone `autopilot-port`
  deferral.
- **`PalApiRequestValidator` 45.7% → 100%**: new file
  `tests/PalLLM.Tests/PalApiRequestValidatorTests.cs` with 19 unit
  tests covering every request-type branch — ChatRequest
  (UserMessage, CharacterId, Temperature negative/NaN/Infinity,
  MaxTokens, RequestId length, image cap, image MIME), MemoryRecall
  (Query, Limit below/above, CharacterId), VisionDescribe,
  VisionWorldState, TtsSynthesize (missing/over-cap), and the
  unrecognized-type no-op path.
- **Overall coverage**: line `81.1% → 88.1%` (+7.0pp),
  branch `73.2% → 75.3%` (+2.1pp), method `89.1% → 90.5%` (+1.4pp).
  README badge + CHANGELOG pinned to the new numbers so a future
  coverage regression surfaces in review.
- **No production-code changes**: pure test additions, so the risk
  profile is zero for end users. The new tests run in <100ms so CI
  time is unaffected.

### Distributed logging (OpenTelemetry)

- **OpenTelemetry log export** now rides on the same
  `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` switch that activates tracing. When
  the env var is set, every `ILogger` record the sidecar writes is
  shipped as an OTLP log record in parallel with the existing console
  sink. Off by default — zero per-log-record overhead unless the
  switch is flipped.
- **Trace-log correlation for free**: OTel's ILogger integration
  automatically attaches the active Activity's `trace_id` + `span_id`
  to every log record, so opening a `pal.chat` span in the tracing
  backend surfaces the exact ILogger messages emitted during that
  turn. No manual scope push, no per-call-site correlation code.
- **Console logs keep working** unchanged — the OpenTelemetry logger
  provider is chained onto the existing `LoggerFactory` via the
  `AddOpenTelemetry().WithLogging()` unified pattern (stable since
  OTel .NET 1.9), so operators with SSH into the box keep seeing
  familiar output while the OTLP backend gets structured copies.
- **Three pillars of observability** now complete with a single env
  var: traces (existing), logs (new), metrics (existing via
  Prometheus `/metrics`; OTel metrics deferred since Prometheus
  scraping already covers the ground and OTel Collector can bridge if
  needed).
- `docs/OPERATIONS.md` § "Enabling distributed tracing" gained a
  "What about logs?" sub-section covering log-record attributes and
  the off/verify semantics.
- Feature catalog grew `47 → 48` with a `distributed-logging` entry
  referencing the shared OTLP switch.

### Distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry)

- **Opt-in OpenTelemetry tracing** on the sidecar. OFF by default;
  activates only when `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` is set in the
  process environment, so the localhost default carries zero OTel
  overhead — no listener registered, no DiagnosticListener adapters
  installed, `ActivitySource.StartActivity` returns null.
- When ON, spans flow through the standard OTLP wire protocol to any
  conforming backend (Tempo, Jaeger, Honeycomb, Datadog, Grafana
  Cloud, New Relic, Azure Monitor). Standard OTel env vars
  (`OTEL_SERVICE_NAME`, `OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES`,
  `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS`, `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL`) are
  honoured unchanged.
- **Three span types** per chat turn: incoming `HTTP POST /api/chat`
  (ASP.NET Core instrumentation), runtime `pal.chat`
  (`PalLLM.Runtime` ActivitySource, tagged with `pal.request_id`,
  `pal.character_id`, `pal.task_tag`, `pal.response_path`,
  `pal.used_fallback`, `pal.fallback_strategy`,
  `pal.inference_attempted`), and optional outgoing
  `HTTP POST /v1/chat/completions` (HttpClient instrumentation, only
  when live inference runs).
- **Filters**: `/health/*` and `/metrics` are excluded from incoming
  spans so Prometheus/orchestrator scrape traffic doesn't drown
  the interesting chat/bridge spans.
- **Semantic conventions**: the `PalLlmTelemetry` ActivitySource
  lives in `PalLLM.Domain/Runtime/PalLlmTelemetry.cs` with no
  dependency on any OpenTelemetry package — the Domain project stays
  portable. The OTel packages are referenced only from the Sidecar.
- **Two regression tests** pin the behaviour: one registers an
  `ActivityListener` and verifies `ChatAsync` emits a `pal.chat` span
  tagged with the right fallback metadata; the other asserts that
  **when no listener is registered, PalLlmTelemetry has no listeners**
  — so the zero-overhead promise can't silently regress.
- **Docs**: new `docs/OPERATIONS.md` § "Enabling distributed tracing"
  with env-var table, Docker+Jaeger local-stack recipe, span tag
  reference, and on/off/verify matrix. Feature matrix gained a
  `Distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry OTLP)` row.
- **Packages added** (Sidecar only): `OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting`
  1.15.2, `OpenTelemetry.Exporter.OpenTelemetryProtocol` 1.15.2,
  `OpenTelemetry.Instrumentation.AspNetCore` 1.15.1,
  `OpenTelemetry.Instrumentation.Http` 1.15.0 — all latest stable as
  of 2026-04. No dependencies added to `PalLLM.Domain`.
- Feature catalog grew `46 → 47` with a `distributed-tracing` entry.
  Test count `118 → 120`.

### Code-coverage reporting

- **CI now collects line + branch coverage** on every push and pull
  request via [`coverlet.collector`](https://www.nuget.org/packages/coverlet.collector)
  10.0.0 driven by `dotnet test --collect:"XPlat Code Coverage"`.
- **Per-assembly summary** (line + branch percentages for every class
  under `PalLLM.Domain` and `PalLLM.Sidecar`) is rendered into the
  GitHub Actions step summary using
  [`dotnet-reportgenerator-globaltool`](https://www.nuget.org/packages/dotnet-reportgenerator-globaltool)
  5.5.5 in `MarkdownSummaryGithub` mode, so every run shows the
  health snapshot in the job output with no clicks required.
- **Clickable HTML drilldown** is uploaded as a `coverage-report`
  build artefact (14-day retention), so any reviewer can download the
  report and see line-level hit markers without re-running the tools
  locally.
- **`tests/PalLLM.Tests/coverlet.runsettings`** tunes the collector
  to exclude generated files, test assemblies, obsolete members, and
  compiler-generated display classes so the reported numbers reflect
  real production code — not the test harness measuring itself.
- **Coverage runs only on the Ubuntu matrix leg** to keep the Windows
  leg fast and avoid double-posting the step summary; line/branch
  numbers are platform-independent so single-OS collection loses
  nothing meaningful.
- **Baseline on day one**: `81.1%` line / `73.2%` branch coverage
  across `PalLLM.Domain` + `PalLLM.Sidecar`. Useful for spotting
  regressions in future PRs and for prioritising test backfill
  (background workers like `BridgeInboxWorker` and `ScreenshotWatcher`
  currently read `0%` because they're `IHostedService`-based —
  future work, not regression).
- Feature catalog grew `45 → 46` with a `code-coverage-reporting`
  entry; `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`, and
  `docs/OPERATIONS.md` updated. CI drift audit retargeted to the new
  count so coverage wiring can't silently drop.
- **Local developer flow**: same invocation works locally —
  `dotnet test --collect:"XPlat Code Coverage" --settings tests/PalLLM.Tests/coverlet.runsettings`
  produces `TestResults/**/coverage.cobertura.xml`, and
  `dotnet tool install --global dotnet-reportgenerator-globaltool` +
  `reportgenerator -reports:... -targetdir:CoverageReport -reporttypes:HtmlInline`
  generates the same HTML report CI uploads. Both dirs are
  `.gitignore`d so no artefacts leak into commits.

### API-key authentication

- **Optional bearer-token auth** on the sidecar HTTP surface. Off by
  default so localhost deployments stay backwards compatible; opt in
  by setting `PalLLM:Auth:ApiKey` to any non-empty string (via
  `appsettings.json` or the `PalLLM__Auth__ApiKey` env var, so it
  works unchanged inside the Docker container).
- `/api/*` requests require `Authorization: Bearer <key>` when the
  key is configured; unauthenticated requests get **401** with a
  `WWW-Authenticate: Bearer` header per RFC 6750.
- **Constant-time comparison** via
  `CryptographicOperations.FixedTimeEquals` avoids timing-side-channel
  prefix leaks.
- **Operational routes stay open by default**: `/metrics`,
  `/health/live`, `/health/ready`, `/openapi/v1.json`, and the static
  dashboard remain reachable without a credential so Prometheus,
  container orchestrators, SDK generators, and the dashboard keep
  working. `PalLLM:Auth:ProtectMetrics` and
  `PalLLM:Auth:ProtectHealth` flags tighten those when exposed to an
  untrusted network.
- **Six regression tests** pin every branch: no-auth-configured,
  missing header, wrong key, correct key, ops-routes-open-by-default,
  `ProtectMetrics` flag working.
- `SidecarTestFixture` gained an optional `extraConfig` parameter so
  any test can override config without touching the other 100+ test
  fixtures. Backwards compatible — existing `new SidecarTestFixture()`
  calls are untouched.
- New `AuthOptions` block in `PalLlmOptions` documented in
  `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` § "Configuration model" alongside the other
  seven option blocks.
- `docs/OPERATIONS.md` § "Enabling API-key authentication" with full
  flip/verify/rollback matrix; `SECURITY.md` updated with the
  "Authentication when deploying beyond localhost" guidance;
  `README.md` Docker section shows the env-var form inline.
- Feature catalog grew `44 → 45` with an `api-key-authentication`
  entry. Test count `112 → 118`.

### Lua lint CI

- **[`.github/workflows/lua.yml`](.github/workflows/lua.yml)** runs
  [`luacheck`](https://github.com/lunarmodules/luacheck) on the
  UE4SS Lua bridge on every push or PR that touches `mod/` or the
  config. Closes the asymmetry where C# had 112 tests + CodeQL but
  the 3700-line `main.lua` had no automated validation at all.
- **[`.luacheckrc`](.luacheckrc)** at the repo root declares the
  UE4SS-injected globals (`RegisterHook`, `LoopAsync`,
  `ExecuteWithDelay`, `NotifyOnNewObject`, `FindFirstOf`,
  `FindAllOf`, `RegisterKeyBind`, `Key`, `ModifierKey`) as valid,
  targets Lua 5.4 (the UE4SS runtime), and scopes the check to
  `mod/ue4ss/Mods/PalLLM/Scripts` so unrelated Lua in gitignored
  trees doesn't trigger warnings.
- **Path-triggered**: the workflow only runs on mod or config
  changes, so a pure-C# PR doesn't wait on a Lua job.
- **Feature catalog** grew `43 → 44` with a `lua-lint-ci` entry;
  CONTRIBUTING.md supply-chain defences list now covers it.

### Supply-chain attestation

- **Sigstore keyless signing + SLSA v1 provenance** for every tagged
  release. The release workflow now calls
  [`actions/attest-build-provenance@v2`](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/security-guides/using-artifact-attestations)
  after the zip is built; the attestation records the workflow run,
  commit SHA, and repo. Downstream consumers verify with
  `gh attestation verify PalLLM-<tag>.zip --owner <owner>`, which
  exits non-zero on any tamper or forgery.
- **CycloneDX SBOMs** (`PalLLM.Sidecar.cdx.json`,
  `PalLLM.Domain.cdx.json`) generated by
  [CycloneDX .NET 5.0.0](https://github.com/CycloneDX/cyclonedx-dotnet)
  during the release job and published alongside the zip. Feed into
  Dependency-Track, OWASP Grype, or any SBOM-aware scanner.
- Workflow permissions extended with `id-token: write` and
  `attestations: write` — scoped to the release workflow only.
- Release-notes template in `release.yml` now includes the
  verification command and an explanation of each artifact.
- `SECURITY.md` gained a "Supply-chain verification" section telling
  operators to run `gh attestation verify` before trusting a
  downloaded zip, especially before running `install.bat`.
- Feature catalog grew `42 → 43` with a `supply-chain-attestation`
  entry.

### Developer experience

- **`.editorconfig`** at the repo root sets cross-IDE baselines:
  UTF-8 / LF / 4-space-indent (2 for JSON/YAML/markdown/csproj),
  trimmed trailing whitespace on source but preserved on markdown
  (markdown uses trailing double-space for soft-breaks), CRLF for
  Windows-shell files (`*.bat`, `*.ps1`). C# style rules surface at
  `suggestion` severity so the IDE shows hints without breaking the
  build. Matches the existing codebase conventions so no source edits
  are required.
- **README badges**: license (MIT), .NET target (10.0-LTS), platform
  coverage (Windows / Linux / Container), test count, roadmap
  percentage. Static `img.shields.io` badges that render immediately;
  dynamic workflow-status badges noted in an HTML comment for the
  first publisher to activate once the repo has a canonical GitHub
  owner.
- README banner test count synced: `109 → 112`. (Earlier bump missed
  this line because the drift CI only pins the
  `Passed: NN` tail, not prose counts; fixed now.)
- Feature catalog grew `41 → 42` with an `editorconfig-style-baseline`
  entry.

### API surface

- **Native OpenAPI 3.1 document** at `GET /openapi/v1.json`. Generated
  by .NET 10's first-party `Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi` package
  directly from the minimal-API route registrations in
  `Program.cs`, so the spec cannot drift from the actual surface.
  JSON Schema 2020-12 compliant; feeds directly into
  [OpenAPI Generator](https://openapi-generator.tech/) and other SDK
  tooling. Operational route count grew `3 → 4`
  (`/metrics`, `/health/live`, `/health/ready`, `/openapi/v1.json`);
  `/api` route count unchanged at 23.
- New regression test
  `OpenApiEndpoint_ReturnsOpenApi31DocumentCoveringEveryApiRoute`
  asserts the document carries OpenAPI 3.x version, the expected
  `info.title`, and every one of the 23 `/api` paths — so an
  accidental `MapOpenApi()` removal or route rename fails the build.
- `docs/API.md` gained a "Machine-readable spec" section pointing at
  the generated document and noting that this markdown reference stays
  authoritative for human context (task-kind enums, `ResponsePath`
  semantics, `ProblemDetails` shapes) that a spec alone can't carry.
- Feature catalog grew `40 → 41` with an `openapi-document` entry.

### Community health files

- **[`SECURITY.md`](SECURITY.md)** at the repo root documents the
  private-vulnerability-reporting channel, scope (what PalLLM owns
  vs. upstream), response timeline (5-day ack, 14-day triage,
  90-day disclosure coordination), safe-harbour language, and a
  thanks / CHANGELOG-credit commitment for researchers.
- **Three issue templates** at `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/`:
  `bug_report.md`, `feature_request.md`, `compat_report.md` (for
  Palworld / UE4SS hook drift — includes a checklist mapped to the
  startup `[PalLLM][Compat]` line so compatibility reports arrive
  with the right context).
- **Blank issues disabled** via `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml`
  so every report arrives with baseline context; the config adds
  contact links pointing at SECURITY.md and OPERATIONS.md so
  security reports and RTFM cases don't bleed into the issue tracker.
- **[`.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`](.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md)**
  mirrors the five CI drift gates as checkboxes. Contributors tick the
  relevant boxes before pushing; CI catches anything the author
  missed.
- `CONTRIBUTING.md` gained an "Opening issues and PRs" section
  pointing contributors at the new templates.
- Feature catalog grew `39 → 40` with a `community-health-files`
  entry.

### Supply-chain defences

- **[Dependabot](.github/dependabot.yml)** enabled for NuGet packages,
  GitHub Actions, and Docker base images. Weekly grouped PRs bound the
  review load; CVE-driven PRs arrive immediately regardless of the
  schedule. Keeps the .NET 10 base image, the NUnit stack, and all CI
  action versions current without a manual audit loop.
- **[CodeQL](.github/workflows/codeql.yml)** scheduled C# SAST
  scanning. Runs `security-extended` query packs on every push, every
  pull request, and weekly on Monday at 06:00 UTC. Findings surface in
  the repo's Security tab. CONTRIBUTING.md updated to name both as
  release gates.

### Deployment

- **Dockerfile + `.dockerignore`** shipped at the repo root for
  container deployment. Multi-stage build
  (`mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:10.0` → `aspnet:10.0`), non-root
  (`USER $APP_UID`), single `/var/palllm` volume for session state +
  outbox + TTS artifacts + diagnostics. Build from the parent
  directory that contains both `PalLLM/` and the sibling Core library
  checkout:
  `docker build -f PalLLM/Dockerfile -t palllm:latest .`.
  Any `PalLLM:*` option can be overridden via the ASP.NET Core
  `PalLLM__*` environment-variable convention, so the same image
  drives test, home-server, and production deployments. Feature
  catalog grew `37 → 38`; README and
  [`docs/OPERATIONS.md`](docs/OPERATIONS.md) § "Container
  deployment" document the flow including the remote-Lua bridge
  mounting pattern.

### Vision subsystem

- **Structured outputs for world-state extraction**. The
  `/api/vision/world-state` path now sends
  `response_format: { type: "json_schema", json_schema: { name: "palllm_world_state", schema: { ... } } }`
  alongside the chat-completions body. Endpoints that support
  structured outputs (OpenAI, Ollama ≥ 0.5, LM Studio, vLLM) constrain
  the model to the PalLLM world-state schema instead of returning
  prose. Endpoints that do not recognise the field silently ignore
  it — backwards compatible on both sides. The existing graceful-fail
  JSON parser in `VisionOrchestrator.TryParseSnapshot` still handles
  narrated or code-fenced output.
- New kill switch `PalLLM:Vision:UseStructuredOutputs` (default `true`).
- New `ResponseFormat` field on `VisionRequest` forwards any
  OpenAI-style `response_format` value verbatim to the outgoing body.
- Two regression tests
  (`VisionClient_WhenStructuredOutputsEnabled_SendsResponseFormatSchema`,
  `VisionClient_WhenStructuredOutputsOmitted_DoesNotIncludeResponseFormat`)
  pin the outgoing request body shape.
- Feature catalog grew `36 → 37`; test count `109 → 111`.

### Platform

- **Migrated to .NET 10 LTS** from .NET 8. .NET 8 support ends
  `2026-11-10`; .NET 10 (GA November 2025) is supported through
  November 2028, matching the project's long-term outlook. All three
  projects (`PalLLM.Domain`, `PalLLM.Sidecar`, `PalLLM.Tests`) now
  target `net10.0`.
- `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.Testing` package bumped `8.0.8` → `10.0.0`
  to match the test host.
- Obsolete `Microsoft.Extensions.Options` package reference removed
  from the test project — .NET 10 ships it as a pruned framework
  package.
- CI workflows (`ci.yml`, `release.yml`) bumped from `dotnet-version:
  8.0.x` to `10.0.x`.
- Release notes, README, QUICKSTART, OPERATIONS, CONTRIBUTING,
  THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES, package-release.ps1, update-all.ps1 now state
  ".NET 10" throughout.
- Build and all `109` tests pass on .NET 10 with zero source changes,
  zero warnings, zero breaking adjustments. Migration was source-code
  transparent.
- `PalApiValidation.cs` docstring updated to explain that the custom
  filter is kept on .NET 10 because several fields validate against
  live `PalLlmOptions` state (image byte caps, TTS character caps)
  that DataAnnotations can't express as static attributes. Future
  simplification is still possible for the static-bounds fields.

### Added

**Player install and compat**
- `install.bat` one-click Windows installer at the repo root. Wraps
  `scripts/install-mod.ps1` so all install logic stays single-sourced;
  double-click from a released zip to auto-detect a Palworld install,
  copy the mod, and print clear next steps.
- Startup compat probe in [`main.lua`](mod/ue4ss/Mods/PalLLM/Scripts/main.lua)
  (`run_compat_probe`). Reports which core hook classes resolved against
  the current game build — visible on the UE4SS console and carried on
  the first `bridge_boot` event as a `Compat` field.
- `register_hook_safely` helper wraps every game-class `RegisterHook`.
  A renamed class in a game patch logs a clear line and degrades to a
  no-op instead of crashing the mod.
- Player-first QUICKSTART rewrite: game-side path opens the doc;
  standalone-sidecar developer path preserved below.

**Delivery scaffolds (all kill-switched, OFF by default)**
- UMG widget attachment path (`try_native_hud_render`) with an ordered
  target list, `TextBlock`-aware child resolver, `WidgetTree` fallback
  scan, and pcall guards at every engine call. Populates optional
  speaker / path-badge / HUD-accent widget fields from the live
  presentation plan when the widget exposes matching slots.
- Production bridge producer — bounded polling sampler over
  `PalBaseCampManager.BaseCamps` emitting `production` events on
  item+status changes.
- Native waypoint-marker hint in `execute_waypoint_suggest`, trying
  `PalMapManager` hint APIs before falling through to the existing
  travel-feedback path. Disable with
  `waypoint_native_marker_enabled = false`.
- Speech playback retry + file-path dedupe for best-effort local
  playback.

**Runtime**
- `RelationshipTracker` soft-cap retention (256 entries, eviction to 192
  by recency × |affinity| × interactions). Most-recently-recorded id is
  always protected.
- TTS observability metrics `palllm_tts_call_total` /
  `palllm_tts_failure_total` surfaced in `/metrics` and `/api/health`.
- `EnsureDirectories` short-circuit caching via
  `Interlocked.CompareExchange`; chat hot path drops ~18 unnecessary
  `Directory.CreateDirectory` syscalls per turn.
- `ModelsDir` no longer created on startup (PalLLM is HTTP-only);
  property kept on `IPathProvider` for interface compatibility.
- `main.lua`: `ensure_dir` caches created paths in a module-level table
  so write_event, emit_screenshot, and write_ui_probe_dump no longer
  spawn a cmd.exe `mkdir` subprocess on every call. The shell-out fires
  once per unique path for the life of the Lua module.
- `main.lua`: two duplicate helper pairs collapsed:
  `trim_text`/`trim` and `take_first_nonblank`/`take_first_nonempty`.
- `main.lua`: one dead function removed (`append_wrapped_lines` was
  defined but never called). 138 → 137 function definitions; audited
  via a full cross-reference pass.

**Tests**
- End-to-end action-cycle test (`ChatEndpoint_FullActionCycle_*`) —
  chat → outbox-with-ActionIntent → simulated Lua feedback → pre-chat
  sync → memory-with-trace-tags as one transaction.
- Em-dash separator regression
  (`DrainInbox_ActionExecutionFeedback_PreservesTraceTagsAndNotes`) —
  asserts travel and production notes render with a real `—` and never
  carry the classic mojibake byte sequence.
- Relationship-retention regression
  (`RelationshipTracker_BoundedRetention_EvictsLowestScoreWhenCapExceeded`).
- TTS-metrics coverage in `PrometheusExporter_RendersKnownMetricsAndHelpText`.
- Shipped-sample-pack regression
  (`PackValidator_ShippedSamplePack_ValidatesCleanly`) locks the
  starter pack at `docs/examples/camp-guardian-pack.json` against future
  schema drift.

**First-run onramp**
- The starter narrative pack at `docs/examples/camp-guardian-pack.json` is
  now **bundled in the release zip** and **auto-installed** into
  `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Pal\Saved\PalLLM\Packs\` on first install by
  `install-mod.ps1` / `install.bat`. New players see authored
  companion lore immediately instead of only generic deterministic
  fallback replies. Pass `-SkipSamplePack` to the installer to opt
  out; the installer never overwrites an existing copy in the runtime
  Packs folder, so edits are safe across reinstalls.
- Sample pack typo (`Chilllet` → `CampGuardian`) corrected.
- `docs/PACK_AUTHORING.md` now has a "Starter pack" section
  documenting the auto-install flow and the opt-out.

**Tooling and CI**
- `scripts/update-all.ps1` — one-shot developer loop (build + test,
  optionally smoke + delivery replay).
- Shared HTTP helpers (`Invoke-PalLlmApi`, `ConvertTo-PalLlmJsonBody`,
  `Get-PalLlmNormalizedBaseUrl`) in `scripts/PalLLM.Tooling.ps1`. Smoke
  and delivery-replay scripts no longer each redefine their own.
- GitHub Actions workflows: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (build+test
  matrix plus a five-way doc audit that fails the build if
  `/api` route count, feature-catalog count, fallback-strategy count,
  test count, or any markdown link target drifts), and
  `.github/workflows/release.yml` (tagged pushes auto-build a
  `PalLLM-<tag>.zip` GitHub Release).
- Doctor success summary now prints an advisory next-steps checklist
  for optional subsystems.

**Legal / publication readiness**
- `LICENSE` (MIT, © PalLLM contributors).
- `NOTICE.md` — unaffiliated-third-party disclaimer covering trademark
  non-assertion and interop-only framing for any named external API.
- `THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md` — list of runtime components PalLLM depends
  on (.NET runtime, UE4SS, sibling Core library, operator-supplied
  HTTP inference / vision / TTS servers, test-time NuGet packages).
- Disclaimer block at the top of `README.md` pointing at both files.

### Changed

**Documentation consolidation**
- `docs/` trimmed from 15 files / 3584 lines to 10 files / ~3100 lines
  with no content loss:
  - the former Claude handoff note removed; unique debt items migrated
    to `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` §"Known debt".
  - the former operator enablement note removed; feature-matrix table + HUD
    + production-sampler + TTS enablement steps folded into
    `docs/OPERATIONS.md` §"Opt-in feature matrix" and the per-feature
    enable sections.
  - the former sibling-library harvest note removed; replaced by
    `docs/CORE_LIBRARY.md` with generic "sibling Core library" framing.
  - `docs/IMPLEMENTATION_QUEUE.md` trimmed 603 → 384 lines by dropping
    sections that duplicated `docs/ROADMAP.md`.
- `README.md` shortened from 438 to ~248 lines; runtime-surface and
  shipped-behavior sections replaced with pointers into the relevant
  docs to avoid cross-doc duplication.
- Four orphaned files removed from the repo root (`artifacts-dashboard.*.log`,
  `dashboard-*.png`). None were referenced; logs were already
  `.gitignore`d.

**IP-neutrality sweep**
- Every source file audited for non-essential third-party IP mentions.
  Required technical references (UE4SS hook paths, game class names,
  sibling-library namespace imports, NuGet package names) retained and
  disclosed in `THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md`. Decorative brand, academic,
  game-title, and competitor citations removed or genericized across
  `PalLlmFeatureCatalog.cs`, `Configuration/PalLlmOptions.cs`, the
  memory store, reflection service, relationship tracker,
  `PalLlmRuntime.cs`, every inference/vision/TTS client, the circuit
  breaker, `docs/FALLBACK_AI_RESEARCH.md`, `docs/OPERATIONS.md`,
  `docs/QUICKSTART.md`, `docs/ROADMAP.md`, `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`,
  `docs/INDEX.md`, `README.md`, `scripts/doctor.ps1`, and
  `.github/workflows/release.yml`.
- Class renames for IP-neutral naming:
  - `OpenAiCompatibleInferenceClient` → `HttpJsonInferenceClient`
  - `GemmaVisionClient` → `HttpVisionClient`
  - `DashScopeHostMarkers` → `ThinkingToggleHostMarkers`
  - `QwenRecommendedTopP` / `QwenRecommendedPresencePenalty`
    → `DefaultTopP` / `DefaultPresencePenalty`
  - `IsQwenModel` → `HasFamilySamplingPresets`
  - `ShouldSendDashScopeThinkingSwitch` → `ShouldSendThinkingToggle`
- Two test names renamed correspondingly.
- `scripts/package-release.ps1` now bundles `LICENSE`, `NOTICE.md`,
  `THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `install.bat`, and
  `run-delivery-replay.ps1` into release zips. `PLAYER_README.txt`
  rewritten with a clearer fastest-path + troubleshooting + prereqs
  block.
- `README.md` intro rewritten with prerequisites (game install, UE4SS
  v3.x+, .NET 8) and split into a player install path and a developer
  build path.

**Roadmap**
- Player-weighted total moved `87.0%` → `89.6%` across the advance
  work above. Phase 3 `87%` → `91%` (production sampler), Phase 4
  `72.0%` → `78.0%` (HUD scaffold), Phase 5 `84%` → `86%` (speech
  retry, map marker, doctor enablement, relationship retention).
- Test count moved from documented `99` to actual `108` across README,
  ROADMAP, ARCHITECTURE, API, and doctor-drift CI check.
- Feature-catalog entries grew from `32` to `36` (`35 ready /
  1 deferred`) with the four new scaffolds represented.

### Fixed

- **Mojibake in production/travel event notes** in
  `src/PalLLM.Domain/Runtime/PalLlmRuntime.cs`. The runtime had been leaking
  the Windows-1252 re-encoding of an em dash into memory entries, prompts,
  and the dashboard. Replaced the corrupted sequence with a real dash and
  covered it with a regression assertion.
### Removed

- three retired handoff/enablement/harvest docs — content migrated into
  surviving docs (see "Documentation consolidation" above).
- Four orphaned files at repo root: `artifacts-dashboard.err.log`,
  `artifacts-dashboard.out.log`, `dashboard-desktop.png`,
  `dashboard-mobile.png`.
- Empty mod config placeholder directory under `mod/ue4ss/Mods/PalLLM/`.
- Two duplicate Lua helpers (see Runtime section).
